properties.description: A Quinta da Large. ![Image 1]( ## Learn • Build • Grow A Quinta Courses & Workshops ## Join with a friend and receive 10% off • Early bird 10% available • One offer per booking ## Join with a friend and receive 10% off • Early bird 10% available • One offer per booking [![Image 2: The Active Site: Starting the Learning Center, Natural Building Course with Lola Byron 9-12 July 2026]( Jul 9 to 12 Jul ## [The Active Site: Starting the Learning Center, Natural Building Course with Lola Byron 9-12 July 2026]( Step onto a live building site to help us break ground on our new Natural Building Learning Center. Over 4 days, you’ll contribute to the "first chapters" of this living demo house—a project designed to showcase a variety of techniques, from soil analysis to timber framing. Gain a clear roadmap of the building process and the grounded confidence to begin your own journey with the earth. **Price €385 Includes all meals and free camping:** Bring your own campervan or tent and stay on our beautiful off-grid farm at no extra cost. **Join with a friend and receive 10% off** [View Event →]( [![Image 3: The Active Site: Growing the Learning Center, Natural Building Course with Lola Byron 28 August – 2 September 2026]( Aug 28 to 2 Sept ## [The Active Site: Growing the Learning Center, Natural Building Course with Lola Byron 28 August – 2 September 2026]( Step onto the site for a 6-day deep dive into the craft of natural building. We are continuing work on our "living textbook"—a demo house where each wall teaches a different technique. You’ll gain the stamina and skill that comes from a longer build, mastering timber-to-earth integration, soil literacy, and a variety of wall systems. Leave with a clear roadmap for your own future building projects. **Price €620 Includes all meals and free camping:** Bring your own campervan or tent and stay on our beautiful off-grid farm at no extra cost. **Join with a friend and receive 10% off • Early bird 10% available • One offer per booking** [View Event

properties.name: Akasha Hub
properties.description: ## Let’s re-invent the future of society, together. AKASHA Hub is a**civic infrastructure for social innovation** based on community, shared knowledge, and interdisciplinary collaboration to develop projects and tools with social impact and urban regeneration. It is a decentralized and self-coordinated network, with its main node in Barcelona. We are a community made up of diverse professional profiles that connects people, projects, and stakeholders to generate sustainable ideas, explore new possibilities for innovation, and develop new models of society at a glocal scale. ## Participate in our community. We are looking for new collaborators and members who inspire us with their ideas and vision, who value “The Exchange of Knowledge” to build together a better society. ![Image 1]( ![Image 2]( ## 830m2 of possibilities We have spaces for creation, thought/ action, activities, co-working, laboratory, design and manufacturing. Two warehouses located in the Clot, economically supported by its members with the purpose of contributing to the neighborhood and its ecosystem. ## People Since 2017 hundreds of people have passed through AKASHA HUB Barcelona, contributing ideas, connecting, creating projects and inspiring us. **Philosopher, Programmer, Designer, Engineer, Artist, Sociologist, Digital Nomad, Educator, Architect, Producer, Communicator, Doctor…** ![Image 3]( ## Projects We currently have projects created in Akasha Hub and others in collaboration. We are always generating new proposals and connecting with talents with the desire to generate social change. If you want to participate in one of the projects or have an idea, **contact us.** ![Image 4]( ##### Fab Commons Program connects practical technical training, project support, access to infrastructure and connection with an active innovation community. In this program, you will develop skills in manual and digital fabrication, applied innovation, and collaboration within our community ecosystem and the Social a

properties.name: Arcosanti
properties.description: Privacy Policy | Arcosanti, Cosanti & The Cosanti Foundation Home Privacy Policy Privacy Policy Last Modified: June 26, 2020 The Cosanti Foundation and our two architecturally significant sites, Arcosanti and Cosanti, (or “we”, “us”, or “our”, as applicable) value the privacy of our website visitors, system users, and commercial partners. The Cosanti Foundation respects your privacy and is committed to using responsible practices in the collection, maintenance, and use of your personal information. This Privacy Policy, as modified from time to time, describes our practices in connection with information collected through our arcosanti.org website (together with all subdomains and related websites, the “Website”). As a general principle, the information you provide to us will only be used or shared by the Cosanti Foundation as outlined in this Privacy Policy. By visiting or using the Website, you agree to the practices and policies set forth in this Privacy Policy. If you do not agree to the terms of this Privacy Policy, you may not use the Website. 1. Information We Collect We may collect two basic types of information from you in conjunction with your use of the Website – personal information and non-personal information. “Personal information” is any information that can individually identify you and includes, for example, and without limitation, your name, email address, username, telephone number, postal address, and so forth. “Non-personal information” includes information that does not personally identify you, but may include (without limitation) tracking and usage information about your location, demographics, transaction information, use of the Website and the Internet, etc. Personal Information There are a number of circumstances in which you may voluntarily supply us with your personal information. The following lists the most common ways in which we may collect your personal information: ● Sign up to receive information or related services from us ● Regis

properties.name: Bay Area Makerfarm
properties.description: Calendar — Bay Area Makerfarm 0 Recurring and Upcoming Events Compost Hub Wednesdays & Saturdays 10AM-5PM Stop by to pick up free compost, provided by the City of Alameda & StopWaste.org . Please limit yourself to 1 carload or 3 bikeloads of compost per month, thanks! New commercial compost added bi-monthly . Compost Hub open November, 2026. We also have free wood chips for mulching, biochar , and soon selling our own farm compost! Animals Program Youth Open House Days Third Sundays, 12PM-2PM Weekly after school hours coming in December! Come pet goats, chickens, and ducks, and learn about our farm animals! Kids will need to have permission slips signed (at least annually) in order to participate. Farmcycles Free Bike Repair Clinic Spring-Fall, Third Sunday of each month, 12-2PM We have all the tools, some of the expertise, and all the eagerness to get you riding smoothly and safely again. We’ll even show you how to do simple repairs yourself. Community Potlucks Monthly, date varies (see calendar below) Join Makerfarmers, project volunteers, and community members at our monthly potlucks. We welcome you there whether you are able to bring a dish or not. Solar Holiday Open House & Work Parties Come celebrate equinoxes and solstices with us as we prepare the Makerfarm for the upcoming season. 2024 Event Dates, 10AM - 5PM: Spring Equinox: Sunday March 17th Summer Solstice: Sunday Sunday June 16th Fall Equinox: Sunday September 22nd Winter Solstice: Sunday December 15th Annual Summer Music Party & Campout August, see calendar below Starting with a potluck Friday at 5pm, music until midnight, overnight campout, and ending with Saturday brunch. Featuring local bands, and events like clothing swap, workshops from our MakerFarmers, and learning about the farm. Kids welcome! Nobody turned away due to lack of funds, but donations to the farm and the presenters are appreciated. Upcoming Events Join us at the farm in person or online for a volunteer day, a class, or a social mee

properties.name: Chateau Chapiteau
properties.description: Chateau Chapiteau, Tbilisi - Official Site Chateau Chapiteau About Accommodation Booking Contacts Invest The global citizens' haven CHATEAU CHAPITEAU Owned by the community We are a co-living in the Georgian village co-owned by nomads from all over the world. This is our home and it is open for like-minded people. We plan to build decentralised homes around the earth for global citizens. We already have cabins, tents, organic farm, communal house, village house and many other things. We plan to build up to 60 houses on our land. You can already come as a guest and invest becoming our majesty! Common values We get together to support each other and transmit common values to this world – and change it for the better. We create a physical space with nurturing and caring environment for like-minded people to interact more efficiently and charge each other with positive energy. Humans We share love for ourselves, friends and like-minded people, wishing the best to everyone on this planet. We are all kings and queens in our kingdom. Over here, we care for each other and respect personal boundaries, no matter the gender, race, ethnicity and sexual orientation. Science We stand for a scientific approach when making decisions. The world is not perfect, but we are living in the greatest time in history thanks to scientific progress. Security, freedom, comfort, information accessibility and affordable traveling. Democracy We explore progressive ways of collaboration when making decisions. This is a social experiment with future-oriented goals. The Majesties determine the project development. Ecology Clean air, water and food are essential for us as a species to live a full and healthy life. We want to create projects without damaging local eco-systems. We ensure environmental compensation by consuming locally, recycling, reducing packaging waste and using eco-friendly materials. Culture Our project gives space to creativity and self-fulfilment. Everyone is welcome to take part

properties.name: Feytopia
properties.description: Events — Feÿtopia 0 Events & Workshops Welcome to Feÿtopia, where the concept of a “typical day” has yet to be invented. Our days are filled with impromptu, emergent, and participatory events—think improv workshops, music jams, all-night philosophy discussions, intellectual and cosmetic salons, sports (indoor, outdoor, or delightfully unconventional), movie screenings, and talks, just to name a few of the more familiar offerings. Beyond these spontaneous activities, we also host a curated selection of week-long workshops and larger scheduled events, as a chance to go deeper into a topic with a group. From planetary health to love, from science to spirituality : the range of topics is wide. 🗝️ Open Workshops: These are workshops you can join by registering with the listed contact person—reach out, they’re waiting to hear from you! 🔏 Sign-up Workshops: Intrigued by a workshop that’s marked as “Sign-up”? Please contact the facilitator as this workshop requires a dedicated time commitment and full attendance throughout the allocated timeline to participate. 🔒 Closed Workshops: Intrigued by a workshop that’s marked as “closed”? Don’t worry! Even if you can’t participate directly, you’re welcome to stay with us during that time and absorb the vibrant, collective learning atmosphere. And remember, even if you’re not attending the workshops, Feÿtopia is still brimming with joy, spontaneity, and creativity. With plenty of impromptu happenings like music jams, casual sports, and philosophical deep dives, you’ll always find a way to immerse yourself in the magic of Feÿtopia. Feytopia

Events — Feÿtopia 0 Events & Workshops Welcome to Feÿtopia, where the concept of a “typical day” has yet to be invented. Our days are filled with impromptu, emergent, and participatory events—think improv workshops, music jams, all-night philosophy discussions, intellectual and cosmetic salons, sports (indoor, outdoor, or delightfully unconventional), movie screenings, and talks, just to name a few of the more familiar offerings. Beyond these spontaneous activities, we also host a curated selection of week-long workshops and larger scheduled events, as a chance to go deeper into a topic with a group. From planetary health to love, from science to spirituality : the range of topics is wide. 🗝️ Open Workshops: These are workshops you can join by registering with the listed contact person—reach out, they’re waiting to hear from you! 🔏 Sign-up Workshops: Intrigued by a workshop that’s marked as “Sign-up”? Please contact the facilitator as this workshop requires a dedicated time commitment and full attendance throughout the allocated timeline to participate. 🔒 Closed Workshops: Intrigued by a workshop that’s marked as “closed”? Don’t worry! Even if you can’t participate directly, you’re welcome to stay with us during that time and absorb the vibrant, collective learning atmosphere. And remember, even if you’re not attending the workshops, Feÿtopia is still brimming with joy, spontaneity, and creativity. With plenty of impromptu happenings like music jams, casual sports, and philosophical deep dives, you’ll always find a way to immerse yourself in the magic of Feÿtopia. May 1 to May 5 Feÿtopia Feÿnale Fri, May 1, 2026 3:00 PM Tue, May 5, 2026 4:00 PM Château du Feÿ (map) Google Calendar ICS 🗝️ Open Monthly Theme: April | Feÿ Ludens - We play View Event → Apr 20 to Apr 27 Feÿtopia Hyperhuman Mon, Apr 20, 2026 3:00 PM Mon, Apr 27, 2026 4:00 PM Château du Feÿ (map) Google Calendar ICS 🗝️ Open Monthly Theme: April | Feÿ Ludens - We play View Event → Apr 13 to Apr 19 Feÿtopi

properties.name: FractalNYC
properties.description: Fractal Collective Fractal Collective In 2021, our small group of friends decided to live, learn, and build together in NYC. It started as just a single apartment with weekly dinners where people gave 5-minute “lightning talks”, and then we expanded into the apartment next door. And soon, another one. And more. Fast forward a few years, and Fractal has grown into a neighborhood campus: Living near each other has helped us coordinate and incubate a bunch of fun projects. Three we’re especially proud of: Fractal University , where we teach everything from art, to cooking, to coding. Classes are cozy (we teach them from our living rooms), and it’s a lot of fun to learn from and with our friends. Fractal Bootcamp , a three month intensive software engineering bootcamp, and Fractal Tech Hub , our co-working space. merlins place , our first community-run third space (with a 3000-LED lighting installation on the ceiling!) “Is there a vision?” Many of us want to help improve the creative and civic culture in NYC (housing, energy, art, community, flourishing) , but some are just here to live a well-rounded life. ‣ 📚 Fractal University Fractal University offers in-person, community sections of world-class courses, for fun. We have courses in AI, computer science, friendship + community, NYC government, cooking, mind-body sciences, and more. We aim to democratize enjoyable, community education and public research culture by creating an easily replicable model for a decentralized university - and the economic, social, and creative opportunities they create. The dream is 100s of writers, artists, and scientists and 1000s of great works to emerge from this program - but we are really in it just for the love of the game. 🔔 Subscribe to be notified about future semesters here . ❓ Learn more here . 👨🏻‍🏫 If you would like to offer a class as a TA or Instructor, apply here 🏵️ FractalU ‣ 🛌 Stay at Fractal 💡 NOTE : Fractal is a decentralized network of apartments rather than a formal or

properties.name: L'Arbre qui Pousse
properties.description: L'arbre qui Pousse Aller au contenu Bienvenue à l'Arbre qui Pousse Laboratoire de Transition Tiers-lieu rural, nourricier, pédagogique et démonstratif L’Arbre qui Pousse anime un lieu d’émergence de façon collective. Il est espace ouvert de rencontres, qui nous invite à explorer, se développer, se rendre utile et grandir ensemble. L’Arbre est une démonstration tangible qu’une vie collective peut être ouverte et ancrée dans les réalités communes à tous·tes, tout en réinventant nos façons de produire et de consommer, de vivre et d’être en lien. Le Campus de l'Arbre qui Pousse Découvre ici le nouveau projet de formation en écologie engagée que nous mettons en place à partir de mars 2026 Une ferme partagée Un espace ouvert s’étendant sur 6ha de jardins et 2500m² de bâtiments d’exception dans le Brabant Wallon. Des projets multiples Une ruche d’entrepreneur·euses sociaux·ales se déploie à travers des activités économiques au service du vivant Un habitat groupé La ferme accueille plus de 25 habitant·es de tous âges dans différents logements Un lieu d'expérimentation Une invitation ancrée à repenser nos rapports à soi, aux autres, au vivant, au monde Venez nous rencontrer Même hors de nos activités programmées, la ferme est en permanence ouverte et accessible. Notre histoire Le projet est né dans le coeur de quatre jeunes utopistes et pragmatiques en 2017. Trois ans après, accompagné·es d’un groupe de citoyen·nes convaincu·es, ils créent la Fondation Ecotones qui acquiert la Ferme de la Balbrière, afin d’y ancrer le projet. Aujourd’hui, l’Arbre qui Pousse c’est pas moins de 20 porteur·euses de projet, 25 habitant·es actif·ves au quotidien, des centaines d’ami·es et sympathisant·es. Rejoignez l’aventure ! L'Arbre Rencontres, conférences, formations, festivités. Un lieu de vie et de travail vibrant, au service d’une communauté rurale dynamique… En savoir plus Les projets À l’Arbre, nous avons pour mission de promouvoir l’entrepreneuriat social à travers des activités économi

properties.name: Mars College
properties.description: [Video 3]( ## Mars College: A 3-month experiment building an off-grid village to explore future technology and self-preservation. ![Image 1: Construction of pallet rack structures in the desert.]( ### Build a Campus We start on barren land. Martians organize into camps, build their own shelter and infrastructure, and cooperate to take care of everyone's basic needs. ![Image 2: A community gathering for a talk inside a structure at night.]( ### Research & Learn Harness AI, solar, and off-grid tech to explore new frontiers in self-reliance and creativity. ![Image 3: A group doing yoga at sunrise in the desert.]( ### Find Your Joy Pursue self-actualization, develop new skills, and form deep connections with like-minded people. ## Academics Academics at Mars revolve around technology, off-grid living, and other subjects contributed by residents through an open calendar and shared spaces. We build an auditorium, classroom, library, and gallery, then invite Martians to activate them through workshops, installations, and experiences. We extend the idea of self-reliance to AI technology. We believe everyone should know how to build their own personal AIs that help them live, learn, and thrive.[The Eden team]( to run the AI program, grants Martians free compute, and the program culminates in a final exhibition, gallery, and film festival. [Learn more about academics]( ## Life on Mars ## Timeline Nov 1, 2025 ### Build begins Invited build teams arrive to construct major structures and infrastructure. Jan 12, 2026 ### Quarter begins All Martians arrive for orientation, Ideas Week, and the start of the academic program. March 16, 2026 ### Mars Electronica A multi-day final showcase to share projects, research, and art with the community, along with our 4th annual AI Film Festival. April 6, 2026 ### Unbuild Ends All structures are disassembled and stored, returning the land to its original state. ![Image 4: Mars College desert community]( 5: Animating character]( 6: Animating ch

properties.name: MolinoLab
properties.description: > ### Actualización > > > Hay un [post más reciente sobre este proyecto]( > > Éste otro queda a modo de recuerdo del plan original que trazamos. El objetivo de este proyecto es reformar la nave para poderlo convertir en un espacio de trabajo cómodo y agradable. En un espacio multidisciplinar dónde realizar actividades, desde talleres a actividades de expresión corporal, desde proyecciones a exposiciones. Queremos que la nave cumpla las siguientes funciones: * Taller de creación (Un espacio dónde organizar y utilizar las herramientas de madera, metal, electrónica, plástico así como las herramientas de prototipado, impresora 3D, cortadora CNC) * Espacio diáfano dónde realizar actividades de expresión corporal, danza, yoga, etc * Espacio de coworking (Con mesas, sillas, enchufes, etc) * Cafetería (Dónde prepararnos los cafés, tés, infusiones, etc) * Estudio de música. ![Image 1]( ## Nave taller (180.65 m2) * El suelo es de cemento * Las paredes son de cemento con algunas partes de adobe * Tres portones metálicos * Techos de madera * Tejado de teja árabe y uralita * Uralita – 76.1 m2 * Teja – 103.46 m 2 ![Image 2]( 3]( 4]( ## Estudio música (53.90 m2) * El suelo es de cemento * Las paredes son de cemento con algunas partes de adobe * Techos, vigas de hormigón armado * Tejado de uralita – 53.90 m2 ![Image 5]( 6]( 7]( Plano técnico Nave Taller * * * Revelado analógico con Caffenol – MolinoLab Close modal window Enormes gracias a las amigas del coletivo Ropa Tendida por su increible taller de fotografía analógica y revelado utilizando la técnica de Caffenol. Pusieron a disposición del grupo varias cámaras y entre todas decidimos la temática de cada una de ellas; paisaje, antifoto, desnudos, son algunas de esas temáticas. Con las mejores de esas fotografías hicimos una exposición en las tenadas de MolinoLab. El lugar que antaño vió crecer a las terneras acogió durante varios meses la exposición fotográfica. Os dejamos algunas de las fotos del proceso. caffenol foto fotograf

Queremos que la nave cumpla las siguientes funciones: * Taller de creación (Un espacio dónde organizar y utilizar las herramientas de madera, metal, electrónica, plástico así como las herramientas de prototipado, impresora 3D, cortadora CNC) * Espacio diáfano dónde realizar actividades de expresión corporal, danza, yoga, etc * Espacio de coworking (Con mesas, sillas, enchufes, etc) * Cafetería (Dónde prepararnos los cafés, tés, infusiones, etc) * Estudio de música. ![Image 1]( ## Nave taller (180.65 m2) * El suelo es de cemento * Las paredes son de cemento con algunas partes de adobe * Tres portones metálicos * Techos de madera * Tejado de teja árabe y uralita * Uralita – 76.1 m2 * Teja – 103.46 m 2 ![Image 2]( 3]( 4]( ## Estudio música (53.90 m2) * El suelo es de cemento * Las paredes son de cemento con algunas partes de adobe * Techos, vigas de hormigón armado * Tejado de uralita – 53.90 m2 ![Image 5]( 6]( 7]( Plano técnico Nave Taller * * * Revelado analógico con Caffenol – MolinoLab Close modal window Enormes gracias a las amigas del coletivo Ropa Tendida por su increible taller de fotografía analógica y revelado utilizando la técnica de Caffenol. properties.name: OBJEKT4000
properties.description:
We aim to harness and transform the historical significance of the abandoned bunker into a meaningful place for people to explore, create, and express themselves. Other than churches and libraries, our urban environments lack places for people to just exist in, not primarily consume. Objekt4000 relieves us from being consumers, and invites us to connect with ourselves .
Reach out to:
https://www.instagram.com/und.er.berlin/?hl=en

properties.name: Raddish
properties.description: Raddish. 🗞️ [Video 5]( ## Sharing life with friends in Oakland ![Image 1: image]( ## About Radish Radish is an intentional community for people who believe in the **"Obvious Truth"** - _that we are happiest and healthiest surrounded by people we love and admire_. We design our home around the people who make us the best versions of ourselves. We are 20 adults and 8 kids (all born at Radish) living in a 6-building, 10-home compound in the Temescal/Longfellow neighborhood in Oakland. It’s an experiment in whether parenting with young kids can be easier and more joyful. ![Image 2: image]( The community was founded in 2019 and is proudly [co-owned]( by the residents and the wider community ## Media coverage on Radish * [It shouldn’t be so hard]( to live near your friends (Vox) * [Should more of us be moving]( to live near friends? (Architectural Digest) * [What it’s like to parent with your friends]( Forum) * [Baby happy hours: How to make parenting less lonely]( (Guardian) * An [article]( one of our residents wrote in the NYTimes Modern Love column: * A [video tour]( Radish from Kirsten Dirksen with interviews of residents * A [case study on Radish]( in Supernuclear a blog about coliving * [Life with babies]( Radish - “Baby Happy Hour” and living at “Baby Monitor Distance” * A [feature]( in the California Sunday Magazine “12 Roommates Trying to Avoid the Suburbs” * [Co-buying property with friends]( (Star Tribune) [Video 6]( A tour of Radish with Kirsten Dirksen ## How to start your own Radish * [Live Near Friends]( - Buy homes right next door to friends and family (started by Radish founder Phil Levin) * [How to live near friends]( - a webinar on 6 models that work * [Co-buying property with friends]( - the mega guide * [The Radish LLC Model]( how the financial plumbing works * [Planting a flag]( Getting started ![Image 3: image]( ![Image 4: image]( ![Image 5: image]( ![Image 6: image]( ![Image 7: image]( ![Image 8: image]( ![Image 9: image]( ![Image 10: image]( ![Image 11: im

Raddish

Raddish. 🗞️ [Video 5]( ## Sharing life with friends in Oakland ![Image 1: image]( ## About Radish Radish is an intentional community for people who believe in the **"Obvious Truth"** - _that we are happiest and healthiest surrounded by people we love and admire_. We design our home around the people who make us the best versions of ourselves. We are 20 adults and 8 kids (all born at Radish) living in a 6-building, 10-home compound in the Temescal/Longfellow neighborhood in Oakland. It’s an experiment in whether parenting with young kids can be easier and more joyful. ![Image 2: image]( The community was founded in 2019 and is proudly [co-owned]( by the residents and the wider community ## Media coverage on Radish * [It shouldn’t be so hard]( to live near your friends (Vox) * [Should more of us be moving]( to live near friends? (Architectural Digest) * [What it’s like to parent with your friends]( Forum) * [Baby happy hours: How to make parenting less lonely]( (Guardian) * An [article]( one of our residents wrote in the NYTimes Modern Love column: * A [video tour]( Radish from Kirsten Dirksen with interviews of residents * A [case study on Radish]( in Supernuclear a blog about coliving * [Life with babies]( Radish - “Baby Happy Hour” and living at “Baby Monitor Distance” * A [feature]( in the California Sunday Magazine “12 Roommates Trying to Avoid the Suburbs” * [Co-buying property with friends]( (Star Tribune) [Video 6]( A tour of Radish with Kirsten Dirksen ## How to start your own Radish * [Live Near Friends]( - Buy homes right next door to friends and family (started by Radish founder Phil Levin) * [How to live near friends]( - a webinar on 6 models that work * [Co-buying property with friends]( - the mega guide * [The Radish LLC Model]( how the financial plumbing works * [Planting a flag]( Getting started ![Image 3: image]( ![Image 4: image]( ![Image 5: image]( ![Image 6: image]( ![Image 7: image]( ![Image 8: image]( ![Image 9: image]( ![Image 10: image]( ![Image 11: im

Sende is an ideal location for hiking🚶🏾 running and biking 🚴🏻. You can actually cross the border with Portugal, see wild horses, by following the mountain trail. properties.name: Silverto
properties.description: ## A fusion of regenerative agroforestry and quiet living Silverto is 1.5 hectares of land near Paredes de Coura, in the green hills of northern Portugal. Part forest, part food forest in the making, part home — it is a place being shaped with intention, patience, and a deep respect for the landscape that holds it. ### What we are building The stone house at the centre of Silverto is partly renovated and lived in. The land around it is being managed as a food forest — trees establishing, soil improving, diversity returning. This is not a finished project, and that is precisely the point. We are learning, adjusting, and building something that is meant to last. Beyond the immediate land, Silverto sits within a wider fabric of neighbours and initiatives across northern Portugal who share a similar outlook — people looking for a different relationship with land, with work, and with one another. ### How we work We practise regenerative agroforestry — working with natural systems rather than against them, increasing the diversity and resilience of the land over time. We welcome guests and volunteers who want to learn alongside us, contribute meaningfully, and experience what it feels like to live at the edge of a forest. If you are curious, self-directed, and drawn to this way of living, Silverto may be the right place to spend some time. ### Partners * [![Image 1: Moving Cause]( * [![Image 2: Reflorestar Portugal]( * [![Image 3: Decentralised Society Research Project]( ### Talk to us We are happy to meet you! Send us an email or join our chat to start the conversation! * [info@silverto.pt](mailto:info@silverto.pt) * [Telegram]( * [LinkedIn]( Quinta do Silverto Silverto Spring Party 2026 We're thrilled to announce our annual Spring Party, taking place on Saturday, May 30th, 2026 â a full moon night! â from the afternoon at 14:00 into the evening and through the next morning at Silverto. Help set up on Friday the 29th, and join the collective cleanup on Sunday the 31s

properties.name: Sirius Community
properties.description: [![Image 1: Community Center]( Community Center and Greenhouse ## **Sirius Celebrates Its 40 th Year!** As we enter our fortieth year of innovative sustainable living in community, we embrace a new era of growth and expansion. New member and interim Executive Director John Broucek have initiated a process of **_strategic planning_** for community development that includes: * Modifications of our governance and decision-making process to enhance responsiveness to new ideas and initiatives, * Expansion of our off-grid, green energy production to not only satisfy our own needs but also supply enough excess for local power companies to provide a steady income for the community, * New in-house educational modules in the areas of green energy production, sustainable agriculture (permaculture), and spiritually attuned community living. ## **Saying Goodbye to Founding Member Bruce Davidson** [Video 3]( Bruce Davidson passed on November 15th surrounded by family, friends, and community mates. Those of us with him during his final hours saw the humor and wisdom of him at his best. He was ready to go, excited about the next journey, and satisfied with a life that enriched and reached too many to calculate. More than 200 gathered to send him off on December 3rd, first in the Community Center Octagon, and then in the Standing Stone Circle for a candlelit celebration of his life. Both sites were carefully constructed over time under his direction and care, and none of us could help but be aware of his presence seen and felt in every board, nail, and stone. At his request, gifts and donations earmarked for the community’s future development via the Bruce Davidson Memorial Fund can be given through the DONATE button to the right. As with all donations to Sirius, all gifts are tax-deductible. ## **40 th Anniversary Gala Celebration** Please plan to join us to celebrate Sirius Community’s 40th anniversary in September (date to be announced). We have a vision for spectacular gathering

properties.name: Sundari Gardens
properties.description: ![Image 1]( Today is the second anniversary of us acquiring the land. It was love at first sight for us, standing there on the Kaimu hillside, in April of 2017- the hypersaturated color and open horizon, the mature fruit trees and the feeling of magic. The owners had long vacated and left shacks and debris and a leaking container home. Jeff and Amie and their little boys were inhabiting one of the structures. We set about manifesting the vision. The first 6 months went pretty well- making the moon cabin into a temporary home (a love shack in the winter rains and a haven of tranquility), clearing the land, upgrading systems, finding a rhythm, listening to the what wanted to be born here. And then not so well for a while. Disagreements and budgets and artistic debates and differing values. Conflicts between the guys. Then Pele made the decision for us: she shook the land so strongly that only a few people remained. David Art Lawell filmed the sunrises and skyscapes through the smoke and painted a mural of her using the glassy brown “hair” coming off the volcano. Ken Reid heroically rescued and salvaged all summer long, taking the community in his arms. When she stopped flowing, we had a decision to make, changed the plans somewhat and ended up choosing to repair the damage and see the commitment and vision through. Craig and Lauren and Megan showed up, and the rest is miraculous. John Shiva Gray and I are overjoyed by Suzanne Sterling’s message below, as she leaves the land after an Artist in Residence period. This is what we hoped for. 2 years in, there are a few more things to complete, a well, a gathering space, Craig has a vision for a propagation and extraction/pressing space. My daughter dreams of a nanofarm. Our much dreamed-of pradakshina pathway. We will get to them eventually. In the meantime, the family of writers and artists and yogis and friends that have come is our pure joy. We are hoping this will be a semi public garden and a benefit to the community

properties.name: Traditional Dream Factory
properties.description: Traditional Dream Factory. #### 2021 Keys to the Farm & Basics * Event venue (100 guests) * 10 glampers * 2 000 trees planted * 7 kW solar * Workshop #### 2022 Operational Co-living * 1 000-tree food forest * Sauna installed * Coworking barn * DAO prototype * Architecture plans #### 2023 Platform & Token Launch * Starlink online * 14 glampers, 8 volunteer beds * Market garden * Grey-water wetlands * Booking platform + token * Engineering plans #### 2024 Water Infrastructure & Fundraising * Raised €400 k in private debt * Broke ground on the natural swimming pool (now 90 % complete) * Excavated the first lake and added a second swale feeding it * Installed a 50 m³ rain-harvest storage tank #### 2025 Build-out & Scaling * Upgraded floors, windows & doors for event space, cowork, restaurant shell * Added French drains to capture all roof runoff * Renovated the team house (new roof, full interior refit) * Grew token-holder community to 280 members; enabled bank payments for tokens * Developed our masterplan - and started zoning process for cohousing * Advanced the mushroom farm to 70% completion — we are now eating mushrooms! * Began construction of "silo hobbit houses"; tree-house concept approved * Natural pool edging and filtration finish-out scheduled for Q4 #### 2026 Hospitality Build Out * Build the en-suite co-living rooms, restaurant, and studios * Install a commercial kitchen and finish the farm-to-table restaurant * Finalise co-housing construction drawings and permitting #### 2027 Operations & Staffing * Hire core operations team for hospitality and land stewardship * Launch full-scale hospitality operations with licensed accommodation ## Become Part of Our Journey Join us in building a regenerative future. Choose how you want to be part of the Traditional Dream Factory story. properties.description: Hiring! | Join Our Community Today — Wild Seeds Ranch 0 Wild Seeds Ranch MGMT 5/11/26 Wild Seeds Ranch MGMT 5/11/26 Wild Seeds General Manager Position Wild Seeds Ranch, a creative coliving community outside San Diego, is looking for a part-time General Manager to grow into full operational responsibility of the property. The role is 16–20 hours a week with $1,280/month in rent credit and a 0.5% annual equity stake in the company. If you've lived in community before and want to help steward something already alive and growing, we'd love to hear from you! Check out the JD here: Read More Wild Seeds General Manager Position — Wild Seeds Ranch 0 Wild Seeds General Manager Position May 11 Written By Wild Seeds Ranch MGMT The Role Intro to our Community: Wild Seeds Ranch is looking for a General Manager to grow into full operational capacity of a living, working coliving community outside of San Diego, California. This is the flagship property of Lizzie Maggie DeCo (named after the woman who invented monopoly as a critique on real estate), which has designed a shared ownership model that allows for shares to be earn via labor or investment, and rewards people for improving the place they live. This role is available because the visionary community builders involved have now decided to relocate to Europe and build more regenerative village projects over there. This is not a traditional property management role, but prior experience of operating colivings is highly preferred. You'll be stepping into a community with an established culture, a growing revenue base, and a clear mission, and over the course of your first year, you will be growing roots to thrive here. Ultimately, this is your dream come true if you want to live in community and you’re someone who wants to continue to make Wild Seeds amazing, not just maintain it. Commitment: 16–20 hours per week Compensation: $1,280/month in rent credit offset + 0.5% ownership stake in the company annually for 4 years, with an

properties.description: Yestermorrow Design/Build School Skip to main content Back Close Home Think with Your Hands Our Approach Design/Build with us We believe that architects should know how to build, and builders should know how to design. By uniting these concepts into a continuous feedback loop, we create better buildings and well-rounded tradespeople. We Have a Course for You From true beginners to professionals, we have a course for you. Most of our workshops are suitable for absolute beginners looking to dive more deeply into the trades, while others are perfect for professionals who want to "green up" their skillset. All Are Welcome We welcome people of all backgrounds, identities, and experiences. Whether you've never picked up a hammer or have had a lifelong career in the trades, we have a place for you here. properties.name: Ängsbacka
properties.description: Ängsbacka Course & Festival Center - Welcome Home to Yourself ; Ängsbacka Course & Festival Center ~ welcome home to yourself! Ängsbacka is more than a venue for courses, festivals and volunteer programs. The daily running is secured by a core working community of predominately volunteers, who offer their dedication and service to the vision of this unique meeting place. Countless people relate to Ängsbacka as a safe haven and a home away from home; a place where they are welcome exactly as they are. We long to meet You! For more than 25 years, since the start in 1996 thousands of people have come here with the longing to explore and celebrate life through openhearted connection and presence. Today we are a well-established venue and source of inspiration with world-wide visitors and a thriving network of renowned teachers and musicians. Our offers include supportive and holistic tools and practices such as yoga, tantra, meditation, dance, coaching and much more! properties.name: Aurora Community
properties.description: aurora lifestyle – Living with purpose and connection Hit enter to search or ESC to close Living and Working Together Aurora Lifestyle A lifestyle about individuality and togetherness , living with purpose and connection . A positive influence in society with a low impact in the ecosystem Close to nature with all the comfort and the beauty of simplicity Living and working together for self-sufficiency and resilience Doing what we love most with other like minded people Land Stewardship It's time to make nature a priority. Let's change the world together. Know more about the Permanent Forest Fund and how you can support the protection of endangered forests and its conservation across generations, in the region of the Carpathian Mountains, in Romania. Know more We have a long track record of wide-reaching effects in the areas of community building , social entrepreneurship and environmental innovation for the conservation and regeneration of natural ecosystems and climate change mitigation. Contact us Help us to buy and protect Aurora's beautiful surrounding endangered forest. Donate The Western Romanian Carpathian Mountains , are home to the last wild wolves and bears in Europe. This region is unfortunately at great risk of deforestation. We don’t own the land where we live. We are merely the stewards of 25ha of land and forest. The next goal is to protect the total of 50ha. 4612 Funds raised 19 Donnors 45388 Funds needed Our Projects Where some people see challenges, we see opportunities. We live and work remotely, and we love it. Visitors and Volunteers Thinking of staying with us? Due to Covid19 situation we have been close to visitors and volunteers. We hope to re-take all our activities as soon as possible. Contact us We believe in happiness and harmony for the present and the future generations, living together, united in diversity, in a more sustainable way. We work to comply with the UN Sustainable Development Goals. This is a short video of our first year's

properties.name: Cambia
properties.description: We hold a variety of income sharing, cost sharing and profit sharing agreements, based on circumstance and individual need. Our goal is to equalize labor and effort, maintain motivation for efficiency and value, provide for those with varying levels of ability, encourage growth, and share in our abundance. Attitude, intention, and attention are highly considered, but so are results. We try to understand the pitfalls of both capitalism and socialism, and create a human-centered system for meeting our needs, feeling reciprocity, and encouraging achievement. This being said, we are trying to reduce our need for "money" as we make a lot of what we need on our own or by trading labor with the other local communities. For example, we generate our own energy with our off-grid systems, we build our own infrastructure and furniture, we grow a ton of food and preserve the abundance, we chop our firewood, we butcher deer that the neighboring hunters don't want, we ferment foods like sauerkraut and kombucha, save seeds, and plant fruit and nut trees. We can sew clothes and repair tools. In this way, we reduce our need for money. Our main income areas currently include: Farming with [Commonwealth Seed Growers]( Custom made tiny houses [Our education center,Rustling Roots]( Hosting [Airbnb]( Hosting [Hipcamp]( Personal off-site or part-time online jobs [![Image 1: Picture]( 2: Picture]( [![Image 3: Picture]( [![Image 4: Picture]( 5: Picture]( **Neighboring Communities for the Local Currency:**We work at our neighboring income-sharing communities for labor exchange hours. [![Image 1]( [![Image 2]( [![Image 3]( [![Image 4: Picture]( is nestled within 15 acres, with about 5 of which is mostly a thicket of young scrubby vegetation and about 10 acres of mature (80 year old or so) forest. We have a small old house (over 100 years old) that we have restored and currently using as our common house: it has our kitchen, living room, and office. Personal dwellings are small and modest. properties.name: Cristaland - Peaceful Kingdom
properties.description: Funny & smart facts about the birds at Cristaland (part 1) top of page Search Azure winged magpie: The Azure winged magpie occurs from sea level up to an altitude of 1600 meters. Like almost all corvids, the Azure magpie is an omnivore. The food spectrum includes insects and their larvae as well as nuts and fruits. The Azure magpie reaches a total length of 33-38 centimeters with a weight of 76-118 grams and a wingspan of 38-40 centimeters. Did you know? Known for its striking azure wings, the Azure winged magpie is truly a sight to behold in the avian world. With its elegant demeanor, one might imagine this bird engaging in sophisticated activities like sipping tea with its pinky up, adding a touch of whimsy to its already charming presence. Bee-eaters: The bee-eater prefers warm climates. Its distribution area extends from southwest and western Asia, northwest Africa and southern and southeast Europe northwards to southeast Poland. It is one of the most colorful, hard-to-mistake birds in Europe. In flight, the bee-eater primarily preys on the hymenoptera bees, wasps, bumblebees, hornets, but also dragonflies, cicadas and flying beetles. Bee-eaters have a body length of 25-29 cm and a wingspan of 36-40 cm. Did you know? The Bee-eaters are like the trendsetters of the bird community, always flaunting their vibrant and stylish plumage. Their fashion-forward approach to bird attire makes them stand out as the runway models of nature, effortlessly impressing onlookers with their colorful and chic appearance. Blackbirds: The blackbird's typical concert is simply part of a warm summer evening, as is its warning call when a cat sneaks up on you. The males are black and have a yellow beak; the females' plumage is mostly dark brown. Blackbirds are flexible and adaptable omnivores, but rely on small amounts of animal food throughout the year. When this becomes scarce they feed on berries and other fruits. The body length is between 24 and 27 centimeters. The wing length is b

Like almost all corvids, the Azure magpie is an omnivore. The food spectrum includes insects and their larvae as well as nuts and fruits. The Azure magpie reaches a total length of 33-38 centimeters with a weight of 76-118 grams and a wingspan of 38-40 centimeters. Did you know? Known for its striking azure wings, the Azure winged magpie is truly a sight to behold in the avian world. With its elegant demeanor, one might imagine this bird engaging in sophisticated activities like sipping tea with its pinky up, adding a touch of whimsy to its already charming presence. Bee-eaters: The bee-eater prefers warm climates. Its distribution area extends from southwest and western Asia, northwest Africa and southern and southeast Europe northwards to southeast Poland. It is one of the most colorful, hard-to-mistake birds in Europe. In flight, the bee-eater primarily preys on the hymenoptera bees, wasps, bumblebees, hornets, but also dragonflies, cicadas and flying beetles. Bee-eaters have a body length of 25-29 cm and a wingspan of 36-40 cm. Did you know? The Bee-eaters are like the trendsetters of the bird community, always flaunting their vibrant and stylish plumage. Their fashion-forward approach to bird attire makes them stand out as the runway models of nature, effortlessly impressing onlookers with their colorful and chic appearance. Blackbirds: The blackbird's typical concert is simply part of a warm summer evening, as is its warning call when a cat sneaks up on you. The males are black and have a yellow beak; the females' plumage is mostly dark brown. Blackbirds are flexible and adaptable omnivores, but rely on small amounts of animal food throughout the year. When this becomes scarce they feed on berries and other fruits. The body length is between 24 and 27 centimeters. The wing length is b

properties.name: Eco Adventure Org
properties.description: Homebase - Eco-Adventure.Org - Sustainable trips for savvy travelers Homebase Print Email FOR ADVENTURE TRAVELLING . * Live without limits. * Enjoy a virtual adventure trip right now. Have you ever thought of living for a while like Robinson Crusoe ? Have you ever dreamed to explore the world like Indiana Jones ? Would you like to roam the wild nature like Crocodile Dundee or ride through the country, free like Lucky Luke ? You can do it. Join us on the sunny island Sicily ! Live the adventure of your life ! Travel throughout the world. By foot or on Horse . About us: The Eco-Adventure.Org is a NPO and NGO for Adventure Travelling and Animal-assisted Therapy . "NPO" means we are a "Non Profit Organisation", so the focus of our projects is non-monetary. NGO (Non Government Organisation) means we are independent from any government or politics. So we are free to do what we want. And everything is possible! As we are working on the self-efficacy and the self-confidence of the individual, we are as well working on the society as a whole. We believe that life gets better together! That's why you profit and finally also the governments. We offer as diverse experiences as possible to young people and families who have little or no chance to travel. And that's not all... Our Offers : + Adventure Travels throughout Europe especially for teenagers and young adults. + Adventure and Extreme Sport on the ground, in the water and in the air. + Animal-assisted Therapy with dogs, donkeys, horses, ponys... + Team and Manager Training , e.g. in the "sicilian jungle" + Outdoor Education „Learning by Doing“ Learn practical things for Life. + Live free in selfsufficiency on the sunny island of Sicily! Our Vision: An "Adventure Base Camp" with Action Playground, a farm for animal assisted therapy and self sufficiency by permaculture, wind, water & solarpower. To realize this vision we found the perfect place, in the middle of paradise: Our Adventure Hill : 200.000 square metres in the no

properties.name: Gemeinschaft Sulzbrunn
properties.description: Wir sind eine bunte Mischung von Menschen aller Altersstufen, mit unterschiedlichsten Berufen, Erfahrungen und Lebenssituationen. In Sulzbrunn leben etwa 50 Erwachsene und knapp 20 Kinder. Uns eint eine Überzeugung: Der Mensch ist ein Gemeinschaftswesen. Er braucht andere Menschen und soziale Beziehungen, um seine Fähigkeiten zu entwickeln und ein sinnerfülltes Leben zu führen. Deshalb haben wir uns entschieden, unsere bisherigen Leben als Single, Paare oder Kleinfamilie zusammenzulegen, damit etwas Größeres daraus entstehen kann – eine Gemeinschaft. Nicht als freundliches Nebeneinander – wie Nachbarn in einem Mietshaus. Sondern als bewusstes, aufeinander Zugehen, sich einlassen, in Beziehung treten. Vertrauensvoll, offen und achtsam. Wir haben kein geistiges, politisches Dogma oder Glaubensvorgaben irgendwelcher Art. Aber jeder von uns hat auf seine Weise erkannt, dass alles miteinander verbunden ist und es eine geistige Wirklichkeit gibt, die größer ist als unsere persönliche. ## Drei Säulen Wir haben unsere Gemeinschaft in drei Säulen organisiert, die zusammen Sulzbrunn tragen: **Der Stiftungsfond Sulzbrunn** ist Eigentümer von Grund und Boden. Damit ist Sulzbrunn auf Dauer gemeinnützigen Zielen verpflichet. Verwaltet wird unser Fond von der [grund-stiftung am Schloss Tempelhof]( **Die Gemeinschaft Sulzbrunn eG**hat als Genossenschaft die Immobilie in Erbpacht vom Stiftungsfond übernommen und bewirtschaftet Sulzbrunn. **[Der Förderverein Sulzbrunn e.V.]( führt gemeinnützige Projekte vor Ort durch. ## Arbeiten und … Sulzbrunn zu bewirtschaften und unser Seminarhaus zu betreiben, erfordert jede Menge ehrenamtliche Arbeit. Um die einzelnen Verantwortungsbereiche kümmern sich vier Arbeitskreise mit klar definierten Kompetenzen. Die vielfältigen Aufgaben sind auf alle Bewohnerinnen und Bewohner aufgeteilt. Wir teilen uns die Verantwortung meistens in kleinen Teams. Übergreifende Angelegenheiten berät und entscheidet ein Lenkungskreis mit 8 bis 10 Menschen. Wir haben u

properties.name: Khula Dharma Eco Village
properties.description: We are currently going through a transition period and as such are on a pause with volunteers and retreats. We may have medium to long term accommodation options available, so you can contact us about this. ![Image 1: Rondavel at Khula Dharma]( We are here Experience Natural Living ## Khula Dharma Natural Farming Community Khula Dharma is a natural farming community situated 10km from the coastal hamlet of Haga-Haga in the Eastern Cape, South Africa. The farm is in a sub-tropical climate zone that allows for year-round cultivation. Our retreat space includes traditional thatched accommodation huts, a beautiful functions hall, and communal facilities that support holistic healing and learning events. ## Not a hotel. A living community. Khula Dharma is a self-catering, self-serving sustainable stay. Guests are welcomed into a real off-grid environment with shared responsibility, natural rhythms, wildlife awareness, and practical daily care. ## Stay Earth rooms, camping, and simple self-catering stays close to the land. [Explore]( ## Volunteer 4+ week stays contributing 15-20 hours weekly in exchange for accommodation. [Explore]( ## Host a Retreat Yoga, meditation, healing, or learning retreats in a rustic nature setting. [Explore]( ## Explore River, dam, trails, wildlife, and 180 hectares of Wild Coast land. [Explore]( ## Life on The Hill Earth-built rooms, a yoga hall, lapa, Pixie Palace, river and dam access, hiking trails, food forest, wildlife, and a daily rhythm shaped by practical sustainable living. ![Image 2: Earth-built room]( 3: Food forest and plants]( 4: Community life]( The Hill is our social hub of learning and co-living, where guests, long-term residents, volunteers, and facilitators share space, skills, and community. ## Our Farm @khula_dharma ### Natural Buildings Our buildings are made from cob, clay, thatch, and local materials from the land wherever possible. ### Home-Grown Food We maintain large gardens and food forests packed with healthy organic

properties.name: LebensGut Cobstädt
properties.description: ## Herzlich willkommen - es wurde Herbst und Zeit zum Saften... und... ## nun bereiten wir die Jahresplanung für 2026 vor... ![Image 1]( Ihren Besuch in 2025 haben wir uns sehr gefreut - auch und gerade im realen Leben. Bei jenen, die sich für uns Zeit nahmen... die z.B. beim Saften halfen und beim gemeinsamen Plausch für nette und oft frohe Gespräche sorgten... bedanken wir uns hiermit nocheinmal. Wir freuen uns auf ein Wiedersehen in 2026... Herzlicher Dank geht auch an die deutsche Post-Code-Lotterie, mit Hilfe deren Zuwendung wir 2025 die Komplettierung der Infrastruktur unseres "Lernort Bauernhof" realisieren konnten. Herzlich, Ihr LebensGut Cobstädt e.V. _„Die Probleme, die es in der Welt gibt, sind nicht mit der gleichen Denkweise zu lösen, die sie erzeugt hat.“_ Albert Einstein, 1929 ![Image 2]( 3]( 4]( Die biologische Landwirtschaft des Vereins wird subventiniert (KULAP 2014/2020) im Rahmern des Filet-Programmes aus Mitteln der europäischen Union: [![Image 5](

properties.name: Lifechanyuan International Family Thailand Branch
properties.description: [Video 3]( Welcome to the**Lifechanyuan International Family Thailand Branch**! Our branch is located in a peaceful and beautiful ecovillage supported by the local temple. It is approximately**180 km**away from**Chiang Mai city**and**80 km**away from**Chiang Rai city**. The ecovillage was started in September 2020 and currently has 12 members living together. Our aim is to create and build a new lifestyle model for people who are kind, diligent, simple, honest, trustworthy, and who would love to contribute themselves to human peace and sustainable development of the world. Our farm has a beautiful natural environment, with faraway mountains surrounded by fairyland clouds and mists. You can hear melodious birdsong from the rain trees throughout the day. We have beautiful flowers blooming under the sunshine, and all kinds of organic vegetables are lush and neatly planted in the fields. Ducks are chasing and playing in the river happily, while buffalos and horses are grazing leisurely on the open grassland. People living here are diligent ,kind, happy and most civilized, they joke and laugh a lot every day, creating a paradise filled with love and auspiciousness. The Thailand branch has a total area of 80 acres, including residential areas, a public kitchen, activity halls, pavilions, grassland, woodlands, vegetable and flower gardens, orchards, banana forests, rivers, and small lakes. In addition to members of our community, we also have some neighbors living in the courtyard. We help and get along with each other happily and harmoniously. We welcome people from all over the world, regardless of your backgrounds, races, religions, cultures, and nations. As long as you are interested in this new lifestyle mode, please come to visit us, experience this happy and simple life together, and help create, improve, and perfect this new lifestyle mode for all kind and lovely people on earth! We wish you a happy and unforgettable stay. Please contact us**at least 10 days before
properties.popup:
Lifechanyuan International Family Thailand Branch

[Video 3]( Welcome to the**Lifechanyuan International Family Thailand Branch**! Our branch is located in a peaceful and beautiful ecovillage supported by the local temple. It is approximately**180 km**away from**Chiang Mai city**and**80 km**away from**Chiang Rai city**. The ecovillage was started in September 2020 and currently has 12 members living together. Our aim is to create and build a new lifestyle model for people who are kind, diligent, simple, honest, trustworthy, and who would love to contribute themselves to human peace and sustainable development of the world. Our farm has a beautiful natural environment, with faraway mountains surrounded by fairyland clouds and mists. You can hear melodious birdsong from the rain trees throughout the day. We have beautiful flowers blooming under the sunshine, and all kinds of organic vegetables are lush and neatly planted in the fields. Ducks are chasing and playing in the river happily, while buffalos and horses are grazing leisurely on the open grassland. People living here are diligent ,kind, happy and most civilized, they joke and laugh a lot every day, creating a paradise filled with love and auspiciousness. The Thailand branch has a total area of 80 acres, including residential areas, a public kitchen, activity halls, pavilions, grassland, woodlands, vegetable and flower gardens, orchards, banana forests, rivers, and small lakes. In addition to members of our community, we also have some neighbors living in the courtyard. We help and get along with each other happily and harmoniously. We welcome people from all over the world, regardless of your backgrounds, races, religions, cultures, and nations. properties.name: NeoTribe Gurukulam
properties.description: Deep Ecological Fellowship – Greencross Foundation Skip to the content 61/23, Palakkad, Kerala, India -678102 91-4923-227-877 Deep Ecological Fellowship Feb 25 2025 0 Deep Ecological Fellowship: A Path to Harmonious Coexistence The Deep Ecological Fellowship is a transformative initiative that nurtures a profound connection between humanity and nature. Rooted in the principles of deep ecology and ecosophy, this fellowship seeks to cultivate an ecocentric consciousness that transcends anthropocentric perspectives and fosters a holistic, sustainable way of life. The Philosophy of Deep Ecological Fellowship Deep ecology, as a philosophy, asserts that all forms of life have intrinsic value, independent of their utility to humans. It challenges the dominant worldview that places humanity at the center of existence and instead promotes a relational understanding of life, emphasizing interconnectedness and mutual respect. The Deep Ecological Fellowship embodies these values, guiding individuals toward a more compassionate and responsible engagement with the environment. Core Objectives Cultivating Ecological Awareness – Encouraging a shift from egocentric to ecocentric perspectives through education and experiential learning. Promoting Sustainable Practices – Integrating permaculture, regenerative agriculture, and self-sufficient living into daily life. Fostering Eco-Spirituality – Exploring the spiritual dimensions of ecological consciousness, inspired by indigenous wisdom and ancient traditions. Community Building – Establishing eco-communities that function as models of sustainable living and collective well-being. Environmental Advocacy – Engaging in ecological activism and policy advocacy to protect natural ecosystems and biodiversity. Activities and Engagement Participants in the fellowship engage in a range of activities designed to deepen their ecological understanding and practical application, including: Workshops and Retreats – Immersive sessions on deep ecology

properties.description: Welcome friends, to Shri Hairakhandi Surya Kiran Dham! The ashram in the hills of Bulgaria is meant as a place where people from all backgrounds can meet in truth, simplicity and love. An ashram means a spiritual home. Surya Kiran Dham is based on the teachings of Haidhkahan Babaji and Amritanandamayi Mata. It is open to people of all backgrounds to pursue their spiritual development. Learn more here. You can also learn about our schedule and find out everything you need to know before visiting. Hearty welcome!!

properties.name: Windberg Gemeinschaft
properties.description: Windberg Gemeinschaft. []( **Willkommen beim Lebenslernort am Windberg ...** ![Image 1]( ... lichtvoller Kraftort im Herzen Deutschlands ![Image 2]( ... Zuhause einer generationsübergreifenden Lebensgemeinschaft ![Image 3]( ... Lernumfeld einer freien Gemeinschaftsschule ![Image 4]( ... Veranstaltungsort für zukunftsweisende Seminare und Festivals ![Image 5]( ... Begegnungsraum für Menschen der Region ![Image 6]( ... Forschungsraum für das "wahre Ich" und ein "neues Wir" Das ehemalige Kinder- und Jugenddorf "Am W indberg" am Rande des kleinen Dorfes Beichlingen in Nordthüringen bietet uns als Lebensgemeinschaft mit seinem zehn Hektar großen, naturrreichen Gelände einen wundervollen Raum, unsere Vision eines neuen Miteinanders zu erforschen. Hier leben, gestalten und bieten wir einen inspirierenden, weltoffenen Lebenslernort für zukunftsfähiges, nachhaltiges, heilsames und spirituelles Zusammenleben und Wirken. Wir gehen für ein bewusstes, friedvolles, kreatives und freudvolles Zusammensein. **Projektaufbau** **seit:** **2015** **Aktuelle** **Mitgliederzahl:** **40** ![Image 7]( **Du magst uns gerne** **mal vorort besuchen?** ![Image 8]( **Du magst gerne regelmäßig** **über****uns informiert werden?** ![Image 9]( **Du magst uns dabei unterstützen** **unsere Vision umzusetzen?** _"Wenn einer allein träumt, ist es nur ein Traum._ _Wenn viele gemeinsam träumen, ist das der Anfang einer neuen Wirklichkeit._ _"_ _(Friedensreich Hundertwasser)_ []( **Das "Windland"** (Stand 10/2024) Die Windberg-Gemeinschaft besteht aktuell aus 14 Frauen und 13 Männern (Alter: 36 - 76 Jahre), sowie 13 Kindern (1 - 16 Jahre). Etwa 50 Erwachsene plus Kinder sind in den vergangenen Jahren in die Umgebung gezogen, um uns nahe zu sein (ugs. "Windtäler"). Außerdem sind weitere regionale Schulfamilien und LernbegleiterInnen, unser festangestellter regionaler Handwerker, einige regelmäßig wiederkehrende GasthelferInnen und andere[Menschen]( der Windberg-Vergangenheit Teil unserer "Groß-Familie". **Die WindbergerInnen**

We're a model community leading the way forward with cutting-edge sustainable technology to meet our water, energy, and waste treatment needs. Learn More Robust, Sustainable Design Explore the details of our special, sustainable infrastructure Join Us Today! View plots and houses currently available for sale Press Check out recent Abundance EcoVillage press Copyright © 2011 Abundance EcoVillage. All Rights Reserved. * * * This is an example page. It’s different from a blog post because it will stay in one place and will show up in your site navigation (in most themes). Most people start with an About page that introduces them to potential site visitors. It might say something like this: > Hi there! I’m a bike messenger by day, aspiring actor by night, and this is my blog. I live in Los Angeles, have a great dog named Jack, and I like piña coladas. (And gettin’ caught in the rain.) …or something like this: > The XYZ Doohickey Company was founded in 1971, and has been providing quality doohickies to the public ever since. properties.name: Aderema by Oluokos - Smile With Nature
properties.description:
Aderema by Oluokos – Smile With Nature
Setting: Rural Type: Eco Project (Eco Farm, Eco Tourism Site, Restoration Site), Ecovillage (Indigenous, Traditional)
Status: Aspiring In Planning Since: 
Main Language: English
Aderema Hills – Standing Stones is a community-led tourism initiative that aims to foster an authentic human-nature connection and transformational slow holiday experience that mutually benefits the community, saves the environment, and enriches the guests’ experience.
Our program runs from one to three weeks where learning from the host community, sharing your knowledge with them and the cultural immersion offers guests a rare opportunity to indulge themselves in the rich culture and farming traditions by becoming a part of the Iteso community of Aderema in Western Kenya.
Aderema is so much more than your ordinary safari experience. We’re a responsible experiences designer, a passionate community changer, and above all nature conservationists.  At Oluokos, we specialize in achieving your best holiday experiences by influencing our community positively.
Membership & Visitors
Current members: 16
Open to new members
Open to visitors
We believe that the best, most effective, and immediate way to help both the conservation of wildlife and the interests of the communities who own the land where our activities take place is to visit us. Through the payment of daily conservation fees – money that we collect goes a long way towards financing nature conservation.
We honestly understand that the communities can only protect the wildlife, the flora and fauna only if they get immense and tangible benefits from sustainable tourism. At Aderema by Oluokos, making nature a profitable business is an important way to ensure a long-term win-win scenario for both nature and communities.
Your visitation and stay at Aderema creates direct and indirect job opportunities with more than 95% of our staff sourced from these communities and also contributing to the GDP of Kenya.
Here’s where you come in….
Through this appeal, contributors can ‘Acquire an Acre for Nature’ in Aderema through a donation that will fund the land purchase so that nature can continue flourishing. Contributors can be confident that all the money is going directly to safeguard the future of our ecosystem.
More
About Ouma Oluoko
Having an in-depth understanding of the lack of alternative livelihoods is a key issue that both, directly and indirectly, affect the ecosystems. Our response to this alongside our guests and, other conservation organizations should identify feasible alternative activities that could both, directly and indirectly, generate income for the native communities from the natural resources sustainably. Travel for good with Oluokos Signature.
Reader Interactions

properties.name: African Peace Village
properties.description: #### Due to the civil war in Cameroon, since 2016, part of the [**Better World Cameroon - BWC NGO**]( team was forced into exile. Portugal was the country of destination where, in 2019, with the support of the Tamera community, it was possible to continue the work previously developed out in Cameroon. ##### The civil war in Cameroon and the genocide that have accompanied it are both devastating and almost completely unreported. The arrival of Covid and the resulting governmental controls have created a situation where most people are unable to move or work, exacerbating the crisis for many into an immediate struggle for survival. The [**Bafut Ecovillage**]( the heart of a larger community project called Better World Cameroon, [**has been destroyed**]( and its people dispersed. #### A welcoming message from the President of the Parish Council of Relíquias to Joshua Konkankoh, Celestine Nchang, Crystel Koh, Elizabeth Nyemkuna, Grace Neba and Malcolm Koh, members of Better World Cameroon, who lived in the small town in southern Portugal as soon as they arrived in the country. With the support of the [**Tamera**]( community, BWC NGO regrouped some of the core team in Reliquias and then Frexio do Meio offered. BWC NGO is now operating in exile. From the communities on the ground, we are receiving increasingly distressing and urgent requests for our attention and resources; spiritual and emotional strength as well as for basic items like food, medicine, sanitary products and small sums of money for people taking refuge in the bush or in hiding in obscure corners of cities. We are under strong financial pressure to pay rent for rooms where some of our team and family members are taking shelter. The worst cases are those of women and children caught between the crossfire. ![Image 1: African Way BWC Team]( Thanks to the [**BWC NGO Team**]( supported to come to Portugal by [**Tamera**]( and international volunteers, we have designed **African Way project** and revised the [**

properties.name: Alaya Ecolodge
properties.description:
Alaya Ecolodge is located in North-West Bali, Indonesia. Alaya means 'Home of the Soul' in Sanskrit. It is the perfect place to retreat, enjoy the silence, and to feel in touch with your soul again. It is about a 15min walk, or .
Alaya campus is a lush green oasis, with wooden heritage cottages, tastefully furnished with antiques, and designed in a way to reduce negative ecological impacts. Alaya embrace's GEN's 4 Dimension design for sustainable living and whole systems design. Many people that visit this place have said that it has a very magical healing energy. It is situated at the foothills of the Bali National Park – known for its stunning pristine flora and fauna.
One part of Alaya is a venue for daily bookings and for wellness related workshops and retreats. It is also possible to engage longer term, as Alaya is now expanding to include accommodation agreements for more collective living - welcoming community members to join who feel called by "Ecovillage" values. It is developed over 5000m2 area, with an additional 5000m2 currently being designed for expansion.
The Alaya team would like to ensure that your experience here is special – so we look forward to hearing about your requests in advance, and we will do our best to help you realise your ideal holiday or group retreat - or for your interest in joining the community.
Activities at the Ecolodge include: Wellness Practices eg: Yoga, AcuDetox, Ayurvedic Cooking, Massage Workshops, Watsu in Pool, Meditation, Art Therapy, Hikes, Bonfire & Music, Movie Nights etc.
What to do in the area: Temple Visits, Organic Farming, Bird Watching, Boating, Hot Springs, Snorkelling and Diving.
Alaya team is composed of members from the local Hindu & Muslim Balinese community - and various International members connected to the Global Ecovillage Network.
Please visit us first and open discussions.
Kavitha Urvasie Selvaraj is third generation born into the Auroville intentional community. She is Indian and Dutch by nationality.
Kavitha studied Sustainable Development in the Netherlands, and then settled in Auroville where she worked in Alternative Education, Communications, Networking and Alternative Governance.
In Auroville, she founded the YouthLink Service and the GEN-Auroville Desk, with the support from Joss Brooks (GEN Elder). She has worked for sometime as GEN International
properties.address: 5 min drive away from the closest beach. There are various restaurants, pharmacies and grocery shops nearby, as it is very centrally located in Pemuteran town

Maybe you want to turn a hobby into something more. Or maybe you have a creative project to share with the world. Whatever it is, the way you tell your story online can make all the difference. Don’t worry about sounding professional. Sound like you. There are over 1.5 billion websites out there, but your story is what’s going to separate this one from the rest. If you read the words back and don’t hear your own voice in your head, that’s a good sign you still have more work to do. Be clear, be confident and don’t overthink it. The beauty of your story is that it’s going to continue to evolve and your site can evolve with it. Your goal should be to make it feel right for right now. Later will take care of itself. properties.name: Aldeas de Paz
properties.description:
Aldeas de Paz
ADP Eco-Village and Community shares the values of peace, justice and tolerance
Setting: Rural Type: Eco Project (Eco Farm, Learning Centre, Permaculture Project), Ecovillage (Intentional)
Founded: 2002
Main Language: Spanish Other Languages: English, German
Aldeas de Paz (ADP) is a sustainable and transparent eco-village, community and grassroots NGO based in Santa Elena de Uairen, Venezuela, near the borders with Brazil and Guyana in South America. ADP is independent from political, religious or business interests
The community promotes the -Culture of Peace- through enriching cross-cultural exchange for people of all races and nationalities and provides additional opportunities in educational, recreational and environmental fields and assists its beneficiaries to develop their potential and improve their own lives.
ADP Eco-Village and Community shares the values of peace, justice and tolerance, fundamentally focusing on the preservation of indigenous cultures and the empowerment of young people while valuing local knowledge and working with cultural sensitivity.
We welcome individual volunteers, families and groups regardless of age, experience or background. Volunteering with ADP is a great opportunity to work side by side with locals, learn new skills, study Spanish, and immerse oneself in the Latin and indigenous culture. You are interacting with the community, building relationships and making a positive impact.
History: In the 1990’s, Manfred Mönnighoff, left his successful business career in Holland behind to become a social activist and entrepreneur. With his intention of helping to create and promote a “Culture of Peace” by putting his professional skills to work he joined small grassroots NGO’s in Asia, Central and South America as a volunteer and finally in 1997 started his first humanitarian project in Merida, Venezuela and succeeded in 2001 in forming the legal constitution for a volunteer-based NGO in Caracas, Venezuela, called Fundación Aldeas de Paz. In 2003 he founded the Aldeas de Paz community in Santa Elena thanks to donor support as well as the collocation of his own life savings.
Membership & Visitors
Current members: 8
Open to new members
Please apply at: info@aldeasdepaz.org
Let us know more about your intentions and tell us about yourself.
More

properties.name: Almarte
properties.description:
Almarte
Permaculture cacao & food forest — building ecovillage/NGO structure, researching post-capitalism.
Setting: Rural Type: Eco Project (Eco Farm, Permaculture Project), Ecovillage (Intentional)
Status: Aspiring In Planning Since: 
Main Language: English Other Languages: German, Polish, Spanish
Almarte is a regenerative initiative in the Dominican Republic, combining permaculture, cacao cultivation, and community building to explore new models of living and loving beyond the boundaries of capitalism.
Located on 120 hectares of reforested land in a sacred region surrounded by tropical mountains, our project has already planted over 100,000 trees, including 60,000 cacao trees grown under organic and biodiverse agroforestry systems.
At its heart, Almarte is a living laboratory — a place where ecology, art, and consciousness meet. The name unites alma (soul) and arte (art), reflecting our belief that true regeneration begins both in the soil and in the human spirit.
Currently, four of us live next to the farm, as we build the essential infrastructure — water systems, renewable energy, and community spaces — to bring the ecovillage fully to life.
We are now forming the Almarte Foundation (NGO) as the social and legal structure behind this vision. The foundation researches and implements post-capitalist structures based on cooperation, circular economies, and shared resources — where value is created through connection, creativity, and care.
Almarte seeks to inspire a global movement for regenerative communities that honor both the Earth and the human heart — cultivating cacao, culture, and consciousness in harmony with nature.
Membership & Visitors
Current members: 4
Open to new members
Rarely open to visitors
Please contact us on info@almarte.com
Images & Video
General Video
More
About Sascha Stockem
Co-Founder of Almarte Foundation — uniting art, soul, and earth through regenerative cacao farming and permaculture in the Dominican Republic.
We are currently four people living near our 120 ha farm, building the basic infrastructure to grow into a fully self-sustaining ecovillage.
Reader Interactions
