properties.name: Ananda Valley
properties.description:
Ananda Valley project was born as a dream with solid background: 60 skilled volunteers focused on different aspects of six complementary associations, and a clear 
Our Aims are: to create a model for sustainable 21st century living, based on neo-humanistic values and all-round service, which will be a replicable educational, research and service resource for society at large; to act as a social hub,bringing together people who are interested in music and the arts, education, culture, health, economics, innovative scientific techniques and consciousness studies; to help improve the quality of life of all living beings,both human and non-human, and to create a supportive environment which provides for the needs of the residents and the surrounding community within a governance framework based on sociocratic principles; to strengthen the local economy and the surrounding communities,through interaction and by helping them in their efforts to become self-reliant in terms of food and energy as well as through the creation of employment opportunities; to promote a healthy and ecological lifestyle,supportive of activities that lead to joy and happiness and stimulate the regeneration of biodiverse sustainable ecosystems.
Everyone is welcome to visit our community. To do so, please contact us.
Ananda Valley project was born as a dream with solid background: 60 skilled volunteers focused on different aspects of six complementary associations, and a clear 
Deep ecology, sustainable agriculture, vegetarianism, life-long learning, art, social service and social activism are the focus areas of these non-profit associations; few more are ready to join.
In 2010, in the center of a Portugal in full crisis, was discovered a perfect pla...
I am a 25 year old Frenchie from Paris, I have been teaching yoga and baking for a cafe in Dublin for the last two years.
Joaquin is a 31 year old Mexican who use to be an accountant and traded the corporate life to manage and barista for a cafe-restaurant-event place in Dublin for the last 4 years.
Together we have taken a year off to travel around the world and discover and learn from communities that live differently.
We are both traveling down to Portugal in two weeks (4th - 20th) and would love to spend a couple weeks with you. We are both super enthusiastic, to work and learn from you, in any areas you would be needing help in. We have our own tent and can pitch it in your garden !
We would absolutely love to exchange working hours for accommodation, and be a part of what looks like a fantastic place.
Let us know what you think, and have a fab

properties.name: BEND Eco-Neighbourhood
properties.description: _We acknowledge the Djiringanj people, the Traditional Owners of the land on which we live, and their continuing connection to land, sea and community. We pay our respects to their Elders past, present and future._ * * * **Bend is an eco-neighbourhood in Bega, a small town on the far south coast of NSW, Australia.** Bend has been designed using permaculture principles, and we value environmental sustainability, social diversity, food production, and energy conservation. Our homes include passive solar design, composting toilets, rainwater tanks, and solar hot water. As a community we share conservation areas and certified organic agricultural land, as well as neighbourhood fire-fighting, photovoltaic, and greywater recycling systems. Our neighbourhood house includes a gathering area, a kitchen, guest accommodation, and a communal laundry for all to use. We enjoy coming together for regular meetings (where we make decisions by consensus), monthly shared dinners, and the occasional party! There are 22 households currently living at Bend, including 10 affordable rental homes, with 6 other blocks yet to be built on; our growing community includes 31 adults of all ages and 19 children and teens. If you’d like to get in touch with us to find out more, please email:bendna@bend.org.au **Bend on the Radio** [Click here to read and listen to a recent ABC story about Bend.]( Or send email enquiries to:[bendna@bend.org.au](mailto:bendna@bend.org.au) News – Bend Neighbourhood Association Skip to content It seems we can’t find what you’re looking for. Perhaps searching can help. properties.name: Bioaldeia Arawikay
properties.description: Se você quiser maiores informações, tirar alguma dúvida, se inscrever em um de nossos programas com datas publicadas, ou ainda propor a sua própria data para alguma experiência, **[clique aqui para se cadastrar e enviar sua mensagem.]( Antônio Carlos / Santa Catarina / Brasil +55 (48) 98455-2813 / (48) 99993-3491 – solicitaremos seu cadastro arawikay@ecoaldeia.org ♥ Se você têm uma profissão ou habilidade muito boa nas áreas citadas a seguir e deseja fazer uma troca, podemos combinar uma permanência. Esta poderá ser de 5 a 10 dias (ou mais), onde a jornada de até 5 horas será levada com atividades de manutenção local a combinar antes e mais outras do seu interesse. TRABALHAMOS COM DOIS TIPOS DE TROCAS: A. SEUS SERVIÇOS TÉCNICOS COM “BOA EXPERIÊNCIA X NOSSOS SERVIÇOS LOCAIS” ♥ Oferecemos: Diminuir ou anular o valor da hospedagem original, usufruindo de espaço de pernoite, lazer, salas, utensílios e áreas verdes harmonizadas. ♥ Oferecemos parte de alimentação ou deixar mantimentos para que realize suas próprias refeições. ♥ Oferecemos instrumentos para aprendizagens em algumas áreas práticas e/ou acompanhamento e orientação de um foco de trabalho pessoal (auto-conhecimento, cura, etc) que venha procurar desenvolver. Observação: Desde seu início, Arawikay promove trocas de serviços locais por serviços técnicos sem a prática da contratação. O QUE NECESSITA PARA PROPOR ESTA TROCA: ♥ Que use profissionalmente, por exemplo, máquinas elétricas e a gasolina – motosserra, roçadeira a motor com faca e fio, serra elétrica manual e outros; ♥ Marcenaria/Carpintaria/Obras e reformas como pedreiro convencional/Bioconstrução com muita experiência/Agricultura com habilidade e ritmo/Eletricidade/Eletrônica. ♥ Costura a máquina; Costura manual com experiência. Tecelagem. ♥ Chefe de cozinha (ocasionalmente para cursos de 10 a 20 pessoas com datas estipuladas). Não se permitem atendimentos corporais entre visitantes nem cerimônias com uso de medicinas sagradas ou fumos. Observação: Não p

properties.name: Biosphere Foundation
properties.description:
Biosphere Foundation is a non-profit US organisation with operations in Indonesia managed by PT Biosphere Stewardship Center and a partnership with Yayasan Biosfir Indonesia to work in Bali Barat National Park. In 1991 we embarked on a 30-year mission around the planet to fuse our Biosphere 2 heritage with local knowledge, creating a collaborative movement for ocean and land stewardship. We share our visual library of film, sound, and data curated over three decades of direct observation and immersion, to give a global voice to the biosphere. Our three main areas of action:
We are building on a body of knowledge about ocean health that includes direct observation and measurements from 49 coral reef sites around the world, as well as studies of key indicator species such as great whales and sea turtles. Working with local dive leaders, we apply techniques for coral restoration, such as installing mooring buoys, removal of coral predators, and replanting of living but broken corals.
We are building skills and information with our local community for sustainable agriculture and forestry in NW Bali - a dry-monsoon region where the rains last only for about 4 months a year, and deforestation for wood, cash crops, and animal fodder is a local problem.
For the past 30 years we have led ocean-going expeditions to map and monitor coral reefs with over 150 students from around the world who attended a 9-month immersion program. In the last 10 years, we additionally led 45 ‘Biosphere Stewardship Education Programs’ in NW Bali for 925 local and international students. These applied learning programs, which deliver activities involving the care of nature, for people of all ages, include snorkeling camping trips for Balinese youth, customized programs for schools & universities, and International Stewardship Programs accredited by United World Colleges.
If you are interested to work with us or conduct a project with us, please check our website and contact us by email. We look forward to hearing from you!
Written on June 16, 2020,

It’s really exciting to grow this project and make connections across the territory to promote regenerative cultures, and network with amazing projects! [![Image 3]( "Regenerative Weekend May 2026!") ## [Regenerative Weekend May 2026!]( This weekend was exceptional; practicals, afternoons in the river, and much more. What I’m trying to say is- join us August 21-23 to do it all again! [![Image 4]( "Imagine sharing a bottle of wine with friends.. knowing that your enjoyment is also nourishing the land.") ## [Imagine sharing a bottle of wine with friends.. knowing that your enjoyment is also nourishing the land.]( The Rovira Regenerativa wine comes from vines that are part of a living regenerative system on the border of Catalonia and Aragón — a landscape where we are restoring soil, planting trees, slowing water into the earth, and creating opportunities for people to learn directly from the land. properties.name: Bosque Fluir
properties.description:
What we design?
It is forest school for community learning and unlearning focused on good living to develop resilient generations. Located in the high-Amazon tropical forest with a focus on young people between 18 and 60 years old.
What happens?
People form teams/collectives to unlearn degenerative patters and learn useful knowledge and techniques for a good life. Where the forest school serves as a learning space, STEAM (science, technology, engineering, arts, math), agroecological plots, nutrition and culinary classes, balanced forms of coexistence and many types of artistic expressions are co-created. Infants, young people, grandparents and regenerating wild animals live in this forest.
For what?
To co-create the generation of the future that can be resilient and continue to be generous ancestors. The goal is to recognize and disengage with degenerative patterns that block us in conflict and drama. Where we practice unlearning toxic social agreements and learn agreements that resemble the life of our dreams. Believing in the beauty and power of life that flows in us. A space where we understand that life gives us in abundance and that we can maintain that abundance of local biodiversity with holistic design techniques. Learning to be generous and kind to ourselves. Place to develop mastery in the transformation and creation of our personal paradise. This dream of life is made of thousands of dynamic little dreams.
I want visitors and residents to stop sacrificing our personal freedom to be good enough for everyone else, according to the point of view of family, couples, institutions, religion and society.
In Bosque Fluir you are invited to relearn how to love yourself, accepting yourself as you are unconditionally. In this way, project unconditional love to your true/authentic self. We want to reprogram our minds for our freedom and happiness but not for a toxic belief system.To co-create the generation of the future who can be resilient and remain memorable ancestors.
You can first apply to be a resident for a year.  We'll then discuss further on becoming an permanent member of the community
Peruvian permaculture teacher and founder of the Permaculture school Bosque Fluir, in the North Peruvian Amazon

properties.name: Braziers Park
properties.description: * * * **Celebrating Braziers Park School of Integrative****Social Research: 76 Years of Curiosity,****Community, and Conscious Change:** Braziers Park is a community, charity for alternative education, and an architectural treasure hidden deep in the South Oxfordshire countryside in an Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty. * * * ## OPEN CALL 2026-27 **Apply for our Academic & Creative Residency Programme** Braziers Park is inviting applications for our self-funded ACRE (Academic and Creative Research) Residency Programme. We are welcoming up to 12 residents between 1 December 2026 and 8 March 2027. We welcome artists, researchers, writers, and thinkers interested in developing work through communal living and shared enquiry. There is no fixed submission deadline; however, we recommend applying early, as the open call will close once we have identified 12 suitable candidates. [**Click here to find out more**]( * * * ## Retreat Venue Hire **Bring your retreat to Braziers Park** With beautiful grounds, characterful spaces, accommodation, and catering, we offer a peaceful residential setting for wellbeing retreats, workshops, and group gatherings. Available for whole-site and individual space bookings, our historic house and rural Oxfordshire grounds provide a welcoming place to slow down, connect, and spend meaningful time together. Groups can make use of shared gathering spaces, comfortable bedrooms, home-cooked meals, and the surrounding gardens and woodland. [**Click here to find out more**]( * * * ## Soil, Soul, Society: **Braziers Park’s Education Guiding Vision for 2025 and Beyond** At Braziers Park School of Integrative Social Research, we are embarking on a renewed journey in 2025, guided by the triad of Soil, Soul, Society – a profound framework of ecological and spiritual consciousness articulated by educator, activist, and thinker Satish Kumar. This philosophy will shape our calendar of events, workshops, and courses inspiring participants to engage with the n

properties.name: COLEGIO JOHANNES KEPLER
properties.description: How to adapt teaching methods to diverse learning styles - T4 Education How to adapt teaching methods to diverse learning styles Learning Science How to adapt teaching methods to diverse learning styles Tagged under: Learning Science Carolina Caceres January 29, 2024 Download PDF Pedagogy is a dynamic process that evolves constantly, and as educators, it’s our responsibility to keep pace with innovative, effective and affectionate methods. As Jean Piaget stated in 1969, the difficulty in teaching is the fact that the most successful teaching methods are often the most challenging. Shifting from traditional to innovative teaching styles can be a challenging task, especially for a teacher who is deeply rooted in conventional teaching. Embracing change Educational change is a slow process, marked by time passing and shifts in generations. As teachers, our own learning experiences heavily depend on the way we were taught and it influences our pedagogical approaches. Embracing change is crucial, especially within today’s classrooms, to ensure that every student benefits from our teaching delivery. We must be mindful of the fact that we don’t teach for an average kind of student, because there is no average student. We teach for each and every student we have in class. Diverse learning styles Recognising and addressing diverse learning styles is an obvious and sometimes unnoticeable aspect of teaching. Every student learns differently and as educators, it’s our mission to adapt and cater to different learning styles. While it’s challenging, taking time to plan lessons that adjust to each student’s needs is a fundamental aspect of effective teaching. Strategies to adapt teaching methods Carol Ann Tomlinson provides valuable strategies for teachers seeking to adapt their teaching styles to accommodate diverse learning needs. In her book, she names over 15 strategies for managing a diverse classroom, but these are the ones that I found the easiest way to start implementing i

properties.name: Catfarm
properties.description:
The Catfarm is an off-grid community of travelers, (digital) nomads, artists, healers, entrepreneurs, and purpose-driven people with the will to explore an alternative lifestyle closer to nature. Armed with old knowledge, instant communication, miraculous technology, and sheer strength of will, we hope to inspire all social classes to switch to a more respectful way of living in this high-speed changing world.
We merge people’s skills to develop projects, trainings, events, and hope to flourish into a self-sufficient paradise. Together, through sharing our knowledge openly in a place of safety we can make anything possible. We invite you to join us in building a bright and fulfilling future.
So far, as well as adapting the farmhouse and surroundings to produce food and gather solar energy, our biggest projects have included upcycling an old Peugeot into a bedroom, building a tipi, and starting to host youth exchanges run by our friends at Catfarm Education. We have now set our sights on building the Hackersfarm, a series of geodesic domes where we will delve further into permaculture and create spaces for art and upcycling protected from the weather. Or at least, that’s the plan at the moment, although like all our plans it may adapt and change as new travelers with their many skills and ideas make their contributions.
We have been providing a home for roaming souls, and have slowly gathered together an international community of creatives and nomads that design co-owned projects. We aim to develop our own lifestyle in harmony with nature, as well as building links with the local community and educating people to reduce waste, work together, express their creativity and understand the benefits of community life and learning from one another.
If you would like to join us please hop over to our website at www.catfarm.net, have a good look around, read more about us, and if you still want to join us then fill in the sign form.  We look forward to seeing you.
Currently looking for some paws to help us ^.^
The Catfarm is an off-grid welcoming community of travelers, (digital) nomads, artists, healers, entrepreneurs and anyone with the will to explore an alternative lifestyle closer to nature.
Recently, I checked the you have an opportunity to work and live there in WorldPackers, the problem is that I don't have Money to apply on the website.
I'm studying english in Ireland, I've worked here in a temporary job before, but I want to improve my studies in French, so I would like to go to some place that I can practice. I really think this is some job that's match with my profile, because I love meet new people and I'm open minded.
I have upper intermediate English and Im working as a performer in Ireland, I know how to cook, clean, how to work in the reception, I also understand about photography, drawing and theater.
And I already worked as a cleane

properties.name: Children Development Program
properties.description:
Our children’s development program is one of the major activities in our integrated community approach supported by our full time staff and volunteers. The aim of the program is to protect the right of all children in the community by providing access to a basic, quality education, developing their life skills, and providing access to health services through our community health program.
For children, the goal of children development program is to help schools become communities in which they feel cared for and learn to care in return – communities in which they are helped to develop the practical skills needed to function productively in society, and the ethical and intellectual understanding needed to function humanely and wisely. For teachers, the goal of children development program is to help schools become respectful and inclusive communities that support their continued learning and provide opportunities for them to collaborate with one another and to contribute to decisions.
Children-development-NVDP-org-Nepal-Volunteer-development-program
Anybody who has a strong interest in supporting children from the most marginalized communities and who enjoys working with young children. Prior experience in Montessori or early childhood education methods will be an advantage.
Assist local teachers.
Teach children motor and kinesthetic skills.
Play games and rhymes with children.
Supervise supports activities.
Provide childcare support.
Create teaching/learning resources.
Organize student excursions.
Requirements:- There are no specific requirements for the programs. However the best things you can have are patience and a genuine desire to teach, plus a sense of humor!
Accommodation:- A simple room with a bed. We advise you to bring sleeping bag and mosquito net with you. Or you can always buy here which is 2-3 times cheaper than back home.
Meals:- Breakfast occurs at 7:00 am and consists of a cup of tea and biscuits. Lunch is served at 11:30 am and usually dall/bhat( rice, lentils and vegetable) dinner is at around 7:00 pm and is once more dall/bhat. The food is very simple but also extremely delicious and filling.
Start date:- Are very flexible, minimum time of stay in this program is one weeks and the maximum stay is five months in Nepal.
NVDP now supports over 14 early childhood education centers in schools and communities in impoverished Nepalese communities.
We are accepting international volunteer who can really interested of volunteer job in Nepal
NVDP Volunteer organization carries the mission to promote volunteer opportunity throughout the country that bring positive changes to the life of other and the environment in which we all live. Our overall mission is to develop, facilitate, promote and recognize voluntary activities at all levels whilst ensuring that our global projects. Contribute to sustainable development within the country

Supervise supports activities. Provide childcare support. Create teaching/learning resources. Organize student excursions. Requirements:- There are no specific requirements for the programs. However the best things you can have are patience and a genuine desire to teach, plus a sense of humor! Accommodation:- A simple room with a bed. We advise you to bring sleeping bag and mosquito net with you. Or you can always buy here which is 2-3 times cheaper than back home. Meals:- Breakfast occurs at 7:00 am and consists of a cup of tea and biscuits. Lunch is served at 11:30 am and usually dall/bhat( rice, lentils and vegetable) dinner is at around 7:00 pm and is once more dall/bhat. The food is very simple but also extremely delicious and filling. Start date:- Are very flexible, minimum time of stay in this program is one weeks and the maximum stay is five months in Nepal. NVDP now supports over 14 early childhood education centers in schools and communities in impoverished Nepalese communities. We are accepting international volunteer who can really interested of volunteer job in Nepal NVDP Volunteer organization carries the mission to promote volunteer opportunity throughout the country that bring positive changes to the life of other and the environment in which we all live. Our overall mission is to develop, facilitate, promote and recognize voluntary activities at all levels whilst ensuring that our global projects. Contribute to sustainable development within the country

properties.name: Coastal Roots Farm
properties.description: 5 Reasons to Attend the Sukkot Harvest Festival - Coastal Roots Farm Nourish your neighbor this holiday season. Donate today to give 1,500 families a “fresh” start. 5 Reasons to Attend the Sukkot Harvest Festival Aug 23, 2018 by Coastal Roots Farm 5. Play, Dance, Celebrate, & Learn Stop by our DIY Fermentation Station; dance to live music by our headliner Shamati; join a learning workshop on Sukkot, agriculture, or food justice; or visit the Kids Zone for the perfect photo op. Kids Zone activities will include a petting zoo, crafts, DIY flower crowns, and more! 4. Let your Foodie Flag Fly Awaken your taste buds by noshing on food from your favorite local vendors, from fish tacos to wood-fired pizza, kefir soda to fresh juice, and organic farm to table creations! Guests ages 21+ can mix and mingle in our “Libation Station” featuring alcoholic kombucha from Boochcraft, beer from Golden Coast Mead, and others. Plus, get an exclusive taste of Coastal Roots Farm’s very first, unreleased wine made from grapes grown in our own vineyard! 3. You Shall Have Nothing But Joy Ancient Jewish sages refer to Sukkot as ushering in “the season of our joy.” Together, we will celebrate the end of an abundant summer harvest and the beginning of fall, which will eventually bring rain and make the next harvest possible. With your neighbors, reconnect with earth’s incredible cycles. It is through these moments that we make memories, create connection, and build community. 2. Your Donation Makes an Impact According to the latest research provided by the San Diego Hunger Coalition, an estimated 486,000 (1 in 7) people in San Diego county experience food insecurity and an additional 185,000 people at risk of food insecurity. Our ticket prices are suggested donations and are offered on a pay-what-you-can basis. Donations will fund our food distributions which serve needy Holocaust survivors, military and veteran familes at Camp Pendelton, and at-risk families at Vista Community Clinic. 1. Feel

properties.name: Comunità rigenerative
properties.description:
Co-create & live inter-generational, open, richly diverse and responsive places to meet the needs emerging from the crises that afflict us; places that are autonomous and self‑sufficient, for food & energy, then fiber and other materials, for our health & education, while fully respecting the ecology of the planet and human relations.
Reclaime and regenerate homes, land and villages, cultivate the land with agro‑ecological approaches, without external inputs, aiming to get as close as possible to 100 per cent self-sufficiency in food, energy, and other needs. Food self-sufficiency is a fundamental priority that shall also leave room for the other dimensions of being and well-being.
Reduce dependence on the monetary system to a minimum, valuing approaches of gift economy, financial co-responsibility, barter. Open to the outside world, we work actively for the transformative changes needed to overcome the interconnected climate, ecological, geopolitical, social crises, and to accommodate those who will seek refuge from the devastations to come.
Create vital spaces and realising resilience dynamics, ready to welcome people of all origins and needs, in search of support and sustenance, and meaning. Networking with those with similar goals.
Create person-centred, empathic, nonviolent and non-directive learning and living spaces for growth, for babies, children, adolescents and adults.
Activate educating communities with a dynamic 'non-school' approach, inspired by - for example - Maria Montessori, Paulo Freire, democratic schools, Marshall Rosenberg's Nonviolent Empathic Communication.
Explicitly and intentionally co-create the dialogical systems that support our functioning based on shared agreements: decision-making, resource distribution, feedback, tension and conflict transformation systems, etc.
People wanting to join are requested to come and stay with us for one or two weeks, experiencing work and daily life with us. After this period, they can activate our dialogical system for integration. We will have between four and five meetings, to explore whether all are willing to be together on this wonderful journey.
Comunità rigenerative is an ecological intentional community settlements in NW Italy, which aim for food, energy, and material self-sufficiency, in radical ecological harmony with the planet and caring for the needs of all living beings. We build a relationship ecology informed by the spirit of Nonviolent Communication, co-designing and facilitating the dialogical systems that support our living and acting together, including for shared power, decision-making, feedback and to transform conflict.
How can my family apply to visit and potentially stay to help you achieve more with the land and projects you are a part of?
This sounds good,where in italy are you?
We are a family(my husband,(37),our son(3), and me(35)) looking for a community to

properties.name: Consejo de Visiones Guardianes de la Tierra Mexico
properties.description:
Itinerant multidisciplinary meeting: Temporary Peace Village that brings us together for 7 days to learn, share and get to know a great diversity of cosmogonies, projects and initiatives of various approaches aimed at a more conscious, sustainable and collaborative life, in harmony with Mother Earth, native cultures and all living beings in our common home.  we have councils for education, health, ecology and permaculture, spirituality, tradition, social movements, sacred time, as well as for children, youth, women and the elderly. We all exchange knowledge and co-create proposals for a kinder, more beautiful and sustainable world.
We are environmental educators, artists, therapists and entrepreneurs from these areas, as well as representatives of native American tribes who work voluntarily and cooperatively to change consciousness and attitudes towards Mother Earth, native cultures and all living beings in our common home.
We represent a diverse spectrum of organizations and people, as we believe that with the integration of many visions, we can create inclusive initiatives and projects, applicable at the local, bioregional and planetary levels. Each call is named after some element or related to the local community and/or situation of importance, as a thematic axis to generate a positive impact on it.
In these times, the Guardians of the Earth trust in the proposals more than in the protests, since the former are based on a critique of the existing paradigm and provide the elements to build the ecotopia: "Another world is possible" and here we are building together.
Vision Councils touch the heart of each participant. We re-unite relationships and remember our origin, our essence, dissolving the great illusion of separation.
Write to the emails that come in the contact information and tell us what your interests, skills and talents are.
CASA México is a national network, as one of the nodes of the CASA Latina network (Council of Sustainable Settlements of Latin America) th within GEN.
We are a team of psychologists, pedagogues and educators from Mexicali, Baja California, who want to know if you are going to meet in November of this year 2018 because we are interested in assisting your community. We work in the field of education and we want to learn and share ideas and experiences

properties.name: Crystal Waters
properties.description: Discover the Transformative World of Permaculture Design at Crystal Waters | Crystal Waters Eco Village Introduction Are you passionate about sustainability and eager to learn how you can create a positive impact on the environment and your community? If so, the Permaculture Design Course (PDC) at Crystal Waters may be the perfect opportunity for you. Situated in the picturesque surroundings of Crystal Waters, in Queensland Australia, this immersive and hands-on course offers a deep dive into the principles and practices of permaculture, empowering participants to create sustainable and resilient living systems. In this article, we’ll explore what you can expect from the PDC at Crystal Waters and why it’s an experience you won’t want to miss. The Permaculture Design Course Experience At the heart of the PDC is a comprehensive curriculum that covers a wide range of topics, including permaculture ethics and principles, design methods, soil fertility, water management, food production, energy systems, natural building, and social and economic aspects of sustainable living. The course is designed to accommodate individuals of all backgrounds and skill levels, making it an ideal learning opportunity for beginners and experienced permaculture practitioners alike. During the 2-week intensive program, participants will engage in a mix of classroom learning, hands-on activities, and site visits to local permaculture projects. The practical experience gained from these activities allows participants to not only understand the theory behind permaculture design but also see it in action and apply the techniques in real-world situations. The PDC is led by a team of experienced and passionate instructors who are dedicated to sharing their knowledge and expertise with participants. With a typical student-to-teacher ratio of around 15:1, learners can expect personalised instruction and support throughout the course. In group activities we always have an additional assistant support

properties.name: DES Development and Empowerment Society , Kakuma Refugee Regeneration , Kenya
properties.description: Spread the love Please feel free to share your feedback and ask for further information. We will get back to you as soon as we can. People from all over the world are coming together to support our work, each bringing special talents and resources. If you would like to join our allies or contribute funds, please fill out the form below and we will be in touch. * * * Tafadhali jisikie huru kushiriki maoni yako na uulize habari zaidi. Tutarudi kwako haraka iwezekanavyo. Watu kutoka kote ulimwenguni wanakusanyika ili kusaidia kazi yetu, kila mmoja akileta talanta na rasilimali maalum. Ikiwa ungependa kujiunga na washirika wetu au kuchangia fedha, tafadhali jaza fomu iliyo hapa chini na tutawasiliana. Syntropic Agroforestry Comes To Kakuma - Development and Empowerment of Society Skip to content Spread the love By Kadjosi Matabishi I was so grateful for the opportunity to go to Rusinga to learn Syntropic Agroforestry, which I think will be very helpful here in Kakuma. Not only will it help every refugee to grow vegetables and have a better diet, creating a forest will also promote a better air and climate. Now I am able to teach my fellow refugees in collaboration with two other Kenyans and I would like nothing better than to open up this knowledge to other residents of Kakuma and to those living in Kalobeyey, both of which are located in a desert. Nilishukuru sana kupata nafasi ya kwenda Rusinga kujifunza Syntropic Agroforestry, ambayo nadhani itasaidia sana hapa Kakuma. Sio tu kwamba itasaidia kila mkimbizi kulima mboga mboga na kuwa na chakula bora, kujenga msitu pia kukuza hewa bora na hali ya hewa. Sasa nina uwezo wa kuwafunza wakimbizi wenzangu kwa ushirikiano na Wakenya wengine wawili na singependa lolote zaidi ya kuwafungulia wakaazi wengine wa Kakuma ujuzi huu na wale wanaoishi Kalobeyey, ambao wote wako jangwani. properties.name: Damanhur Federation
properties.description:
Founded in 1975, the Federation of Damanhur is an eco-society: a federation of communities and eco-villages with their own social and political structure in continual evolution.
The Federation of Damanhur is a centre for spiritual, artistic and social research known throughout the whole world. Its philosphy is based on action, optimism and upon the idea that every human being lives to leave something of themselves to the others and to contribute towards the growth and evolution of the whole of humanity.
The Federation of Damanhur is comprised of communities, with about 20 people living in each of them. Around 400 supporters live in the surrounding valleys and regularly participate in the activities of Damanhur. Community life encourages the creation of a social model based upon solidarity – a strong expression of which is voluntary work – with respect for others and the environment and the sharing of ethical and spiritual values.
The settlements of the Federation in the area of Valchiusella extend across more than 1,200 acres of territory which includes woodland, farmland, residential areas with around a hundred private dwellings, arts workshops, craft studios, businesses and farms. Damanhur has centres in Italy, Europe and Japan and collaborates with spiritual and community groups throughout the world.
The Federation has it own schools, from nursery school to Junior High:The education of the little children is very important and the chosen educational path is well defined and at the leading edge, while . Just as important is the continuing education of the adults: the Federation has a Free University, offering courses and experiential platforms open to debating with researchers from all around the world.
Damanhur has a Constitution, an alternative currency, a daily newspaper, magazines, arts and craft workshops, a conference centre and laboratories for applied medical and scientific research.
Damanhur was founded in 1975 when a group of people came together around the figure of philosopher and healer Oberto Airaudi, to plan the creation of a new society where everyday life could be the practical application of spiritual values. The goals of Damanhur are, in fact: freedom and the re-awakening of the human being as a divine, spiritual and material principle; the creation of a model of life based upon ethical principles of living together well and with love; the harmonious integration and collaboration with all the Forces linked to the evolution of Humankind.
In Damanhur the uniqueness of each person is considered important and of value. The talents of each person add towards the realisation of an innovative and complex society in constant evolution and growth, where kindness, a sense of humour and creativity are the foundations of its values.
Damanhur is renowned throughout the world for the Temples of Humankind, an underground work of art, completely realised

properties.name: Dana Village holistic eco village in Japan
properties.description: ![Image 1]( ## Message from the Host I am Minori and the founder of Dana Village, an eco village located in Nishi-Aizu, Fukushima. I am a certified nurse but decided to pursue this path after working in hospitals and living in Auroville (an international eco village in India). Together with my partner, three kids and volunteers from around the world, we have been practicing holistic and sustainable lifestyle centered around growing own food and communal living for 8 years. Anyone who shares our values and wants to experience an authentic rural lifestyle in Japan and learn about a sustainable community is welcome to our place. We look forward to having more international volunteers, so please do not hesitate to contact us. Hope to see you soon! ## **Preferred Volunteer Profile** * Open-minded and willing to take on a variety of tasks, especially farmwork and housework * Comfortable with communal living, kids and vegan diet * Living in environmentally conscious manner, such as saving water, avoid using chemical, and etc * Not obsessed with cleanliness (but we welcome those who like cleaning and tidying!) ![Image 2]( ![Image 3]( ## Volunteer Program ## ｜Duration of Stay Starting from 2 weeks ### ｜Fees If you stay longer than a month or have special skills, we can discuss the cost. In such cases, we may request a video call beforehand. ### Notes * Arrivals are accepted on Mondays (if Monday is not feasible, Thursdays may also be considered). * Short-term stay less than 2 weeks is possible during busy periods but the day charge will be higher given the experiential aspect rather than volunteering. * Private rooms and shorter volunteering hours can be arranged at additional cost. Professionals who want to work remotely while volunteering are also welcome! ## **A Day as a Volunteer (example)** ### ｜AM 6:00 Farm work 9:00 Breakfast 10:00 Free time ![Image 4]( ### ｜PM 14:00 Farm work 17:30 Break 18:00 Dinner 19:30 Free time ![Image 5]( ## Where is Dana Village? ### ｜Address
properties.popup:
Dana Village holistic eco village in Japan

![Image 1]( ## Message from the Host I am Minori and the founder of Dana Village, an eco village located in Nishi-Aizu, Fukushima. I am a certified nurse but decided to pursue this path after working in hospitals and living in Auroville (an international eco village in India). Together with my partner, three kids and volunteers from around the world, we have been practicing holistic and sustainable lifestyle centered around growing own food and communal living for 8 years. Anyone who shares our values and wants to experience an authentic rural lifestyle in Japan and learn about a sustainable community is welcome to our place. properties.name: Dankunku Ecovillage Project The Gambia
properties.description:
Dankunku Ecovillage Project .
The Gambia is a small country in West Africa with a population of 2.3 Million with a land area of 10,698 square kilometres, varying in width between 42 kilometres near the mouth of the river to 24 kilometres further upstream and stretching about 480 kilometres in length. The Country is divided in to half by The River Gambia. This runs the entire length of the Country from Futa Jallon Highlands in The republic of Guineau. It is bounded on the North, South and East by Senegal and to the West by the Atlantic Ocean. The Economy of The Gambia is heavily reliant on agriculture. Due to the Country unavailability of significant minerals or other natural resources, and has a limited agriculture base. Statistics shows 75% of the population depends on crops and livestock for its livelihood. Small-scale manufacturing activity features the processing of peanut, fish and animal hides with a unemployment rate of 41%.
BRIEF HISTORY OF  DANKUNKU ECOVILLAGE PROJECT.
MOTTO: Maintain Indigenous Legacy Alive
To build a sustainable future for the Gambia community through ecotourism and ingenious people legacy preservation.
To promote responsible tourism that supports the local economy and preserves the ingenious legacy of the Gambia community.
We are open for new members and visitors, feel free to send us email through dankunkuecovillageproject220@gmail.com
I am Mr.Momodou Barrow The founder & CEO of this project, i have extensive experience in horticultural work and a passion for cultivating crops. With a certificate in Hotel Service from the Italy Vocational college ,ive work and volunteering in Estonia,Italy & Germany. I have developed a strong work ethic, attention to detail, and dedication to providing exceptional service.I am patient, compassionate, and detail-oriented, with strong communication skills and a commitment to personalized care

properties.name: Earth Deeds
properties.description:
Earth Deeds offers innovative online tools for users to “onset” the social cost of their carbon footprints and support sustainability projects of their choice. We help ecologically-minded groups and individuals give back to the Earth and become “carbon conscious” by 1) measuring the carbon footprints of their activities and events, 2) raising funds to acknowledge their impacts, 3) efficiently distributing these funds to small, local sustainability projects of their choosing, and 4) receiving a Carbon Conscious Certification and promote their efforts
Daniel is an educator, social entrepreneur, and communitarian whose mission in life is to support the development of sustainable communities and local solutions to global warming. After living at the Findhorn Foundation in northern Scotland and completing his Ph.D. in community-based education the early 90s, Daniel founded Living Routes, which partnered with the University of Massachusetts Amherst to run study abroad programs based s around the world. He also helped found Gaia Educa

properties.name: Earthen Heart
properties.description: Earthen Heart LLC Shri Pushti Parivar Home Events Farmland Commons Membership visit Contact Farmland Commons By end of this year, 2025, we will be forming the first ever Earthen Heart Farmland Commons in Southwest Michigan at the Bangor Property in collaboration with the Farmers Land Trust. Julian Lauzzana met Ian McSweeney and Kristina Villa at the Biodynamic Conference in Colorado in 2023 and deeply resonated with the heart intentions of this collaborative property management model. Since 2011, Julian has been working to deconstruct many of the false narratives that form the glue of modern society and also propel us into guaranteed destruction if we don’t find a way to redirect. An unquestioning belief in the concepts of fiat currency, private land ownership, Gaia as a “natural resource” that has value only once extracted or used by humans, a need to go to school to become educated, to go to the grocery store for food, the hospital to get healthy, etc. etc. There are a LOT of dangerous norms that can only be addressed simultaneously if we are to reach a heightened awareness and deeper alignnmet with the rest of creation. This is why Julian has been rather immersed and somewhat obsessed with the notion of Community Homesteading as Comprehensive Eco-Social Realignment. It holds the promise of addressing multiple challenges at once: food, housing, land use, energy, community, entertainment, education, etc. The journey of the last 14 years has been rich and rewarding while deeply challenging on many levels as well. With the upcoming partnership and transition of the Bangor property to a Farmland Commons, we can begin to more confidently expand the trusted and skilled and compassionate pool of collaborators to more effectively and broadly address the needs we all have for more community, more health, better lifestyles in the midst of the meta-crisis of modern humanity. properties.name: Eco Caminhos
properties.description:
Located in the mountains of Nova Friburgo, Rio de Janeiro, Eco Caminhos is a working permaculture farm and a reference project  for regenerative practices, education, and community living.
Our planet is facing growing environmental crises: climate change, extreme weather events, pollution, deforestation, and the depletion of natural resources. The world needs concrete, working examples of a different way of living — and we believe the answers already exist in how we grow food, build homes, and live together. That is what we practice here every day.
Since 2014, we have built a farm that is almost self-sufficient in energy and produces 60% of its own food, including vegetables, fruits, milk, eggs, and meat. We have planted over 10,000 trees, developed 6 agroforestry systems with more than 260 plant species and 4,500 coffee trees, and built several bioconstruction structures using natural and local materials.
We are currently developing Ecolibrium, an ambitious project designed to become a regional permaculture learning center.
We work with native species, reforestation, water source recovery, soil management, natural building techniques such as cob and wattle-and-daub, and we are constantly improving our systems.
Living here means getting your hands dirty, sharing responsibilities, learning by doing, and being part of something meaningful.
We offer different programs depending on your interests, availability, and how long you can stay, from short immersive experiences to free long-term volunteering for those ready to commit and grow with us. We receive volunteers, students, researchers, families, and groups from all over the world, all year round.
If this resonates with you, we would love to hear from you.
We have different volunteer oportunities and programs, some short term and others long term.
Please visit our webpage to find out more and get in contact with us: www.ecocaminhos.com
Eco Caminhos is a sustainable eco farm and community in the Atlantic Forest high in the sierra of Rio de Janeiro. We focus on natural building (bioconstruction), growing food organically, agroforestry, and permaculture as ways to achieve positive personal and social development in harmony with nature.
Eco Caminhos is a great program! It teaches you about bio construction and permaculture, it gives you the opportunity to experience Brazil, a great country with many beautiful things to see, hear, taste and experience, and also puts you in contact with people from all over the world that come to volunteer here. The whole Eco Caminhos experience helps you get in contact with nature, with people, and most important with yourself. Being away from the city "jungle" and the hectic life it requires, you'll be more able to clear your thoughts and you'll have more time to connect with yourself and people and nature around you. Because of all these, experiencing E

The shirt is available at Teespring.com/CreativeCommons , with all 100% of the proceeds going to support Creative Commons. It will be available until April 8, 2015 and sales start right now. Help us spread the word in all of your channels #celebrateCC The shirt is comprised of dozens of unique, freely reusable and Creative Commons (or PD) licensed icons from the Noun Project (see a list of designers/artists below) as well as each of our primary sharing logos. Noun Project staff designers created this special edition of the logo, which was originally conceived by designer/animator Ryan Junell or Creative Commons in 2002. You can read more about the history of the Creative Commons logos, and their recent acquisition by the Museum of Modern Art in New York, over here . Our huge thanks to Noun Project and Teespring for helping us with this great initiative. We know Creative Commons supporters love apparel, and we think this is one of the best we’ve ever seen. We also want to proudly recognize the work of the following Noun Project artists, illustrators, and designers whose icons and symbols make up the special edition, commemorative CC logo: Edward Boatman Stanislav Levin Scott Lewis Ryan Oksenhorn Ethan Clark Nathan Driskell John Caserta Thibault Geffroy Jakub Ukrop Travis Yunis Hafizh P.J. properties.name: Ekoby Växhuset
properties.description: Ekoby Växhuset. Växhuset är en grön oas i Södra Hälsingland. Här finns ett vandrarhem öppet året runt och inspiration för en hållbarare värld. Trädgårdsodlingar, kurser och föreläsningar om odling, miljö, ekobygg och Permakultur. Hur ska vi sköta vår planet på ett klokt sätt? Det är en fråga som jag har ställt mig i över 40 år. Växhuset som projekt och vision kan ses som ett sökande efter svaret på frågan. Från att ursprungligen vara ett privat boende har det utvecklats till ett företag, Växhuset Ord & Jord, och en liten ekoby, Ekoby Växhuset, med fyra hushåll. "Vi gör inte anspråk på att sitta på alla svar, men vi hoppas att vi ändå kan vara en del av lösningen och bidra med konstruktiva idéer för en mer hållbar utveckling." Ralf och Sonja Palmpers, initiativtagare och ägare av Växhuset. Ekoby Växhuset. Efter att i många år ha drivit Växhuset själva tog vi steget att erbjuda andra att dela vår underbara plats. Idag finns fem andelar, varav fyra ägs av likasinnade och den femte fortfarande söker sin ägare. Här i byn har vi egna privata tomter och en samfällighet med vatten, avlopp, tvättstuga, verkstad, solenergi och gemensam mark att odla och hämta ved från. Förutom oss vuxna och barn finns här höns, kaniner, katter och bin. Vandrarhem i en grönskande oas. I mer än 20 år har vi drivit vandrarhemmet som numera är öppet året runt. Sommartid kan du bo i en enkel men spännande, 12-kantig byggnad. I huvudbyggnaden, integrerat med ett växthus, finns reception, gästkök och frukostservering. Här kan du numera även bo i ett bekvämt rum i direkt anslutning till växthuset. I rummet som vi kallar "Studion" finns även en liten bio för filmentusiasten och ett film- och trädgårdsbibliotek. Vandrarhemmet ligger mitt i vår trädgård med frukt och bär och mycket att upptäcka för stor och liten. Runt Växhuset breder hälsingeskogarna ut sig med berg och sjöar. Bara 150 meter från vandrarhemmet kan du bada i Edesjön eller ta en tur med en av våra kanoter. Läs mer och boka under "vandrarhemmet". Permaku

properties.name: Erigiendo Dipam
properties.description:
* Water: The first priority is to locate a land close to some source of fresh water that can be made drinkable (river, stream, estuaries) or, failing that, fresh water that can be extracted from a well (in Corrientes, fresh water is easily available, in Chaco it presents more difficulties, but they are not insurmountable).
* Electrical lines: if there is an electrical network line nearby, that will facilitate the construction of the eco-village, although it aspires to be independent later, the possibility of having a nearby electrical network line allows for a faster start and an emergency plan in case the internal networks fail.
* Accessibility: The space should ideally be 
* Flooding: In the NEA region, flooding is a major problem, so it is a priority to choose a location that is not so easily flooded and/or that can be prepared to protect structures and crops from potential flooding. The region is characterized by the presence of Malaria, Dengue and recently Chikungunya, which are viruses transmitted by mosquitoes that reproduce in stagnant waters, it is necessary to avoid this through good channeling of water at surface level, in the location.
* Security: It is advisable to analyze the state of justice in the municipality where the placement is planned, not only to see what the position and behavior of the locals is in relation to people from outside, but also of the local authorities in front of them. It is highly recommended to fence the land and evaluate how to maintain a safe space there.
Considerations about the first Building to build:
* Base: The cement/brick base must be built in a central point to provide an effective foundation (mechanical tools will probably be required) in the face of climatic conditions.
* Materials: The most used (and easily available) elements in the area are fired bricks, zinc sheets and pine tongues, unless a viable alternative is found, it is recommended to use them.
* Size: The first structure would fulfill multiple functions, from a meditation room, a shed to store things and even a basic living space; Therefore, its estimated size should be around 7 meters long by 5 meters wide and have a height of 2 and 30 meters.
* Electricity: As already mentioned, the proximity of power lines would greatly facilitate the initial tasks (at least until the first local renewable energy network is activated in the building). This building must have a base wiring designed to have lighting, sockets (at a good height, p

properties.name: Folleterre Faerie Sanctuary
properties.description:
Welcome to Folleterre: part of the global network of radical faerie communities.
Folleterre aims to be a “pink lighthouse” where queers expand their special gifts and nature, explore the relationship between sexuality and spirit, and aims to provide a place of safety, homecoming, inspiration – a home for an ever-growing family of kindred souls.
Folleterre is a sanctuary from the materialism and consumerism of much of gay life: a place for respite and healing in the beauty of nature. This is a home where faeries can be gentle and intimate together and also as wild and creative as their spirits move them to be.
Folleterre was founded in 2005 following ten years of Eurofaerie gatherings and four years of searching for land. The local forest already had the perfect name: “bois de la folleterre,” translatable as “land of the queers” or “land of the crazies.”
The Folleterre Sanctuary consists of  several hectares of land within a nature reserve in the Vosges Mountains of eastern France.  The land features mountain trails, forests, streams, meadows, gardens, fruit trees, a farmhouse, and a barn.
The ceremonial opening of Folleterre took place on the Autumn equinox of 2005, with 30 queers gathering from several countries – Holland, France, Germany, UK, Spain and the USA – to ignite a fire of passion, healing and love. Since then, hundreds of faeries have visited the land during gatherings and at quieter times. People have started living on the land consistently during the summer months.
Sanctuary life involves living, working and playing together–in the kitchen, chopping wood, growing and harvesting fruit and vegetables, constructing and improving the property, going with the natural flow of life on the land and simply being. Much skill sharing happens at the sanctuary, with faeries learning from each other in areas such as massage, building, cooking, gardening, make up, performance and more.
Gatherings are the liveliest times at the sanctuary, when you can meet people from many cultures, join in workshops, hold parties, share in heart circles, enjoy vegetarian feasts and revel in unlimited glamour!
Folleterre is a multi-lingual space and the best possible accommodations are made for all abilities and disabilities. The sanctuary is dedicated to providing welcoming hospitality to all who come.
Steward of Folleterre Faerie Sanctuary (www.folleterre.org), a space for queer/LGBTQI+ people in the foothillls of the Vosges mountains in eastern France

properties.name: Fundacion Escuela Sintana y Tierra Negra
properties.description: [![Image 1: Picture]( Mama Jose Miguel Nolavita and Family Stayed at Ugaeichi.** Mama Jose Miguel Nolavita and family came down from the high Sierra to do pagamentos at the mouth of the Buritaca river, completing the sacred cycle of the waters. They stayed at Ugaeichi. He and his family work closely with the Foundation. [NEXT STORY]( **2023-11 Volunteers from Europe and South America working on Tierra Negra.** A Venezuelan woman and her baby have been living on Tierra Negra. We've had indigenous help, as well as volunteers from Norway, Canada, Spain, the US and Colombia clearing and cleaning crop areas. The volunteer from the US left a very inspired message. Kogi Bring Homemade Panela to Barter With. The first structure was built on Ugaeichi, a roofed rectangle store supplies and equipment. We are hooked up to the village aqueduct. Now we are collecting wood and sourcing green palm leaves for the kankurua. Eventually, we would like to make a kitchen and also a room to operate as the Coop. Kogi friends brought their homemade panela to barter with. This is sugar as organic and natural as it gets. The canes are squeezed and the sweet water is boiled until it is a solid chunk. properties.name: Fundación Durika
properties.description:
Open to visitors since 1995 and accessible by road since 1997, the Durika Biological Reserve (DBR) offers the true naturalist a unique opportunity to get to know this extraordinary area with little known biodiversity  of Costa Rica. Durika is a private reserve that protects approximately 8500 hectares localized in the top of the Talamanca Mountain Range, in the south west part of the country, about 17 kilometers North East of the town of Buenos Aires.
Motivated because of environmental deterioration and the conditions of the area, a group of professional Costa Ricans founded 'The Durika Association of Agricultural and Cultural Development'. The community originally started with 50 members and has since grown to over 100 members of different nationalities. An average of 30 people live permanently in the community. All those here are committed to help in the protection of nature and the environment, especially in the area, as well as to contributing in a positive way to culture, with emphasis placed on Costa Rican culture. Over time new members of other cultures have of course also given their support to the project, and in so doing have inspired others so that they too can continue in the search for a better way of life on our planet

properties.name: Futures Growing Together
properties.description:
What an incredible moment to be alive
Here we stand, at the convergence of the intertwining crises that threaten the future of all life on earth, and the growing awakening to our place in the web of life as caretakers of this precious blue-green sphere floating in space.
With the technology and knowledge now capable of both destroying and regenerating life on earth, we have become the keystone species of this planet.
The question now is can we overcome the powers of greed and hatred in time to evolve into a civilization of equality, compassion and regeneration, or will nature bring us back to living within planetary boundaries?
If you’re reading this, then you’ve probably also decided that a future that supports life is rooted in community. A community of humans living and working with each other, and living consciously and respectfully within the larger community of life that supports us.
This is what we have been working to develop for 10 years in the Southern Gulf Islands off the coast of British Columbia. We have poured our minds, hearts, spirits and bank accounts into creating a space that can support life through this great transition we are now in the midst of. With a healthy mix of Permaculture and trial and error we have been diligently setting up resilient and off grid systems of energy, water, food, shelter and community.
We have reached the point where we can no longer continue expanding our vision without the support of a larger community of people living and working together. Our core team is made up of extended family members who are either too young, too old, too tired or moving in a different direction, for us to be able to hold what we have created alone anymore.
This is an invitation for anyone who shares our vision of a future that is grounded in regenerative food systems, interdependent community living, love and reverence for all forms of life, a commitment to personal and collective growth and healing, and a desire to build this future together.
Reach out for more information and to start the conversation.
Written on January 18, 2025,  2 Comments
Hello, I am interested in connecting more about the community you are building. I am holding many skills regarding ecovillage design and development and community living and learning. I resonate with the words you use to describe your project and I love the Salish coast. I have 2 kids ages 4 and 8 who I currently homeschool and want to have in a community nature connection school, which I could start and also have friends on Salt adoring Island with a school we love. What island are you on?
Pender Island is listed as the location

properties.name: GROW BIOINTENSIVE CENTRE KENYA
properties.description: Thika, Kenya — Registered NGO — Est. 2008 ## Growing Food. Rebuilding Soil. _Transforming Lives._ Empowering Communities, Transforming Lives. G-BiACK is a training and demonstration center that promotes agroecological agriculture, food sovereignty and environmental conservation through the GROW BIOINTENSIVE method. 19638+ Farmers Trained 157 Demo Beds on Campus 18+ Yrs Sustained Impact 100% Chemical-Free Farming Our Purpose ## What Drives Us Every Day ### Build Living Soil Our double-dug beds and cold composting methods regenerate even the most depleted soil up to 60 times faster than nature — with no chemicals, ever. Healthy soil is the foundation of everything. ### Feed Families Kenyan smallholders trained by G-BiACK consistently produce 2 to 6 times more food on the same land — enough to nourish their families, share with neighbours, and sell at local markets. ### Empower Communities From seed saving to artisan income generation, we train women, youth groups, schools and farmer cooperatives across Central, Eastern and Nairobi Counties — building lasting self-reliance. ![Image 1: Daily Nation feature on G-BiACK model farm]( **Thika**40 km north of Nairobi Who We Are ## A Model Farm & Community Classroom Founded by Samuel and Peris Nderitu — both graduates of the Manor House Agricultural Centre — G-BiACK is a registered Kenyan NGO and training centre that promotes agroecological agriculture, food sovereignty and environmental conservation through the GROW BIOINTENSIVE method. The organisation has trained over 20,000 smallholder farmers since 2008. **160+ organically grown double-dug beds** — vegetables, grains, legumes, fruit trees, and a man-made river for year-round irrigation. **Ecology Action (USA) partner organisation** — part of the global GROW BIOINTENSIVE movement researched over 50 years by John Jeavons. **Winner of the 2012 Food Sovereignty Prize** — recognised internationally for transforming food systems at the grassroots level. **100% of international

properties.name: Gaia Terra
properties.description:
Gaia Terra
Setting: Rural Type: Eco Community, Eco Project (Eco Tourism Site, Restoration Site)
Status: Established Founded: 2017
Main Language: Italian Other Languages: English, Slovenian, Spanish
WHY? Something we learn to ask around our 3 years of life, when our curiosity takes over.
Somehow over time we unlearn to be curious, to ask (and ask ourselves) questions and consequently to give us answers.
Here at Gaia, the most banal and perhaps the most complicated one is: “who are we”? Visionaries? Crazy? Wacky? For which the answer is much more “childish”: we are people who want to live “lightly” from what the world outside imposes: individualism, fake necessities, consumerism, greed, selfishness.
The ex-factory with 115 years of history behind its inveterate production (bricks first, then electrical engineering) has been making room for the Gaia Terra project for a few years. An intentional community that firmly believes in the renewal of a society, seeking balance on planet Earth, through tools such as Manitonquat’s “circle way”.
Equality, free expression and lightness therefore, we bring into all our activities, from cooking (strictly vegan, organic and local), to the cultivation and maintenance of our permaculture gardens; from the search for self-production and food self-sustenance, to the construction of the environments strictly necessary in green building. The icing on the cake is the phytodepuration system, which allows us to give back to our “Stella” river pure, clear and crystalline water as it comes to us from the subsoil of Mother Earth.
As a venue for renovation and very careful not to waste any resources, from food to energy, Gaia offers its spaces in green building (dormitory, 200 m2 multifunctional space, dance floor, kitchen and canteen) to a myriad of activities, from a Biobirthday to the most complex and articulated events (workshops and residential courses over several days).
In any case, the “Gaiaterrestri” are ready to welcome every person open and outstretched minded with curiosity, just like a child expressing their most tender and light wonder towards the world that is temporarily hosting them.
Membership & Visitors
Current members: 15
Open to new members
Open to visitors
To join Gaia Terra, you can be a volunteer:
1. Straw, who collaborates to the project by giving a few hours or days of work from time to time
2. Clay, who participates in specific projects during limited periods
3. Wood, who stays from 15 days to 6 months as a member of the community
4: Brick, the permanent residents that form the decisional circles
Or a guest:
1. At the eco-camping (considering inner and outer spaces)
2. As a worker/student who stays for longer periods enjoying the co-working spaces
3. As an attendee of any of the events promoted here
At this moment, we are specially open for brick volunteers: people who has made this lifestyle a life choice and can offer consistency and stability to a decisional group, strengthening the base.
Image Gallery
More

properties.name: Ganas Community
properties.description: Ganas Community Ganas Community Home Paying Jobs Contact Our stores web site GANAS COMMUNITY Home Paying to live at Ganas Job opportunities Contact info Our stores web site Every Thing Goes Thrift & Vintage The next ONLINE prospective member information session will be on Friday, July 10, at 7:00pm. Email Susan at info@ganas.org for the meeting link. anas Community started on Staten Island in 1979. The six original founders came together to form a self-selected extended family based on an intention to care for each other while sharing work, having fun, and addressing whatever problems arise, together. Since then our population has grown to about 65. Our purpose is to bring reason and emotion together in daily problem-solving, in order to create our world, with love, the way we want it to be. Open minds make it possible to talk together and understand each other better. We ongoingly strive to better cooperate, care for each other, and share resources. Over the years we have developed a method of solving problems called Feedback Learning. This is an ongoing exploration and we welcome those who want to join us in exploring together. Why "ganas"? The Spanish word "ganas" means "motivation sufficient to act". We chose it as the name for our community because it represented an important central value better than any word in English. Community living is full of interpersonal challenges and opportunities. With âganasâ we use our energy to focus on working out problems together. This allows us to embrace diversity, which makes community living sustainable. Sustainability at Ganas We are taking what steps we can to minimize our carbon footprint and contribution to climate change and reviewing what more might be possible. Members who want to contribute to this work are very welcome. Life at Ganas Currently because of the COVID-19 pandemic we encourage but do not require masks and social distancing in public spaces, hold many community gatherings outside if the weather perm

properties.description: ![Image 1: np-white]( Today Creative Commons, the [Noun Project]( and [Teespring.com]( are [excited to announce]( an awesome, limited edition commemorative t-shirt celebrating our wonderful logo. The shirt is available at [Teespring.com/CreativeCommons]( with all 100% of the proceeds going to support Creative Commons. It will be available until April 8, 2015 and sales start right now. Help us spread the word in all of your channels [#celebrateCC]( The shirt is comprised of dozens of unique, freely reusable and Creative Commons (or PD) licensed icons from the Noun Project (see a list of designers/artists below) as well as each of our primary sharing logos. Noun Project staff designers created this special edition of the logo, which was originally conceived by designer/animator Ryan Junell or Creative Commons in 2002. You can read more about the history of the Creative Commons logos, and their recent acquisition by the Museum of Modern Art in New York, over [here]( ![Image 2: np-shirt-reg.png]( 3: np-shirt-fitted.png]( Our huge thanks to Noun Project and Teespring for helping us with this great initiative. We know Creative Commons supporters love apparel, and we think this is one of the best we’ve ever seen. We also want to proudly recognize the work of the following Noun Project artists, illustrators, and designers whose icons and symbols make up the special edition, commemorative CC logo: * Edward Boatman * Stanislav Levin * Scott Lewis * Ryan Oksenhorn * Ethan Clark * Nathan Driskell * John Caserta * Thibault Geffroy * Jakub Ukrop * Travis Yunis * Hafizh * P.J. properties.name: Global Tribe Biodynamic Eco-village
properties.description: Global Tribe Cart 0 Back Boutique Adopt a Tree Back Membership Back About Contact Find us Scroll A fun & conscious eco-living space for all of us who care! Here at Global Tribe, we do our best everyday to live sustainably according to the cycles of Mother Nature, while inspiring everyone to live simply, and love fully. Located in the heart of the Doñana region, a natural world heritage site in Andalucia, the South of Spain, we’re always ready to welcome visitors, digital nomads, volunteers, and long-term residents to join our biodynamic revolution. Understand our philosophy Powered by nature W Welcome to our eco-living project! Here at Global Tribe, we put our best effort into living sustainably and in harmony with the cycles of nature. Our philosophy is based on developing, educating and disseminating habits of sustainable and self-sufficient life, through forms of cooperation between human and nature. We train with tools that allow development and growth of social, individual and collective conscience. We promote biodynamic living, governed by the principles of recognizing the earth, plants, animals and people as a one integrated organism. Everything we grow here is fully organic. We even have our very own goji berry plantation, crowned as one of the most essential elements in traditional Chinese medicine. Goji berries have long been an important health supplement in many cultures. So you know that your superfood is indeed super good. We are aware of the importance of care, respect and defense of the environment, implementing waste reduction procedures, recycling and maximizing the use of energy in order to optimize resources. By co-exisiting in community, we acquire values ​​of tolerance and respect for the individual and the environment, encouraging each other to further build co-living places, based on the pillars of peace, unity, respect, freedom, culture, creativity and conservation. We encourage you to live simply and love fully with joy! Download our Corpor

properties.description:
Green Valley EcoVillage
Setting: Rural, Urban
Founded: 2003
Main Language: English
We are promoting various schemes under the Eco-Village concept, namely Eco-Farm, Eco-Landscape, E-Business, Farmstay & Cottage Development.
Our Vision is to realize a community living in a setting that is very much Pro-Environment, sustainable to our future generation. The vision shall be realized via design and planning of Nutrimental planting schemes.
We stresses on educating our visitors and staff in creating ecologically sustainable living spaces. In short, we work, play and stay harmoniously with nature!
Membership & Visitors
Current members: 50
Non-working Guests welcome, Work Exchange welcome, Volunteers welcome,

properties.name: Guarda Rios Ecovillage
properties.description: This was our first full Summer on the land. We had some productive weeks with several different volunteers and some of the participants from the first and second founding gatherings who stayed on to help with the work on the land. Clearing the Small Ruin Hari has been working with an architect to start applying for a campsite licence, including a renovation of the small ruin close to the entrance of the lower land. Jefke and Simone did a great job clearing all the mimosa trees and brambles so that we could go inside with the architect to see it better and take measurements. ![Image 1]( Before and after ![Image 2]( ![Image 3]( Before and after ![Image 4]( ![Image 5]( Before and during ![Image 6]( The building is unusual in that the floor is almost two meter below the level of the ground outside it, so it would need a good drainage design. ![Image 7]( ![Image 8]( The walls are in good condition and the architect told us we should be able to extend the floor area by 30% and possibly add a second floor. The current area is 42 square meters, so will be around 54 square meters, just big enough for apply for a one bedroom house design. ![Image 9]( We would keep the original end wall, and build it up to make the roof higher. ![Image 10]( We imagine extending the building on this side. We would probably build up the other end wall with stone to match the look of the original build. We are aiming for a house design that can function as the reception and small office space for the campsite, while also providing a bedroom to rent. Finishing the common house After the Spring gathering, we had several volunteers continuing the work on the clay plastering of the walls next to the kitchen. ![Image 11]( After two layers of plaster, we built some extra storage, aiming to finish the upper part of the wall later, and possibly a third layer of finer plaster in the future. For now, we were prioritising getting all of the interior walls plastered with at least two layers, so that we could

properties.name: Gut Alaune
properties.description:
Gut Alaune
Ecological, social, cultural project and living space.
Setting: Rural Type: Eco Community, Eco Project (Permaculture Project, Restoration Site)
Status: Established Founded: 2016
Main Language: German Other Languages: English, Polish, Turkish
We are around about 10 people (adults and children, including guests and helpers) who are living as a community on a beautiful piece of land near the river Saale in the area Franzigmark. Close to Halle Saale Germany Here we are working on all different kinds of projects.
On the land, which we bought in the beginning of 2018 as a nonprofit organisation, is next to a lot of beautiful green space and a few neighbours.
Since than we invested a lot of time, energy and love to restore some of the existing buildings, and cleared up the land from the trash and junk from the past. We are giving birth here to a new alternative lifestyle as a socio-cultural, eco-friendly community doing our best to live in harmony with each other and with nature.
The main motivation and drive behind all our actions is to live sustainability. 
Therefore, we started to renovate the sheds in an ecological and aesthetical way, turning them, step by step, into open workshop spaces. Also, the main house, which we use as our common kitchen and dining area at the moment, is still under construction and is waiting to get a second floor on top, which will be used as a seminar room.
In our community we live by a horizontal decision-making structure, where we learn a lot from each other in our daily lives. We are thriving and evolving with many different kinds of creativity. Mainly in the fields of art, music, social work, carpentry crafting and building. All these skills come together in our common living as a group. They are also visible in the construction processes of all of our building projects.
We try to keep our ecological footprint as small as possible by recycling and upcycling all different kinds of materials. We also try to spread more awareness and sensitivity about sustainability in our environment.
We enjoy to create a space to share our passions (Building, Crafts, acrobatics, theatre, Clown, Ceramics, Instrument building, Yoga, Group process’)  and open spaces for people to realise theirs.
Membership & Visitors
Current members: 8
Not currently open to new members, but might be in the future
Open to visitors
Drop us a mail. Find out when its good to visit pass by for a few days. Come and be a guest for a while longer.
If you like us and we like you and we are open to more people! you can apply to join which leads into a probation time living together and seeing how it is.
More
About Gut Alaune
We are Gut Alaune a small living community/ecological, cultural project space close to Halle Salle in Germany.
Founded in 2016 we are currently around 8 people building up and creating this space.
