properties.description: Home - Hawaiian Sanctuary Skip to content A Tropical Retreat Experience Book Now About Our Accommodations Accommodations, Eco Retreats, and Spa Facilities Hawaiian Sanctuary Retreat Center is an Eco Retreat nestled in the jungles of Big Island, Hawai’i. We are dedicated to providing accommodations, retreats, permaculture education as well as opportunities for personal and collective growth. We open our arms and our home to you with the warm-hearted spirit of Aloha. Accommodations Events Retreats Elixir Bar Come re-connect with nature in an enchantingly powerful way. Rooms Events Retreats Elixir Bar Body Work Yoga Gym Farm to Table Our Accommodations We aren’t your typical resort vacation. Nestled in the remote jungles of East Hawaii, we offer an authentic taste of island life combined with eco-conscious comfort. Rest, recharge, rejuvenate and reconnect with nature. Comfortably. At Hawaiian Sanctuary you can enjoy a unique stay on a permaculture farm and spa retreat, with lots of perks… Book Your Stay Retreats Retreat Package 1 Build Your Own Retreat Retreat Package 2 See All Retreats Book Your Stay We invite you to join our daily yoga classes at the Hawaiian Sanctuary Retreat Center. Whether you’re a beginner or an experienced practitioner, our classes cater to all levels and are led by knowledgeable and passionate instructors. Immerse yourself in the serenity and beauty of our natural surroundings as you flow through each pose and connect with your breath. Our classes provide a refreshing and rejuvenating practice that nurtures your mind, body, and spirit. Come join us and experience the transformative power of yoga! Yoga & Healing Arts Schedule A Blissful Few Days at the Hawaiian Sanctuary Just spent a blissful few days at the Hawaiian Sanctuary. The premises is beautiful, lush and so full- full of good energy, full of great character! The sense of community was so beautiful. In particular the community kitchen was awesome! If you like to live free and experience

properties.name: Hearth & Soul Eco Farm
properties.description: Where are we? | Hearth & Soul Eco Farm Hearth & Soul Eco Farm The Joy of Growing Home About Us Sybille’s Story Where are we? Workshops & Seminars Our Rooms Where are we? The Hearth & Soul Eco Farm is the ideal venue for team-building get-aways and hosting courses/workshops/retreats and small conferences. The setting lends itself to weddings and private functions with a natural flavour, our function room can comfortably seat 120-150 people. We also have a cosy cellar room for smaller groups. We are situated in a secluded valley with diverse mountain fynbos and mixed farming, halfway between the hamlets of Stanford and Napier in the delightful Sandy’s Glen area, one of the best-kept secrets in the heart of the Overberg. We are under two hours drive from Cape Town and 50km from Hermanus. Grid reference: 34 27 58 S – 19 40 57 E Directions from Stanford Take the R326 out of Stanford for about 4km. Turn right toward Elim/Papiesvlei. Set your km tachometer on you car speedo – we are 20km from this turn off. After 11km you find ‘Oom Tat’ restaurant on your right. Cross the river, turn left to Sandy’s Glen. At the next unposted T-junction turn right . At the 17km mark, after the piece of tarred road, you pass ‘Sandy’s Glen Farm’ on your left, and at 20km you will find Hearth & Soul Eco-Farm where you turn left into a driveway sloping steeply downhill. The Car park is on the right at the bottom. Directions from Caledon -N2 From the N2 take the Bredasdorp turn-off through Caledon, and take the R316 towards Napier. After 21km past the ‘van Brakel Stoor’ crossroad, turn right to Sandy’s Glen. Continue for 14km and turn right where you see our “Hearth & Soul” sign. The drive slopes steeply downhill. The Car park is on the right at the bottom. Directions from Riviersonderend – N2. Take the R326, 10km after Riversonderend, until you reach the crossing at Van Brakel Stoor. Turn left towards Bredasdorp/Napier. After 21km turn right to Sandy’s Glen. Continue for 14km and turn right wh

properties.name: Holy Land Trust
properties.description:
Holy Land Trust
Through nonviolence, Holy Land Trust aspires to strengthen and empower the peoples of the Holy Land.
Setting: Urban Type: Unspecified, Other
Status: Established Founded: 1998
Main Language: Arabic Other Language: English
The Holy Land Trust is neither a political nor a religious organization; we aspire to learn from the spiritual teachings and values of all faiths and traditions that bring unity to the human family and closeness to the Creator of all things, practicing and promoting love, truth and goodness, between all people, and acting always with empathy and compassion.
The core values of the Holy Land Trust are:
Empathy and Compassion, recognizing that true humanity accepts the equal rights and existence of others. We are called to listen to and understand the needs of all individuals and communities and to honor, support, and protect everyone equally and treat others as we wish to be treated ourselves.
Nonviolence, recognizing that violence and oppression have no place in human life and that lasting peace cannot be achieved through active or passive aggression or violence. We strongly denounce all acts of violence and hold that the active practice of nonviolence is the more powerful force to expose all acts of injustice, end occupation and oppression, and achieve peace, justice, and equality in the Holy Land.
Community and Individuality, recognizing the unique expression of each human life and its fulfilment in what we share and achieve together. Strong communities recognize, value and support individuals; they nurture traditions and provide a supportive environment for development and change. Individuals make up communities and sustain them. We are called to celebrate and uphold distinct and different ways of living that fulfil human potential.
Equality and Justice, recognizing that all human beings and communities are equal and must be granted equal opportunity to prosper regardless of gender, race, religion, ethnic, or national affiliation, we call for all to be granted equal rights to live and prosper in the Holy Land, in an environment that upholds and defends such rights.
Trust and Respect, recognizing that the possibility for real peace can only be achieved when mutual relations of trust and respect are created in the midst of diversity within and across communities. Trust is the cornerstone of human relationships and it gives rise to respect so that the opportunity for love, unity, and commitment for building a future is created. Trust arises from personal integrity and transparency. We are called to engender trust and actively respect the legitimate rights and individual nature of others.
Spirituality, recognizing the sanctity of the human spirit and the Holy Land. We hold that lasting peace is founded on a deep connection and love of the Creator and of others as part of the oneness of humanity. We value spirituality as an essential basis for human living and development.
Membership & Visitors
Current members: 10
Open to new members
Open to visitors
Video
Sami Awad, founder of Holy Land Trust, speaks about loving your enemy and peace

About Mena Vieira
I was born in Portugal with a global heart, from an early age developing love and curiosity for different lands and their people.
I studied Fine Art in Scotland, nourishing my interest in all things beautiful, and soon after realized that this world is in need of more beautiful and healing ways of living together. A search for intentional community and for growing myself in a healthy and sustainable way took me to study and live in Findhorn (Scotland), Tamera (Portugal) and Neot Smadar (Israe

properties.name: Huertos Urbanos de Costa Rica
properties.description: Molinos Verdes de Moringa Skip to content Instagram – Facebook Molinos verdes de Moringa (MVM) promueve la permacultura ofreciendo recursos educativos, asesorías y creando redes de colaboración. La misión de MVM es difundir los principios básicos de cuidado de la tierra, cuidado de las personas e intercambio justo del excedente. Algunos de los ámbitos que MVM trabaja son: Huertos Urbanos Construcción natural Energías limpias Manejo de aguas Consumo responsable MVM busca compartir la Permacultura en Costa Rica y ayudar a conectar grupos e individuos con el mundo. Fundado en 2010, el nombre Molinos Verdes de Moringa, representa una solución simple y versátil, que trabaja con la naturaleza en una gran cantidad de ámbitos, adaptándose a diversas situaciones y con muchos beneficios. El árbol de moringa es tan versátil que todas las partes pueden ser utilizadas de alguna manera. MVM promueve los principios básicos de la Permacultura (cuidar el planeta, cuidar a las personas y compartir el excedente) a través de la educación, la colaboración y asesorías. Únete y sé parte del cambio! ¡Quiero aplicar como voluntario! / I want to volunteer! Permacultura La Permacultura es una filosofía que busca trabajar con , y no contra, la naturaleza. Se trata de observaciones prolongadas y reflexivas, en lugar de labores prolongadas e irreflexivas; de ver las plantas y los animales como un sistema, en lugar de tratar cada área como un elemento aislado. Nuestro enfoque de la permacultura es promover una manera holística de vivir con la naturaleza. Trabajamos para integrar la vida cotidiana con los principios de cuidar el planeta, cuidar a las personas y compartir el excedente. Algunas de las formas en las que incorporamos esas ideas son: diseño jardines comestibles, selección de comida saludable y amigable con el ambiente, soluciones modulares y eficientes para el día a día, reutilización de materiales, y compras inteligentes. Translate » Nosotros ▼ Acerca de Los Objetivos de Desarrollo So

properties.name: Hundredfold Farm
properties.description:
Hundredfold Farm is a small, rural multi-generational, co-housing community located near Gettysburg, Pennsylvania. The co-housing model is characterized by private dwellings, clustered housing, pedestrian-friendly design, and extensive common facilities. We promote sustainability with organic community gardens, active and passive solar components in our energy-efficient homes, and an innovative wastewater treatment facility that recycles water to our homes.
The cornerstone of our common vision for Hundredfold Farm may be expressed as Caring, Paring, and Sharing.
Working in partnership with the 80 acre piece of land that is Hundredfold Farm – after preserving this Christmas tree farm from subdivision and shifting it to organic farming methods – we are committed to keeping the interests of the land foremost in all our decisions.  This is why we are clustering the homes on six acres, siting the community on the steeper portions of the property that is less suitable for gardening, are working with the natural contours of the land in laying out the community, and giving much thought to where to site gardens and activities.
Reducing our ecological footprint – this value underlies decisions about incorporating active and passive solar power into the homes, a system which recycles our waste water for reuse as flush, laundry and irrigation water, growing our own food, vigorous recycling of everything from banana peels to beer bottles to batteries.  We are continually seeking ways to reduce our consumption of non-renewables and live more lightly on the land.
An ethic of finding ways to community as it naturally arises from living lives that consciously intersect with the lives of others.
Please visit the membership page on our website to learn about our pathways to membership: http://hundredfoldfarm.org/our-community/membership/
Written on August 22, 2022,

Discover NextGEN What We Do – Check out our projects! Discover and get inspired by our different projects and initiatives happening all over the world! Learn More Our Worldwide Network Connect to our networks in North America, Latin America, Africa, Europe and Asia & Oceania! Learn More The NextGEN Map Dive into the NextGEN’s community and discover our members and projects across the world! Learn More Ecovillages and Regeneration An ecovillage is an intentional, traditional or urban community that is consciously designed through locally owned participatory processes in all four dimensions of sustainability (social, cultural, ecological and economic) to regenerate social and natural environments. Regeneration is a worldwide movement of people who take action in Renewal, Restoration, Biodiversity, Healing, Conscious Living and Resilience. In order to tackle the systemic crisis we are currently living in, the regeneration movement believes we as humans must align to the natural Earth’s model again. properties.name: IDEAL Society
properties.description:
IDEAL Society
Institute for the Development in Education, Arts, and Leisure
Type: Ecovillage (Religious or Spiritual)
Founded: 1992
Main Language: French Other Languages: English, German, Spanish
IDEAL Society is a collectivity composed of members of all ages hailing from various nationalities, cultures, religions and backgrounds. Uniting this group is a common ideal of fraternity among people. Since 1993 our shared collective life in the beautiful mountains of southeastern British Columbia forms an experimental framework and environment in which to grow in harmony with nature, while contributing to the well-being of the group and larger community in a spirit of sustainable development.
IDEAL’s vision inspires us to experiment in the areas of education, wellness, art and agriculture, taking into account the material and spiritual dimensions.
Our objectives have a local, regional, national and international reach through our services, activities and status as a non-governmental organization affiliated with the United Nations. To implement IDEAL’s Vision, we created a community environment where education, the arts, well being and our relationship with nature hold an important place.
IDEAL seeks to promote a new educational system based on the principles of education through experience in keeping with human and ecological values. To carry through its mission IDEAL will:
• Communicate a vision of humanity as one family.
• Foster moral, social and aesthetic values through educational programs, artistic and leisure activities.
• Establish local, regional, national and international prenatal and perinatal educational programs, birth and youth centers.
• Initiate artistic, musical and cross cultural activities.
• Develop a way of life that embraces the laws of nature and considers the earth as a living organism.
Membership & Visitors
Current members: 50
Not open to new members
More
About Cristallin
Manual Osteopathic Practitioner D.O.
Personal and Entreprise Coach
Development Manager at IDEAL Society since 2009.
www.idealsociety.org

properties.name: InanItah
properties.description: Thanks for all the support! | InanItah InanItah Get in touch! connect@inanitah.com | Getting Here Menu: About | Staying | Learning | Connecting | Giving Contact Us Getting Here Stay Connected InanItah Blog Recent Comments Subscribe to RSS « Water Project Update Choktober Fest 2010 » Thursday, October 21st, 2010 Thanks for all the support! We are excited to announce that over the past month, six professional massage tables, along with enough funding to cover shipping and customs expenses have been donated for the upcoming Professional Massage Training. This course will empower local Nicaraguans with new economic opportunities by making it possible for Ometepe residents to practice the art of professional healing massage at local hotels. Recent arrivals, Angela and Justin, are now leading up our community empowerment programming. They have been integral in reaching out to the community to find locals who want to participate in the massage training program. We are celebrating the amazing support that we are receiving from Valerie, course instructor, who has invested so much of her time and energy in the program and been so active in garnering donations. A chain of synchronistic events is enabling this course to take place and bring some more healing energy to Isla de Ometepe. This post filed under Uncategorized . We are celebrating the amazing support that we are receiving from Valerie, course instructor, who has invested so much of her time and energy in the program and been so active in garnering donations. A chain of synchronistic events is enabling this course to take place and bring some more healing energy to Isla de Ometepe. This post filed under Uncategorized . properties.name: India One Solar Thermal Power Plant
properties.description:
For Brahma Kumaris (BK) and the World Renewal Spiritual Trust (WRST), the need for renewable energy emerged due to the fast growth of the organization and the need to provide the headquarters, visited by hundreds of thousands of people every year, with reliable and sustainable energy supply. Using clean and renewable energy for the operation of Brahma Kumaris centers is an expression of respect for the natural resources. Initiative of “India One” by the team of WRST was driven by the vision of providing undisrupted, round-the-clock solar power to the institution, with the technology that is suitable for India. The vision has been achieved by developing indigenous and simplified Concentrating Solar Power technology, with breakthrough thermal storage: reliable, low maintenance, easy to fabricate and cost effective. “India One” team aims to demonstrate this innovative technology for combined heat and power applications thorough capacity building in local community, facilitating technology transfer to interested companies and dissemination of knowledge to the wide public, inspiring individuals and communities to use the renewable energy.
Specific aim of the project was to design and develop “India One” 1.0 MW el solar thermal power plant with .
If you are interested to join the community of India One Solar Thermal Power Plant, please kindly contact us at info@india-one.net or one of the Brahma Kumaris centers near you (www.brahmakumaris.org).
Coordinator at India One Solar Thermal Power Plant in Abu Road, Rajasthan

properties.name: Infinite Stillness Farm and Retreat
properties.description:
Infinite Stillness Farm and Retreat
Clarity. Focus,. Matter.
Setting: Rural Type: Eco Project (Eco Farm, Eco Tourism Site), Ecovillage (Religious or Spiritual)
Status: Established Founded: 2022
Main Language: English Other Language: Spanish
A developing habitat, emerging within the infinite stillness of the primary forest. This is not a finished retreat, but a seed—a project cultivating a self-sustainable village for those seeking authentic connection. The vision is simple: a cluster of modest dwellings, harmoniously placed, where the profound silence of the ancient woods becomes the backdrop for inner work.
We are building a place for individuals who wish to contribute. It is a collaboration of different people, each adding their energy to the life of the village—tending the land, sharing skills, or simply holding the space. United by shared principles of peace and harmony, we are creating a living example of conscious community.
Here, in the deep quiet, we rediscover our connection to the earth and to each other. For anyone who feels the call, this is an invitation to be part of something becoming.
Membership & Visitors
Current members: 4

properties.name: KITA
properties.description:
KITA is a non-profit premier tropical agricultural college in Ghana networking with local and global institutions to better the lives of rural farmers, unemployed youth, hungry and homeless families, women, aids victims and orphans, needy street children, the aged, and the disabled Through training, seminars and workshops, technology transfer, on-farm research, environmental conservation, volunteer exchange, seeds and food aid, input and clothing distribution and children’s sponsorship.For environmentally sustainable development, that enhances the local economy, the nation and the quality of life in Africa since 1984
The specific objectives include:
To enhance the transfer of modern agricultural
technology to rural farmers through workshops, seminars short courses, correspondence courses, and overseas training
To provide vocational technical agricultural training to the youth both literate and non literate
To protect the environment for increased agricultural productivity
To assist rural farmers to have links to credit and inspire confidence of financial institutions in rural agricultural credit delivery
To undertake farmer-based research to develop appropriate strategies for practicing farmers, both commercial and subsistence
To provide consultancy to prospective and practicing farmers and trainees to establish their own agricultural projects and identify market for their produce
To assist rural communities to draw their own village development plans based on their local resources, to improve upon their own lifestyles
To liaise with local and international institutions and organisations with similar

[Read More]( Blog Post Title Two — Ecovillage Tours 0 Blog Post Title Two May 28 Written By Cynthia Tina It all begins with an idea. Maybe you want to launch a business. Maybe you want to turn a hobby into something more. Or maybe you have a creative project to share with the world. Whatever it is, the way you tell your story online can make all the difference. Don’t worry about sounding professional. Sound like you. There are over 1.5 billion websites out there, but your story is what’s going to separate this one from the rest. If you read the words back and don’t hear your own voice in your head, that’s a good sign you still have more work to do. Be clear, be confident and don’t overthink it. The beauty of your story is that it’s going to continue to evolve and your site can evolve with it. Your goal should be to make it feel right for right now. Later will take care of itself. properties.name: Kaisdhar - KU Learning Centre
properties.description:
Kaisdhar – KU Learning Centre
Bridging the gap between urban and rural communities for sustainable mountain development.
Setting: Rural Type: Eco Project (Eco Tourism Site, Green School, Learning Centre, Youth-led Project)
Status: Aspiring In Planning Since: 
Main Language: Hindi
Kaisdhar is an immensely beautiful place, with grassy meadows and densely forested mountains. The lush green trail and biodiversity rich environment makes it an excellent site to pause, introspect and reflect our interconnectedness with nature.
Our Purpose : The Kaisdhar Community is curating an unique experience to encourage responsible tourism and facilitate deep-rooted cultural exchange. Through ecotourism and environmental conservation initiatives the community is showing pathways how we can foster resilience in mountain communities alongwith generating a sustainable livelihood options. The community is adopting widescale promotion of Agroecology, community based social enterprise model, vibrant cultural performance and preservation of traditional crops/ cuisines as tools to attract urban communities.
Krishi Updates Digital Agriculture Model is as open access ICT platform for Co-creation and sharing of knowledge that fosters Mindfulness based sustainable transformation and resilience in communities. The digital platform also provides capacity building programs through blended learning for upskilling communities.
The Kaisdhar – KU Learning Center aims to –
1- Organise various workshops and write shop for documenting attributes for building resilience of mountain communities
2- Facilitate in marketing and outreach of Kaisdhar Ecovillage Social Enterprise Model
3- Promote peer to peer learning for co creating solutions and knowledge sharing
4- Building capacities of mountain communities for Sustainability
5- Mindfulness based system Transformation through Consciousness approaches and creating safe spaces for collective wisdom
6- Incubating rural enterprises and building consciousness based leaders for sustainability of initiatives
Membership & Visitors
Current members: 200
Open to new members
Open to visitors
Please reach out to us on email - krishiupdates2020@gmail.com
We are looking for Responsible Travellers, who want to rejuvenate in nature and get inner peace; Researchers - Interested in doing agricultural systems and resilience studies; Students - Interested in living in rural setting, pursuing environmental projects and working with local communities; Artists - Interested in exploring rich traditions, cultures and customs of Himalayas; Corporates - Interested in Corporate travels and facilitating market reach for organic mountain products.
Image Gallery
More

Krishi Updates digital platform provides an open access robust ICT infrasructure for Co-creation and sharing of knowledge and building skills of communities through blended learning.

properties.name: Kalikalos Holistic Education Centre
properties.description:
Kalikalos Holistic Education Centre
A simple, safe place in nature for connection, exploration and growth.
Setting: Rural Type: Eco Project (Green School, Learning Centre), Holistic Center
Status: Established Founded: 2001
Main Language: English Other Languages: French, German, Greek
More than just a retreat centre, Kalikalos is a seasonal international community for living, working and playing together. Grounded in the practice of the Sharing Circle, we collaborate in the rhythms of a healthy, holistic, vegetarian lifestyle. In this way, the whole community becomes the container for the transformational learning experience of our workshops.
Our centre rests in a valley surrounded by wild chestnut forest. With only a short drive or walk to crystal beaches, we are ideally situated for those who love land and sea. Our site features a large, covered dance floor that is open to the forest, where we host transformational workshops in nature including dance, yoga and tai chi.
The living space is an authentic Greek farmhouse with beautiful views from the balcony, an eating area shaded by a grapevine and numerous tent sites. We have an abundant garden on two levels of the site, providing us with mouth-watering fresh vegetables and eye-pleasing flowers for most of the summer.
Located on the edge of the village of Anilio, our summer community has the feel of a large happy family made up of volunteer staff, guests and workshop participants. The food is great and abundant and many activities are offered to staff and guests alike.
Ready to come into the circle? You can participate in a high-quality workshop, volunteer or become a Facilitator in Residence (FIR), or join us as a Community Guest.
Membership & Visitors
Current members: 10
Open to new members
Open to visitors
We are a seasonal community without any full-time residents. You are welcome to join us as a Workshop Participant, Volunteer, Facilitator in Residence or Community Guest. Please see our website for details on these opportunities.
Images & Video
The Circle - a short film about Kalikalos
More
Written on March 10, 2009,

properties.name: Kibbutz Gezer
properties.description:
Kibbutz Gezer is a kibbutz, or cooperative settlement, located in Central Israel. Originally founded in 1945, the kibbutz disbanded in 1963 and reestablished by a group of young Americans in 1973.
Economically, Gezer like many kibbutzim has changed and continues to change to fit the needs of the members and to adapt to surroundings and changes in Israeli society. While members are economically independent, Gezer has several commonly owned businesses, including a large dairy, farmland, olives, daycare and after school care (from infants to high school), second-hand shop, library, and grocery store. Our community governs itself through several committees including a central leadership committee, and via 3 elected leaders. Community meetings and direct voting decide crucial issues.
We support religious freedom, with a reform synagogue open to all who wish to attend. Jewish and state holidays are celebrated together in homemade fashion.
The physical design of the community is amenable to communal living. A ring road provides access to homes and businesses, while the center is car-free.
When founded in the 1970's Gezer was into vegetarian, organic food and getting back to the land back, and was considered by our neighbors as American hippie. We take pride in that, although some of us have gone straighter. Our younger generation is picking up the momentum, and the community has a growing environmental and agricultural awareness. We have a new ecological garden, community gardens, and recycling center. We grow organic olives, spices, and lemons. An environmental committee open to everyone organizes events and meetings and tries to bring a more sustainable outlook to the community. Our social action committee helps communities and individuals in need, including 2 refugee families who now live with us. We recently installed solar panels on the roofs of the cowsheds, and are looking into expanding.
We look for wwoofers and volunteers during the olive harvest, from mid-October until January. It is also a good opportunity for people to learn about kibbutz gezer and meet its people.
I'm a member of Kibbutz Gezer since 1984.  I grow olives for olive oil, as well as working as an environmental lawyer in Jerusalem.  My work entails several interdisciplinary environmental improvement projects covering different areas. I am active in Gezer's environmental committee, where we work to intergrate sustainability into the community's actions

properties.name: Kibbutz Lotan
properties.description:
Kibbutz Lotan is located in the far South of Israel, 55 kmorth of Eilat. "Members" of the cooperative fluctuate between 45-65, 4070 children. The community is multi-generational with students and volunteers from Israel and abroad coming for educational programmes. We are a cooperative settlement, jointly owning the land, community buildings and means of production. Joint economic initiatives include date orchards, dairy, photovoltaic electricity production, eco-tourism guest house and our Center for Creative Ecology. Members are responsible for their income and pay community taxes to maintain an extensive level of community services including wages of administrators, youth education and cultural life.
We apply the Biblical injunction "to till and to preserve" (Genesis 2:15) as well as the ancient Rabbinic ruling "do not destroy" into our way of life in our fragile desert environment. To this end we have established the Center for Creative Ecology (CfCE) - an ecological demonstration, education and research institute which deals with recycling, local organic food production, energy efficient housing, renewable energy, water conservation and sanitation, nature conservation and community engagement in environmental awareness and action. Kibbutz Lotan is recognized as a pioneer in community recycling of waste and in using waste in construction. The organic garden was the first CSA (community supported agriculture - subscription farm to table service) in Israel, and today supplies fresh vegetables to our Solar Restaurant. We maintain a Nature and Bird Reserve and two natural history trails adjacent to the community. We have been active in developing natural construction, specializing in earthquake resistant and energy efficient straw bale construction, including testing of these materials with the Standards Institute of Israel.
Non-working Guests welcome, Volunteers welcome

Kibbutz Lotan

Kibbutz Lotan is located in the far South of Israel, 55 kmorth of Eilat. "Members" of the cooperative fluctuate between 45-65, 4070 children. The community is multi-generational with students and volunteers from Israel and abroad coming for educational programmes. We are a cooperative settlement, jointly owning the land, community buildings and means of production. Joint economic initiatives include date orchards, dairy, photovoltaic electricity production, eco-tourism guest house and our Center for Creative Ecology. Members are responsible for their income and pay community taxes to maintain an extensive level of community services including wages of administrators, youth education and cultural life. We apply the Biblical injunction "to till and to preserve" (Genesis 2:15) as well as the ancient Rabbinic ruling "do not destroy" into our way of life in our fragile desert environment. To this end we have established the Center for Creative Ecology (CfCE) - an ecological demonstration, education and research institute which deals with recycling, local organic food production, energy efficient housing, renewable energy, water conservation and sanitation, nature conservation and community engagement in environmental awareness and action. Kibbutz Lotan is recognized as a pioneer in community recycling of waste and in using waste in construction. The organic garden was the first CSA (community supported agriculture - subscription farm to table service) in Israel, and today supplies fresh vegetables to our Solar Restaurant. We maintain a Nature and Bird Reserve and two natural history trails adjacent to the community. We have been active in developing natural construction, specializing in earthquake resistant and energy efficient straw bale construction, including testing of these materials with the Standards Institute of Israel. Non-working Guests welcome, Volunteers welcome

properties.name: Kingdom of Yeshar-el
properties.description:
Kingdom of Yeshar-el
Kingdom of sovereign Living people
Setting: Rural Type: Eco Project, Holistic Center, Shared Housing
Status: Aspiring
Main Language: Hebrew Other Languages: English, French, Spanish
Vision of Yashar – El
Consciously creating a new society of naturally living men and women, harmoniously co-existing, honouring the Earth in the spirit of abundance, respect and trust for the greater good of all – H.E.A.R.T.
Current Vision
Our current vision is to initiate positive change in the world through conscious initiatives which include culturing a new organic and sustainable culture of being that enhances human experience in balance with nature and all things natural and organic.
We are doing this by being the change we wish to see and sharing our inspiration with the world via various social and community initiatives.
This includes researching and developing new sustainable ways of living particularly in the fields of eco friendly living methods, nature preservation, regenerative and sustainable organic food production.
Using technological mediums like the internet and social media platforms we are able to research and share knowledge to encourage happy and healthy human well-being and animal welfare, this in accordance with Universal and Yashar – El Tenets and laws.
We fully understand that the Yashar – El mission can only reach its full potential if we manage to effectively communicate our vision.
We are continually endeavouring to reach out and connect with other individuals, communities, societies and organisations that are aligned with our vision, mission and aim.
With this in mind, Yashar – El is currently affiliated with various communities around the world with whom we share and exchange skills, ideas, knowledge and resources. This approach of organic community networking is at the heart of our current vision.
Ultimately, we have and are creating a new Trust initiatives and territories for all current and future Yashar – El custodian beneficiaries.

Membership & Visitors

Open to new members
Open to visitors
We are starting a new ecovillage on a beautiful ground with buildings that were deserted àbout 30 years ago.
We would love to have more good, loving, soviern living people to help us grow and free éveryone who wishes to be free.

properties.name: Kuthumba Ecovillage
properties.description:
Founded in 1993 on the ethics of care for the nature that surrounds it, Kuthumba encompasses a vision of a healthy lifestyle and the opportunity for people to co-create an environment that allows each individual to harbour a sense of peace and growth. Homes at Kuthumba are built using green principles, and the community hopes to serve as a model for how others can contribute to the protection of the environment and foster environmental wisdom.
More recently an influx of community-minded members has seen an increasing cohesion among the people on the land, looking at ways to live together in a more connected manner. Those living permanently at Kuthumba have a diverse range of passions, which include spiritual exploration and personal growth, permaculture, self-directed education, 'alternative' health and a form of eco-tourism that is educational.
Since 2020, more than half a dozen families have moved to Kuthumba, bringing a new focus on raising the next generation in a regenerative manner. A forest circle has been initiated as well as a playgroup for the younger children, known as The Nest. In 2022, an initiative to create a self-directed learning centre that connects us to the broader community in the area was started.
Kuthumba is located  of Plettenberg Bay, South Africa, and is surrounded by indigenous forest. The ecovillage is uniquely positioned with direct access to the Tsitsikamma National Park, which offers one of the area's most stunning walking routes.
Written on August 25, 2011,

properties.name: La Bolina
properties.description:
Our Vision:
A dynamic and thriving multicultural and ecological revolution in the rural area where we live which creates new sustainable livelihoods for locals and newcomers*, regenerates the ecosystem and celebrates cultural diversity.
*By newcomers we refer to people from all countries wanting to settle in this area particularly refugees and migrants.
Our Mission:
To create sustainable solutions for real-life issues, specifically the depopulation of rural villages, the degradation of land, high local unemployment, and the unmet basic needs of refugees and migrants. We aim to provide trainings, develop micro-enterprises and sustainable livelihoods, set up housing cooperatives, and coordinate cross-cultural integration to transform multilevel problems into genuine opportunities.
La Bolina is working to create systemic change by connecting, educating, and empowering young locals and migrants to create sustainable ‘collective-enterprising’ using local social and solidarity economy.
Our Activities:
We run trainings in permaculture, agroecology, and commercialisation, we host volunteers and interns and support locals and newcomers to set up sustainable collective-enterprises. We run a veg box scheme in collaboration with a network of local organic producers that enables us to offer conserves, olive oil, wine, etc. and to sell our produce in shops, hotels, and enterprises in the Lecrin Valley and Granada.
Our Origins:
We met in summer 2016 on an Eroles Project residency in Spain where 16 people from all over the world gathered to create a project which offered an integrated and holistic approach to borders, climate change, new economy, human rights, and migration. During these three weeks a set of visions and set of values emerged, along with a deep understanding of each other and a commitment to radical friendship, which resulted in the group deciding to live, work and develop the Project La Bolina together.
Our priority is to involve refugees, migrants, and local people. We also consider long term volunteers whose expertise might be valuable according to our project needs. The project also considers people who respect our core values and principles and visions. We put gender equality and respect for women as a priority. Regeneration, sustainability, and integration is an important principle we put into our practice. This approach pushes us into respecting the values of the local people in the rural community we are living in. We are also keen to have people who are innovative in local businesses and small scale enterprises. We are also interested in experienced people in funding applications and fundraising. As a community and a project, we are interested in someone to support us in our communication strategy preferably a fluent Spanish and English speaker. We are a scattered community across the valley and we share different houses.
La Bolina are a collect

properties.name: La Espiral
properties.description:
La Espiral is a comprehensive project that expands along the axes: Ecological, Social and Spiritual. It is located 1km from the town of Cerro Corá, being a natural area of ​​50 hectares, inhabited from a biocentric paradigm with community practices. The space is designed from Permaculture to be able to regenerate and generate systems of abundance.
The care of the Earth is developed in the Regenerative Natural Reserve project in 2022, to consolidate its agreement with the Ministry of Ecology of Misiones in 2024. This area is dedicated to preserving and regenerating biodiversity, the health of the territory and that our interventions have a positive footprint on the ecosystem. There is a document that develops in depth the objectives and the Management Plan. On the site there are several areas intended for agroecological production, such as Agroflorestas, Huerta and Nursery, considering that growing what we can in our farms is an act of autonomy and coherent sustainability.
The care of people and education is a constant dynamic that opens from space. Self-observation and the internal work of the Being are proposed that promote attitudes of personal development. With the coexistences, workshops, talks, training and meetings, we seek to create peaceful and nutritious relationships, with a perspective of planetary linking intelligence, where the social fabric is strengthened.
Caring for the Spirit is cultivated through various lineages and spiritualities that connect with Universal Love. The sacred plants of the native peoples are part of the cult and offering to their wisdom. The solar Christ consciousness and the paradigms of multidimensional reality are anchors to channel messages and purposes. We conceive of Mother Earth and all of her nature as a greater living Being with superior and sentient intelligence, subject to rights and in complete connection with the source of Life.
To join the community you have to contact us via WhatsApp.
Then arrange a visit. Do an internship or volunteer.
Permaculturist for several years. We founded the Ecovillage La Espiral, in Misiones, Argentina.
I am part of CASA LATINA, we hosted ECCO 2025

properties.description: Las Cañadas - Bosque de niebla Skip to content Inicio José Manuel Irra Bocanegra 2026-05-05T17:24:10-06:00 Cursos Recorridos y Visitas Tienda Somos una comunidad cooperativa que implementa y comparte alternativas de vida y producción para la regeneración del territorio y el cuidado de las personas. ¿Qué hacemos? Permacultura Utilizamos muchas técnicas, métodos y estrategias guiados por la PERMACULTURA. Para nosotros es “una herramienta de diseño de sistemas humanos regenerativos”. Ver más Agroecología Logramos una producción sin agrotóxicos, conservamos la agrobiodiversidad, evaluamos diferentes sistemas agroecológicos y buscamos la soberanía alimentaria. Ver más Transformación de la producción Damos valor agregado a lo que producimos: leche, carne, bambú, plantas medicinales y semillas, para mejorar nuestra alimentación y sostener nuestra economía. Ver más Conservación del bosque de niebla En 1995, sembramos 50,000 árboles nativos. En la actualidad conservamos 265 has. de bosque de niebla, uno de los ecosistemas tropicales más escasos del mundo. Ver más Bioconstrucción Aprendimos a minimizar el uso de fierro y cemento. Ahora usamos tierra, pasto, madera y bambú con diferentes técnicas de bioconstrucción en nuestras construcciones. Ver más Ecotecnologías Implementamos, evaluamos y promovemos una gran variedad de ecotecnologías para el desempeño de actividades minimizando el impacto en los espacios que habitamos. Ver más Educación y cultura Ofrecemos una educación transformadora para la formación de nuestr@s niñ@s, para el desarrollo de nuestr@s soci@s y compartimos toda nuestra experiencia en cursos. Ver más Salud y espiritualidad Buscamos una plenitud espiritual a través de nuestro trabajo diario, respetando la Madre Tierra, cultivando alimento vivo y sosteniendo relaciones sanas. Ver más Economía y finanzas Generemos y distribuimos de manera equitativa los ingresos y producción entre l@s soci@s y mantenemos una administración financiera transparente para todos. Ve

properties.name: LebensGut Miteinander
properties.description: Willkommen am Lebensgut - lebensgut-miteinanders Webseite! Home Kennenlernen Vermögenspool Angebote Kontakt Home Willkommen am Lebensgut Wir sind ein familienfreundliches, naturnahes Gemeinschaftswohnprojekt im Mostviertel in Niederösterreich. Gemeinsam gestalten und verwalten wir einen Ort, wo Menschen sinnstiftend, herzlich und nachhaltig zusammenleben. Das gelebte Miteinander ist unsere Motivation, darin liegt unsere tägliche Freude. Gemeinschaft Du möchtest uns und unser Projekt kennenlernen? Oder überlegst ob du am LebensGut wohnen willst? Wir bieten unterschiedliche Möglichkeiten an, um unsere Gemeinschaft und den Ort kennen zu lernen. Mehr dazu Nachhaltigkeit Dank eigenen Waldes, Quellen, Biokläranlage & Bio Gemüse Landwirtschaft ist es uns möglich, teilautark zu leben. Ein Permakultur Konzept ermöglicht uns immer mehr ressourcenschonende Kreislauf- und Subsistenzwirtschaft. Mehr dazu Vermögenspool Ein Vermömgenspool schafft gemeinschaftliche Besitzverhältnisse fernab des klassischen Privateigentums. Durch ihn hat das Lebensgut zu dem Ort werden können, der es heute ist, mit dem Potential, noch weiter zu wachsen. Mehr dazu Inhalte von Vimeo werden aufgrund deiner Cookie-Einstellungen nicht angezeigt. Akzeptiere die funktionellen Cookies im Cookie-Banner , um den Cookie-Richtlinien von Vimeo zuzustimmen und den Inhalt anzusehen. Der Kurzfilm "Ein Hof für das gute Leben", gedreht von den Filmchronisten, zeigt ein Portrait unseres Projekts. Bewohner*Innen ermöglichen persönliche Einblicke wie alternative Wohn- und Lebensformen gelingen können. Aktuelle Termine & Neuigkeiten Werkeltage SA 11. 04. 2026 | 09:00-13:00 SA 27. 06. 2026 | 09:00-13:00 Wir werkeln gemeinsam drinnen und draußen. Im Anschluss gibt es ein gemeinsames Mittagessen mit gemütlichem Austausch. Mehr Infos hier... Kennenlernnachmittage MI 20. 05. 2026 | 14:30-17:30 Bei einer Haus- und Hofführung bekommst du Einblicke in unser Wohnprojekt, zu unserem Aufnahmeprozess und mehr bei Kaffee und Kuchen.

Maybe you want to turn a hobby into something more. Or maybe you have a creative project to share with the world. Whatever it is, the way you tell your story online can make all the difference. Don’t worry about sounding professional. Sound like you. There are over 1.5 billion websites out there, but your story is what’s going to separate this one from the rest. If you read the words back and don’t hear your own voice in your head, that’s a good sign you still have more work to do. Be clear, be confident and don’t overthink it. The beauty of your story is that it’s going to continue to evolve and your site can evolve with it. Your goal should be to make it feel right for right now. Later will take care of itself. properties.name: Lifechanyuan International Family Society (Thailand Branch)
properties.description:
Started in March 2019, New Oasis for Life Thailand Branch began with several members from New Oasis for Life China. It is a farm supported by our resonant friend PINAN, who is a sincere self-cultivator on Buddhism and visualize for a common global world regardless of races, religions, backgrounds, and culture. He offered this wild land of 10 acres to us, with many banana trees, some simple huts and houses, and a small lake. From March 2019 to March 2020, over 20 members of New Oasis China branch went to support the construction of Thailand Oasis, under with it becomes more and more beautiful, and grow into a beautiful natural garden, and a harmonious international family.
We welcome people from different cultures, nations, religions, and backgrounds to join this big family, to know about Lifechanyuan Values, experience the new life mode created by Lifechanyuan, and become one member of this big international family.
Life is a short journey, let's start the new chapter of life together!
1, Study 800 Values for New Era Human Being, resonate with the Values, don't like traditional life, longing for a new life mode very much.
2, Consider yourself a diligent, honest, trustworthy, and sincere person.
3, No financial debts and troubles from your traditional family.
4, Single, or both of the couple resonate with the values and can accept to be independent after joining the community.
5, Can getting along with people from different background harmoniously.
Love nature, love life, revere the Greatest Creator, and create a colorful future!

properties.name: Lifechanyuan Thailand Home
properties.description:
Founded in 2019, Lifechanyuan Thailand Home serves as a living model of how humanity can create paradise on Earth through soul purification, ecological balance, and communal harmony.
The vision of the Thailand Home is to pioneer a lifestyle that transcends traditional family and social structures. Members live as one big family without marriage, private ownership, or hierarchy. Instead, they embrace freedom, equality, sharing, and mutual trust. This liberates individuals from possessiveness and attachment, while deepening their connection to others through love, service, and gratitude.
Daily life centers on cooperation and simplicity. Members cultivate the land using ecological methods, grow vegetables, fruits, and flowers, and care for gardens and animals in ways that nurture both earth and soul. Work, meals, and celebrations are shared, while music, art, and spiritual practice enrich community life. Visitors are welcome to join daily activities and experience a lifestyle that blends inner peace with practical sustainability.
The spiritual foundation is guided by the teachings of Xue Feng, founder of Lifechanyuan, who emphasizes purifying the soul from selfishness, ego, and attachments to align with the will of the Greatest Creator. By following these principles, members aim to live joyfully and harmoniously while serving all beings and growing spiritually.
The Thailand Home is open to the world. People from diverse countries and cultures are welcomed as brothers and sisters, contributing unique talents and learning from one another. This diversity embodies the vision of a united human family beyond nationality, religion, or race.
As part of the Global Ecovillage Network, Lifechanyuan Thailand Home shows how ecological sustainability and spiritual awakening can merge into a holistic lifestyle. It offers inspiration to anyone seeking a new way of living—one that nurtures both people and planet, creates freedom and joy, and builds foundation of a harmonious global family.
Lifechanyuan Thailand Home welcomes people from all backgrounds, cultures, and faiths who feel called to explore a new way of living. The people most suitable to join us are those who:
Value simplicity and sharing – enjoy living in a cooperative lifestyle without private ownership, hierarchy, or competition.
Have an open mind and heart – willing to learn, respect different beliefs, and embrace diversity.
Seek inner growth – interested in purifying the soul, letting go of ego and attachments, and discovering deeper peace and freedom.
Love nature and community life – appreciate gardening, ecological living, and taking care of the land together.
Wish to serve – motivated to contribute talents, skills, and energy for the good of all beings, not just personal gain.
Respect healthy living – free from addictive habits, and aligned with a clean, harmonious lifestyle.
Lifechanyuan Thailand Home warmly welcomes visitors and friends from around the world who wish to experience our w

properties.name: M.L Jallow Ecovillage ,The Gambia
properties.description:
The M.L Jallow Ecovillage , The Gambia is a transformative initiative focused on promoting sustainability, environmental consciousness, and community engagement. Founded by Momodou Barrow in 2019, Named   in honour of her late indigenous mother (Lalla Jallow) ,the project embodies the motto "Think Globally, Act Locally" emphasizing the importance of local actions in 
The project not only aims to improve the local environment but also enhances the quality of life for the community by fostering economic security through sustainable eco-tourism practices. This includes initiatives such as waste management and renewable energy utilization, which are designed to minimize negative environmental impacts while maximizing local economic benefits
Additionally, the M.L Jallow Ecovillage  seeks to empower the local population by promoting volunteerism and engaging community members—especially women and youth—in meaningful activities that enhance their skills and knowledge about sustainable practices
By aligning its objectives with the principles of global biodiversity and local empowerment, the project serves as a model for sustainable community development in The Gambia.
M.L Jallow Ecovillage has successfully implemented various programs, including large-scale tree-planting initiatives to combat deforestation, sustainable livelihood programs empowering women and youth through climate-smart agriculture, and waste management initiatives promoting circular economy practices. Additionally, we have been at the forefront of climate change education and advocacy, conducting training programs and community engagement activities to enhance resilience and awareness.
M.L Jallow Ecovillage, with objectives that center around building local capacity and supporting marginalized groups, including women, youth, and people with disabilities. Through skill-building programs and training, M.L Jallow Ecovillage empowers community members to become self-reliant, equipping them with the knowledge and skills needed to improve their livelihoods and make positive environmental contributions. This empowerment extends beyond economic opportunities to include environmental education and advocacy, allowing community members to become active stewards of their environment. By promoting empowerment, M.L Jallow Ecovillage ensures that its impact is sustainable, driven by a community that is knowledgeable and confident in its abilities.

properties.description: ## अध्ययन मनन गोष्ठी Location: अभ्युदय संस्थान, अछोटी, जिलादुर्ग, छत्तीसगढ़Date: 2 से 8 दिसम्बर 2024 Prabodhak: श्रीराम नरसिम्हन भैया, पुणेLanguage: हिंदीPhone/Email: चंद्रशेखर राठौर भैया 9893025307, मंजीत भैया 9981186657 Registration Link: [ Note**मान्यवर,🙏अभ्युदय संस्थान, अछोटीमेंप्रथम “अध्ययन - मनन गोष्ठी” (अध्ययन मेंगहराई एवंगंभीरताहेतु...) केलिए.. आरक्षित करेंअपनासमय🙏. 2 से 8 दिसम्बर 2024 @ अभ्युदय संस्थान, अछोटी‘अध्ययन’ एवं ‘अभ्यास' पर मैत्रीविधिसेविचार विमर्श; यह सत्र परस्पर ‘शोध-गोष्ठी' केरूप मेंकियाजायेगासमन्वयक:श्रीराम नरसिम्हन, पुणे. . सूचना:आइये, सार्थक और मानवीय शिक्षाकेसाथ यह शिविर मित्रोंऔर परिवार सहित जरुर अटेंड करें..- पंजीयन अनिवार्य हैव सूचनाहेतुव्हाट्सप्प ग्रुप जरूर जॉइन करें.... पंजीयन फॉर्म लिंक-[ WhatsApp Group-[ यह 7 दिन काआवासीय शिविर है।- शिविर काऔर रहनेकाकोई भीशुल्क नहींहै, शिविर मेंरहनेकीसामान्य व्यवस्थाउपलब्ध है।और खानेकेखर्च कावहन होसके, इसलिए भोजन हेतुसहयोग राशिअपेक्षित है।(अनुमानित खर्च केअनुसार, सहयोग राशि ₹150 प्रतिव्यक्तिप्रतिदिन अपेक्षित है).शिविर संबंधित सूचनाकेलिए एक व्हाट्सएप ग्रुप बनायागयाहै, यदिआप इस शिविर कोअटेंड करनेवालेहैंतोजॉइन करें🙏 नीचेलिंक पर क्लिक करें.. ‘अध्ययन-मनन शिविर’ केबारेमें..: -मध्यस्थ दर्शन सहज पठन, अध्ययन केसाथ ‘अध्ययन' एवं ‘अभ्यास' कोलेकर बहुत सारेमुद्दोंपर स्पष्टताकीआवश्यकताकाहोनाहम विद्यार्थियोंकेलिए सहज है | जैसे, ‘अध्ययन' क्यामात्र बौद्धिक प्रक्रियाहै? अध्ययन और अभ्यास मेंक्यासम्बन्ध है? ‘जीना' कामतलब क्याहै? ‘व्यवहाराभ्यास’ केबिंदुक्याहैं? कैसेकरें? कर्माभ्यास, श्रम, स्वावलंबन एवंस्वधन मेंसम्बन्ध क्याहै? ‘व्यवस्थामेंभागीदारी’ एवं ‘समाज परिवर्तन’ एक हैं, कीभिन्न हैं? लोकव्यापीकरण योजनाकेसाथ ‘स्वयंकेपहचान’ कास्वरूप क्याहै?अध्ययन विधिमेंबाधाएंएवंउनकानिदान क्याहै?चिन्तानाभ्यास कामतलब क्याहै? ‘जीना', ‘अभ्यास' केविभिन्न आयामोंमेंसंतुलन कैसेबनाएं?. परिस्थितियोंकोझेलना:हमारेवर्तमान केपारिवारिक, आर्थिक, शैक्षणिक, व्यावहारिक, सामाजिक वस्तुस्थितयांकोकैसेसमझेंएवं ‘पूर्णता' केवस्तुकेसाथ कैसेतालमेल बैठाएं? आइये, इन मुद्दोंपर ज्ञानार्जन करें. संभावित उपलब्धि:-स्पष्टता: - पुस्तक पढनेकेसाथ / बाद क्याकरें? शास्त्राभ्या

लोकव्यापीकरण योजनाकेसाथ ‘स्वयंकेपहचान’ कास्वरूप क्याहै?अध्ययन विधिमेंबाधाएंएवंउनकानिदान क्याहै?चिन्तानाभ्यास कामतलब क्याहै? ‘जीना', ‘अभ्यास' केविभिन्न आयामोंमेंसंतुलन कैसेबनाएं?. परिस्थितियोंकोझेलना:हमारेवर्तमान केपारिवारिक, आर्थिक, शैक्षणिक, व्यावहारिक, सामाजिक वस्तुस्थितयांकोकैसेसमझेंएवं ‘पूर्णता' केवस्तुकेसाथ कैसेतालमेल बैठाएं? आइये, इन मुद्दोंपर ज्ञानार्जन करें. संभावित उपलब्धि:-स्पष्टता: - पुस्तक पढनेकेसाथ / बाद क्याकरें? शास्त्राभ्या

properties.name: Maia Earth Village
properties.description:
Maia Earth Village is an intuitive healing and spiritual community that has interests in raw food, permaculture and what is called innerdance process.  The Village was founded initially by Pi Villaraza and members of the innerdance communities around the world and is now independently run by like-minded residents that act as caretakers of this sacred 7-hectare land. The community hosts the emergence convergence gatherings, which is a collaboration of various networks, primarily the Global Ecovillage Network Oceania and Asia.
Hi there.  I am the founder of Maia Earth Village and Bahay Kalipay, two raw food, healing and gardening communities in the Philippines. I am a healer who's hope is to bring hope to others through their ability to shift their consciousness back to more natural states of resonance.
My work in healing and transformation has been published in media across the world, where I have conducted more than 14,000 workshops, sessions and teacher trainings in the last 6 years. I have written two books cal

properties.name: Medicina Costa Rica
properties.description: It takes significant preparation on each of our parts to prepare for 10X community living. Please take the time to invest in your preparation. Consider this preparation necessary for joining this community (it is a self-selecting process in which those who show up are in) but also a giant step towards a life of more conscious well-being even if you decide not to join or to start your own version of this project. 1. Take the time to read or listen to the [media titles on this page]( I like listening on audible since it makes multi-tasking easy and fun. 2. Choose a role for yourself from this page (link to roles). Prepare for this role by learning competency in an area needed for community. 3. Be of service: Volunteer! Just e-mail us with how many hours you have, a few things you are good at or want to contribute and you can get started! 4. Save your money: You will need a minimum of $15,000. at the time you come on site (with a source of at least $1,500/monthly income) or a minimum of $50,000 without a source of monthly income at the point you come on site. 5. Invest: There is a period of seven years where cash-flow will be a bottle-neck without a low or no-interest loan. If you wish to make: a) A tax free donation. b) A no-interest loan secured by the ever-improving land. or b)A 2-4% interest-bearing loan secured by the land then share the terms. The amount of cash-flow determines the speed at which we grow and build community amenities, as well as the location of the community. Starting amount is $700,000 with $5million being the most that we need.

properties.name: Misriyati
properties.description:
Misriyati was inspired by the growing intolerance among the different components of the Egyptian society: religions, social classes and cultural groups. These symptoms revealed the need to work on gaining a new awareness of our reality with its structures and relationships, of improving our competences and skills in dealing with diversity or in meeting conflict, and our re-adoption of values that can enhance peaceful living.
Deep discussion led the founding team to consider “education” as the main entry point for social and cultural change: and hence, Misriyati was conceived as an educational “space”:
•  A space for exploration: of the self, the other, the structures and the culture, the underlying fears and stereotypes, the lacking skills, the missing values… and the alternatives.
•  A space of interaction: sincere human interactions, between the “real” people… each with his/her own legitimate luggage of experiences, feelings and thoughts.
•  A space of learning and growth…
Our Vision is that of the Egyptian society knowing about principles of peaceful living and nonviolent
ways of dealing with conflicts, and having the possibility of applying
those principles in its daily life.
Our mission:
(1) Providing the knowledge about peaceful living: Misriyati create these spaces where the knowledge  can be explored, shared and created together.
(2) Creating the success stories and peaceful living model: by encouraging an action that is congruent with values.
 (3) Building the community for peaceful living: and by this we mean the network of individuals sharing the same values and ideals and supporting each other in their efforts to promote the values of a culture of peace

properties.name: Nannilam EcoVIllage
properties.description:
Nannilam EcoVIllage
A self-sustaining residential community that fosters alternative living
Setting: Rural Type: Cohousing, Eco Community, Eco Project (Permaculture Project), Ecovillage
Status: Aspiring In Planning Since: 
Main Language: English Other Languages: Hindi, Tamil
Nannilam Eco Village is a 125-acre residential eco-living project near Salem, Tamil Nadu, perched atop a scenic hill, offering breathtaking views.
Our eco-village is a model that blends Ecological Sustainability (ECO) with the simplicity and closeness of Village life (Village).
People here enjoy living in a fruit forest, eating fresh food from their organic gardens, living in beautifully crafted natural homes and houses while participating in workshops, events, and gatherings that help to build a community.
Nannilam is built on the principles of Nilam, Valam, and Nalam – Health, Wealth, and Soil. Each action here reflects a deeper connection to these essential elements. Here, the land nurtures both body and soul, offering peace, well-being, and a sense of purpose.
Whether you’re seeking a fresh start with eco-living or yearning to reconnect with nature, our community is open to all. At Nannilam, you’ll find not just a home, but a lifestyle that enriches your heart, mind, and spirit.
Membership & Visitors
Current members: 1
Open to new members
Open to visitors
At Nannilam, we invite like-minded individuals to join us in creating a sustainable future. Here are the ways you can get involved:
1.Investor: Own an acre of farmland and support permaculture-based cultivation. Invest in a greener future.
2.Volunteer: Help create a vibrant, welcoming community and make a positive impact on the lives of our residents.
3.Intern: Gain hands-on experience in sustainable agriculture and eco-friendly living through our rewarding internship program.
4. Co-Creator: Bring your expertise and innovative ideas to help shape a sustainable future for our planet.
5. Join the Team: Contribute your skills to our eco village and become part of our mission for a better, sustainable tomorrow.
Image Gallery
More
About Ashutosh Dhasmana
I am a certified photographer and cinematographer with a passion for sustainable living. For the past four years, I’ve worked with off-grid farms, eco-villages, and intentional communities. Originally from Uttarakhand, I draw inspiration from the Himalayas, where I spent my early years. My love for nature led me to build treehouses and backpack across India for three years. Currently, I am a Co-Creator at Nannilam Eco Village, a freelance videographer, and I run a podcast stud

properties.name: Ndanifor Permaculture Eco Village
properties.description:
Ndanifor Permaculture Eco Village
Reconnecting Youth & Women to their Community Values
Setting: Rural
Founded: 2010
Main Language: English Other Language: French
Ndanifor Permaculture Eco Village (NPE)
VISION: Restoration of the broken communities of Cameroon and the Central African region using African Permaculture that creates a new sense of purpose for unemployed youth.
MISSION: Deliver Eco Village Education for Sustainability Design Courses using Global Eco Village Network strategy and GAIA Education to form a corps of Trainers of Trainers for the African Eco Village Movement.
OBJECTIVES:
*Empower bottom up decision making by giving voice to youth and women.
*Use the YouthCamp of Better World Students Nature Club and Exchange Programmes to network with other intentional communities .
*Work with “right of Passage Programme” Ndanifor Dance & Theatre Troupe to reconnect youth with Nature and their Roots through conserving traditional festivals, Art & Craft.
*combining indigenous knowlege with science ,promoting renewables and ICTs
*Social Business Trainings and accompanying traininees onto self employment.
NPE is a Community owned ecovillage association called Better World Cameroon registered in 1996, using the holistic approach of Permaculture to promote social resilience, environmental pro­tection, restoration of nature, sustainable livelihood and peace.
The original dream of the founder, youth activist Konkankoh Joshua was the conservation of natural habitats , North West Region of Cameroon using the concept of ecovillage as a model that can support youth development.He resigned from his job in government to devote his life to the de­vel­opment of the Better World Ecological Literacy Center in Yaounde, capital city, for unemployed graduates and network platform for organizing the “Together for a Better World” International Summer Workcam.
NPE is one of the youth changemaker’s projects in Africa actively working for change in agriculture and environmental protection through regenrative education. It is one of the membership projects of GENAfrica that is emerging with the support of Ndanifor Garden’s UK Trust, and open to a wide range of public private partnership projects regardless of their political, religious or cultural backgrounds.
Read more about ABOUT Better World Cameroon: www.betterworld-cameroon.com
WHAT WE DO
We make visible to the scientific community research solutions developed by our Nature Clubs and search for expertise for institutional building.
We develop networks that connect youth initiatives with each other and to like-minded individuals and organisations worldwide.
We facilitate South-South and South-North exchange of inspiration, information and training through organizing youth camps.
NPE is designed to take 3 years funding to establish with learning laboratories, vocational school advanced courses for tomorrow’s leaders
It is a demonstration center for traditional village farmer groups also soon to be institutionalized into a Development Education Summer School.
NPE is an African Capacity Development challenge that can be handled by Change Maker Volunteer professionals willing to take time off their jobs to spend some time in the field working on specific tasks on the project. We have stepped up our volunteer program within social networks for more visibility and calling on young professionals from the North to take advantage of this inter-cultural opportunity.
Better World Cameroon is able to make basic accommodation to assignees.
Contact Joshua Konkankoh:
foundation@betterworld-cameroon.com
Membership & Visitors
Current members: 30
Workshop-visitors welcome, Non-working Guests welcome, Work Exchange welcome, Volunteers welcome,
More
About Joshua Konkankoh
GEN Regional Representative
Joshua Konkankoh studied Mass Communication and spent 22 years working in the National Social Security Department of Cameroon as a communications officer. He is the founder and director of Better World Cameroon, which was founded in 1996, and the Bafut Ecovillage Project.
In 2004, he left social security work and has devoted 19 years of study and work to youth and agricultural development in Africa. He has been part of the development of youth, social security, forest and conservation policie

properties.name: New Oasis for Life Commune
properties.description:
This earth becomes one big family, where traditional families and countries no longer exist. There will be only one global government and all resources are shared worldwide. Everyone on earth will be able to move freely around the world and there will be no famine, wars or social unrest. The weather are always favorable; no one will pocket anything on the road and no one will need to lock their door at night. Everyone enjoys free, happy, joyous and blessed lives.
To call on all individuals and groups with insight to transcend traditional family limitations and the consciousness infused by countries, so that they can become global citizens, form an international family, and eventually realize the one world collective ideal.
Resonate with Lifechanyuan Values (800 Values)
Love the new life mode created by Lifechanyuan
Ready to contribute one's talent and wisdom into the establishment and development of the commune
Longing for a simple, sustainable, enjoyable, happy and free life, as well as has the motivation of self-improvement and self-cultivation in spirituality
Love nature, love life, revere the Greatest Creator, and create a colorful future!

properties.name: Noordeland
properties.description:
Noordeland
Noordeland realizes an ecovillage based on social, economical and natural restoration in Ter Apel.
Setting: Rural Type: Eco Project (Restoration Site), Ecovillage (Intentional)
Status: Established Founded: 2019
Main Language: Dutch
The ecovillage Noordeland started to realize it’s community in April 2019 on the premises of the former Potato starch factory in Ter Apel, Netherlands. The area exists of 4.5 hectares of buildings, roads, storage halls and storage pits partly covered with asbestos roofing and infested by some pollution, the other 4.5 hectares are flood fields which do need cleaning but are suitable for house construction. At this moment there are 9 permanent inhabitants and 11 intending to become so within the next year. In September 2019 the municipality has granted us a license to specifically develop an ecological community here and now the site can be taken over from the former owners for the amount of €1,- including the obligation to resolve the pollution.
We are in the process of constructing our group center, installing the storage buildings for production purposes and enterprises, improving the sewage water treatment and water supply, the electricity and a multi purpose rocket stove as well as the insulation of buildings. Meanwhile we focus on the team building and social network with the outside community. We investigate the best practices for cleaning the asbestos and other pollution.
We are on our way.
With grateful and kindest regards,
visit our ecodorpnoordeland.nl,
Fedde Jorritsma for all of us Noordeland, Ter Apel
Membership & Visitors
Current members: 9
Open to new members
Open to visitors
First write us and come for a visit, than participate in work days, than apply for aspiring membership which - if granted - may change into a full membership becoming an inhabitant of our village.
More
About Rik Mulder
I have been involved with Noordeland since 2016 and worked closely with the group of legal and financial issues i.e. with Fedde, Terra, Susan, Pim and Marjorie.
Reader Interactions
Comments

properties.description: [Пропустить и перейти к тексту]( []( [Video 7]( 8]( Жилье[Video 9]( 10]( НОВОЕ Впечатления Впечатления (новинки).[Video 11]( 12]( НОВОЕ Услуги Услуги (новинки). Начать поиск Местоположение ![Image 2]( Искать везде Прибытие и выезд Любые даты Гости Кто едет? # OxygenHouse в эко-посёлке «Нескучное» Поделиться Сохранить ![Image 3]( ![Image 4]( ![Image 5]( ![Image 6]( ![Image 7]( Показать все фото Фото Удобства Отзывы Местоположение ## Дом целиком, Kurovo, Россия 1. 3 гостя · 2. · 1 спальня · 3. · 2 кровати · 4. · 1,5 ванные Рейтинг: 5,0 из 5 звезд. 5,0 ·[1 отзыв]( ![Image 8: Фото в профиле хозяина]( Хозяин: Виктория 1. 6 лет принимает гостей ## Плюсы жилья ### Можно с домашними животными Возьмите в поездку домашних животных. Наш дом расположен в экологически чистом Истринском районе рядом с красивым реликтовым лесом, что гарантирует проживающим эстетическое удовольствие чистый воздух и тишину. На участке и в окрестностях растут полевые цветы. В окрестностях нет промышленных предприятий, крупные населенные пункты находятся на значительном удалении. До ближайшего крупного водоёма, Истринского водохранилища, от «Нескучного» всего 15 минут на машине. Рядом находится конно-спортивный комплекс и питомник Хаски. Жилье Дом, в котором много кислорода не только снаружи, но и внутри. Панорамные окна, эко-материалы, молодые сосны, бескрайние поля, реликтовые леса, водохранилище. Вдохните настоящий кислород вдали от городской суеты, насладитесь чашечкой травяного чая, сидя на уютной веранде. Посёлок ограждён, Охрана, свободный выход в реликтовый лес и на поле. Участок 10 соток, ограждён по периметру забором. Важная информация Хозяевам с животными предоставляются пакеты и салфетки для уборки за питомцами. Показать еще ## Где вы будете спать * ![Image 9: Спальня]( Спальня 1 двуспальная кровать * ![Image 10: Гостиная]( Гостиная 1 диван-кровать ## Какие удобства вас ждут Кухня Wi-Fi Бесплатная парковка на территории Можно с питомцами Телевизор Стиральная машина Камин в помещении Детски

properties.name: OTEPIC
properties.description: ![Image 1]( Dear Gabu and Philip, it is rainy season. We have to dig and plant! But instead of joining us to work on the fields, you spend your mornings in town. You run from one shop to another to compare the prices of timber, sand and stones or wait for hours for a truck to carry the new materials. This has been going on for weeks now. Philip, you even miss lunch! It seems we have lost you to the timber shops ! But don’t get us wrong! This message is not about complaining, it is about acknowledging. ![Image 2]( Because we see the great progress the construction is making. We see the trucks that come nearly every day with new materials. We enjoy the view from the second floor of the building in the sunny hours. And we see how tired you both are from rushing around town. So next time you are in a rush (probably tomorrow morning) just remember, that there are people who care about you. Who miss taking, joking and laughing with you. Who always keep a plate back for you. Rome was not built in a day, like you keep on saying Philip. – Don’t get crazy while hurrying from one place to another. Take a break from time to time! Relax. Breathe. You do a great work! And we miss you guys! Mili, Charity, Judith, Esther, Kevin, James, Ben, Sam and Ann! May 15, 2017 * [EN]( * [DE]( * [Tel.: +254 725429179](tel: +254 725429179) * []( * []( "Facebook") * [Donate]( # [![Image 2]( "OTEPIC | Permaculture in Kenya - ") MENU MENU * [About us]( * * * [OTEPIC]( * [Blog]( * [Big Vision]( * [Agenda]( * [Next step]( * * [Permaculture in Kenya]( * [Inspiration]( * [Challenge]( * [Approach]( * [Europe tour 2023]( * [Tour flyer, press information and pictures]( * [Projects]( * * * [Earth care]( * [Sabwani Garden]( * [Amani Garden]( * * [People care]( * [Orphanage]( * [Dance Group]( * * [Fair share]( * [Clean Trinking Water]( * [Conference and training centre]( * * [Further]( * [Further activities]( * [Training]( * [Support]( * * * [Donors and supporter]( * [Visitors Welcome Page]( * [Visit us!](

Rome was not built in a day, like you keep on saying Philip. – Don’t get crazy while hurrying from one place to another. Take a break from time to time! Relax. Breathe. You do a great work! And we miss you guys! Mili, Charity, Judith, Esther, Kevin, James, Ben, Sam and Ann! properties.name: OUR Ecovillage - Shawnigan Lake, British Columbia, Canada
properties.description:
OUR Ecovillage – is located on an extraordinary 25 acres near Shawnigan Lake. OUR Ecovillage began operations in 1999 with a vision to create a model “Sustainable Living Demonstration Site and Education Centre” rooted in social, ecological, and economic well being. As a multi-stakeholder organizational structure, there are a number of organizations, industry partners, government/regulatory authorities, researchers and local to global community members participating in OUR Learning Community. With K-12 School Groups, 13 Universities/Colleges, corporate sponsors, and a wide variety of interdisciplinary educators – all walks of life are considered for training of how to live and learn into OUR collective future. Tours, Farm-stays, class trips (including longer-term stays), celebrations, eco-conferencing — with accommodation services and break out rooms, industry and professional development, Green Burial, and much more.
If you would like to find out more about joining OUR Ecovillage  please visit this page: https://ourecovillage.org/visit/
Written on October 1, 2019,

properties.name: Omuthiya Natural Springs EcoVillage
properties.description:
Omuthiya Natural Springs EcoVillage
San Family and Community Project for the Oldest Hunter Gatherers of South Africa.
Setting: Rural Type: Eco Project (Eco Tourism Site, Green School, Permaculture Project, Restoration Site)
Status: Aspiring In Planning Since: 
Main Language: English Other Languages: Afrikaans, Portuguese
Situated 10km outside of Etosha National Park, this aspiring Ecovillage is being developed as part of the EcoVillage Development Education program in GEN Africa. Omuthiya EcoVillage supports the United Nations Millennium Development Goals for the preservation of Indigenous Peoples, Cultures, Animals, and Land and serves the oldest hunter-gatherer community of South Africa. The EcoVillage produces Green Hydrogen and provides immersive Edu-tainment experiences through workshops, retreats, and Safari excursions supporting the preservation of the Omithuya water source, its minerals, and regional habitat for the “Big Five” (Elephant, Rhino, African Buffalo, Leopard, and Lion).
Omithuya Springs EcoVillage will officially open in 2023 and is home to a global Permaculture team empowering knowledge-transfers in the fields of biochar, renewable energy, EcoVillage Design, waste management, and sustainability.
Membership & Visitors
Current members: 50
Not currently open to new members, but might be in the future
Open to visitors
Please submit a request via email to nathanael.shikongo@digitalskillsacademy.me
Image Gallery
6946121 – zebras on lake
More
About Patty Rangel
Patty is a 2022 GEN (Global Ecovillage Network) NGO representative to the United Nations Environment Program (General Assembly in Nairobi, Kenya), a graduate of the United Nations Summer Intensive Program (UN Headquarters in New York City), Honorary Burning Man Earth Guardian, and a member of EcoVillages in France, Italy, Mexico, and the USA. Patty is passionate about ARTivism and empowering Indigenous Nations. She has been involved with GEN since 2007.
