properties.name: One Island
properties.description: ## [Welcome to One Island Sustainable Living]( Subscribe to our **[Newsletter]( ##### Art + Food + Farms + Sustainable Living **Join the [Great Pollinator Hunt]( winner of Jane Goodall Institute award** * * * _[![Image 1: One Island logo courtesy Rob Schouten]( **WELCOME**One Island is a non-profit sustainability program in the Pacific / North West serving rural coastal areas of Hawaii, California and Washington. In response to the challenges of our time, our programs foster connections between inspired teachers and learners to strengthen creativity and resiliency in remote rural communities. Programs focus on short, easy-to-implement projects and longer term strategic sustainability efforts. **‘Green is a Verb’ – it is an _empowering action_ we each can choose to do** **Cornerstone initiatives have included:** local food system strengthening, art and culture place-making events, renewable energy / green building, watershed protection, green economy incubation, and health and wellness efforts. We host regular volunteer learning days, green building and farm tours, and present special guest speakers and events that demonstrate a variety of ‘green values’ as sustainability options. Educational and fun hands-on ‘maker’ workshops turn our farm partner’s local resources into rewarding hand-built products – from food to art to body care, from building materials to home gardens to renewable energy – valuable skills you can take with you and enjoy where ever you live. ###### **Come visit our programs and partners in Hawaii, California & Washington** ###### **rural coastal communities.** **We are all in the Same Canoe – grab a paddle!** Bamboo garden art work provided courtesy [Rob Schoutten Gallery]( ## [Affordable Housing Initiative & Tiny Houses]( [![Image 2: tiny 10]( Island has hosted a series of discussions on Tiny House solutions to Hawaii’s affordable housing crisis, and for farm worker housing to help increase local food production. properties.name: Otamatea Ecovillage
properties.description: Welcome to the Otamatea Eco-Village web site. We are a community using organic principles to meet our members needs and care for the native eco system. The site has information about our permaculture community and its projects and events. We run an annual Permaculture Design Course and open days. If you want to know more about us please get in touch: Contact - Marijke: Tel - (+64) 9 431 2656 Email - [email protected] Land occasionally comes up for sale. To find out more click here. To read Marijke's poem click here. What is Permaculture? Permaculture is a practical concept applicable from the balcony to the farm, from the city to the wilderness. It enables people to establish productive environments providing for food, energy, shelter, material and non-material needs, as well as the social and economic infrastructures that support them. Permaculture means thinking carefully about our environment, our use of resources and how we supply our needs. It aims to create systems that will sustain us not only for the present, but for future generations. Most residents have completed a Permaculture Design Course. The Land Gently rolling pastures slope away from a central ridge. Pockets of native bush are protected by conenant and are being regenerated and extended. The sheltered harbour position has unspoilt panoramic views. There is one large lake and many more have been dug for water catchment. The peninsular situation offers good fishing and other water activities. Its wetlands are home to a wide variety of birdlife. Otamatea Eco-Village: Will practise permaculture in a spirit of co-operation, mutual support and respect for one another and the land. Will preserve and enhance the native ecosystems on the land. Will create fertile, holistically integrated agricultural systems and a village culture that abundantly provides our community's needs for healthy living at all levels: physical, social, emotional, intellectual, and spiritual. Will do this sustainably, ethically, and

properties.name: PachaMama
properties.description:
PachaMama is an oasis of an alternative lifestyle. It’s a place to dive into a transformative spiritual voyage, rejuvenate the body, connect with nature and celebrate life. The village is located on the Pacific coast of Costa Rica in a forested valley. PachaMama was founded 11 years ago by Tyohar and fellow travelers who responded to the invitation to manifest together a commune that embraces a new way of life. Ever since it was founded, the oasis became a gathering place for people from all over the world, creating a colorful and unique energy field.
Some info about the village:
The land size is 500 acre, (2,300,000 m²). 11 years ago this land was used as a cattle farm, without electricity, water system, roads or any living space.
Year by year, tens of thousands of trees were planted and facilities were built to a graceful and ecological standard, as PachaMama was developing and evolving, both practically and spiritually.
There are plans to allow the village to grow continuously as more spiritual seekers, individuals or families, hear the call and want to enjoy the experience of PachaMama on a more permanent basis by putting down their roots here.
The PachaMama experience is a profound journey into the depth of one's being. It is designed to challenge and to embrace. It dares one to experience the depth of life in its totality, be it laughter or tears, the liveliness of togetherness or the silence of aloneness.
It is a Temple of Transformation
Non-working Guests welcome, Work Exchange welcome,
PachaMama is a powerful gateway to profound, transformative growth. The ecological, spiritual community of PachaMama located in Guanacaste, Costa Rica is a platform for a deep voyage into healing, self-acceptance, and expansion of consciousness. PachaMama is also an intentional and alternative off-grid village where people live their normal lives in harmony with nature. There is no belief system practiced here, rather a safe space is created for each fellow traveler to shed old layers, transcend...
The Ecovillage Impact Assessment is a tool for communities to track and boost regenerative development at the local scale. Assess, reflect, compare and improve your impact, and see your results live.
PachaMama, San Juanillo, Guanacaste, 5233

Discover NextGEN What We Do – Check out our projects! Discover and get inspired by our different projects and initiatives happening all over the world! Learn More Our Worldwide Network Connect to our networks in North America, Latin America, Africa, Europe and Asia & Oceania! Learn More The NextGEN Map Dive into the NextGEN’s community and discover our members and projects across the world! Learn More Ecovillages and Regeneration An ecovillage is an intentional, traditional or urban community that is consciously designed through locally owned participatory processes in all four dimensions of sustainability (social, cultural, ecological and economic) to regenerate social and natural environments. Regeneration is a worldwide movement of people who take action in Renewal, Restoration, Biodiversity, Healing, Conscious Living and Resilience. In order to tackle the systemic crisis we are currently living in, the regeneration movement believes we as humans must align to the natural Earth’s model again. properties.name: Paojilhuasca anncestral medicine camp
properties.description:
Paojilhuasca anncestral medicine camp
preserve and spread the use of ancestral plant medicine
Setting: Rural Type: Ecovillage (Indigenous, Traditional), Holistic Center, Eco Project (Permaculture Project)
Status: Established Founded: 2018
Main Language: Spanish Other Language: English
As a plant medicine center we see the importance of preservation of the nature around us from destruction by illegal wood exploitation and mono cultivation. 70% of the chemical components in western medicine is extracted from local Amazonian plants. This incredible richness must neither be overexploited nor lost.
For this reason we started to build an ecovillage with low impact on the natural resources: we try to use natural products that don’t pollute, we started our permaculture garden on an edible forest base and a medicine plantation (Ayahuasca, Chacruna, Huambisa, Mocura etc).
People with the same spirit are welcome to join our project. We offer the land to build tiny houses and support for building them. In return we ask for time and knowledge to be given to the community.
We hope to reach a critical mass and to show examples for the local people to get inspiration to start their own permaculture cultivations in hope that they will entirely drop the use of chemicals that pollute the land and the water of nearby rivers.
Our community finds inspiration in the native people’s egalitarian way: we discuss problem together with the aid of ancestral medicine like ayahuasca or mambe’, medicines that increase the collaborative spirit and the understanding of the relations between humans and nature.
We are just starting out and plan to 
Membership & Visitors
Current members: 7
Open to new members
Open to visitors
we are a medicine center, often we have people around with some psychological or medical issues, for this we need respect and keep the space safe and friendly. If you are agree on this terms you can build your own hut here and help us to cultivate the land and get involved in our project. Actually we are open to new project as well. Newcomers with new ideas are welcome. we believe in sharing and not in money: people are more important than money.

Jose Perez says
August 28, 2022 at 7:43 am
Saludos!!
Me llamo José y soy un español con muchas ganas de visitar vuestro centro y colaborar en todo lo posible.
Actualmente trabajo en la construcción y pintura en estados Unidos.
Tengo experiencia en diferentes comunidades que he visitado en México y Europa mientras viajaba en bicicleta.
He practicado vappassana, aquaponics, soy entrandor personal… Vamos, muchas experiencias vitales que me gustaría compartir con la comunidad. Estoy interesado en permacultura, educación…
No se, si tienen alguna pregunta, podríamos hablar por internet cara a cara.
Ha sido mi sueño de hace mucho tiempo, tras unas búsquedas de visión el conocer a la madre ayahuasca.
Este invierno tengo pensado viajar a Perú y me gustaría pasar un tiempo con vosotros y ayudar en todo lo posible.
No tengo las fechas claras todavía, otro quizá enero, febrero y marzo… Esto no lo tengo claro aun, por eso quisiera hablar con vosotros.
Espero que podamos conocernos,
Un saludo,
Jose

properties.popup:
Paojilhuasca anncestral medicine camp

Paojilhuasca anncestral medicine camp preserve and spread the use of ancestral plant medicine Setting: Rural Type: Ecovillage (Indigenous, Traditional), Holistic Center, Eco Project (Permaculture Project) Status: Established Founded: 2018 Main Language: Spanish Other Language: English As a plant medicine center we see the importance of preservation of the nature around us from destruction by illegal wood exploitation and mono cultivation. 70% of the chemical components in western medicine is extracted from local Amazonian plants. This incredible richness must neither be overexploited nor lost. For this reason we started to build an ecovillage with low impact on the natural resources: we try to use natural products that don’t pollute, we started our permaculture garden on an edible forest base and a medicine plantation (Ayahuasca, Chacruna, Huambisa, Mocura etc). People with the same spirit are welcome to join our project. We offer the land to build tiny houses and support for building them. properties.description: Homepage - NextGEN Skip to main content Learn More NextGEN works to empower, educate, connect, and inspire youth to participate in the ecovillage movement, and contribute to community-led change for eco-social Regeneration. Discover NextGEN What We Do – Check out our projects! Discover and get inspired by our different projects and initiatives happening all over the world! Learn More Our Worldwide Network Connect to our networks in North America, Latin America, Africa, Europe and Asia & Oceania! Learn More The NextGEN Map Dive into the NextGEN’s community and discover our members and projects across the world! Learn More Ecovillages and Regeneration An ecovillage is an intentional, traditional or urban community that is consciously designed through locally owned participatory processes in all four dimensions of sustainability (social, cultural, ecological and economic) to regenerate social and natural environments. Regeneration is a worldwide movement of people who take action in Renewal, Restoration, Biodiversity, Healing, Conscious Living and Resilience. In order to tackle the systemic crisis we are currently living in, the regeneration movement believes we as humans must align to the natural Earth’s model again. properties.name: Pura Vida Village at Tico Time River Resort
properties.description:
Pura Vida Village at Tico Time River Resort
We aim to cultivate holistic wellness living in a premier riverfront area at Tico Time River Resort
Setting: Rural Type: Eco Project (Eco Tourism Site, Permaculture Project), Ecovillage, Holistic Center
Status: Established Founded: 2019
Main Language: English
WELLNESS – We will aim to:
Grow/raise food within each parcel, the village, and nearby
Put substances into our bodies that cause more good than harm
Seek spirituality through individual/group meditations, ceremonies, etc.
Seek increased connectedness to ourselves, the land, and each other
Live lives free from addiction through spirituality and living selfless lifestyles
Use recreation/exercise to stay active in order to be physically fit
UNITY – We will aim to:
Cultivate a community that puts the community first.  The resilience and health of each Resident depends on the overall prosperity of the community.
Live a life of service to free ourselves from selfish motives which cultivates a life of courage and love rather than fear.
Enjoy Group activities, gatherings, ceremonies, and eating together frequently.
Be very inclusive to temporary visitors and accept them into the village experience with unconditional love while they are on site.
Avoid usage of any medication or substances that cause harm to, disturb other residents with.
Be accountable to ourselves and each other to live according to the Ethos/Intention of the Community.
SUSTAINABILITY – We will aim to:
Build sustainable housing that integrates naturally with the landscape including Yurts, Micro Homes, Teepees, Earthen Dwellings, & Hillside Cave Homes
Use solar and energy storage as much as possible.
In order to preserve the natural landscape we aim to use small electric vehicles as much as possible for transportation on the East side of the bridge.
Minimize usage of outside resources and maximize usage of onsite resources
Dedicate a minimum, small amount of time to work for the betterment of the community on a monthly basis.  ~8 Hours per month working on community improvements/maintenance
Membership & Visitors
Current members: 30
Open to new members
Open to visitors
simply click Get Involved on our website
More
About Robert Holmes
COMMUNITY PILLARS
WELLNESS - Overall mental, physical, and spiritual health are crucial
UNITY - Connection and Community Focus cultivates a thriving community
SUSTAINABILITY - Intentional sustainability provides long term security and success for all
The village model focuses on a PLAY - WORK - LIVE scenario where we focus on spending a majority of our daily lives on site and nearby. This cultivates a simple, healthy, connected lifestyle that allows for abundance for Residents and the Com
Reader Interactions

properties.name: RareBirds Housing Co-operative
properties.description:
RareBirds Housing Co-operative
Intentional eco-community and equity co-operative in an urban green home
Setting: Urban Type: Eco Community, Shared Housing
Status: Established Founded: 2012
Main Language: English Other Language: Spanish
We are currently a group of adults – singles and couples. We completed construction of our new co-op home in February 2014, after three years of community building, design and construction. We have six equal shares in the value of our property and house. Beyond this initial investment, we share equally the other costs of living together. We describe our project as an ‘intentional community’. We share expenses and live co-dependently with a common kitchen, shared evening meal, dining and living room space as well as private studio rooms. We are structured as an equity housing co-operative and our by-laws conform to the BC Co-operative Act.
Our mandate is to provide community housing designed to lessen our environmental footprint, share resources, and living costs, provide social enrichment and community support and to showcase a new model for interdependent living.
Our community life includes consensus decision making, communication based on modified circles practices, conflict resolution and respectful listening.
The RareBirds members are aware that our co-op housing model is innovative. Our research indicates that we differ from the majority of housing co-op developments in that we are building a single-family home with design features to allow both private and common living spaces for adult oriented, multi-generational community living.
Our goal is to document the path of this project from inception to completion to develop awareness and education in our own community, and the larger population, in more viable and sustainable alternatives to housing ourselves.
The RareBirds welcome people of all ages who hold a deep set of values regarding sustainability and sharing of resources.
One of our objectives is to showcase a model for a more sustainable, co-operative way of living together in a modern construct of shared housing.
Membership & Visitors
Current members: 10
Open to new members
Open to visitors
Please review our history and FAQ at www.rarebirdshousing.ca
You can then contact the co-operative at rarebirdshousing@gmail.com

About Dan Hines
GEN Ambassador

properties.name: ReTribe at NorthernShire
properties.description: After School | Vermont | ReTribe top of page Wild Woods Afterschool ​ Ages 6 - 13 3 - 5:30pm Mondays & Thursdays in Underhill You can register for one day or for both days ​ Fall Session: September 14, 2026 - November 19, 2026 Spring Session: March 8, 2027 - May 20, 2027 No Afterschool during Spring Break School break dates are provisional and may be updated when we receive the district calendar. ​ Bus drop off provided by Jericho Elementary and Underhill Central. ​ Tuition is $550/year for one day or $1100/year for two days Register Registration Info Meet Our Staff Come join us in the forest after school to explore the woods, play games, make fires and crafts, and go on magical adventures.​ Wild Woods Afterschool is held in the beautiful woods of Underhill where we enjoy the rolling hills and forests as well as our brook and pond. Daily Rhythm The Structure of the day is very flexible at Afterschool as we have a lot of children coming from public school and need an outbreath of freedom and exploration. The general schedule is something like this: ​ 3pm: Arrival and Free play as well as tag games and other animal games in the meadow and woods. ​ 3:45pm: Head into woods camp, cut wood, collect wood, build a fire, sing some songs and eat snack around the fire. ​ 4:15pm: Activity Options which could include: craft by the fire such as carving play running/sneaking games hike or adventure to a cool spot like the pond or upstream and explore there "adventure village" where they have characters in a fantasy world and trade things they've crafted or found ​ 5:15pm: Gather belongings and head out. ​ 5:30pm: Pick up. "We are so grateful our child has a place to grow. He comes back transformed and I am forever grateful for this gift." bottom of page Contact-us-and-calendar | Vermont | ReTribe top of page Send Us A Message Sign Up For Our Newsletter I want to subscribe to the newsletter. Submit Thanks for submitting! ReTribe contact: Julia Martin at ReTribeTransformation@gmail.

properties.name: Red Colombiana de Reservas Naturales
properties.description:
It is the mission of the Colombian Network Association of Natural Reserves of Civil Society, to contribute to the knowledge, consolidation and positioning of conservation initiatives of civil society, through processes of sustainable use and management of biological diversity, for the construction of social fabric, alternative life models and development, with criteria of generational, ethnic and gender equity.
The Colombian Network Association of Natural Reserves of Civil Society will be a leading environmental organization, private in nature, that interacts with local communities. It will be a solid, flexible, autonomous, dynamic and qualified organization: technically, humanly, environmentally, legally and economically; made up of natural and legal persons from diverse cultures and generations building social fabric, dedicated to the development of processes of conservation and sustainable use of biological diversity, sustainable production and other environmental goods and services.
The Ecovillage Impact Assessment is a tool for communities to track and boost regenerative development at the local scale. Assess, reflect, compare and improve your impact, and see your results live.
, Cali, Valle del Cauca

properties.name: Rocky Corner Cohousing
properties.description: - Rocky Corner Cohousing Rocky Corner Cohousing was planned to be an intentional, sustainable community in the heart of New England. In 2021, with construction nearly complete, the construction lender began foreclosure proceedings, which were completed in early 2022. No one lives in the community (as of August 2023), and there has been no communication from the bank, which is working with a developer, about future plans for the property. Thanks to all who supported our efforts to build a special place in Connecticut. This site is archived for historical reference only. Planting garlic at our organic farm We are creating a beautiful old fashion style cohousing neighborhood in Bethany, Connecticut, just 5 miles from New Haven. Rocky Corner is small eco-village designed and maintained by neighbors with attention to sustainability, conservation, and aesthetics. Itâs about being in community with caring neighbors. Itâs about 30 energy efficient homes clustered to make the best use of our 33 acre organic farm. Aerial view Sept. 2019 We invite you to learn about what makes us unique Rocky Corner is an inclusive community. Families are welcome. Elders are welcome. All LGBTQ+ people are welcome. All abilities are welcome. All races and ethnicities and beliefs are welcome. properties.name: Rubha Phoil, a nature based permaculture community on the Isle of Skye, Scotland
properties.description: Our News : Rubha Phoil - Our Ancient Rock on Skye Skip to content Have you ever been on this rock? Did you come for a walk to see the seals? Did you stay overnight? Did you volunteer or live here for a while? Do you have a story or an experience to share? Even if it was only for a fleeting visit, please share it with us. If you can answer some of our many questions we ask on the timeline, please let us know. Do come back regularly to see our progress of the history or subscribe to our newsletter on the right hand side of this page. This is how you can share your experience with us: We would love to sit around the fire with you to capture your story in person. You can write to us using snail mail via: Rubha Phoil, Armadale Pier, Ardvasar, IV45 8RS You can email us or ask for a phone or video call, on the form below. We are aware that for some people there is also history that is more painful. We are happy to support you in sharing your story in any way we can and help you heal any wounds there might be left behind from any possible bad experiences you may have had on this rock. Your name* (Required) Your email* (Required) If you give us a phone number, we are happy to call/sms/whatsapp/signal/telegram ... you. If you have any documents, pictures, or anything else interesting, send us a message we''ll reply, so you have our email address. properties.name: SFEL (Strawberry Fields Eco-Lodge)
properties.description:
Strawberry Fields Eco Lodge (SFEL) delivers an eco-tourism service, embedded in the local cultural landscape and tailored for the environmentally aware visitor to Ethiopia. SFEL offers cultural hospitality and fresh organic food to national and international guests. It also forms the epicentre of a series of ethically-minded, community-based, education and adventure-tourism activities. Our unique Cultural Immersion Trekking Programs promote community inclusion in tourism activities, while our Permaculture Design Courses (PDC) help to develop sustainable living and effective resource management in Ethiopia and elsewhere.
Workshop-visitors welcome, Non-working Guests welcome, Work Exchange welcome,
The Ecovillage Impact Assessment is a tool for communities to track and boost regenerative development at the local scale. Assess, reflect, compare and improve your impact, and see your results live

properties.name: Sawyer Hill Ecovillage
properties.description: Welcome | Sawyer Hill EcoVillage Skip to main content Welcome Are you looking for a new energy-efficient home in a great neighborhood? Check out our Homes Available page. Our Neighborhoods Mosaic Commons Camelot Cohousing EcoVillages Sawyer Hill EcoVillage uses the best practices of sustainable development and land planning. Our homes are clustered to reduce our footprint, so that over 75% of our 65 acre site can be left as open space, including more than 25 acres of permanent conservation land. Common facilities and a close community encourages sharing of resources and low vehicle use. The latest construction techniques ensure that our buildings are highly energy efficient. Read more about our EcoVillage Our town Berlin is a picturesque New England town of about 3100 people. Its idyllic rural feel belies its convenient location near the intersection of routes 495 and 290, just 45 minutes from Boston and 20 minutes from Worcester. Berlin is nestled in in a low range of hills between the Nashua and the Assabet River Valleys, and boasts excellent schools and easy access to shopping, dining and entertainment. Incorporated in 1812, the town has a rich history of agriculture, manufacturing and lumber. Sawyer Hill EcoVillage is located at 46 Sawyer Hill Road . Affordable Housing Sawyer Hill EcoVillage was developed under the Massachusetts "40B" affordable housing law. Seventeen of our 68 homes are set aside for income-qualified buyers through a state-regulated system. Cohousing Sawyer Hill EcoVillage is comprised of two cohousing neighborhoods, Mosaic Commons and Camelot Cohousing . Cohousing is a modern style of residential neighborhood in which some resources are shared in common by the residents. Each family owns their home, as well as a share of the common facilities, which include things like playing fields and gardens. Our community values green space, a safe, healthy environment for our children and conservation of our natural resources. The cohousing development m

properties.name: Schloss Tempelhof
properties.description: The eco-village and community, Schloss Tempelhof, was founded in 2010. As a visionary place and future workshop venue it is going to build the first approved Earthship in Germany. Thanks to solar power and water stills , these Earthships are almost ‘off-grid’ : not reliant on any external electricity, gas, water and sewerage systems. Construction will begin on September 28, 2015. Stefanie Raysz reports.

properties.name: Schloss Tonndorf
properties.description:
The community at Tonndorf Castle brings together the people of the cooperative who want to renovate and revitalize Tonndorf Castle together, our children and our FöJlers and apprentices.  We are both families and single people. Our origins, life experiences and respective economic situations are different - the desire for mutual support in everyday life, a stimulating human atmosphere and a rich, social environment is equally present in everyone. The group currently includes around 60 people aged 0 to early 60.
The members of the community see themselves as a community of people of all ages who live together on the basis of freely agreed agreements and thus primarily pursue the following goals:
the development of self-responsibility, self-help and self-management as a fundamental principle at all levels of coexistence from childhood to old age,
supporting initiatives that serve the development of the individual and the community,
the creation of a cross-generational living model in chosen families that offers a variety of caregivers not only to the elderly, but especially to the children growing up here,
the creation of family-oriented jobs that give children, in particular, an insight into the world of work and thus make it visible and understandable,
the creation of socially and ecologically responsible housing & Working conditions and the increased use of renewable energies such as sun and wind and renewable raw materials,
reducing the consumption of natural resources,
extensive self-sufficiency with ecologically sound, home-grown food,
respect for nature and the earth and careful handling of everything that emerges from them,
the development of social skills, with the help of which it is possible to creatively use tensions and conflicts between members as growth potential and thereby reduce them more and more or to counteract their emergence through good, non-violent communication.
This happens on the basis of self-responsible action and appreciation of others for their difference.
The Ecovillage Impact Assessment is a tool for communities to track and boost regenerative development at the local scale. Assess, reflect, compare and improve your impact, and see your results live

properties.description: Homepage - NextGEN Skip to main content Learn More NextGEN works to empower, educate, connect, and inspire youth to participate in the ecovillage movement, and contribute to community-led change for eco-social Regeneration. Discover NextGEN What We Do – Check out our projects! Discover and get inspired by our different projects and initiatives happening all over the world! Learn More Our Worldwide Network Connect to our networks in North America, Latin America, Africa, Europe and Asia & Oceania! Learn More The NextGEN Map Dive into the NextGEN’s community and discover our members and projects across the world! Learn More Ecovillages and Regeneration An ecovillage is an intentional, traditional or urban community that is consciously designed through locally owned participatory processes in all four dimensions of sustainability (social, cultural, ecological and economic) to regenerate social and natural environments. Regeneration is a worldwide movement of people who take action in Renewal, Restoration, Biodiversity, Healing, Conscious Living and Resilience. In order to tackle the systemic crisis we are currently living in, the regeneration movement believes we as humans must align to the natural Earth’s model again. properties.name: Shambhala Eco-Castle
properties.description: Shambhala - Shambhala Eco-Castle Home Shambhala Story Community Fellows Living Arts Fayres Event Calendar Living Faire Tickets Volunteer Contact Shambhala Eco-Castle Eco-Castle Community Shambhala Eco-Castle Story Imagine you are living 100 years from now, in 2120. The world is a very different place, and humanity has changed along with it. In recent history, the world economy as we know it collapsed in 2022 from a world war that started in North America, and spread like a plague consuming country after country. This war was an economic war, with it’s beginnings stretching back hundreds of years, to before the industrial revolution, maybe all the way back to the beginning of agriculture when we first assigned ownership of the land to some, but not others separating humanity into two groups, the ones that have what they need and the poor. Not a soul was left untouched as the violence raged for 25 years, separating families, and creating entire generations on earth knowing nothing but conflict. Almost 25% of the world’s people died in this war, from conflict, or diseases, or starvation. At the end of this war that did end all wars, in 2048 a bacteria emerged, or re-emerged that stopped responding to antibiotics, and devastated the planet, taking another half of the world’s people with it. Power and electricity are gone, even solar only worked for a while until batteries could no longer maintain their charges, and no new ones were available. Gasoline is no longer available. Cars sit idle, growing plants until the rust consumes them, and there are just rusty pieces of frame and tires left. People were in such a weak state from the consequences of war, they had little resistance to any infections, and children and elderly were particularly decimated by minor injuries and infections. More than half of the world’s people were now gone…the war had carried people from all over the world to every place on earth creating a giant mixing pot of people and cultures, stranded when

properties.name: Shangri-La
properties.description: Stefan Novellino, Portugal projekt SHANGRI-LA. Home updates, what is going on... Fotoalbum Visits, accomodation "philosophical reflections " The farm Contact/link Stefan Novellino, Portugal projekt SHANGRI-LA. Välkommen till min hemsida på Cybersite visits,accomodation Shangri-La is open to receive people with different needs and interests, look also at: You may visit as : 1 ) paying guest / visitors and choose to be completely by yourself and just use the place as, a B & B or hostel or a camping or park your camper. As a visitor, you can always,when possible and available, choose to be part of the farm's activities if it is something that interests you. At the moment we have no restaurant service, but there is the posssibility for basic costs and sharing the work, to eat together with us on the farm. 2 ) woofer, volunteer. We offer Practice location / volunteer in biodynamic farming / horticulture with great focus on fruit tree cultivation. We offer for volunteering a 2 week stay period for bord and lodging , for one volunteer at time. 3 ) Resident , if you are looking for and you feel that this might be a possible long-term intresting place, we can offer you to stay here for longer periods . Maybe you are interested in coming here regularly, perhaps as a pensioner? Maybe you want to create here your own "space" ? There are possibility to be discussed , get in contact for more info and make a first visit. Faro is the nearest airport. There are no main connections to Shangri-la , we are 5 km from the town of silves, and 12 from the train station of silves. If you are not renting a car and may wish to be pick up at the silves train staion we can arrange that , previous arrangement , for 15 €, and from the faro airport for 150 € .Taxi is of course also possible and cheaper. PLEASE by any visit , be responsible ! be respectful ! don t come here with parfums !! not on you , not on your cloths , not with shampoo etc. We are living here sustainable, chemicals goes in the

В июле 2025 Ширам, Тверская область Часть 1: Апгрейд УМА Путешествие в Чертоги Разума Апгрейд Самого Себя Очищение вашего кэша Выход из зависа Перепрограммирование устаревших кодов Транссерфинг реальности Основы метапрограммирования Многозадачность и концентрация Все это, Чтобы Постичь Тайну Бытия Мы планируем: развернуть пространства, в которых участники смогут: Решать задачи и механических головоломок; Проходить разнообразные лабиринты на нашем поле: Участвовать в соревнованиях на меткость и ловкость; Лекции, презентации, игры о развитии ума и креативности; Все это в нашем Шираме, на берегу реки Тверца, рядом с деревней Боровое, и трассой М10-М11. с 1 по 7 июля у нас Волонтерская неделя Феста. а с 9 по 17 - Неделя: Расширенные и Изменненые состояния сознания. properties.address: 22 hectar plot outside of the village Poppau, Germany. Since then, the village has significantly grown. We now have several big houses for community members and all are built with high ecological standards using mostly local materials, many with straw bale insulation and mud plastering.Others still live in trailers. We run a thriving seminar center, a forest kindergarten, a mailorder business, and a big garden

properties.name: Skala Ecovillage
properties.description:
Skala Ecovillage is an attempt to form and accomplish an ecological friendly and collective way of living.
Currently, four adults and three children live permanently . Last year (2014) it has been created a core group of 8-10 people, which is working intensively on the implementation of the vision.At the same time, a wide network of friends and collectives, both in Greece and abroad, supports us in several ways.
Ecovillage for us means connecting the political and social vision with the idea of a life freed of fear. The ecovillage is a school of life where the training of personality is getting enriched by the participation in the common affairs.
We dream the Ecovillage as a vibrant and open social proposal which can replace the conventional structures of competition and fear with the constants of trust, solidarity and cooperation with ourselves and to life herself.
The Ecovillage Impact Assessment is a tool for communities to track and boost regenerative development at the local scale. Assess, reflect, compare and improve your impact, and see your results live

properties.name: Smiltenei un Latvijai
properties.description: Biedrība Smiltenei un Latvijai: Eiropas Solidaritātes korpusa projekta ietvaros jaunieši mācās viens no otra un māca citus Permakultūras festivālā Lapas1 Aktualitātes Par mums About us Telpu noma Ziedot AIF OffGrid DIY Projekti Foto Privātuma politika M.Svīķes kultūrvieta ceturtdiena, 2026. gada 25. jūnijs Eiropas Solidaritātes korpusa projekta ietvaros jaunieši mācās viens no otra un māca citus Permakultūras festivālā Ir noslēdzies Eiropas Solidaritātes korpusa projekts, kura laikā divu nedēļu garumā jaunieši no Gruzijas, Francijas, Portugāles, Spānijas, Latvijas, Itālijas dzīvoja kopienā, mācījās viens no otra un kopīgi radīja Permakultūras festivālu. Divu nedēļu laikā kopā tika radīts ne tikai Permakultūras festivāls, bet arī jaunas draudzības, zināšanas un pārliecība, ka jaunieši paši spēj būt pārmaiņu veidotāji savās kopienās. Festivāls kļuva par projekta kulmināciju, taču patiesībā tas bija tikai viena daļa no visa piedzīvotā. Divu nedēļu laikā jaunieši ne tikai apguva permakultūras principus un ilgtspējīga dzīvesveida idejas, bet arī paši plānoja aktivitātes, pilnveidoja vidi, mācījās sadarboties, uzņēmās atbildību par kopīgu rezultātu un pierādīja, cik daudz iespējams paveikt, uzticoties jauniešu iniciatīvai. Festivāla rītā daļa laika tika veltīta paveiktā iemūžināšanai video formātā. Zinājām, ka daudzi, kuri vēlējās apmeklēt festivālu, tajā dienā bija aizņemti, gatavojoties uzvedumam "Skroderdienas Silmačos" vai piedaloties citās pirmssvētku aktivitātēs. Tāpēc video kļuva par iespēju dalīties festivāla atmosfērā arī ar tiem, kuri klātienē nevarēja būt. Nākotnē šie un citi projekta laikā tapušie video tiks publicēti īpaši izveidotā YouTube kanālā, lai ikviens interesents varētu iepazīt jauniešu radītās meistarklases, praktiskās idejas un iedvesmojošus ilgtspējīga dzīvesveida risinājumus arī pēc projekta noslēguma. Pēcpusdienā "Lejas Varicēnos" ieradās apmeklētāji, kuri ar patiesu interesi iesaistījās jauniešu sagatavotajās aktivitātēs. Katra meistarklase bij

properties.name: SolarGarden
properties.description:
Land "reset" by the epic fires of 2017 that charred central Portugal. Venerable cork-oaks thrive again, protected by their unstripped bark; olives respond rewardingly to recent intensive care. An oasis of diversity surrounded by a monoculture desert of commercial eucalyptus forestry. Very steep & rugged, with a ruin, old schist terrace walls, a spring & ravines, & now fruit & vegetable plots using experimental techniques. Can fluctuate seasonally between brutal sun & relentless cloud & rain. Not for the fragile of mind or body!
Two of us live here: me 67, as fit as in my 20s, working full-time on the land, & the owner Allen, 72, young at heart & able to ebike up the mountain. We both sing, play & compose music, one on guitar, the other on piano, with long experience of Entheogens. Allen's daughter will be here in spring.
My base is a "moored" motor-home I purpose-built in the UK, with super-insulation, underfloor heating, solar thermal & heat-reclaim. Living in it heats it. During the 40 deg C summers, a tiny solar heatpump keeps it under 30 deg. Never intended as a runabout or tourer. Longer term prospects include earth-sheltered, heat & fire-resistant dwellings. Tents are only really liveable-in between the cold rainy autumns & the oven-like summers. Allen has also built temporary solarpunk accommodation, a steep climb up the land.
Potential “members” are technical, scientific, creative, analytically minded with a core love & knowledge of nature - horticulture, wanting an emotional support network working to transcend the habits "too heavy for my planet" which wealth-harvesters pressurise or seduce us to cling to. Visitors need to be self-supporting while here. Portuguese study encouraged. Self awareness, ability to express oneself & give reciprocal quality attention is expected.
A half-hour Camino do Xisto walk or steep mountain-bike ride from Benfeita - accessible by bus. Events, maybe you, may yet affect where it continues to grow; maybe even at other bases.
Please initiate an email conversation, by responding in detail to the description posted here, and about how you see yourself as part of this community.
* You are welcome to write in any of the languages mentioned.
*  Please be realistic about your potential involvement, including mentioning other communities or futures you are investigating.
*  I will likely respond with a SIMILAR amount to what you write. So if you write "Hi, please send me more information", I will reply "What specifically would you like to know"...etc.
* A few zany words or other evidence of human-ness is always welcome.
Born in 1957, lived many lives, best just check my web site - wavesculptor.net
Very interesting. I have got an Ebike and some agricolture experience in Italy .  I thought to try a new experience this year in Spain or Portugal.  Could you tell me how is the weather  there in the winter time?

Skriv et svar Annuller svar Du skal være logget ind for at skrive en kommentar. KONTAKT Administrationen Tlf.: +45 42 14 08 80 adm@svanholmgods.dk Svanholm Gods 4A 4050 Skibby PR og presse pr@svanholm.dk CAFÉENS ÅBNINGSTIDER : LØRDAG: 10-16 SØNDAG: 10-16. Søndag er der salg af vores friske, rå mælk til 25 kr./liter SVANHOLMS VIRKSOMHEDER Svanholm Børnehus Klinikken Svanholm Vandværk SAMARBEJDSPARTNERE Økologisk Landsforening Simonsen & Illeris Leg & Landskab Permakultur – Lev bæredygtigt LØS Hansens Flødeis Eksempelside – Svanholm Storkollektiv Videre til indhold Dette er en eksempelside. Den er forskellig fra et blogindlæg fordi at den vil forblive ét sted og vil dukke op i dit websteds navigation (i de fleste temaer). De fleste folk starter med en Om side der introducerer dem til potentielle besøgende på webstedet. Der kunne for eksempel stå: Hej! Jeg er et cykelbud om dagen, aspirerende skuespiller om natten, og dette er mit websted. Jeg bor i Los Angeles, har en fantastisk hund der hedder Jack, og jeg elsker piña coladas. (Og at blive fanget i regnen.) …eller noget i denne stil: Firmaet XYZ Dingenot blev grundlagt i 1971, og har siden da leveret kvalitetsdingenoter til offentligheden. XYZ er beliggende i Gotham City og beskæftiger over 2.000 mennesker og gør alle slags fantastiske ting for Gotham-samfundet. Som ny WordPress-bruger bør du gå til dit kontrolpanel for at slette denne side og oprette nye sider til dit indhold. Hav det sjovt! properties.name: Synergia Ranch (HQ of Ecotechnics)
properties.description: ## New video!!Great views of Synergia Ranch facilities and vistas. Are you looking to schedule that next workshop, conference or retreat and want a location that is relaxing, inspiring and stunning? If so, Synergia Ranch is for you. We’re situated on 135 acres property located 30 minutes from Santa Fe Plaza and 45 minutes from Albuquerque. Since 1969, our charming, southwestern style setting, friendly staff and reasonably priced facility has been the perfect place to get away from it all. Synergia Ranch is a magical oasis in the desert surrounded by mountain views, with a four acre orchard, organic gardens, and a diversity of wildlife in this ecological sanctuary. Amenities include delicious meals, comfortable rooms, several meeting spaces, wireless internet. Camping is available in the Orchard. Located at 26 Synergia Road in Santa Fe, New Mexico. Avalanche - Synergia Ranch Avalanche by Deborah Snyder | October 5, 2014 | synergia ranch organic I know an orchard where four hundred fruit trees branch And every spring send down an avalanche Of snow white blossoms burying the ranch Beneath six feet of blooms I hurry there and help to dig them out To find their bodies, back and forth we shout And there they are, content and strewn about Like birds in flowery tombs I often wonder if they notice that Ten tons of flowers fill their habitat Because, as usual, they work and chat Inside adjoining rooms And when late summer, early fall becomes An avalanche, this time of sugarplums And apples, pears, upon the rooftop drums And we need power brooms To sweep away the hills, the peaks, the butte The mountaintop of ever ripening fruit To find the ranch hands, cheerful, resolute Intact as rare perfumes. 2013 Organic Farm News - Synergia Ranch 2013 Organic Farm News by Deborah Snyder | August 23, 2013 | Ranch August 2013 saw the return of the fantastic vegetables from Synergia Ranch Organic Farm, selling at the Santa Fe Farmer’s Market and being delivered to discerning restaurant chef

Read More Blog Post Title Two — Ecovillage Tours 0 Blog Post Title Two May 28 Written By Cynthia Tina It all begins with an idea. Maybe you want to launch a business. Maybe you want to turn a hobby into something more. Or maybe you have a creative project to share with the world. Whatever it is, the way you tell your story online can make all the difference. Don’t worry about sounding professional. Sound like you. There are over 1.5 billion websites out there, but your story is what’s going to separate this one from the rest. If you read the words back and don’t hear your own voice in your head, that’s a good sign you still have more work to do. Be clear, be confident and don’t overthink it. The beauty of your story is that it’s going to continue to evolve and your site can evolve with it. Your goal should be to make it feel right for right now. Later will take care of itself. It always does. properties.name: Tamera
properties.description: Tamera Peace Research & Education Center in Portugal Welcome to Tamera â Peace Research & Education Center Watch Our Trailer Vision & Mission To support a global system change from war to peace, from exploitation to cooperation, from fear to trust, by building Healing Biotopes : research centers that model a nonviolent culture. Tamera empowers people around the world to build a regenerative nonviolent culture by: Research Exploring the ethical, social, sexual, ecological, technological and economic foundations of a peace culture Investigate research Education Training those committed to inner and outer peace work and creating community-based alternatives Learn about education Outreach Building a planetary community rooted in mutual support and collaborating for a future without war Reach for outreach "There is the world that we create, and there is the world that has created us. These two worlds must come together. This is the goal of our journey." DIETER DUHM, co-founder On-site courses We are looking forward to welcome you in Tamera. Get to know us in an " Introduction to Tamera " or book a seminar about one of our research areas. Unless stated otherwise all courses are held in English: On-site Course Community Service Kitchen On-Site Event Water Retention Landscape of Tamera On-site Course SD Arts – The Art of Self-Discovery See all Events Online Courses Join our interactive learning adventures from wherever you are. Between 5 and 22 December 2025, Sarvodaya reached over 6,205 people across 15 districts, delivering food, water, dry rations, medical support, and psychosocial care while maintaining 24/7 emergency coordination through its Emergency Operations Centre. Sarvodaya’s Cyclone Ditwah Emergency Situation and Rapid Response Report details the transition from emergency relief to early recovery, livelihoods support, and village-led rebuilding efforts. properties.name: The Foundry
properties.description: about – The Foundry Skip to content The Foundry is a collective and self-organized project, where we are all using and taking care of a shared space in a non-hierarchical way. This is a work in progress and we are not claiming to have a perfect system. We try to put this vision in practice through the following principles: • Doers decide. You don’t need to ask permission to show a movie or plant a tree. Communication is important, but feel free to develop initiative; that’s what the space is for! • Make things possible for others. Leave space for others; don’t occupy all the physical space. Support other’s initiatives. Keep, help and take care of other people’s work, and work together to keep the space clean and organized. This also means there are limits to freedom, and we expect you to take others needs into account when you organise something. • Act as host and guest at the same time. Some know the place better than others, but we all use and care for the place together. When new residents arrive those who already know their way around are expected to welcome them and show them around. When neighbours drop by, please represent the Foundry as best as you can. Making a horizontal space like the Foundry work requires more communication rather than less, and a willingness to question your own position. We are all socialised in cultures that are steeped in patriarchy, capitalism and colonialism. None of us are pure and perfect, and we invariably reproduce patterns of exploitation and oppression, often without realising. Living together is an opportunity to learn about our selves as well as the kind of society we hope to see in future. This does not have to be revolutionary and complex. We can start by serving the aim of the Foundry to become a vibrant commons – not owned by anyone in particular. Those who make use of the space take care of the space; they renovate it, maintain it, and pay for its expenses. If you want to spend time here, think of what you want to cont

properties.name: The Northern Lights NPO
properties.description:
The Northern Lights is a non-profit organization based in France, in a beautiful and typical farm. An alternative & ecological place ths nearby around permaculture values and cultural events.With a pedagogic vocation, it is a dream of local resilience and an experimentation of living together close to nature on our teritory and in the world that suround us. The organization is located in 'Le grand Matrignat',  of Saint Nizier le Bouchoux, North of Lyon (90km), where we cultivate earth, mind and togetherness. Our mission is to spread knowledge, techniques and the mindset for regenerative practices in all domains of human activity. Through workshops, networking and building active and alive examples of regenerative practices in close collaboration with other organizations, public entities and ecological enterprises.
The Northern Lights is an educational and land stewardship non-profit organisation that actively regenerates eco-systems in which people, inspired by nature, create and maintain healthy and abundant livelihoods.
Our work is to empower people of all ages and backgrounds with knowledge, skills, and inspiration essential to cultivating regenerative habitats, inhabitants and habits, while enhancing their professional capabilities to initiate their own projects and/or businesses.
If you would like to join us and volunteer for any amount of time, please reach out through our  to give us your planned arrival dates and a small description of your area of interest and skillsets regarding eco-living.
The Northern Lights is an educational and land stewardship non-profit organisation that actively regenerates eco-systems in which people, inspired by nature, create and maintain healthy and abundant livelihoods. Our work is to empower people of all ages and backgrounds with knowledge, skills, and inspiration essential to cultivating regenerative habitats, inhabitants and habits, while enhancing their professional capabilities to initiate their own projects and/or businesses.

Am from Kenya and a practicing farmer.
I am impressed by your holistic approach towards nature.
I would love to visit your organisation for a benchmarking mission.
My whatsapp number is @ +254721484534 and  buyapo@yahoo.com

properties.name: The Park, Ecovillage Findhorn
properties.description: 50th Birthday Book – Editorial – Celebrating One Incredible Family Skip to content 50th Birthday Book – Editorial Adriana Sjan Bijman | 2026-03-12T15:25:05+00:00 July 10th, 2022 | Categories: 50th Birthday Book | Tags: 50th Birthday Book , transformation | 0 Comments View Larger Image This is not about a book, this is about transformation Editorial This is not a history book about 50 years of Findhorn, or The Findhorn Foundation Community. And yet it is. It is full of history. And it is not complete as such, as it was not made with that intention. So you might not recognise your Findhorn experience, but your own story might come to the surface while reading and leafing through, even if you have never physically been here. This project started because I felt strongly called to mark these 50 years by creating a beautiful book from, with and for the community and its worldwide circle of friends. I answered the call and set up a first meeting. I have used all my skills in organising, editing, photography and graphic design. The task became much more than that. The start in July 2011 was slow, with hesitation from the official bodies and lots of meetings before the project was supported. Margot Henderson, Angus Marland and I made the book’s first structure,starting with the three pillars of our community and then a chapter per decade. Hopes that the community creative writing evenings with Margot and Harula Ladd would get people writing their stories were not fulfilled. Then Margot had to withdraw from the project with no one to take her place to manage the text. I felt outer resistance to making it happen and did not feel very trusted. Con fronted with personal issues it became a painful process for me. At one point I wanted to give upthe whole idea, forgetting why I had started it. While talking with Jonathan Caddy in my studio, the penny dropped: this is not just a book, this is about Transformation! A personal transformation. This is what Findhorn is about for the ma

properties.name: The Wild Cooperative
properties.description:
The Wild Cooperative
Cultivating bio-centric lifestyle where all life is part of our family and relatives.
Setting: Rural Type: Eco Project (Learning Centre, Permaculture Project), Ecovillage (Intentional)
Status: Established Founded: 2015
Main Language: English
We are a small rural community that cultivates cooperative and facilitating relationship with nature and each other and that shares the knowledge of our regenerative and responsible living experiences. We are a permaculture education and demonstration site. We are creating a natural and regenerative ecosystem that energizes all life, allows for individual growth, and shares and returns the surplus of energy and knowledge. This healthy village with resilient social, cultural, economic, educational, and ecological guilds is a building block of a biotic culture.
Membership & Visitors
Current members: 4
Open to new members
Open to visitors
Ideally, for our forming community, prospective members will be skilled natural builders with a wide range of maintenance and repair skills and be experienced in off grid living as well as a good communicator. That being said, a variety of skills and knowledge may be an asset to our community – we strive for diversity on many levels. When the whole community accepts the prospective member, they will agree to act in accordance to the covenants and the operating agreement by signing both documents. To apply, please email us and request an application form at: wildcooperative@hotmail.com.
Video
Promotional video
More
About Ewelina Bajda
The Wild Cooperative is an education and demonstration site and an small rural intentional regenerative community in Western Colorado. In the spirit of biocentrism and collaboration, this site is offering creative space for workshops, activism, and community-building. Our vision is to be surrounded by energy efficient homes, perennial food forest gardens, healthy soils and clean reliable water and healthy air. Just as we design this resilient system on the ground we nurture similarly supportive …

properties.name: Tribe of Likatien
properties.description:
The Tribe Füssen Eins is also known as Tribe of Likatien. Likatien means “residents at lik”, wich retrogrades to the last ancient celtic tribe, living at the river Lech.
One of our central visions is to become a tribe – in a modern meaning. For us that means a special quality of admitting with each other and close to our emotional and spiritual reality.
In the way people get involved with the the tribe and eachother the tribe becomes sustainable. In the inner circle the people swear to say here for the whole life. That founds the base for the tribe and for following generations.
We want to develop a cultur of love and trust as a sign for menkind`s original destiny. A culture wich includes the conciousness of a balance in relationships – between people and nature but also between eachother. So communication is very important for us in the dirctest and clearest way wich is possible.
 of Füssen we own ca. 20 houses, where we live and work.
We want to develop the possibility for all our members to work in our own companies. We have an esoteric book-shop, a bio-shop, a computer-shop, a company wich organizes esoteric-fairs, internet shops, lawyers,
 a healerschool with holistic doctors and there is a group of people making agriculture for the tribe, trying to develop a permaculture. We’re just at the beginning of this development, because we’re in the need of more dedicated people.
Our tribe is structured like a mandala with different circles expressing the degree of identifying with the tribe. Our community-decisions we make consensual.
People in the inner circle meet every evening for diskussing problems and working together.
Every week there are single meetings of women, men and youths, where we diskuss interpersonal, cultural, social, spiritual and many other subjects.
And there are also many festivities. The tribe loves good party spirit, because enthusiastic lively party elation creates a feeling of paradise. And that’s a target the tribe aspires to: to create a possibility for Paradise on earth.
Non-working Guests welcome, Volunteers welcome,

properties.name: UF4D GreenLab Ecovillage
properties.description:
The SING community is a vibrant, intergenerational settlement nestled along the White Volta Basin in the Kumbungu District of Northern Ghana. Rooted in traditional Dagomba culture and guided by the values of unity, mutual aid, and respect for nature, SING has long embraced communal living and sustainable farming practices. However, over the past decades, the region has experienced increasing environmental stress land degradation, erratic rainfall, youth unemployment, and a decline in traditional livelihoods.
In response to these challenges, our community, supported by United Force for Development International (UF4D), came together to form the UF4D GreenLab an ambitious initiative to create a regenerative ecovillage that not only restores the land but also empowers people. Our journey began with local youth groups, elders, and women's cooperatives working side-by-side to envision a future where sustainability, dignity, and innovation could thrive hand-in-hand.
We are united by shared principles of ecological responsibility, local empowerment, participatory governance, and circular economy thinking. Our goal is to transition from linear, extractive models to circular, regenerative systems that honor both people and planet. Together, we are building a living laboratory—a place to prototype sustainable farming, green technologies, eco-housing, and community-owned enterprises.
We aspire to become a beacon of possibility for Northern Ghana and beyond: a place where climate-smart agriculture, renewable energy, waste-to-resource innovation, and cultural resilience come together to shape a new rural narrative—one led by communities, for communities.
Anyone interested in joining or collaborating can reach out to us via email or our website. We usually begin with an informal conversation to learn about each other, align intentions, and explore possible synergies. For longer-term engagement, we’re working on a participatory onboarding process that reflects our values of care, inclusion, and shared responsibility.
The Ecovillage Impact Assessment is a tool for communities to track and boost regenerative development at the local scale. Assess, reflect, compare and improve your impact, and see your results live

properties.name: Uba Qynza
properties.description:
Uba Qynza
Uba Qynza is learning community born in Aldeafeliz run by parents, inspired  learning
Setting: Rural Type: Eco Project (Learning Centre)
Status: Established Founded: 2018
Main Language: Spanish Other Languages: English, French
Uba Qynza is learning community in constant co-creation of a living space, cared for and prepared for the respectful development, coexistence, and exploration of girls and boys in a multi-age group between the ages of 5 and 12 years of age. Mothers, fathers and teachers actively participate in the operation of the project and are committed to their ability to grow and evolve in the service of life.
Our name is in the Mhuysqa language, the ancient language of this territory; in English it means Hummingbird Seed. Our purpose is making alive a pedagogy based on Care; care for the seed, care for life, children and the territory we inhabit. This education seeks to accompany children so that they find the deep knowledge of life that lives in  them and help them raise their flight listening to the voice of their own heart and inner guidance.
We were born with and thanks to this support we watch over the learning processes of everyone involved, in an environment nurtured by the right conditions for curiosity, play, learning and the love for life. Today we are an independent community th principles and network.

Uba Qynza seems exactly what we are looking for, however, our Spanish is limited. Would this be an issue? Is it only for fluent Spanish speakers?

Family Matters introduces the mysterious dynamics of power, conflict and love in parent – child relationships. It offers an unique vision and practical practices parents can use immediately with beauty, grace and care that creates healthy personal power for the children and more love in the family. A longtime friend of Upland Hills School, Ted is an integral psychologist, licensed Master Social Worker, author, therapist, mentor, a martial artist and musician. Family Dinner and child care are available for this event. To determine food quantities and child care support please RSVP to for this evening. RSVP is not necessary to attend the presentation only. properties.name: Utopia vida autossuficiente.
properties.description:
Utopia vida autossuficiente.
Setting: Rural Type: Eco Project (Eco Tourism Site, Learning Centre, Other), Holistic Center, Ecovillage (Religious or Spiritual)
Status: Established Founded: 2017
Main Language: Portuguese Other Languages: Japanese, Spanish
Auto introdução: Katsumasa Egoshi
Nasceu no Japão, onde morou até os 20 anos, onde fez diversos cursos nas áreas de Shogi e Terapias Orientais.
Pratica Shogi desde criança se tornando Bicampeão Mundial, anos mais tarde. É instrutor de jogadores profissionais pela Federação Japonesa de Shogi.
Viajou por diversos países (Suíça, Alemanha do Oeste, Dinamarca, Suécia, Noruega, Finlândia, Países Baixos, Bélgica, Reino Unido, Irlanda, França, Áustria, Itália, Checoslováquia, Alemanha Oriental, Polônia, Romênia, Hungria, Jugoslávia, Espanha, Portugal, Grécia, EUA, Canadá, Tailândia China, Hong Kong, Coréia,  Guiana Francesa, Peru, Bolívia, Uruguai, Paraguai, Argentina, Chile), até chegar ao Brasil. No Brasil se dedicou na construção de comunidades autossustentáveis no Estado do Amapá e em Goiás. Também criou uma comunidade no Sul do Chile, onde também fundou a Associação de SHOGI do Chile.Durante todo esse tempo continuou aperfeiçoando sua técnica em terapias orientais. Isso lhe proporcionou mais de 25 anos de experiência em técnicas como ANMA, SHIATSU, SEITAI,REIKI, tornando-se também mestre em REIKI e membro de Associação Brasileira de Terapeutas Holísticos.
Estrutura e objetivos:
Construção de Instituto Cultural Luz Infinita: comunidade autossuficiente.
Procuro pessoas com os seguintes interesses: Aqueles que querem aprender e aplicar atividades culturais (shogi, terapia, música, artes marciais, etc.).
Aqueles que querem alcançar a saúde física e mental.
Aqueles que buscam uma vida autossustentável (fazer casa e comida por conta própria).
Aqueles que querem viver em comunidade.
Aqueles que querem contribuir para a paz mundial.
Membership & Visitors
Current members: 1
Open to new members
Open to visitors
Tenho o sonho de construir uma comunidade autossustentável, voltada para trocas de ideias, compartilhamento de saberes e serviços, em busca de um mundo mais harmonioso. Nosso espaço fica próximo a parques nacionais e estaduais, em uma região de montanhas e trilhas, ideal para conexão com a natureza.
Aqui priorizamos práticas orientais japonesas em grupo, realizamos trabalhos coletivos, alimentações coletivas e vivemos em quartos coletivos. No entanto, caso alguém necessite de mais privacidade, oferecemos alternativas como se estabelecer no galpão, na estrutura octogonal ou ainda optar por acampar em barraca.
Regras de acolhimento:
É essencial respeitar os preceitos e modos orientais, que fundamentam nossa vivência comunitária.
Este é um espaço de aprendizado, cooperação e transformação. Se esse chamado ressoa em você, será uma alegria compartilhar esta experiência.

About HIKARI
Nasceu no Japão, fez diversos cursos nas áreas de Shogi e Terapias Orientais. Pratica Shogi desde criança se tornando Bicampeão Mundial, anos mais tarde. É instrutor de jogadores profissionais pela Federação Japonesa de Shogi. Viajou por diversos países depois Durante todo esse tempo continuou aperfeiçoando sua técnica em terapias orientais. Isso lhe proporcionou mais de 20 anos de experiência, tornando-se também mestre em Reiki e membro de Associação Brasileira de Terapeutas Holísticos.

properties.popup:
Utopia vida autossuficiente.

Utopia vida autossuficiente. Setting: Rural Type: Eco Project (Eco Tourism Site, Learning Centre, Other), Holistic Center, Ecovillage (Religious or Spiritual) Status: Established Founded: 2017 Main Language: Portuguese Other Languages: Japanese, Spanish Auto introdução: Katsumasa Egoshi Nasceu no Japão, onde morou até os 20 anos, onde fez diversos cursos nas áreas de Shogi e Terapias Orientais. Pratica Shogi desde criança se tornando Bicampeão Mundial, anos mais tarde. É instrutor de jogadores profissionais pela Federação Japonesa de Shogi. Viajou por diversos países (Suíça, Alemanha do Oeste, Dinamarca, Suécia, Noruega, Finlândia, Países Baixos, Bélgica, Reino Unido, Irlanda, França, Áustria, Itália, Checoslováquia, Alemanha Oriental, Polônia, Romênia, Hungria, Jugoslávia, Espanha, Portugal, Grécia, EUA, Canadá, Tailândia China, Hong Kong, Coréia,  Guiana Francesa, Peru, Bolívia, Uruguai, Paraguai, Argentina, Chile), até chegar ao Brasil. No Brasil se dedicou na construção de comunidades autossustentáveis no Estado do Amapá e em Goiás. Também criou uma comunidade no Sul do Chile, onde também fundou a Associação de SHOGI do Chile.Durante todo esse tempo continuou aperfeiçoando sua técnica em terapias orientais. Isso lhe proporcionou mais de 25 anos de experiência em técnicas como ANMA, SHIATSU, SEITAI,REIKI, tornando-se também mestre em REIKI e membro de Associação Brasileira de Terapeutas Holísticos. properties.name: VFS Dargeluetz
properties.description: Termine Schenker Dargeluetz Start Blog Termine Aktuelles Vergangene Veranstaltungen Biomeilerworkshop 26.9.- 1.10.2017 Die letzte Septemberwoche steht ganz im Zeichen der sinnvollen regionalen Energiegewinnung und Nutzung. In diesem Workshop bauen wir einen Biomeiler um damit Wärme und Biogas zu gewinnen. Des weiteren werden eine Showerloop und eine Lehmwandheizung installiert um die Wärme zu nutzen sowie eine kleine Gaskochanlage zur direkten Nutzung des hergestellten Biogases. Weitere Informationen und Anmeldung Sommercamp 29.7.2017 - 4.8.2017 Ab dem 29.7. bis zum 4.8.2017 findet in Dargeluetz im direkten Anschluss an das Schenkertreffen das Sommercamp statt. Hier habt Ihr die Gelegenheit Euch in Vorträgen und Workshops theoretisch und praktisch fortzubilden, nette Menschen kennenzulernen und den Sommer in Mecklenburg Vorpommern zu genießen. Das Programm könnt und sollt Ihr dabei mitgestalten. Schenkerbewegungstreffen 22.7. - 29.7.2017 Vom 22.7. - zum 29.7. findet das alljährliche Schenkerbewegungstreffen in Dargeluetz statt. Dort erwarten Euch spannende Vorträge und Workshops zu den Themen , mit denen sich die Mitglieder des Vereins zur Förderung des Schenkens und Angehörige der Schenkerbewegung beschäftigen. Auch Mitgestaltungsmöglichkeiten sind gegeben. Gleichzeitig lädt das Treffen zum Kennenlernen, Vernetzen und schönem Beisammensein ein. Weiter zu Programm und Anmeldung Powered by Create your own unique website with customizable templates. Get Started Start Blog Der VFS e.V. Schenker Dargeluetz Start Blog Der VFS e.V. Der "Verein zur Förderung des Schenkens e.V." möchte dazu beitragen, dass die Menschen wieder lernen, einander zu beschenken und vorhandene Mittel sinnvoll nach Bedürfnissen zu teilen, anstatt gegeneinander zu konkurrieren und durch gewaltsame Beziehungen uns und unsere Umwelt zunehmend zu zerstören. Der VFS unterstützt, dass Menschen diese Werte in ihrem Leben möglichst weitgehend umsetzen können, wie die so genannten Schenkerinnen und Schenke

properties.name: Vestigium
properties.description: ENGLISH | vestigium top of page This website was built on Wix. Create yours today. Get Started ​ Vestigium responds to the need of the community through its members and users… Vestigium Association (lat. footprint) is an ecological-creative association for improving the quality of our community by approaching and promoting a healthy and sustainable way for our future. The idea of founding the association arose in 2011 by six women listening to the needs of their neighbours in creating a multifunctional space where initiatives could take place and develop. The space given by Vestigium should improve the quality of living together as a community and to solve problems such as the growing distance among people, the lack of places to gather and to socialize, the lack of content for children and adults in their lives and the exclusion of families and their children as well older residents. ​ …to give people a space and a platform to help shape their community creatively and freely… The Vrbana area is a typical young district of Zagreb with many residents being families. Services such as a market, a library, a health center and a community center have long been missing. Vestigium provides some of these by holding a farmers market, creating a free library, having poetry evenings, arranging storytelling sessions for the youngest and by organizing lectures and workshops on living a healthy lifestyle, cooking, sewing, relaxing as well as on educational, ecological and eco-creative content. The subject is based on the active input of volunteers and members who want to help shape and support the community to increase the quality of life for their fellow members, their children and themselves. The ethics are reflected in our community supported agriculture initiative which encourages direct relationships between small local organic farmers and city consumers switching to fresh food grown within 60 km. We also promote small ethical local businesses, run workshops on no-dig and urb

properties.name: Villa Monte Reserve
properties.description: Contact - Villa Monte Reserve Skip to content For all reservations and other inquiries please feel free to contact us. info(at)villamonte.com.ar +54 387 4885868 Ruta 6 S/N, Villa Monte, Palma Sola, Jujuy, Argentina (4501) Transport To reach us by public transport you can take a bus or shared taxi from San Salvador de Jujuy or San Pedro de Jujuy to Palma Sola where we can pick you up. Please inquire if you need private transport from Salta or Jujuy. For traveling by own transport please see the map with directions. Acknowledgements Furniture and stained glass by Nadia Khan Map and sketches by Marie Hungler Loading Comments... You must be logged in to post a comment. [Skip to content]( [![Image 4]( # [Villa Monte Reserve]( Menu * [Welcome]( * [About]( * [Conservation]( * [Arts]( * [Stay]( * [Contact]( ![Image 5]( ## Welcome Located in the subtropical northwestern region of Argentina and sprawling over 400 acres of mountainous native forest, Villa Monte Reserve is a serene place in harmony with nature and community. Surrounded by spectacular natural landscapes, wildlife, thermal waters, caves, rivers, springs, waterfalls and archeological sites, Villa Monte Reserve offers an inspiring destination! properties.name: Vía Orgánica
properties.description: Connecting the Dots Between Pollution, Global Hunger, and Water Scarcity - Regeneration International Global hunger, pollution, and water scarcity – how are these interrelated? Courtney White, a former archaeologist and a Sierra Club activist, connects the dots for us in his book Grass, Soil, Hope: A Journey Through Carbon Country. Growing up in Phoenix, Arizona, he first became concerned about some of the environmental issues going on in the 1990s, at which time he joined the Sierra Club. “I met a rancher at a Sierra Club meeting in 1996. His name was Jim Winder… This was back in the mid-’90s when ranchers and environmentalists were going after each other in court, in public opinions, in newspapers, and all kinds of things. Jim said, ‘Let’s find some common ground between ranchers and environmentalists.’ He said, ‘I ranch differently. I move my cows around the ranch in a certain way trying to mimic natural grazing behavior of wild animals – bison, for example. ‘I said, ‘That’s interesting.’ I went to his ranch and saw what he was doing. He’s growing grass. He had water, wildlife, and all these things.” The Quivira Coalition In 1997, the activist and the rancher formed a non-profit organization called The Quivira Coalition, along with conservationist Barbara Johnson. Together, they advocated land management practices that help restore land back to health.One of the keys to land restoration is carbon sequestration. Carbon is the most abundant element on Earth after oxygen. Dark, rich soils contain high amounts of carbon. This element is the tie that binds grazing management, land health, food, water, and rising pollution levels together. Ke spokojenému životu stačí zdravý obytný dům, klidně i mobilní nebo byt s malým, klidně i mini hospodářstvím, což je takový homemarket. Tak máme vše k životu potřebné kolem sebe! Jako zdravé potraviny, lékárnu, ale též zábavu, ... Případně je možná i práce v místě (pobyty, akce, apod.). Prodej podílů apartmánů od 5000€. Možnost postupného odkupu. Apartmány v Tanzánii nebo Zanzibaru více zde... Tvoříme volné společenství se sousedy, chápající výhody soběstačné ... SPOLUJÍZDA 01.09.2025 Jelikož často na semináře jezdíte z dálky, tak je velmi pravděpodobné, že třeba zrovna pojede někdo další od vás :)... Zde se prosím domlouvejte sami na spolujízdě... Stačí když napíšete datum, od kud, kudy, kam a v kolik asi

properties.name: Witchcliffe Ecovillage
properties.description:
Witchcliffe Ecovillage
One of the world's most sustainable residential communities.
Setting: Rural Type: Ecovillage
Status: Established Founded: 2020
Main Language: English
Self-sufficient in renewable water, solar energy and fresh food produce, the Witchcliffe Ecovillage is an innovative, semi-rural community that will eventually be home to more than 700 like-minded people who are motivated to reduce their carbon footprint without compromising comfort, privacy, amenity or affordability.
The unique joint venture development utilises state-of-the-art renewable technologies, regenerative agriculture practices, and passive solar and low carbon building techniques. Importantly, its focus on integrated, people-focused design outcomes will help to create a thriving social community that is inclusive, well-connected and resilient.
The Ecovillage is located in the historic hamlet of Witchcliffe, three hours south of Perth in the mild climate of Western Australia’s beautiful South West. Nestled within the world-famous Margaret River region, which is renowned for premium wine and world-class food, this biodiversity hotspot is embraced by sea, forest and abundant farmland.
The privately-owned project began in 2010 when the 120ha farmland site was first purchased in a 50/50 joint venture partnership between Sustainable Settlements (also the project manager) and long-time business partner Perron Developments.
Our objective  is to create the most sustainable, fully featured Ecovillage possible: a model to demonstrate what can be achieved in a residential subdivision with good planning, strong environmental principles, sensible financial management, technological innovation, and community engagement.
We currently have (December 2024) about 100 residents and another 40+ homes under construction. We are selling lots in our final residential stage (Stage 5) and expect to be fully built out in about 3-5 years.
For more information visit www.ecovillage.net.au
Membership & Visitors
Current members: 60
Open to new members
Open to visitors
Anyone can visit our website and register their interest. They will be kept up to date with the project's progress with newsletters, and informed about future events. To become part of the Ecovillage, future residents will purchase a survey strata lot .
Images & Video
A beautiful and informative overview of the Witchcliffe Ecovillage project.
More
About Michelle Sheridan
Co-founder and Sales Manager of the Witchcliffe Ecovillage
