properties.name: St. James Town Community Co-operative
properties.description: December 6th National Day of Remembrance - St James Town Skip to content Back to all December 6th National Day of Remembrance December 6, 2022 Every year on December 6th, Central Neighbourhood House and SPN members host their annual event for the National Day of Remembrance and Action on Violence Against Women in honor of the 14 women that were murdered during the École Polytechnique massacre in 1989. This year’s virtual event, ‘Shaping our Future Through Allyship and Action: Exploring the financial crisis, mental health, and gender-based violence’, examined the impact of the financial crisis, mental health and gender-based violence that Black, Indigenous, racialized, newcomer women, trans, and non-binary communities have faced during a global pandemic. The event included a panel of professionals and residents that shared their thoughts on the impact of the crisis, and the tools and resources that folks can use in their own life. Host, Wendy De Souza from Metrac opened up the event by introducing the activities for the afternoon, starting with a land acknowledgement read by Armela Marano from TNO followed by an amazing traditional opening from Kevin Myron and youth artists from Counsel Fire. The keynote speaker for the event, Sharlene Henry, shared her experience on how her tenant association was formed and what they did to stop an unfair rental increase in their building. As an advocate for affordable housing and tenant rights, Sharlene encouraged folks to work together as a community to bring change and utilize their strength in numbers. For the second performance of the night, spoken word artist Nasim Asgari shared a powerful and passionate performance with the audience. The event continued with a panel discussion with guest speakers from various backgrounds who shared their thoughts on how BIPOC communities have been unfairly impacted by the economic crisis. The discussion was moderated by Walid Khogali Ali with panel speakers Premila Chellapermal from Women Abu

Something always needs fixing on a farm. It’s… [Read More] After many years of service, it was time to retire our 1996 Dodge Ram – held lovingly together well past its normal life with lots of duct tape, love and bondo by Peter, our stalwart truck point person. Thanks to Peter and Haynes for all of the searching, calling, visiting, and test driving, we now… [Read More] They slid down Woodchip Mountain last week, but the snow’s gone for now so it’s just a pile of woodchips again! Before we know it they will just be stepping stones, for now they are boulders to climb. David, Tes and I tapped three trees last Sunday. We kept it simple, old school – brace and bit (manual drill), metal spiles (spouts), and nice old maple sap buckets with “hats” cut from sheet metal. We used snowshoes to get to the trees since there was deep and crusty snow. It was barely sunny… [Read More] This is not a painting! We are so grateful to live in this beautiful spot on the planet. properties.description: Cookie Policy – WordPress.org Skip to content WordPress.org Privacy Cookie Policy Cookie Policy Our Privacy Policy explains our principles when it comes to the collection, processing, and storage of your information. The Cookie Policy specifically explains how we, our partners, and users of our services deploy cookies, as well as the options you have to control them. What are cookies? Cookies are small pieces of data, stored in text files, that are stored on your computer or other device when websites are loaded in a browser. They are widely used to ‘remember’ you and your preferences, either for a single visit (through a ‘session cookie’) or for multiple repeat visits (using a ‘persistent cookie’). They ensure a consistent and efficient experience for visitors, and perform essential functions such as allowing users to register and remain logged in. Cookies may be set by the site that you are visiting (known as ‘first party cookies’), or by third parties, such as those who serve content or provide advertising or analytics services on the website (‘third party cookies’). Cookies set by WordPress.org We use cookies for a number of different purposes. Some cookies are necessary for technical reasons; some enable a personalized experience for both visitors and registered users; and some allow the display of advertising from selected third party networks. Some of these cookies may be set when a page is loaded, or when a visitor takes a particular action (clicking the ‘like’ or ‘follow’ button on a post, for example). Below the different categories of cookies set by WordPress.org are outlined, with specific examples detailed in the tables that follow. This includes their name and purpose. Certain cookies are only set for logged-in visitors, whereas others are set for any visitors, and these are marked below accordingly. Where a cookie only applies to specific subdomains, they are included under the relevant header. Strictly Necessary : These are the cookies that are essen

properties.name: The Climate Emergency Mobilization Task Force
properties.description: New Home CLIMATE ACTION BEGINS AT HOME Climate Emergency Mobilization Task Force We are the Climate Emergency Mobilization Task Force (CEMTF), a Bay Area coalition of elected officials, city & county staff, community based and nongovernmental organizations, youth, environmental and social activists, and front line communities addressing inequities and causes of the climate emergency. Learn More DONATE See All Events Featured CEMTF 7th Virtual Summit Series: 3RD F R I D A Y S • 9 A M - N 0 0 N JULY 2026 - MARCH 2027 ‍ (bimonthly) REGISTER TODAY! ‍ -------------------------------------------------------------- ‍ JULY 17th: Climate & Global Indigenous Leadership Take 2 SEPTEMBER 18th: Climate & Fossil Fuels ‍ ‍ ‍ NOVEMBER 20th: Climate & War ‍ ‍ JANUARY 15th: Climate & Activism ‍ MARCH 19th: Climate & You Our Impact CEMTF has worked to build a coalition with the civic will and power to move the Bay Area together to a just, regenerative economy that can not only meet, but exceed the carbon mitigation standards set by the IPCC. Education 2000+ Organizations Over the years our education-action virtual summit series have had over two thousand unique organizational participants. Convening Partners Participants from 75% of Bay Area Cities Some of the CEMTF Virtual Summit Series had participants from the 101 Bay Area cities. As well as participants from across the United States, Africa, Australia, Canada, Lebanon, Nepal, Norway, Peru, the UK, Spain, Turkey, and beyond. Did You Know? Legislation #3 in the US, #6 in the World CEMTF was founded to continue the work of the ad-hoc subcommittee that was formed after the Climate Emergency Declaration passed in Berkeley, CA. Berkeley's Declaration on June 12, 2018 made it the 3rd jurisdiction in the United States and 6th in the world to declare. As of today, there are 2,366 jurisdictions that have declared Climate Emergencies around the world. The City of Berkeley's electrification ordinance for new construction was the brainchild of

properties.name: The School of Lost Borders
properties.description: Winter 2018 – Solstice – School of Lost Borders Skip to content Winter 2018 – Solstice Newsletters Solstice. The Owen’s Valley is cold and quiet. The sun will set at 4:47pm this afternoon, but it will already sink behind the crest of the mighty Sierra Nevada Mountains around 3:30pm and quicken the pace of those still venturing about on the trail. The cold has a way of coming on fast once that sun is gone. All of our guides are done with their trips for this year and home with their loved ones now, the last three programs having just concluded last month in Death Valley. A couple of days ago, we submitted the final of three permits for operating our 2019 programs. Winter admin stuff. Insurance renewals are looming and year-end financials are next as we prepare for our annual board meeting in January. View This Issue. Recent Issues: Fall Newsletter 2020 Transitions – A Letter from Petra Lentz-Snow Summer 2020 – Solidarity Spring 2020 – Threshold Fall 2019 – The Power of Seeds Summer 2019 – In Celebration of Summer Check out "Peregrinations", our quarterly bulletin full of news, course info, stories from the field and more. properties.name: The Sustainability Institute and Lynedoch Eco-Village
properties.description: Taking a closer look at a sustainable food basket - Sustainability Institute Taking a closer look at a sustainable food basket Sep 5, 2019 The Sustainability Institute was invited to exhibit at the 2019 WWF Living Planet Conference. This annual conference provides the opportunity for various stakeholders to come together around a specific topic. This year’s theme, Nourish, placed the focus on food and food systems, and various exhibitors from these fields attended. Farmers, chefs, civil society, scientists, government and the private sector shared their knowledge, experiences and built partnerships in ‘Nourishing the Nation’. As stated by WWF, the conference comes at a time where most of humanity has lost its sense of connection to one of the greatest sources of human nourishment and planetary health – our food choices. “As more South Africans move to cities and their income increases, so do their diets change. People eat more meat and dairy, waste more and over-rely on packaged and processed food. Yet on the other hand, South Africa needs to produce 50% more food by 2050 in order to address the biggest challenges of undernutrition, over-nutrition and environmental degradation. This is expected to be done without transformative change happening in our food system, while climate change continues to affect food production”. In this regard the aim of the conference was to explore different facets of regenerative food systems that can provide food for all. As the SI, this provided a unique opportunity to bring together students from our different academic programmes to co-create an insightful and interactive exhibition for the conference. The team composed of Diploma students Megan Hale and Khensani Nkatingi, Postgraduate Diploma students Lauren Bauer, and Paige Garbutt and MPhil student Anne Gacheri. They worked together with SI staff members Rirhandzu Marivate, Vanessa von der Heyde and Amelia Pretorius. The team looked at what it means to have a sustainable food bask

properties.name: UnaVision Initiative
properties.description: ## UnaVison Übersicht In einer sich rasch verändernden Welt ist die Fähigkeit zu schneller evidenzbasierter Reaktion und zur Anpassung an neue Situationen zunehmend wichtig. Dafür bildet die **UnaVision** Initiative selbstlernende Strukturen innerhalb eines globalen Netzwerks und lädt Sie ein, mitzumachen bei der Gestaltung eines Systems aus Prototypen für gutes Leben, die eine vielfältige globale Gesellschaft respektieren sowie die komplexe Biosphäre unseres Planeten. Unsere Vision für ein erfülltes Leben erwächst aus einem systematischen Testen, Verbessern, Anpassen und Erweitern des Systems aus **UnaVillage** Regionen in ländlichen Gebieten. Dabei haben wir eine globale solidarisch teilende Gemeinschaft vor Augen. Die **UnaVersity** unterstützt diese Vision durch einen systematischen transformativen Lernprozess, innovative Lernansätze und den internationalen Austausch von Erfahrungen und Kompetenzen. Wir übernehmen Verantwortung für die Würde von Menschen verschiedener Geschlechter, Altersgruppen, Bildungshintergründe, Rassen und Glaubensrichtung und den Schutz und die Regeneration von Umwelt und Natur. UnaVillages und das UnaVision Netzwerk arbeiten mit einem soziokratischen System. * **UnaVision** steht für ein wachsendes globales solidarisches Netzwerk aus Menschen, die aktiv an der Verwirklichung der Vision arbeiten, ein gutes Leben für alle Menschen und künftige Generationen zu ermöglichen, sowie UnaVillage Regionen zu unterstützen und UnaVersity Angebote einzurichten. * **UnaVillage** Regionen sind Prototypen anpassungsfähigen nachhaltigen Lebens mit gemeinsamem Leben, Lernen und Arbeiten für ein erfülltes Leben. * **UnaVersity** bietet transformative Lernprozesse und innovative Ansätze zum Lernen und Forschen über nachhaltige Lebensstile und veröffentlicht die Ergebnisse. * [![Image 1](blob: ![Image 2]( ### Inhalt Wir streben an, für alle Mitglieder der UnaVillage Gemeinschaft den Zugang zu bezahlbaren gesunden Lebensmitteln zu gewährleisten sowie Unterkun

They ensure a consistent and efficient experience for visitors, and perform essential functions such as allowing users to register and remain logged in. Cookies may be set by the site that you are visiting (known as ‘first party cookies’), or by third parties, such as those who serve content or provide advertising or analytics services on the website (‘third party cookies’). ## Cookies set by WordPress.org We use cookies for a number of different purposes. properties.name: Universidad del Medio Ambiente (UMA)
properties.description: First Mini Coach Pavilion will debut at Motorcoach EXPO in Nashville | United Motorcoach Association First Mini Coach Pavilion will debut at Motorcoach EXPO in Nashville by Kris Wysong | Aug 7, 2019 | News | 0 comments (Alexandria, VA | August 7, 2019) The market for mini buses and executive coaches remains strong, according to a recent survey conducted by the United Motorcoach Association. Sixty-seven percent of private motorcoach operators responding to a survey about their fleet composition indicated that they own such vehicles, and that they plan to purchase more of them within the next 12 months. Responding to this trend, the 2020 UMA Motorcoach EXPO , January 19-23, in Nashville, will feature an all new Mini Coach Pavilion on the trade show floor. The Mini Coach Pavilion will allow motorcoach operators to fully explore the most innovative offerings in this vehicle class as they seek to expand, diversify and upgrade their fleets. Motorcoach EXPO is the largest private motorcoach trade show in North America, showcasing the broadest collection of full- and mid-sized coaches under one roof, along with the most innovative products and services for the industry. “UMA Motorcoach EXPO is where private bus and motorcoach operators go to see and learn about the most innovative equipment each year as they prepare to upgrade, diversify and maintain their fleets,” according to Stacy Tetschner, President & CEO of the United Motorcoach Association. “Adding the Mini Coach Pavilion will empower operators to see even more vehicle options in one place. Mini buses and executive coaches allow operators to give the modern consumer flexibility in vehicle capacity and the latest onboard solutions for comfort, efficiency, and technology.” More than 90 percent of attendees at the UMA show are decisionmakers who plan major purchases for additions to their fleet in the coming year, according to a survey of 2019 EXPO operator attendees. In that same survey, the average attendee at the 201

properties.name: Society for Alternative Learning and Transformation
properties.description: ![Image 1: saltLogo]( * [Home]( * [About us]( * [Where we work]( * [Our Contribution]( * [News & Articles]( * [Gallery]( * [Get Involved]( Menu ## We are the salt of the PLANET Earth ### Among the indigenous peoples and local communities, salt has a deep socio-cultural, ecological and spiritual meaning. Salt plays a great role in ritual purity through cleansing and restoring harmonious relationship between people and Planet Earth. The world today needs the same salt to bring meaning to life and to re-connect the humans with true sense of who they are. We are the salt; the Society for Alternative Learning & Transformation (SALT) - Simon Mitambo, CEO, SALT. ## "...natural phenomenon have slowly lost their symbolic implications. Thunder is no longer the voice of an angry god, nor is lightening his avenging missile. No river contains a spirit, no trees contain the life principle, no snake contains the embodiment of wisdom, no mountain cave is the home of the great demon. No voices now speak to man from stones, plants and animals, nor does the man speak to them believing they can hear. Man's contact with nature has gone, and with it has gone the profound emotional energy that this symbolic connection supplied.” ## ~ JAMES HOLLIS ### Vision SALT envisages resilient communities living in harmony with Mother Nature and conscious of their inherent rights to self-determination in decision making. ### Mission SALT engages communities using diverse nature-based experiential learning methodologies to interrogate mind-sets with regards to depths and transformation. ### Core Values * SALT is an organization that supports and builds relationships and links indigenous peoples and local communities that share a common vision and values. * SALT derives its strength and orientation from diverse cultures and the natural world and has great respect for the rights of nature and dignity of humans. * SALT values reflections, experiential learning, creativity and innovations and transformati

No river contains a spirit, no trees contain the life principle, no snake contains the embodiment of wisdom, no mountain cave is the home of the great demon. Man's contact with nature has gone, and with it has gone the profound emotional energy that this symbolic connection supplied.” ## ~ JAMES HOLLIS ### Vision SALT envisages resilient communities living in harmony with Mother Nature and conscious of their inherent rights to self-determination in decision making. ### Mission SALT engages communities using diverse nature-based experiential learning methodologies to interrogate mind-sets with regards to depths and transformation. ### Core Values * SALT is an organization that supports and builds relationships and links indigenous peoples and local communities that share a common vision and values. * SALT derives its strength and orientation from diverse cultures and the natural world and has great respect for the rights of nature and dignity of humans. * SALT values reflections, experiential learning, creativity and innovations and transformati

properties.name: Kurdish Movement
properties.description: Following the uprising led by the Kurdistan Workers' Party (PKK) in 1978, the foundation for clandestine self-governance was built in rural areas and towns in the 1980s and 90s. Actually, the building process of the PKK in itself was based on practicing radical democracy within its own structures and relations. This became a source of inspiration and an example for the self-organising of the Kurdish society. The ways of how to organise collective life and economy, how to develop the political struggle and education, how to realise socialist values, friendship, solidarity, respect towards human and nature in one’s own personality and relations where central points of discussions and establishing a revolutionary culture – at first among the guerrilla on the mountains, political prisoners and activists. ![Image 1]( 2]( 3]( 4]( ![Image 5]( ![Image 6]( [+]( "Zoom in")[−]( "Zoom out")[]( "Full Screen") [Leaflet]( "A JS library for interactive maps") | © [OpenStreetMap]( contributors © [CARTO]( Kurdistan, , , Kenya | Author(s) | Jineology Academy | | --- | | Topics | [Democracy]( "Category:Democracy"), [Education]( "Category:Education") | | Case Report | [Radical Democracy: recovering the roots of self-governance & autonomy]( "Report:Radical Democracy: recovering the roots of self-governance & autonomy") | Kurdistan is located at the center of the Fertile Crescent, which has been home to some of the earliest human civilizations, agricultural and village revolutions since 10,000 BC. It is a region enjoying all four seasons and having rich botanical diversity and fertile lands. Kurds, Assyrians, Syriacs, Armenians and Arabs have lived together in Kurdistan for years. Kurdish Movement

Following the uprising led by the Kurdistan Workers' Party (PKK) in 1978, the foundation for clandestine self-governance was built in rural areas and towns in the 1980s and 90s. Actually, the building process of the PKK in itself was based on practicing radical democracy within its own structures and relations. This became a source of inspiration and an example for the self-organising of the Kurdish society. The ways of how to organise collective life and economy, how to develop the political struggle and education, how to realise socialist values, friendship, solidarity, respect towards human and nature in one’s own personality and relations where central points of discussions and establishing a revolutionary culture – at first among the guerrilla on the mountains, political prisoners and activists. ![Image 1]( 2]( 3]( 4]( ![Image 5]( ![Image 6]( [+]( "Zoom in")[−]( "Zoom out")[]( "Full Screen") [Leaflet]( "A JS library for interactive maps") | © [OpenStreetMap]( contributors © [CARTO]( Kurdistan, , , Kenya | Author(s) | Jineology Academy | | --- | | Topics | [Democracy]( "Category:Democracy"), [Education]( "Category:Education") | | Case Report | [Radical Democracy: recovering the roots of self-governance & autonomy]( "Report:Radical Democracy: recovering the roots of self-governance & autonomy") | Kurdistan is located at the center of the Fertile Crescent, which has been home to some of the earliest human civilizations, agricultural and village revolutions since 10,000 BC. properties.name: Endorser:Australian Earth Laws Alliance
properties.description: Endorser Australian Earth Laws Alliance - AltMap Beta Endorser Australian Earth Laws Alliance From AltMap Beta Jump to: navigation , search AELA is a not-for-profit organisation working to increase the understanding and practical implementation of Earth-centred (ecocentric) governance, with a focus on systems change across law, economics, education, ethics and community participation in Australia. Our vision is an Australian society that embraces an ecocentric or ‘life-centred’ culture, with governance systems that enable human communities to thrive within ecological boundaries, while nurturing biodiversity and ecosystem health. Our programs provide a range of services and support for people, communities and organisations working to create ecocentric systems change. properties.name: Endorser:Civil Diplomacy Center - North and East Syria
properties.description: The Civil Diplomacy Center aims to promote solidarity and cooperation between the region of North and East Syria and the rest of the world, in all fields, on the basis of democratic values. ![Image 1]( 2]( 3]( 4]( ![Image 5]( ![Image 6]( [+]( "Zoom in")[−]( "Zoom out")[]( "Full Screen") [Leaflet]( "A JS library for interactive maps") | © [OpenStreetMap]( contributors © [CARTO]( Qamishlo, Syria, , , Syrian Arab Republic | Creation date | 2021 | | --- | | Topics / Areas | [Agroecology]( "Category:Agroecology"), [Decolonization]( "Category:Decolonization"), [Democracy]( "Category:Democracy"), [Economy]( "Category:Economy"), [Education]( "Category:Education"), [Gender]( "Category:Gender"), [Health]( "Category:Health"), [Social change]( "Category:Social change"), [Social justice]( "Category:Social justice") | | Languages | Arabic | | Funding models | Donations | | Type of organization | Network | | Representative's name | [Issa](mailto:civil.dip@nesyria.com) | In parallel with the fight against Daesh and other jihadist factions, the communities (Kurds, Arabs, Christians, Yazidis) of North and East Syria (NES) have formed an autonomous region based on an alternative political project having pillars such as direct democracy, the emancipation of women, ecology, and the inclusion of all ethnic and religious components of society. The Center for Civil Diplomacy of North and East Syria (CDC-NES) has set itself the task of strengthening and expanding relations at an international level between NES and societies abroad. Our center offers itself as an intermediary to facilitate and assist the implementation of cooperation and solidarity projects with NES. The CDC-NES began its activity in January 2021. It is a non-profit organization based in Qamishlo, in North East Syria. The areas of cooperation and solidarity that the CDC-NES intends to promote are: political solidarity; humanitarian aid; education; Women's rights; restorative justice; economy, ecology, culture; health; munici

Officially recognized as a not-for-profit organization in August 2003 under the law Number 1 of Charitable Societies and Non-governmental Associations, 2000, by the Ministry of Interior of the Palestinian National Authority, Alrowwad holds license number BL-360-CU. Additionally, it obtained license number 239/B/100/2009 from the Ministry of Culture. Catering to children, youth, and women, Alrowwad is deeply embedded within the local community. properties.name: Endorser:The Convivialists
properties.description: Convivialism, a resolutely relational philosophical approach to the world, sets out 5 principles and an imperative to guide the reconstruction of our societies. The manifesto presents them and gives directions in which to move towards a post-neoliberal world. If liberalism has brought freedom to flourish, and modernization, convivialism will bring conviviality, individual and collective emancipation. It is signed by 275 personalities from 33 countries: a nascent Convivialist international. ![Image 1]( 2]( 3]( 4]( ![Image 5]( ![Image 6]( [+]( "Zoom in")[−]( "Zoom out")[]( "Full Screen") [Leaflet]( "A JS library for interactive maps") | © [OpenStreetMap]( contributors © [CARTO]( Association des Convivialistes, Bât. properties.name: Indigenous Biocultures and Rights of Nature in Uganda - an Interview
properties.description: The interview with Dennis Tabaro outlines how AFRICE works with indigenous communities in Uganda—particularly the Bagungu—to revive customary governance and protect Sacred Natural Sites (SNS) as a foundation for the Rights of Nature. Tabaro explains that Rights of Nature are inseparable from human rights, emerging through harmonious coexistence with ecosystems that have their own right to regenerate and thrive. Beginning in 2013, AFRICE built trust with custodians who had long been marginalized, supporting them in documenting ancestral knowledge, mapping ecosystems, restoring ritual practices, and protecting indigenous seeds. Their efforts helped inspire Uganda’s first Rights of Nature law and later a district-level ordinance recognizing customary laws for SNS protection. Yet implementation remains challenged by colonial legacies, stigma against Indigenous spirituality, lack of government regulation, and destructive industrial agriculture. Despite this, Bagungu custodians continue strengthening community-led ecological governance, hoping legal recognition will fully protect their sacred landscapes and allow future generations to inherit intact biocultural systems. ![Image 1]( 2]( 3]( 4]( 5]( 6]( ![Image 7]( ![Image 8]( [+]( "Zoom in")[−]( "Zoom out")[]( "Full Screen") [Leaflet]( "A JS library for interactive maps") | © [OpenStreetMap]( contributors © [CARTO]( Lake Albert in the Albertine Rift (Buliisa, Hoima, Masindi districts), , , Uganda | Author(s) | Dennis Tabaro; Imke Horstmannshoff | | --- | | Topics | [Ancestral Knowledge]( "Category:Ancestral Knowledge"), [Commons]( "Category:Commons"), [Earth jurisprudence]( "Category:Earth jurisprudence") | | Case Report | [Radical Democracy: recovering the roots of self-governance & autonomy]( "Report:Radical Democracy: recovering the roots of self-governance & autonomy") | **Dennis Tabaro interviwed by Imke Horstmannshoff (IH)** ## IH: What is your own and AFRICE' vision of Rights of Nature? Rights of Nature are inextric
properties.popup:
Indigenous Biocultures and Rights of Nature in Uganda - an Interview

The interview with Dennis Tabaro outlines how AFRICE works with indigenous communities in Uganda—particularly the Bagungu—to revive customary governance and protect Sacred Natural Sites (SNS) as a foundation for the Rights of Nature. Tabaro explains that Rights of Nature are inseparable from human rights, emerging through harmonious coexistence with ecosystems that have their own right to regenerate and thrive. Beginning in 2013, AFRICE built trust with custodians who had long been marginalized, supporting them in documenting ancestral knowledge, mapping ecosystems, restoring ritual practices, and protecting indigenous seeds. Their efforts helped inspire Uganda’s first Rights of Nature law and later a district-level ordinance recognizing customary laws for SNS protection. Yet implementation remains challenged by colonial legacies, stigma against Indigenous spirituality, lack of government regulation, and destructive industrial agriculture. Despite this, Bagungu custodians continue strengthening community-led ecological governance, hoping legal recognition will fully protect their sacred landscapes and allow future generations to inherit intact biocultural systems. ![Image 1]( 2]( 3]( 4]( 5]( 6]( ![Image 7]( ![Image 8]( [+]( "Zoom in")[−]( "Zoom out")[]( "Full Screen") [Leaflet]( "A JS library for interactive maps") | © [OpenStreetMap]( contributors © [CARTO]( Lake Albert in the Albertine Rift (Buliisa, Hoima, Masindi districts), , , Uganda | Author(s) | Dennis Tabaro; Imke Horstmannshoff | | --- | | Topics | [Ancestral Knowledge]( "Category:Ancestral Knowledge"), [Commons]( "Category:Commons"), [Earth jurisprudence]( "Category:Earth jurisprudence") | | Case Report | [Radical Democracy: recovering the roots of self-governance & autonomy]( "Report:Radical Democracy: recovering the roots of self-governance & autonomy") | **Dennis Tabaro interviwed by Imke Horstmannshoff (IH)** ## IH: What is your own and AFRICE' vision of Rights of Nature? Rights of Nature are inextric

properties.name: Endorser:NOW NET
properties.description: We in the Network Economic Transformation (NOW - NET or NOW NET for short) advocate an economy that serves the good life of all. Everyone is welcome to advance this vision. Many of us have been active in various alternative economic movements for a long time. Endorser:International Tribunal on Rights of Nature

The Tribunal aims to create a forum for people from all around the world to speak on behalf of nature, to protest the destruction of the Earth, destruction that is often sanctioned by governments and corporations, and to make recommendations about Earth’s protection and restoration. The Tribunal also has a strong focus on enabling Indigenous Peoples to share their unique concerns and solutions about land, water, and culture with the global community. ![Image 1]( 2]( 3]( 4]( ![Image 5]( ![Image 6]( [+]( "Zoom in")[−]( "Zoom out")[]( "Full Screen") [Leaflet]( "A JS library for interactive maps") | © [OpenStreetMap]( contributors © [CARTO]( , Quito, , Ecuador | Creation date | 2020 | | --- | | Topics / Areas | [Environmental justice]( "Category:Environmental justice"), [Rights]( "Category:Rights"), [Social justice]( "Category:Social justice") | | Languages | English, Spanish; Castilian | | Type of organization | NGO | | Representative's name | [Francesco Martone](mailto:francescomartone1@gmail.com) |

properties.name: Endorser:Symbiosis
properties.description: Endorser Symbiosis - AltMap Beta Endorser Symbiosis From AltMap Beta Jump to: navigation , search Symbiosis is a network of organizers and activist-researchers across North America dedicated to building community-based, popular power. Participants consist of organizations as well as individuals who work toward building a free, egalitarian, and ecological society from the ground up. We organize in neighborhoods, develop movement resources and materials, and help new projects get off the ground. We are working to create a continental confederation of radically democratic, community-based municipalist institutions. properties.name: Endorser:Global Diversity Foundation
properties.description: Endorser Global Diversity Foundation - AltMap Beta Endorser Global Diversity Foundation From AltMap Beta Jump to: navigation , search Since 2000, Global Diversity Foundation has carried out vibrant applied collaborative research programmes with Indigenous communities in Mesoamerica, North Africa, Southeast Asia and southern Africa. In collaboration with local and national institutions, we provide training and advocacy to support communities as they design and implement their own initiatives to maintain local environments and enhance their livelihoods and wellbeing. Our areas of focus depend on community interests and priorities. To date, we have worked on community-based biocultural diversity conservation, sustainable livelihoods, community access to lands and resources, the continuity of ethnobiological knowledge, community health and wellbeing. properties.name: Cooperation Jackson
properties.description: ![Image 1: In Ghana, West Africa, the Asante people use wisdom symbols known as adinkra to recall the virtues and values of traditional life. Our Cooperation Jackson logo incorporates three adinkra: At the center is Boa Me Na Me Mmoa Wo, or “Help me and let me…]( In Ghana, West Africa, the Asante people use wisdom symbols known as _adinkra_ to recall the virtues and values of traditional life. Our **_Cooperation Jackson_** logo incorporates three adinkra: At the center is _Boa Me Na Me Mmoa Wo,_ or “Help me and let me help you,” the symbol of cooperation and interdependence. On the left is _Nkonsonkonson,_ a reminder to contribute to the community, that “In unity lies strength.” Finally on the right is _Wo nsa da mu a,_“If your hands are in the dish, people do not eat everything and leave you nothing,” which explains the importance of participation in self-government. ## **_Cooperation Jackson_** is an emerging vehicle for sustainable community development, economic democracy, and community ownership. Our long term vision is to develop a cooperative network based in Jackson, Mississippi that will consist of four interconnected and interdependent institutions: a federation of local worker cooperatives, a cooperative incubator, a cooperative education and training center (the Kuwasi Balagoon Center for Economic Democracy and Development), and a cooperative bank or financial institution. Cooperation Jackson’s basic theory of change is centered on the position that organizing and empowering the structurally under and unemployed sectors of the working class, particularly from Black and Latino communities, to build worker organized and owned cooperatives will be a catalyst for the democratization of our economy and society overall. Cooperation Jackson believes that we can replace the current socio-economic system of exploitation, exclusion and the destruction of the environment with a proven democratic alternative. An alternative built on equity, cooperation, worker democ

properties.name: Place-based, Earth-centred governance in Australia
properties.description: Case Place-based, Earth-centred governance in Australia - AltMap Beta Case Place-based, Earth-centred governance in Australia From AltMap Beta Jump to: navigation , search This text explores how rising ecological and social crises are prompting renewed interest in bioregional and Indigenous governance in Australia, highlighting collaborations between Future Dreaming and the Australian Earth Laws Alliance to promote ecocentric, place-based approaches. It traces the roots of Western bioregionalism and contrasts it with the continent’s far older Aboriginal governance systems, which for over 65,000 years have been grounded in relationality, “First Laws” of caring for Country, and non-hierarchical community structures shaped by specific ecosystems. The authors argue that these Indigenous knowledge systems offer vital guidance for reimagining governance today, and they introduce AELA’s “Greenprints” framework as a way for Western practitioners to connect ecological limits, cultural understanding, and social justice within bioregional boundaries. Together, these approaches aim to reshape modern governance toward Earth-centred, sustainable, and culturally grounded ways of living. properties.name: Hin Lad Nai
properties.description: Hin Lad Nai is an Indigenous community of Pgakenyaw people living in Northern Thailand. They’re made up of about twenty households living in a forest village which was first constructed in the 1960s (although the Hin Lad Nai people have lived throughout the region for much longer). Their way of life is deeply connected with the forest, as they sustain themselves almost entirely from the plants they cultivate within it. ![Image 1]( 2]( 3]( 4]( 5]( 6]( ![Image 7]( ![Image 8]( [+]( "Zoom in")[−]( "Zoom out")[]( "Full Screen") [Leaflet]( "A JS library for interactive maps") | © [OpenStreetMap]( contributors © [CARTO]( , Hin Lad Nai, Chiang Rai, Thailand | Author(s) | Thea Walmsley | | --- | | Topics | [Ancestral Knowledge]( "Category:Ancestral Knowledge"), [Commons]( "Category:Commons"), [Earth jurisprudence]( "Category:Earth jurisprudence"), [Environmental justice]( "Category:Environmental justice") | | Case Report | [Volume 1: "Stories of Regeneration and Resilience"]( "Report:Volume 1: \"Stories of Regeneration and Resilience\"") | [![Image 9]( Photo Credits: Akanksha Bhushan A few weeks ago, I had the privilege of spending a couple of days with an Indigenous hillside tribe here in Thailand called Hin Lad Nai, and I wanted to share some of that story with you today. It’s the story of a small community who, when faced with a multitude of challenges to their land and food sovereignty, held steadfast to their culture and their traditional land management practices. Through years of resistance and advocacy, they have managed to not only stay on their land, but have significantly influenced both domestic and foreign policy on forest management biodiversity. Hin Lad Nai is an Indigenous community of Pgakenyaw people living in Northern Thailand. Their way of life is deeply connected

properties.name: Ecovillage Finder
properties.description: Blog - EcovillageFinder Zum Inhalt springen Blog Ökodorf in Europa und Weltweit, suchen und finden Ich zeige euch die Internetseiten und wie ihr Gemeinschaften in Deutschland, Europa und der Welt findet. Es gibt nicht nur alte große Ökodörfer wie Weiterlesen Reisen, von der Pauschalreise zur Entspannung Hier möchte ich nicht darauf eingehen, den Job zu wechseln, sondern Urlaub anders zu gestalten. Bei mir war es die Winterverkürzung im Frühling und Herbst Weiterlesen Gemeinschaften im Vergleich Nach so vielen Reisen in Gemeinschaften, endlich der Vergleich. Was ist in welchen los. In großen etablierten, gibt es oft viel Seminar-betrieb, einen eigenen Weiterlesen Sunny Hill in Istrien, Slovenien den Hügeln von Istrien liegt Sunny Hill. Im letzte Ort vor der kroatischen Grenze. Die Mensch hier, sind sehr Erfahrung in Gemeinschaft und mit freiwilligen Helfern. Sie fragen nach den Gefühlen und Weiterlesen Gaia Terra bei Udine, Italien Schön gelegen, an einem kleinen Ort im Gebiet Udine. Die haben einen großen Garten, wo sie auch Workshops anbieten. Es gib eine Wasserreinigungsanlage, deshalb Weiterlesen New Earth Tribes bei Koblenz Das tiefe Gefühl glücklich da zu sein, im nahen miteinander ist die Kernbotschaft hier. Wenn Trigger kommen Weiterlesen Biohof, Hart 7, Österreich Eine Ebene zwischen den Bergen in Südösterreich. Nicht weit von Klagenfurt und Völkermarkt. Das schöne ist, hier sind kleine Kinder und Tiere. Wer das mag, Weiterlesen Gemeinschaftswerkzeuge, wie wir zusammenwachsen Diese Übersicht ist der Schlüssel für tiefe Verbindungen in Beziehungen. Ob neue Gruppen/Gemeinschaften gründen, oder ob etablierte sich weiter entwickeln. Wenn du diese Werkzeuge Weiterlesen Oase in Paraguay Hier ein Projekt, wo ich nicht vor Ort war. Die haben ein Video erstellt und ich gebe die Informationen weiter. Sie möchte Gleichgesinnte die Weiterlesen Gemeinschaft um sich selbst aufbauen Wer nicht gleich in eine Gemeinschaft ziehen möchte, fängt zu Hause an. Je mehr du die alte

properties.name: Nachbarschaftshilfe Immenstadt/Oberallgäu
properties.description: Komplettlösung für Haushalts- und Geschäftsauflösungen Allgäu - Neuland - Umziehen | Renovieren | Entsorgen im Allgäu umziehen | renovieren | entsorgen Grias di einfach leicht umziehen, renovieren, entsorgen Herzlich Willkommen bei NEULAND Sie wollen einen Haushalt oder ein Geschäft auflösen, renovieren oder umziehen? Dann sind Sie bei uns richtig! Als Meisterbetrieb bietet NEULAND Komplettlösungen für Wohnraumveränderung und nachhaltige Dienstleistungen rund um Haus und Wohnen in Immenstadt und im ganzen Allgäu. Haushalts- und Geschäftsauflösungen, Umzüge, Malerarbeiten & Innenraumgestaltung, Bodenlegearbeiten, Sanitär- und Elektroinstallationen in Kooperation mit unseren lokalen Handwerkspartnern. > > zu unseren Leistungen! So einfach gehts: 1. Anrufen, Situation und Auftrag beschreiben 2. Ablauf besprechen, beraten lassen und Auftrag erteilen 3. Freuen Sie sich über ein hervorragendes Ergebnis zum gewünschten Termin! Wir freuen uns auf Ihre Nachricht! Tel. 08323 709 91 95 oder Mobil 0174 625 89 75 [email protected] Komplettlösungen für Wohnraumveränderung im Allgäu Als Meisterbetrieb beraten und unterstützen wir Sie bei komplexen Aufträgen und sichern mit unseren regionalen Partnern die Qualität der fachlichen Ausführung und Abläufe. Wir nehmen uns Zeit, hören Ihnen zu, beraten Sie und planen dann gemeinsam mit Ihnen die nächsten Schritte. Eine gute Zusammenarbeit, Qualität, Zuverlässigkeit und die Fertigstellung der Leistung zum fixen Termin ist uns dabei wichtig! Vereinbaren Sie einen Besichtigungstermin mit uns, um den Umfang des Auftrags und die Kosten zu besprechen. Selbstverständlich arbeiten wir mit 100%iger Rücksicht und Diskretion. Was immer Ihre Idee ist, wir bieten Ihnen so viel Unterstützung wie Sie brauchen, um sie zu verwirklichen und setzen Ihre ganz individuellen Wünsche um. Nehmen Sie Kontakt auf! Wir brauchen Verstärkung! Das NEULAND Team sucht Dich: • Maler m/w/d • Mitarbeiter Haushaltsauflösungen m/w/d . . zum Stellenangebot Büro Öffnungszeite

properties.description: Transition Town Jericho - Contact Transition Town Jericho Contact About Events Blog Food Hub Initiative Transition Town Jericho ​Steering Committee Members Laura Markowitz Guest speakers, Website Laura, [email protected] Dave Clift Kids Garden Contest Extra Row project ​Dave, [email protected] Adam Wechsler Topics in Resiliency and Transition [email protected] Jeff Moskovics [email protected] We are seeking additional Steering Committee members! If you are interested in helping to guide TTJ into the future, please contact us at [email protected] . Powered by Create your own unique website with customizable templates. Get Started Contact About Events Blog Food Hub Initiative Transition Town Jericho - Blog Transition Town Jericho Contact About Events Blog Food Hub Initiative Resilience Hub Toolkit 6/24/2026 On May 18th, 2026, Transition Town Jericho was joined by small group at the Deborah Rawson Memorial Library to hear from Lena Greenberg (they/them), co-director of Community Resilience Organizations (CROs). CROs is a small non-profit based in Montpelier, VT that emerged after Hurricane Irene in 2011. CROs is a grassroots organizing hub and technical assistance provider focused on helping communities build resilience and racial and climate equity. After the devastating floods in 2023 and 2024 in Vermont, CROs released their Resilience Hub Toolkit, which is what Lena came to discuss. ​YouTube recording HERE . Lena began with some history, explaining that when CROs started after Irene, their founder kind of looked around and said: "Hold on, all of the communities that are better connected…where people already have relationships, are much more able to thrive after disaster, not to mention survive and recover. Let's try to invest in those relationships before the next disaster so that we can be better equipped to meet the moment.” That ethos drove their work into the Toolkit. For their part, Lena grew up in New York City and was there in 2012 during Hurricane Sandy. The

properties.description: Join the evolution To navigation Contact Cart Login Join the evolution The time is now. 2016-01-10, 17:01 Martin Kæstel Nielsen think.dk Change is energized when you decide to make a difference. With a holistic approach to sustainability we develop alternative solutions, raise awareness, unfold human potential and strengthen human connection. Our co-creative community is characterized by openness and a desire to make tomorrow better. Starting today. No matter who you are or where you are from. The time is always now . properties.name: Horsham
properties.description: Transition Horsham Transition Horsham - the sustainable way forward Home Links About Transition Community Allotment Membership Past Events Contact us Welcome to Transition Horsham! Transition Horsham is a community group of local Horsham area residents who are concerned about the effects that climate change and other environmental and economic problems will have on our lives in the near and medium future. We support the ideas and practical solutions of the Transition Towns movement set up by Rob Hopkins in 2006 in Totnes, Devon, where people like us wanted to do something positive to build in flexibility and resilience to their community, so that we not only survive future changes, but thrive. So Transition Horsham is all about building community networks, sharing and learning new skills. It is about cutting our carbon emissions through establishing new community based ways of producing energy and heating our houses efficiently, reducing our individual carbon footprint, recycling and reducing waste. It is about growing healthy local produce, encouraging local biodiversity through seed swaps, mending clothes and knitting, baking bread and making preserves, learning more through reading groups and film nights, having fun and travelling lightly. We have a number of projects on the go, so look through the website, join us on Facebook, check out the events page, sign up to get notifications, and if you think there is something you could help with or want to start, then don't hesitate, come to an event and let us know. We look forward to getting to know you. To find out more about the Transition idea, you can start here. Transition Horsham Energy Group Transition volunteers are also working in other ways to become less reliant on fossil fuels, particularly in their own homes. It is about cutting our carbon emissions through establishing new community based ways of producing energy and heating our houses efficiently, reducing our individual carbon footprint, recycling and reducing waste. It is about growing healthy local produce, encouraging local biodiversity through seed swaps, mending clothes and knitting, baking bread and making preserves, learning more through reading groups and film nights, having fun and travelling lightly. We have a number of projects on the go, so look through the website, join us on Facebook, check out the events page, sign up to get notifications, and if you think there is something you could help with or want to start, then don't hesitate, come to an event and let us know. We look forward to getting to know you. To find out more about the Transition idea, you can start here. Transition Horsham Energy Group Transition volunteers are also working in other ways to become less reliant on fossil fuels, particularly in their own homes. This means saving energy (and money!) where possible â and moving to renewable sources. Many people now understand why we need to reduce fossil fuel use. properties.name: Haslemere
properties.description: Food Mapping Project – Workshop « Transition Haslemere Home About Take Action Active Travel Projects Home Energy Efficiency Haslemere Trees Tree Wardens Participate Resources Contact us AGM 2026 Transition Haslemere …addressing climate change at a local level… Feed on Posts Comments The Debate – Wood Fuel and Small Wind Turbines » Food Mapping Project – Workshop Sep 7th, 2009 by tth Wednesday 9 September 6.00pm for a 6.30pm start Two hours including food and networking Haslemere Hall Annexe, Bridge Road, Haslemere GU27 2AS The Mapping Local Food Webs project needs your help! As part of our research on local food we are running a workshop during the Haslemere Food Festival to gain in more detail the views of shoppers, residents and businesses in and around the Haslemere area. What are the social, environmental and economic benefits of eating food grown or produced within 30 miles of where it was bought; what are the issues around the affordability and availability of local food? Come and tell us what you think, as well as enjoy some local food! This is your opportunity to have your say on local food as part of a national research project. The results of the project will be promoted to local and national decision makers by a team of local volunteers and the Campaign to Protect Rural England (CPRE) who are leading this project. Tags: CPRE , food , local Posted in Projects Comments are closed. Diary dates Green Drinks Thursday 4th June 19.30 for 20.00 in “The Snug” at The Mill, GU27 3QE Farmers’ Market Saturday 6th June, 10.00-16.00 (normally every first Saturday of the month), High Street, GU27 2LA Repair Cafe Saturday 6th June 10.30-12.30 (& every first Saturday of the month) at The Swan Inn, High Street, GU27 2HG Green Coffee Wed 17th June, 10.30, Courtyard Cafe (& by arrangement through WhatsApp group) Town Tree Walk, with Clive Sat 27th June, 2-4pm, from Georgian Hotel Woodland Tree Walk Sun 28th June, 10-11.30am, The Roughs Community allotment Sundays 10.30 – 12.3

properties.name: Sustainable Bayswater
properties.description: Bats on Our Doorstep Enviro House Bats on Our Doorstep Did you know we have bats right here in Perth? Not the large Fruit bats but the small and 'cute' Microbats. Having attended a bat box building workshop a couple of months ago, it was most interesting to learn more about these fascinating mammals. The most common type of Bat in WA is the Microbat, especially the Gould's Wattled Bat, which is highly adaptable, found over most of Australia and uses a variety of roosting sites including tree hollows. The Whitestriped Freetail Bat is endemic to southwest WA and is one of the few bats whose echolocation calls are audible to the human ear. Microbats are small, nocturnal creatures making them difficult to see with some weighing just 3gms and able to fit through small gaps that are 5mm wide, while larger Microbats can weigh up to 150gms with wingspans of 25cm. Being insectivores, Microbats spend most of their active time hunting for insects and foraging for food but quickly disperse at the first sign of intrusion. One Microbat can eat 1,000 mosquitoes per night. Bats emit high-frequency sound waves that bounce off objects and Microbats use this echolocation to navigate and hunt in the dark. The waves that bounce back to the Microbat tell it a lot about the object – how far it is, its texture, shape, size and if it is stationary or moving. Many bats have special features to help them echolocate e.g. very long ears for hearing. During daylight hours Microbats are known to spend their time inside hollows of tree trunks or branches and urban clearing is creating a major lack of habitat for them. Ways to help Because bats, including Microbats play a vital role in biodiversity, you can you help them by; Planting native habitat trees Installing safely designed and comfortable bat boxes in trees. (make sure to clear away debris or branches from their flight path) Learning more about bats Raising awareness of the importance of bats At the bat box workshop, we had to work in pairs

properties.name: Intern. Begegnung in Gemeinschaftsdiensten e.V.
properties.description: IBG - Internationale Workcamps Ü ber uns V erein P rojektpartnerschaft N ewsletter W orkcamps T eilnahmebedingungen H ighlights & freie Plätze 2026 L änder U mwelt- und Klimaschutzprojekte F reiwilligenteams (ESK) W orkcamps leiten E rfahrungsberichte F reiwilligendienste E uropäisches Solidaritätskorps (ESK) L ängere Freiwilligendienste E rfahrungsberichte A usschreibungen T rainings T rainings für Campleitungen I nternationale Trainings P rojektpraktika K ontakt Let's take action for climate protection! Auch 2026 soll das Engagement für Klimaschutz ein Schwerpunkt unserer Arbeit sein. Unter dem Motto "Anpacken für Wälder und Moore" setzen wir uns in vielen Camps und bei Veranstaltungen für mehr Klimaschutz ein. Reisekostenzuschüsse für nachhaltiges Reisen Wenn du dich bei deinem Workcamp im Ausland für die Anreise mit Bus, Bahn, Fahrrad oder zu Fuß entscheidest, erstatten wir dir gerne einen Teil deiner Reisekosten. Das haben unsere Freiwilligen erlebt Du möchtest wissen, was anderen Freiwillige in den unterschiedlichen gemeinnützigen Projekten im In- und Ausland erlebt haben? Hier findest du Erfahrungsberichte aus verschiedenen Workcamps. Nachhaltigkeit umsetzen In unseren Camps und Freiwilligenprojekten verbreiten wir das Thema Nachhaltigkeit weiter und unterstützen die praktische Umsetzung im Alltag. Gleiche Rechte. Gleiche Chancen. Unabhängig von Herkunft, Geschlecht, sexueller Identität, kulturellem oder sozialen Hintergrund, Einkommen, Ausbildung, Religion und Glauben, physischer und gesundheitlicher Bedingungen oder Einschränkungen steht die Teilnahme an unseren Freiwilligenprojekten jedem jungen Menschen offen. Workcamps leiten Du arbeitest gern in Gruppen und hast vielleicht auch schon an einem oder mehreren Workcamps teilgenommen? Dann mach den nächsten Schritt und leite selbst ein internationales Workcamp in Deutschland! Offen für alle Die Teilnahme an unseren Freiwilligenprojekten soll allen jungen Menschen offen stehen. Wir setzen uns daher dafür ein,

properties.name: Bentley Urban Farm
properties.description: Volunteering Opportunities – BENTLEY URBAN FARM Skip to content Come along and get your hands dirty with us. There are loads of jobs to do and depending on the time of year you could be: harvesting : saving seed : weeding : turning the compost sharpening tools : painting : fixing things : making signs path clearing : bed-making : hoeing : planting or any number of other jobs. We eat lunch together and welcome keen cooks and washer-uppers. And we also welcome people who’d like to help with the admin and finance side of things or who like building maintenance or fundraising or sales! BUF is collectively managed by the folks who choose to take on a share of the responsibilities. All of us are volunteers. If there’s a particular task or job you’re keen to try out, fill in the contact form or give us a call to find out more about it. BENTLEY URBAN FARM Subscribe Subscribed Sign up Log in Copy shortlink Report this content View post in Reader Manage subscriptions Collapse this bar %d About – BENTLEY URBAN FARM Skip to content Bentley Urban Farm (BUF) is an award winning upcycled market garden based in Bentley, Doncaster. We use reclaimed materials to repair and maintain a former horticultural training centre, turning it into a growing project capable of providing affordable, fresh, healthy, local food whilst creating new opportunities for education, training, skill-sharing, employment and entrepreneurship. As well as growing food for people, we grow habitats for our wilder friends. Ecology and rewilding is central to what we do. Come down and watch the dragonflies and, if you’re really lucky, the peregrine while you pick your tomatoes! BUF is a grassroots project created by local people who love Doncaster, but our region has more than its fair share of problems,

We use reclaimed materials to repair and maintain a former horticultural training centre, turning it into a growing project capable of providing affordable, fresh, healthy, local food whilst creating new opportunities for education, training, skill-sharing, employment and entrepreneurship. As well as growing food for people, we grow habitats for our wilder friends. Ecology and rewilding is central to what we do. Come down and watch the dragonflies and, if you’re really lucky, the peregrine while you pick your tomatoes! BUF is a grassroots project created by local people who love Doncaster, but our region has more than its fair share of problems,

properties.name: Pferde bewegen Menschen e.V.
properties.description: Tiergestützte Therapie - pferde-bewegen-menschens Jimdo-Page! Herzlich Willkommen! bei Pferde bewegen Menschen e.V. "Das Leben ist Fülle - Lasst sie uns feiern und miteinander teilen!" Achtsame Erlebnisräume für Mensch und Tier Wir haben es uns zur Aufgabe gemacht, insbesondere Kindern, Jugendlichen und Erwachsenen in schwierigen Lebenssituationen den achtsamen, bewussten und ganzheitlichen Umgang mit Tieren in der Natur zu ermöglichen. Daher sind wir aktiv in den Bereichen Tiergestützte Therapie, Interkulturelle Gruppenarbeit, Tier-, Natur- und Artenschutz, wesensgemäße Tierhaltung sowie der Permakultur (ökologisch-nachhaltige Lebensweise). Wir möchten uns an dieser Stelle auch bei allen Förderern und ehrenamtlichen HelferInnen von ganzem Herzen bedanken! Hier kannst du mit Freude Gutes tun! AKTUELL Bewirb dich jetzt für einen Bundesfreiwilligendienst* oder ein Jahrespraktikum bei uns! (Beginn zeitnah möglich) *Der Bundesfreiwilligendienst und das Praktikum sind altersunabhängig - bewirb dich gerne auch als 30 J. plus zuklappen Aktuell - pferde-bewegen-menschens Jimdo-Page! Aktuell 01. Nov. 25 I Tierisch stark - Selbstvertrauen fördern Wir freuen uns riesig! Durch die freundliche Förderung der Deutschen Postcode Lotterie, der Allianz für die Jugend sowie der Scherer Stiftung können wir das wundervolle Projekt "Tierisch stark- Selbstvertrauen fördern und Verbindung stärken" durchführen. Es ist ein Projekt für Mädchen und junge Frauen zwischen 10-16 Jahren in herausfordernden Lebenssituationen. Die Tiere dienen dabei als Brücke und Mittler zu mehr Kontakten, Selbstbewusstheit, Vertrauen und Lebensfreude bei den Betroffenen. Die Gruppe trifft sich in regelmäßigen Abständen über die Dauer von 12 Monaten. Anmeldung und Infos u nter [email protected] oder T. 07224-6200782 01. April 25 I Gartengruppe Zusammen Wachsen Wir freuen uns riesig! Durch die freundliche Förderung der Schöck Familienstiftung Baden-Baden können wir auch in 2025 unser interkulturelles Gartenprojekt "

properties.name: Ernährungssouverän - Permakultur-Design auf kleinstem Raum
properties.description: WHAT?????? – Ich hab den ersten Preis gewonnen! – Ernährungssouverän Zum Inhalt springen WHAT?????? – Ich hab den ersten Preis gewonnen! 17 September 2023 Ich fasse es immer noch nicht! Erinnert ihr euch, dass ich vom Wettbewerb Deutschland summt – Wir tun was für Bienen berichtet habe? Das war Ende Juli. Ihr könnt euch gar nicht vorstellen, was ich vor dem Einsendeschluss noch rumgeeiert habe. Stundenlang habe ich Collagen aus meinen Makroaufnahmen gebastelt und den Text immer wieder umgeschrieben. Am Abend des 31.7. war ich fertig – und habe erstmal nicht auf „Veröffentlichen“ sondern auf „Beitrag löschen“ geklickt. Zu furchterregend viele supergute Beiträge (man kann die alle einsehen, auch die der Vorjahre) waren da schon online – ich war so klein mit Hut. Dass ich dann doch nochmal wenige Stunden vor der Deadline alles hochgeladen und veröffentlicht habe, war der Tatsache geschuldet, dass ich echt viele Stunden damit zugebracht hatte, den Beitrag zusammenzustellen. Und auch der selbsttherapeutischen Einsagung, dass ich ja schließlich 2019 schon mal beim Umweltpreis der Stadt Stuttgart für unseren Garten den 1. Preis in der Kategorie Privatgärten geholt hatte. Uuuuuund dann… kam eine Mail, dass wir in der Vorauswahl seien. Wie krass ist das denn? Uuuuuuund dann – wurden wir nach Berlin eingeladen, weil wir unter den ersten drei Plätzen der Kategorie Balkone, Terrassen, Vertikalgärten, Dachbegrünung gelandet waren. What???? Ihr müsst euch das mal vorstellen – da haben knapp 500 erfahrende Naturgärtner Beiträge eingereicht, manche davon hatten schon früher einen Preis dort gewonnen. Und dann komme ich mit meinen 6m² daher? OK – da ich Berlin toll finde und jede Ausrede nutze, hinfahren zu können, haben wir 4 Tage dort geplant und waren gestern bei der Preisverleihung im Besucherzentrum der wunderschönen „Gärten der Welt“. So viele Menschen waren gekommen und es war total toll, Birgit Schattling, von bio-balkon.de zu treffen. Ihr und iher wunderbaren Facebookgruppe

properties.name: El Equimite
properties.description: Nuestro Café | El Equimite ¿Quienes somos? Nuestro Café Alimentos biodinámicos Blog de Hortalizas RCR Visitas guiadas y Eco-pedagogía Ubicación Contacto Agricultura Biodinámica El Equimite Cafetal Biodinámico Nuestro Café Aquí podrás ver un Brochure de cafés del Equimite Café Especial de origen único, orgánico, biodinámico y micro-lote. Producimos un café con cualidades extraordinarias y características únicas. Igual que los grandes vinos y el whisky escocés puro de malta, el café de origen único sobresale entre todos los demás cafés de especialidad. Se obtienen cantidades limitadas (micro-lotes) ya que sólo los mejores granos de la finca son cuidadosamente seleccionados para entrar en esta categoría. Nuestro café proviene de una finca con sombra, biodiversa y sustentable. La combinación de la salud y cuidado del suelo, la variedad genética de los cafetos, el cuidadoso manejo ecológico del grano y el uso de los preparados biodinámicos dan a este café sabores únicos difíciles de igualar. Contamos con un modelo de producción especializado, separamos los varietales, damos manejos tecnificados y cuidamos cada planta de café con detalle y atención. Nuestro complejo plan de fertilización, monitoreo de plagas y enfermedades y continua observación, nos convierte en un modelo de producción de la región. Llevamos la biodinámica a su máxima expresión, con la combinación con tecnología de cafeticultura de especialidad, manejamos varietales muy selectos: Gesha, Típica pionera, Caturra, Bourbón Amarillo, Mundo Novo, Garnica; de origen seleccionado, trazabilidad y calidad genética evaluada. El origen único de este café especial se debe a los elementos particulares (históricos, sociales y geo-biológicos) que le da la finca ( El Equimite ) y la micro-región (Coatepec, Veracruz). Tenemos un enfoque de respeto profundo por la naturaleza, todos los colaboradores y la comunidad. properties.name: Samen des Wandels - Permakultur- und Mitweltbildung
properties.description: Sei der Wandel, den Du in der Welt sehen möchtest. - Samen des Wandels Sei der Wandel, den Du in der Welt sehen möchtest. Mahatma Gandhi Aktuelles Dinkelsbühler Samenfest am 15. März 2026 Geboren aus dem weltweiten Bürgerengagement gegen diversitätsmindernde und umweltschädigende GMO-Zulassungen und übergriffige Saatgutverordnungen dient diese Veranstaltung seit dem Jahr 2012 in erster Linie der Erhaltung von samenfestem Saatgut und alten Sorten . Saatgut- & Vielfaltsmarkt, Handwerk & Kunst, kulinarischen audio-visuelle Genüsse, Workshops & Vorträge, Ausstellungen, Kinderprogramm uvm. Wer mitmachen möchte, ob mit einem Verkaufs- oder Infostand oder wer uns unterstützen möchte (organisatorisch oder finanziell), schreibt uns bitte an [email protected] > Mehr Informationen Der Saatgut-Markt ist hart umkämpft. Fünf Großkonzerne, überwiegend aus der Chemiebranche, dominieren in Europa die Produktion und machen ein Milliardengeschäft. Sie züchten Sorten, die Agrarchemie benötigen und die Landwirte nach jeder Ernte neu hinzukaufen müssen. Denn wer die Saat hat, hat das Sagen. Nicht mit uns :-) "SaveSoil"-Frühlingsfest im April 2026 Die Veranstaltung dient der Verbreitung des Wissens von Permakultur und Transition, sowie der Vernetzung von Aktiven Menschen, Projekten und Projektanten in der Region. Das Frühlingsfest findet im Mitweltgarten in Hohenschwärz statt. Wir suchen einen sonnigen Tag aus - damit das Radl mitkommen kann. Wer mitmachen oder uns unterstützen möchte (organisatorisch oder finanziell), schreibt uns bitte an: [email protected] > Mehr Informationen To enjoy this beautiful circle of life with our hands in the earth we live upon, and ya... to save our soil! Permakulturtechniken, Workshops, Musik, Bio-Jungpflanzenbörse, etc... wer wir eigentlich sind? Wir sind eine dynamische Gruppe von Menschen aus Dinkelsbühl, die müde ist vom vielen Nachdenken und Reden, die gemeinsam aufsteht und Hand anlegt für den aktiven Schutz und die Erhaltung unserer Umwelt, unseres

properties.name: netzwerknatur-permakultur
properties.description: netzwerknatur-permakultur.ch - permakultur-ganzheitlich-leben Webseite! "Lebendig zu sein in diesem wunderschönen, sich selbst ordnenden Universum - zugehörig zu sein dem Tanz des Lebens, mit Sinnen, die es wahrnehmen, mit Lungen, die es atmen, mit Organen, die Nahrung aus ihm schöpfen - ist ein Wunder, das Worte übersteigt." Joanna Macy Da gibt es auch etwas in dir, etwas tiefer Liegendes. Besonders in jenen wenigen Momenten, in denen du dich trotz dieser turbulenten Zeiten in deiner eigenen Mitte befindest, kannst du es wahrnehmen. Du spürst dann diese tiefe Sehnsucht, die sich immer wieder in dir meldet. Diese tiefe Sehnsucht nach einem ganzheitlichen achtsamen respektvollen Leben. Und dafür hat die Permakultur Antworten. Blog Der nächste Stammtisch findet am 27.5. ab 18.30 statt, eintrudeln und um 19.00 Uhr gehts los. Wo? im Campo Führung Imkerei Fintan, Rheinau Die Führung beginnt damit, das Desirée uns erzählt, dass sie die Bienen am Tag vorher informiert hat, dass eine Führung stattfindet. Das ist ein alter Brauch, dass die Bienen darüber informiert werden, was auf dem Hof geschieht; so wichtig sind sie. Die Bienen auf der Rheinau werden wesensgerecht gehalten. Das bedeutet, dass die Stöcke genug Honig für den Winter behalten können und nur etwa die Hälfte für die Menschen geerntet wird. Die Bienen produzieren den Honig ja nicht für uns, sondern für ihr gesundes über den Winter kommen. In einem Stock findet das wichtigste Leben in seiner Mitte statt. Da wird eine Weiselzelle gemacht und die Made mit Gelee royale gefüttert. Da schlüpft nach 16 Tagen eine Königin. In den grösseren Zellen wachsen die Drohnen heran, die verbleiben 24 Tage darin, die Arbeitsbienen 21 Tage. Die ausgeschlüpften Arbeiterinnen durchlaufen sämtliche Bienenberufe, wie sich um die Brut kümmern, Wachs herstellen und bauen, die Königin füttern, den Stock putzen und die richtige Temperatur herstellen, den Honig pflegen und das Flugloch bewachen. Erst als letztes fliegt die Biene hinaus, um
properties.popup:
netzwerknatur-permakultur

netzwerknatur-permakultur.ch - permakultur-ganzheitlich-leben Webseite! "Lebendig zu sein in diesem wunderschönen, sich selbst ordnenden Universum - zugehörig zu sein dem Tanz des Lebens, mit Sinnen, die es wahrnehmen, mit Lungen, die es atmen, mit Organen, die Nahrung aus ihm schöpfen - ist ein Wunder, das Worte übersteigt." Joanna Macy Da gibt es auch etwas in dir, etwas tiefer Liegendes. Besonders in jenen wenigen Momenten, in denen du dich trotz dieser turbulenten Zeiten in deiner eigenen Mitte befindest, kannst du es wahrnehmen. Du spürst dann diese tiefe Sehnsucht, die sich immer wieder in dir meldet. Diese tiefe Sehnsucht nach einem ganzheitlichen achtsamen respektvollen Leben. Und dafür hat die Permakultur Antworten. Blog Der nächste Stammtisch findet am 27.5. ab 18.30 statt, eintrudeln und um 19.00 Uhr gehts los. Wo? im Campo Führung Imkerei Fintan, Rheinau Die Führung beginnt damit, das Desirée uns erzählt, dass sie die Bienen am Tag vorher informiert hat, dass eine Führung stattfindet. Das ist ein alter Brauch, dass die Bienen darüber informiert werden, was auf dem Hof geschieht; so wichtig sind sie. Die Bienen auf der Rheinau werden wesensgerecht gehalten. Das bedeutet, dass die Stöcke genug Honig für den Winter behalten können und nur etwa die Hälfte für die Menschen geerntet wird. Die Bienen produzieren den Honig ja nicht für uns, sondern für ihr gesundes über den Winter kommen. In einem Stock findet das wichtigste Leben in seiner Mitte statt. Da wird eine Weiselzelle gemacht und die Made mit Gelee royale gefüttert. Da schlüpft nach 16 Tagen eine Königin. In den grösseren Zellen wachsen die Drohnen heran, die verbleiben 24 Tage darin, die Arbeitsbienen 21 Tage. Die ausgeschlüpften Arbeiterinnen durchlaufen sämtliche Bienenberufe, wie sich um die Brut kümmern, Wachs herstellen und bauen, die Königin füttern, den Stock putzen und die richtige Temperatur herstellen, den Honig pflegen und das Flugloch bewachen. Erst als letztes fliegt die Biene hinaus, um

properties.name: Marbacher Mitmachgarten
properties.description: Mitmachgarten Marburg - Marbacher Mitmachgarten Mehr dazu unter: Aktuelle Veranstaltungen Wir sind dabei: Climatehub für Marburg! Ob Bürgerenergie, klimafreundliche Gestaltung der Mensa oder selbstorganisierter Lastenradverleih: Lokale Klimainitiativen stoßen in ihrer Stadt viel Veränderung an. Mit unseren ClimateHubs fördern wir das und machen es ganz einfach, eigene Klimaideen umzusetzen, selbst aktiv zu werden und wirkungsvolle lokale Projekte auch in andere Städte zu bringen. Was ist der Mitmachgarten eigentlich? Der Mitmachgarten ist ein Gemeinschaftsgarten in der Marbach gelegen zwischen Bürgerhaus und Sparkasse. Auf einer ehemaligen Rasenfläche wachsen in einem Hochbeet seit 2013 Kräuter, einheimische wie auch mediterane. Zudem gibt es noch Beete mit Beerensträuchern, Gemüse und Rosen. Jeden Dienstag von 17:00 - 19:30 Uhr treffen sich hier die Mitmachgartenfreunde, im Sommerhalbjahr zum Kräutern und Gärtnern am Beet und nach getaner Arbeit genießen wir oftmals noch das frisch Geerntete in Form von Smoothies, Blütensalaten, Kräuterquark und -butter. Von Herbst bis Frühling kommen wir sporadisch in der Bücherstube des Bürgerhauses zusammen, um die Kräuter zu Teemischungen, Salben und in kleine Köstlichkeiten zu verwandeln, eine kleine Hausapotheke anzulegen und vieles andere mehr. Z udem werden Workshops zu Kosmetik, Tee, Gewürzmischungen, Gemüse fermentieren, Senf selbst gemacht u.a. angeboten. Jede*r ist willkommen vorbeizuschauen und je nach Möglichkeit und Zeitbudget mitzumachen. Neuigkeiten Begrenzung der TeilnehmerzaHL wegen Corona Wegen Corona sind keine Kinder und nur 10 Erwachsene mit genügend Abstand im Garten am werkeln. Bitte vor der Teilnahme über die Mailadresse oder das Kontaktformular anmelden. Vielen Dank für eurer Verständnis! Permakulturprojekt Seit diesem Frühjahr wird ein rundes Beet im Sinne der Permakultur bewirtschaftet. Und die erste Ernte ist bereits eingefahren, geerntet wurde eine große Menge an dicken Kartoffeln für das verhältnimäß

properties.name: Silent Rixdorf Garten
properties.description: Startseite Silent Rixdorf Start Garten Veranstaltungen Neuigkeiten Verein Impressum Datenschutz Start Garten Veranstaltungen Neuigkeiten Verein Impressum Datenschutz Silent Rixdorf – Verein zur Förderung von Kunst, Kultur und Toleranz Willkommen im Silent Rixdorf Garten mitten in Neukölln Abseits von Trubel und Lärm findet ihr uns im Wanzlikpfad. Hier könnt ihr Zeit im Grünen verbringen, etwas über unsere Philosophie erfahren oder einfach in der Hängematte abhängen. Mehr erfahren Foodsharing Immer dienstags von 14 bis 17 Uhr könnt ihr bei uns Lebensmittel retten Was montags in der Ausgabestelle von Laib und Seele in Gropiusstadt und dienstags bei der Tafel zu viel ist, holen wir ab und verteilen es. Alle die sich über Obst, Gemüse und Brot freuen, sind Willkommen. Wenn du Essen vor dem Wegschmeißen retten willst, komm einfach in den Garten und hol dir was du brauchst. Veranstaltungen Alle Veranstaltungen 48h im Silent Rixdorf Garten 3.7.2026 Mehr erfahren Neuigkeiten Alle Neuigkeiten Wir sind dabei: Kulturnetzwerk Neukölln Seit diesem Jahr sind wir stolzes Mitglied im Kulturnetzwerk Neukölln e.V. Das Netzwerk verbindet Kulturinitiativen und Kulturschaffende aus dem ganzen Kiez, fördert die Vielfalt der Neuköllner Kulturlandschaft und setzt sich dafür ein, dass Kultur als Ort der Begegnung erlebbar bleibt. Wir freuen uns, Teil dieses Zusammenschlusses zu sein und gemeinsam mit anderen Initiativen sichtbar zu machen, was hier in Neukölln und Rixdorf lebt. ‍ 26.6.2026 Silent Rixdorf e.V. Alles über den e.V. Silent Rixdorf stellt sich vor Der Silent Rixdorf e.V. setzt sich für eine nachhaltige und friedvolle Nachbarschaft ein. Die Mitglieder*innen greifen Anregungen aus der Nachbarschaft auf und setzen sie mit Kreativität und Teamfähigkeit um. Das Vereinsziel ist es, Kunst, Kultur und Toleranz zu fördern. Durch die Diversität der Ideengeber*innen entstehen die unterschiedlichsten Formate, ob Feste, Konzerte, Vorträge Lesungen, Filmabende. Außerdem bietet der Verein an d

properties.name: Totnes
properties.description: Transition Town Totnes Devon Grows Flax will uncover and revive flax culture A county-wide initiative supported by the Heritage Fund grant READ NEWS News Could you join our team and become one of TTT’s new Trustees? We’re looking for enthusiastic people wishing to help inspire and empower their community through supporting our charity to thrive. READ NEWS Economy Reconomy Project The Totnes Reconomy Project is a community-driven effort dedicated to regenerating the local economy. We want to inspire an era of economic regeneration that's local, inclusive, and sustainable. VIEW PROJECT Project Incredible Edible We grow fruit, herbs, nuts, vegetables and edible flowers on common land across Totnes, growing food for everyone to share. VIEW PROJECT Taking positive action together in a changing world . We believe community activism can make a positive difference to the way we live our lives. Transition is about change, resilience and adaptation in an increasingly hostile world. And our Totnes community is key to shaping the places we love to enable more people and the local environment to thrive. Check out some current Transition Projects in Totnes Our projects and groups are ever-changing and evolving, and we always welcome new ideas. Local groups and projects in the Transition Movement are helping communities to be more resilient, from increasing low impact affordable housing, sharing skills, creating livelihoods, reducing energy costs and carbon emissions, growing our local food economy and working in partnership with other local projects. South Devon Lifehouse Network Devon Grows Flax Devon Grows Flax Devon Grows Flax is helping communities learn heritage skills and connect to each other through flax. Learn More Reconomy Project Reconomy Project The Totnes Reconomy Project is a community-driven effort dedicated to regenerating the local economy. Learn More Totnes Grows Flax Totnes Grows Flax Totnes Grows Flax is our local version of a Swedish project to keep knowledge

properties.name: Famalicão em Transição
properties.description: FAMALICÃO por um mundo MELHOR: 4ª Ação de controlo de invasoras no Monte Santa Catarina Páginas Página inicial O que nos move Transição Ser sócio Responsabilidade ambiental Órgãos sociais, planos e relatórios Atividades Inscrições Parceiros Política de Privacidade Newsletter Contacte-nos 4ª Ação de controlo de invasoras no Monte Santa Catarina Vamos continuar a proteger o Monte Santa Catarina - 4ª Ação de controlo de invasoras Sábado 20 de junho | 9h00 – 12h30 No Monte de Santa Catarina, junto ao Penedo da Lua, a natureza continua a recuperar. No entanto, também ali, as Acácias e os Eucaliptos competem com os Sobreiros e Carvalhos na ocupação daquele lugar. A nossa floresta cresce mais devagar, mas é mais resistente aos fogos e é mais biodiversa. Nesta fase, ela precisa da nossa ajuda! Sábado, dia 20.06.2026, vamos realizar a quarta ação de controlo de espécies invasoras. Venha connosco e faça parte deste movimento. Juntos vamos criar o parque florestal protegido dos Montes de Santa Catarina e S. João. Ponto de encontro: Capela de Santa Catarina às 9h00; Horário dos trabalhos: das 9h00 às 12h30; Ferramentas e luvas: se puder traga as suas tesouras de poda e podões (serras de poda) (*). (*) teremos ferramentas de reserva para quem não trouxer. Nota: a participação nesta ação não é coberta por seguro, pelo que apelamos à responsabilidade individual de cada participante. A realização da ação está dependente do risco de incêndio rural, podendo ser cancelada, caso tal se verifique. Esta ação é realizada conjuntamente com o Município de Famalicão, através do Serviço Municipal de Proteção Civil. Por Famalicão em Transição - junho 11, 2026 Enviar a mensagem por email Dê a sua opinião! Partilhar no X Partilhar no Facebook Partilhar no Pinterest Sem comentários: Enviar um comentário Mensagem antiga Página inicial Subscrever: Enviar feedback (Atom) FAMALICÃO por um mundo MELHOR: Controlo de invasoras no sítio do Penedo da Lua Páginas Página inicial O que nos move Transição Ser

Ponto de encontro: Capela de Santa Catarina às 9h00; Horário dos trabalhos: das 9h00 às 12h30; Ferramentas e luvas: se puder traga as suas tesouras de poda e podões (serras de poda) (*). (*) teremos ferramentas de reserva para quem não trouxer. Nota: a participação nesta ação não é coberta por seguro, pelo que apelamos à responsabilidade individual de cada participante. A realização da ação está dependente do risco de incêndio rural, podendo ser cancelada, caso tal se verifique. Esta ação é realizada conjuntamente com o Município de Famalicão, através do Serviço Municipal de Proteção Civil. Por Famalicão em Transição - junho 11, 2026 Enviar a mensagem por email Dê a sua opinião! The Environment Centre’s Big Climate Leap initiative is working with local community groups to turn climate anxiety into action. properties.description: # [トランジション・タウン南阿蘇 Transition Town Minami-Aso]( 地域でつながり、循環型で自然と分かち合いながら、持続可能な暮らしをみんなと一緒に作って行くコミュニテー。阿蘇山と根子岳の麓、白川水源に恵まれ、自分達の食べるもの、住む家、エネルギー、小さな学校など楽しく仲間と地域の人と作って行こうと活動しています。 Transition Town Minami-Aso is a Transition Initiative located in Kumamoto,Kyushu at the foot of Mt. Aso. The local community is moving towards a sustainable way of life by helping, caring for each other and living in harmony with mother nature. Please come and have fun! ## Transition Town MInami-Aso is a Transition initiative that locates in Kyushu Kumamoto where Active * [ホーム]( * [About]( * [TT仲間募集中]( * [連絡先]( * [accommodation]( ## 2019年8月23日金曜日 []( 南阿蘇の俊郎です。 この度トランジション・タウン南阿蘇のじろうさんがパーマカルチャー、トランジション、エコビレッジ、小規模オーガニック農家の視点でデンマークに行ってきましたので、報告がてら、日本でもこんなことできたらいいねとか話し合うイベントを企画しました。 日時：8/30 20:00-22:00 9/6 20:00-22:00 場所：Zoom 定員：10名 参加方法：参加されたい方はこちらにどちらかの日程がいいか投稿ください。あとでZoomのurlを送ります。 じろう畑 Meets デンマーク [ "山口 じろう") 幸福度ランキング上位常連の国、デンマークへ2週間行ってきました。 デンマークに関しては予備知識ほぼゼロで、そこにあるコミュニティーのメンバーになって、人、場所、文化を感じてきました。 トランジションタウンイニシアチブ、フォルケハイスクール、CSA、小規模農家、森の幼稚園、シェアハウス、パーマカルチャーサイト、エコビレッジなど、いろんな”いいね”を見てきたので、みんなとおしゃべりしたいと思います。楽しみにしています。 [![Image 2]( 投稿者 [俊郎]( "author profile") 時刻: [21:06]( "permanent link")[![Image 3]( "メール投稿")[![Image 4]( "投稿を編集") [メールで送信]( "メールで送信")[BlogThis!]( "BlogThis!")[X で共有]( "X で共有")[Facebook で共有する]( "Facebook で共有する")[Pinterest に共有]( "Pinterest に共有") []( #### 0 件のコメント: []( #### コメントを投稿 []( [前の投稿]( "前の投稿")[ホーム]( 登録: [コメントの投稿 (Atom)]( ## お知らせ！ **メーリングリスト参加者募集中！** ## ブログ アーカイブ * [▼](javascript:void(0))[2019]( * [▼](javascript:void(0))[8月]( * [南阿蘇の俊郎です。この度トランジション・タウン南阿蘇のじろうさんがパーマカルチャー、トランジション...]( * [►](javascript:void(0))[2018]( * [►](javascript:void(0))[9月]( * [►](javascript:void(0))[8月]( * [►](javascript:void(0))[2016]( * [►](javascript:void(0))[6月]( * [►](javascript:void(0))[2014]( * [►](javascript:void(0))[11月]( * [►](javascript:void(0))[7月]( * [►](javascript:void(0))[5月]( * [►](javascript:void(0))[4月]( * [►](javascript:void(0))[3月]( * [►](javascript:void(0))[1月]( * [►](javascript:void(0))[201
