properties.name: Sustainable Delaware Ohio
properties.description: Join us for a Webinar on December 18 – Sustainable Delaware Ohio Skip to content Home » Blog » General » Join us for a Webinar on December 18 “The Shale Gas Debate: How Industry and Environmental Messages Stack Up” Resources for the Future (RFF) invites you to a webinar on how messaging from the shale gas industry and environmental organizations affects the public’s attitudes toward shale gas development.Recently, US Department of the Interior Secretary Sally Jewell called upon industry to provide better information to the public about the fracking process. She said the public debate is “confused” and “not well-informed,” stating that industry is responsible for making sure “that the public understand what [fracking] is, how it’s done, and why it’s safe.” Industry has also recognized the need to raise the public’s comfort level, especially in states such as Colorado, where three cities recently approved bans or moratoriums on hydraulic fracturing. New research at RFF shows that industry will need to provide not just more information, but better information if it is to improve public confidence.This webinar will examine survey results about the public’s level of concern regarding the potential environmental and health risks associated with shale gas development, and how much people value reducing such risks—how much they are willing to pay in increased taxes, utility bills, and so on to reduce those risks. The briefing will also show how different sources of information (industry and environmental) affect people’s attitudes and beliefs about shale gas development in their states.The webinar is based on a new survey conducted by RFF researchers of a random sample of individuals in Pennsylvania, where residents are still adjusting to the boom in shale gas development and drilling in their state, and in Texas, where residents are more accustomed to these types of energy development activities. Speakers: Dr. Alan J. Krupnick, RFF Senior Fellow and Director of RFF’s Cente

properties.name: Eko Centar Latinovac
properties.description: [![Image 1: logo]( 2: logo]( EKO CENTAR Latinovac ## Održivi razvoj na djelu Mi smo zajednica koja promiče permakulturu, volontiranje i održivi način života kroz projekte, radionice i suradnju. [Kontaktiraj nas]( ![Image 3]( ![Image 4]( 5]( Vizija & Misija ## Vizija, misija i djelovanje Eko centra Latinovac ![Image 6: Eko centar Latinovac]( O NAMA ## Naša misija je stvarati zajednicu održivog življenja. Eko centar Latinovac okuplja ljude koje povezuje želja za zdravijim i pravednijim svijetom. Promičemo permakulturu, volontiranje i interkulturalno učenje kroz projekte, radionice i festivale. Naše aktivnosti obuhvaćaju djecu, mlade i odrasle – od lokalnih mještana do međunarodnih volontera – stvarajući prostor za razmjenu znanja, solidarnost i zajednički rad. Vjerujemo da malim koracima možemo mijenjati zajednicu, jačati ruralna područja i nuditi praktične primjere održivog života. [Saznaj više]( Temeljne vrijednosti Naš rad oblikuju vrijednosti koje čuvaju prirodu, jačaju zajednicu i potiču održiv način života. ### Održivost Promičemo način življenja i djelovanja koji čuva prirodne resurse, njeguje biološku raznolikost i stvara ravnotežu društva, okoliša i ekonomije. ### Zajednica Gradimo snažan osjećaj pripadnosti, međusobne podrške i suradnje u lokalnom i širem okruženju. ### Vodstvo kroz primjer Preuzimamo odgovornost i mijenjamo svijet kroz vlastiti angažman i pozitivan primjer. ### Volonterstvo Vjerujemo u snagu dobrovoljnog djelovanja i doprinos pojedinaca za opće dobro. ### Inspirativno imanje Naše imanje pokazuje kako održivost izgleda u praksi – mjesto učenja, suradnje i zajedničkog rada. ### Profesionalnost Radimo odgovorno i etično, s jasnim ciljevima i visokim standardima u projektima i partnerstvima. ### Učinkovitost Radimo promišljeno, s jasnim ciljevima, dobro planiranim aktivnostima i optimalnim korištenjem resursa. ### Razvoj i nadogradnja Otvoreni smo za učenje i stalno unapređivanje sebe, svojih znanja i same organizacije. ### Siguran prostor Njeg

[Saznaj više]( Temeljne vrijednosti Naš rad oblikuju vrijednosti koje čuvaju prirodu, jačaju zajednicu i potiču održiv način života. ### Održivost Promičemo način življenja i djelovanja koji čuva prirodne resurse, njeguje biološku raznolikost i stvara ravnotežu društva, okoliša i ekonomije. ### Zajednica Gradimo snažan osjećaj pripadnosti, međusobne podrške i suradnje u lokalnom i širem okruženju. ### Vodstvo kroz primjer Preuzimamo odgovornost i mijenjamo svijet kroz vlastiti angažman i pozitivan primjer. ### Volonterstvo Vjerujemo u snagu dobrovoljnog djelovanja i doprinos pojedinaca za opće dobro. ### Inspirativno imanje Naše imanje pokazuje kako održivost izgleda u praksi – mjesto učenja, suradnje i zajedničkog rada. ### Profesionalnost Radimo odgovorno i etično, s jasnim ciljevima i visokim standardima u projektima i partnerstvima. ### Učinkovitost Radimo promišljeno, s jasnim ciljevima, dobro planiranim aktivnostima i optimalnim korištenjem resursa. ### Razvoj i nadogradnja Otvoreni smo za učenje i stalno unapređivanje sebe, svojih znanja i same organizacije. ### Siguran prostor Njeg

Latinovac, Croatia

properties.name: Just Transition Wakefield
properties.description: [![Image 1: Just Transition Wakefield logo]( [](javascript:void(0)) * [Home]( * [About]( * [Useful links]( * [Activities]( * [Nature and Biodiversity]( * [Climate Action Station]( * [Community Wealth Building]( * [Drax and the Global Biomass Industry]( * [Eco-education]( * [Energy]( * [Families Together]( * [Food]( * [Green jobs]( * [Transport]( * [United Nations Annual Conference of the Parties (COP)]( * [Working With Wakefield Council]( * [Latest]( * [News]( * [Blog]( * [Events]( * [Resources]( * [Newsletters]( * [Press releases]( * [Responses]( * [Reviews and Recommendations]( * [Videos]( * [Get involved]( # [Book review – On Guerrilla Gardening]( "Book review – On Guerrilla Gardening") April 9, 2021 **On Guerrilla Gardening – a handbook for gardening without boundaries, Richard Reynolds (Bloomsbury 2008)** Do you know of a neglected space in your area? A space that maybe the council used to garden, but have given up under the weight of austerity? A space owned and ignored by an absentee landlord waiting for a boom in land prices that is spreading weed seed over the neighbourhood? Do you want to do something about it, but have not known where to start? This is the place to start – a simple borrowing of this book from the library will get you in control of your environment. The author gives his own history as a guerrilla gardener, along with examples and case studies from others around the world. From New York Community Gardens to clandestine cultivation of traffic islands, it is all here. This neat little book on guerrilla gardening is an introduction, a handbook, a link into networks – and a quick read. It is not a horticultural manual, although it has some useful hints, tips and ideas. So, if you are thinking about sneaking into the night with your fork and trowel, read this first. If you are not thinking about it, read this and you might well be! [Share 0](javascript:void(0)) [Tweet 0](javascript:void(0)) [Share 0](javascript:void(0)) [Previous]( [Next]( #####

properties.name: Vermehrungsgarten
properties.description: ## [Pflanzenbörse am Lindener Berg]( [![Image 1: Foto: J. Lindemann, Cocktailtomate]( ## Samstag, den 10.Mai 2025, 10 – 14 Uhr ## Vereinsheim Kleingartenverein Linden e.V. (Ernst-Winter-Heim) ## Am Lindener Berg 39, 30449 Hannover Die Veranstaltung ist jedes Jahr ein Ort sich mit anderen Garten-begeisterten zu treffen und interessante Pflanzen und Samen für den Garten zu erwerben. ## [Gärtnern Sie mit!]( [![Image 2: Foto: Jörg L. Mitarbeit im Vermehrungsgarten]( ### Der Vermehrungsgarten sucht verlässliche, ehrenamtliche Unterstützung. Wir suchen Menschen, ## [Führungen durch den Vermehrungsgarten]( [![Image 3: Foto: Jörg L. Königskerze]( Im Rahmen der Offenen Pforte bieten wir einmal montalich eine Führung durch den Vermehrungsgarten an. Während der Führung erfahren Sie viel über Pflanzen und Sorten sowie die Arbeitsweise im Vermehrungsgarten. Dazu erhalten sie Einblicke in die praktische Gartenarbeit und Informationen über die samenfeste Vermehrung von Gemüse und Kräuter. ## [letze Führung durch den Vermehrungsgarten fällt aus]( [![Image 4: Foto: silke, Führung am 14.09.25]( Die für Freitag, den 10.10.2025 geplante Führung durch den Vermehrungsgarten fällt leider aus. Führungen durch den Vermehrungsgarten sind interessante Veranstaltungen. Jede Führung ist anders, weil sich der Garten, die angebauten Kulturen, das Wachstum ändert. Immer wieder gibt es interessantes zu sehen, neues zu erfahren, besondere Pflanzen mitzunehmen. So gingen bisher viele überrascht und zufrieden aus dem Garten. ## [Nach der Baustelle ist vor der Baustelle.]( [![Image 5: Foto: A. Ackermann]( Der Vermehrungsgarten kann weiter arbeiten, er ist neu eingezäunt und verschiedene Einrichtungen, wie Tische und Bänke, Fahrradständer und das Kompostklo sind versetzt worden. Die Situation nach dem Abzug der Bauarbeiter ist aber noch nicht in Ordnung. ## [Gärten aus Stein - Steine im Garten - Einladung zur Führung]( [![Image 6: Foto: A.Witterstein, Ziegelbeet neu angelegt]( Einladung zur Führung am F

Schauen Sie gerne hier, für nähere Informationen zu den Handlungsfeldern, Projekten und Möglichkeiten zum Engagement. engagieren unterstützen auf dem Laufenden bleiben Bleiben Sie auf dem Laufenden! Name Email Ich akzeptiere die Datenschutzbestimmungen Thank you! Your submission has been received! Oops! Something went wrong while submitting the form. Neues Projekt für die Gemeinschaftsverpflegung: Stuttgarter Bohnentag Mehr dazu lesen Unterstützen Sie die Initiative hier mit Ihrer Unterschrift Gemeinsame Erklärung zeichnen Im HuMUS EU-Projekt (Healthy municipal soils – gesunde kommunale Böden) setzen wir uns als Ernährungsrat für den Schutz von Flächen und Ackerböden, urbanes Grün und für die ökologische Aufwertung von Böden ein. ‍ Unterstützen Sie die Initiative, die wir zusammen mit unseren Partnern der Universität Hohenheim, Geschäftsstelle für Bioökonomie gegründet haben, indem Sie bis 28.2.2025 diese Gemeinsame Erklärung zeichnen . Gemeinsam für ein regionales und ökologisches Ernährungssystem Organisatorischer Mittelpunkt im Food Netzwerk ist der Verein Ernährungsrat für die StadtRegion Stuttgart e.V.. Mitglieder sind Unterstützerinnen und Unterstützer aus der Zivilgesellschaft, aus der Landwirtschaft, aus Großküche, Verwaltung, Forschung, Lebensmittelverarbeitung & - Großhandel, kommunalem Klimaschutzmanagement, Stadtplanung, regionalem und fairem Handel. ‍ Im Ernährungsrat wurde 2024 – 25, mit Beteiligung von 100 Stakeholdern, die erste lokale Ernährungsstrategie für Stuttgart und die Region erarbeitet. properties.name: Demeter Ba-Wü Verein für biodynamisches Gärtnern
properties.description: Klima retten, Tierhaltung umbauen! | Demeter Startseite Pressemitteilung zur UN-Klimakonferenz: Klima retten, Tierhaltung umbauen! © Eva Wolf 09. November 2022 © Eva Wolf Dieses Jahr zeigte deutlicher denn je: Die Landwirtschaft leidet schon jetzt unter den Folgen des Klimawandels. Diese Woche treffen sich Gesandte von fast 200 Staaten zur UN-Klimakonferenz in Sharm El-Sheikh – Demeter ruft die Politiker:innen dazu auf, alles zu tun, um die 1,5° Grenze einzuhalten und die Landwirtschaft zukunftsfähig aufzustellen! „Hitzewellen und Starkregenereignisse sind schon heute in vielen Regionen ein Risiko für die Ernten der Landwirt:innen. Schreitet der Klimawandel fort, dann bedroht das die Existenz vieler Bäuerinnen und Bauern – und damit auch die Ernährungssicherheit von uns allen, und zwar weltweit,“ warnt Demeter-Vorstand Alexander Gerber. „Deshalb rufen wir die Verhandelnden auf: Setzt alles daran, mutig verbindliche und wirksame Maßnahmen für die Einhaltung der 1,5°C Grenze zu beschließen! Um die Klimagas-Emissionen deutlich zu senken, braucht es einen sorgsamen Umgang mit den natürlichen Ressourcen, Besteuerung von Emissionen sowie eine konsequente Förderung erneuerbarer Energiequellen. Die Klimakrise betrifft alle Menschen auf der Erde – und ist ebenso nur global zu bewältigen!“ Dabei muss und will auch die Landwirtschaft ihren Beitrag leisten. Etwa indem sie Klimagas-Emissionen senkt, Kohlenstoff im Boden bindet und durch vielfältige Fruchtfolgen und Betriebszweige weniger anfällig für die Folgen von Extremwetterlagen wird. Der Ökolandbau, der diese Punkte bereits erfüllt, muss daher ein wichtiger Bestandteil bei der Agrarwende und einer nachhaltigen Klimastrategie darstellen. Anlässlich der UN-Klimakonferenz fordert der Demeter e.V. die Bundesregierung auf, das Ziel 30% Ökolandbau auch konsequent umzusetzen. „Neben Fördermaßnahmen für die Umstellung von landwirtschaftlichen Betrieben auf ökologischen Landbau sowie tiergerechte Haltungsformen muss die berufliche A

properties.name: Albacete en Transición
properties.description: Albacete En Transición: Nueva web de Albacete en Transición sábado, 3 de mayo de 2025 Nueva web de Albacete en Transición Desde principios del año 2024 se ha formado un nuevo grupo de Albacete en Transición a raíz de la siguiente charla que José Gil Cuesta impartió sobre decrecimiento: Este nuevo grupo tiene una web que os animamos a visitar para seguir sus actividades: Publicado por Albacete En Transición en 9:19 Enviar por correo electrónico Escribe un blog Compartir en X Compartir con Facebook Compartir en Pinterest No hay comentarios: Publicar un comentario Entrada antigua Inicio Suscribirse a: Enviar comentarios (Atom) Albacete En Transición: Herramientas para el cambio. jueves, 26 de noviembre de 2015 Herramientas para el cambio. Estimad@s amig@s: Albacete en Transición retoma las actividades que venía desarrollando tiempo atrás, concretamente las proyecciones Vídeo Forum y las charlas taller. De este modo pretendemos programar una tarde temática para el último domingo de cada mes, realizando en la Casa Vieja una proyección de un tema de interés y preparando una actividad relacionada. No obstante, como presentación y punto de partida, queremos realizar un evento algo especial. Hemos fijado una primera fecha, el 29 de Noviembre, para reunir a asociaciones y grupos que tengan relación con el movimiento de Transición y temas afines para que cada uno exponga su proyecto y actividades, considerando esta una buena ocasión para conocernos y estrechar lazos entre las distintas iniciativas. Hemos llamado al evento “Herramientas para el cambio”. Intentaremos contar con un amplio abanico de iniciativas que pueden tener un nexo en común con los movimientos de transición y decrecimiento, tratando líneas como medio-ambiente, economía, movilidad urbana, etc... PROGRAMACIÓN DIA: 29 de Noviembre HORA: a partir de las 17 horas LUGAR: Casa Vieja – C/ Blasco Ibáñez, 9 17:00 Presentación del evento. 17:20 Proyección de la película “In Transition 2.0” 18:20 Presentación del Movimie

Hemos fijado una primera fecha, el 29 de Noviembre, para reunir a asociaciones y grupos que tengan relación con el movimiento de Transición y temas afines para que cada uno exponga su proyecto y actividades, considerando esta una buena ocasión para conocernos y estrechar lazos entre las distintas iniciativas. Hemos llamado al evento “Herramientas para el cambio”. Intentaremos contar con un amplio abanico de iniciativas que pueden tener un nexo en común con los movimientos de transición y decrecimiento, tratando líneas como medio-ambiente, economía, movilidad urbana, etc... PROGRAMACIÓN DIA: 29 de Noviembre HORA: a partir de las 17 horas LUGAR: Casa Vieja – C/ Blasco Ibáñez, 9 17:00 Presentación del evento. 17:20 Proyección de la película “In Transition 2.0” 18:20 Presentación del Movimie

Albacete, Spain

properties.name: Átalakuló Wekerle – Transition Wekerle
properties.description: Átalakuló Wekerle: Komposztbarát Kerület hírlevél - 2020. szeptember "Először nem vesznek tudomást rólad, aztán kinevetnek, aztán harcolnak ellened, aztán győzöl." Gandhi 2020. szeptember 16., szerda Komposztbarát Kerület hírlevél - 2020. szeptember Kedves Komposztbarátok! Ahogy a régi kertészek mondták, a tavaszt ősszel kell előkészíteni. Ősszel is sok mindent ültetünk, nem csak tavasszal, és az ültetéshez mindig jól jön a komposzt: gyümölcsfákat, bogyósgyümölcsű bokrokat (szeder, málna, ribizli, áfonya vagy egres), virághagymákat, de ilyenkor ültethetjük szét vagy át az íriszeket és a pünkösdi rózsát is. Sőt, ültethetünk fokhagymát is! Az érett komposztot az év folyamán bármikor kitermelhetjük, amikor szükség van rá, de a legtöbben tavasszal vagy ősszel szokták. Ilyenkor a komposztáló teljes tartalmát kiszedjük, rostáljuk, a kész komposztot felhasználjuk, a még le nem bomlott anyagokat pedig visszatesszük a keretbe. Ha ősszel nincs szükségünk a kitermelt komposztra, mert éppen nem ültetünk semmit, akkor bezsákoljuk tavaszig: semmiképp se nejlonzsákba, mert abban nem szellőzik! Erről az előző hírlevelekben többször is olvashattatok. Miben komposztáljunk? Mostanában sokan kérdezték, hogy miben komposztáljanak, ha lemaradtak az önkormányzat őszi komposztláda-osztásáról. (Túl sok volt a jelentkező, de várólistára vagy a jövő évi osztásra bármikor fel lehet iratkozni a Kispesti Önkormányzat Zöldprogram Irodájának elérhetőségein.) A kereskedelemben ötezertől több tízezer forintig kaphatók komposztkeretek, de otthon is elkészíthetjük maradék lécekből, raklapokból, dróthálóból vagy akár téglából is – a téglából vagy lécekből épített falat érdemes hézagosra építeni a szellőzés miatt! A két legegyszerűbb komposztálási módhoz még ennyi sem szükséges. A gödörkomposztáláshoz ásunk egy gödröt, amelybe ugyanúgy vegyesen (száraz, széngazdag és nedves, nitrogéngazdag anyagok) dobáljuk a komposztálnivalót, mintha komposztkeretbe raknánk. Ilyenkor fontos, hogy szellős legyen a gödör

properties.name: Barcelona en Transició
properties.description: A Transition Vision For Barcelona City | Barcelona en Transició Inicio Calendari Enllaços Sobre Vols participar Barcelona en Transició La red mundial de ciudades, barrios, pueblos, islas, valles, bosques,…, en transición Feeds: Entradas Comentarios A Transition Vision For Barcelona City 30 noviembre, 2010 por fuspey Towards a healthier, happier, eco-effective Barcelona. Greening the city, connecting communities, C2C city Many of us in Barcelona en Transició believe that Barcelona city is in a very good position to become an urban leader once again, as it has been before, especially 150 years ago with the Eixample vision of Ildefons Cerdà . Those that are in the position to make calls for how the city moves foreward could take bold steps to try to make the city demonstrate to Europe and the world what a sustainable city could be. We in the Transition group have ideas we wish to share about what such eco urban visions might be… THE SELF-SUFFICIENT CITY: Envisioning the habitat of the future was a recent architectural competition run by IAAC (Institute for Advanced Architecture of Catalonia). A submission from one of the Barcelona en Transició collective, b ased on Transition and Permacultural ideas, was selected as a finalist, published in IAAC´s book, and its 1 minute film summary of the proposal was screened in CCCB . 150 word Summary of the proposal and the 1 minute film screened in CCCB : Create urban greenway / CPUL mesh Develop more bikeways/ walkways to easily connect from within city centre to nature: rivers, sea, surrounding hills. Allow all abandoned spaces to become indefinite temporary gardens. Specify strategic zones as permanent community gardens, including within all existing public parks and schools. Connect these nodes by greenways . Turn greenways into CPUL´s (Continuous Productive Urban Landscapes), Plant fruit and nut trees, berry hedges, diverse vegetation, food. These bio threads allow nature into the city; butterflies, song birds, pollen. Use ci

properties.name: Cardedeu en Transició
properties.description: Fira de Sant Isidre 2018 | Cardedeu en Transició Com en els últims anys, Cardedeu en Transició ha tingut parada pròpia a l’ Espai d’Agroecologia de la Fira de Sant Isidre de Cardedeu , que va tenir lloc els dies 19-21 de maig. Volem donar les gràcies a totes les persones que van visitar la nostra paradeta i van interessar-se en el projecte de Cardedeu en Transició i en els de grups afins al nostre, com De Gota en Gota , Cardedeu Comestible i A Cardedeu en bici i a peu . Arran de la nostra presència a la fira vam aconseguir que un bon grapat de gent s’inscrivís al full per rebre informació sobre les nostres activitats, que de bon segur en seran moltes. Per cert, si us fixeu en les fotos que us adjuntem veureu les boniques bicicletes verdes que A Cardedeu en bici i a peu i Cardedeu en Transició estem preparant per a un nou projecte molt interessant que combinarà bicis, llibres i biblioteques. Esteu atents tots i totes a les #lliurecletes que arribaran aviat a Cardedeu!!! properties.name: Challenge Lab
properties.description: Thesis 2014: Design of a Sharing Platform for Objects in Gothenburg – Challenge Lab Skip to content This thesis consists of two parts. Part I describes the Challenge Lab method, which is a method developed at Chalmers for students to utilize their creativity to come up with innovative solutions for a change towards a more sustainable society. The Challenge Lab was carried out between January and June 2014 by 12 graduate students from seven countries. It used several tools, perspectives, theories and frameworks that should enable the students to act as change agents between academia, public and private sector by introducing solutions for a sustainable transport system. Amongst other methods, the Challenge Lab included dialogue tools, value and leadership workshops, design thinking and backcasting. In Part II of this thesis, a system to reduce the environmental impact of each individual, called a sharing economy, is described and a convenient and accessible technological solution to implement in the public space is illustrated. Through practical methods such as workshops and participation in the Gothenburg Startup Hack a prototype of a smart box was developed. This system could provide a convenient and accessible solution by offering a service similar to public lockers, which can be opened by using a web service and would be at accessible places in public spaces (bus stops, etc.). During the process a possible partnership with a project in Gothenburg, called “ElectriCity”, was identified and could be developed further on after the end of this thesis. The main barrier of the sharing economy for reaching a mass-market was found to be trust amongst the individuals using the sharing platform. Several possible solutions to provide a trust-worthy service were identified and will be presented in the thesis. These include, amongst others, an insurance pool and a review system. Andreas Lehner, 2014 The thesis work is divided to two phases. Phase I focus on Challenge Lab proces

properties.description: AiMaIlleurS Les Gites du Centre d'Ailleurs Accueil Il était une fois... L'histoire du lieu Pierre Eychart Laurent Violet Gîtes Maison de Pierre Le Baroudeur des Mers Studios du Forez > Studio Poésie Studio Martin Studio Bouddha Studio La Galipote Studio Laurent Violet Tarifs + d'infos Activités Plouc's Burger Contact Blog NATURE Le Centre d'Ailleurs côté Nature > Le plus gros Calocèdre de France Le Centre d'Ailleurs côté Nature AiMaIlleurS Les Amis du Ver de terre Bienvenue sur la page d' A I M A I LLEUR S , les Amis du Centre d'Ailleurs ! Dernières infos... 05/03/2025 - Le Centre d'Ailleurs est sanctuarisé pour 99 ans ! En 2026, AIMAILLEURS DEMANDE LE DÉCLASSEMENT DE NOTRE AMI RENARD. Vous aussi, participez, merci ! 25 et 26 mai - Fête de la Nature 2024 5 décembre 2023 : Projet AIMAILLEURS réussi grâce au Budget Écologique et Citoyen du Puy-de-Dôme ! Bonjour, Vous étiez 413 Ami(e)s du Centre d'Ailleurs à avoir voté pour le projet d'AIMAILLEURS au BEC (Budget Écologique et Citoyen) du Puy-de-Dôme. Grâce à vous, nous avons pu réaliser tous les projets prévus : création de la mare, achat des nichoirs pour les martinets (fabuleux oiseaux menacés), plantation d'un verger avec des espèces locales, réalisation de panneaux signalétiques pour guider les petits et grands visiteurs du parc et édition des 300 premières fiches de recensement de la faune et la flore du site. Découvrez dans cette vidéo les différentes étapes réalisées grâce à votre soutien. Toute l'équipe d'AIMAILLEURS vous remercie ! Une inauguration sera prochainement programmée en 2024. Vous pouvez nous suivre ou nous contacter sur lecentredailleurs.com Prenez soin de vous et bonnes fêtes de fin d'année ! properties.name: People into Permaculture
properties.description: People into Permaculture | Magnetic Ideals Skip to content People into Permaculture is a community organisation that takes a people-centred approach to permaculture. Encouraging us to apply principles of permaculture to our lives, relationships and society with the goal of contributing to personal, social and planetary well-being. Our main project is Grow Your Life! growyourlife123@gmail.com Twitter @peepintoperma We are running two free Grow Your Life ‘Little Things’ workshops at Hove Cornerstone Community Centre with @MagneticIdeals Come and learn about the basics of how you can live more sustainably and connect with nature in a way that doesn’t cost the earth! A chance to problem solve with others and make life more manageable. We are running one on 15th Feb … and one on 22nd Feb People into Permaculture Aims: Embedding permaculture philosophies of people care, earthshare and fareshare to connect people, food and the environment Create a community and supportive network of marginalised groups which supports and develop individual skills of community members Creating diverse engagement within the community while supporting sustainable and creative practices Working with other organisations to help create bespoke accessibility and diversity in practical ways Challenging sexism, racism, transphobia and all forms of discrimination in everything we do Many projects centre on the important work of eco-design and learning to grow food and be self-sufficient. However, many people do not have access to the money, resources, gardens, space or time to participate in these activities. Such activities may not be accessible to people of diverse abilities and marginalised groups. People into Permaculture want to recognise the holistic approach of permaculture and aim to bring it to people of all backgrounds and abilities together and promote the interpersonal elements of permaculture. We recognise that what others see as weaknesses in the group are where the learning comes to s

Remove all cookies A day out for the whole family Free entry Tues – Sat: 9:30am – 5pm (Café 10am - 4pm) Sun (whole site): 10am – 4pm Dog friendly Cyclists welcome Download trail map Accessibility guide In Market Drayton, north Shropshire, we are England’s first community-owned farm 1 farmer, 8,000 landlords The Fordhall Community Land Initiative aims to show that low-input organic farming, connected to local communities, offers a viable way of life for generations to come. Following the dedication and passion of the late, Arthur Hollins, Fordhall Farm has been chemical free for over 80 years. ‍ Arthur's son and tenant farmer, Ben Hollins, rears pasture-fed, organic cattle and sheep on an outdoor grazing system called Foggage farming - plus a few fun-loving, free-range pigs - whilst the community use the same land for education, relaxation, well-being and fun. ‍ A partnership that works both ways! ‍ Community-owned, Family-run Following a high-profile campaign in 2006, Fordhall Organic Farm became community-owned. properties.name: Tuttle Law Group
properties.description: Tuttle Law Group. ### **Services to Cooperatively Build a Better World** ## Tuttle Law Group provides comprehensive legal services across the lifecycle of cooperative enterprises, from formation to dissolution. ### **Formation** Advise and counsel on entity formation. We provide initial formation advice for clients deciding whether to form a cooperative, or some other type of entity. We guide clients to select the correct entity to meet their business and legal needs. A typical entity formation consultation includes, advice on entity choice and formation requirements, capital structure, employment law compliance and securities law compliance; drafting governance documents (articles of incorporation, bylaws, member agreements); drafting employee handbooks; drafting subscription and investment agreements; securities compliance. In addition we provide advice and referrals to tax advisors to ensure that your business correctly creates and implements payroll and other accounting systems at the outset of your operations. In some cases other entity types may be more appropriate; in these cases, we also support clients in the creation of democratic, cooperative governance structures. We have expertise in the formation of worker cooperatives, consumer natural foods stores, independent retailer cooperatives, and platform cooperatives. Because each client operates in a different industry, we also provide legal counsel related to your industry—for example research and counsel on the regulatory environment and permitting requirements for your proposed business activity. ### **Conversion** Tuttle Law Group has special expertise in converting businesses to cooperatives. We have represented both business owners looking to sell to their employees, and employee groups, looking to purchase their business and operate it as a cooperative. We advise clients on sale of business entity versus sale of assets, and advise clients on the ongoing relationship between the departing owner/founder and the new coope

properties.description: Accueil - Permaculture sans frontières - Reforestation sans frontières Passer au contenu Permaculture sans frontières – Reforestation sans frontières L'efficacité + l'éthique · · · · · · · · Orchestrer les processus naturels pour produire en abondance et régénérer la Terre Rechercher: Prendre soin de la Terre - Prendre soin des personnes et bâtir la communauté - Reforester massivement Nous soutenir - Faire un don à l'association Accueil contremaitre 2025-09-06T15:10:50+02:00 Nos vidéos sur "YouTube" Depuis novembre 2024, nous avons publié quelques vidéos sur notre chaîne YouTube . Lire la suite... Au cœur de la permaculture et des systèmes régénératifs Version 2.16 Au coeur de la permaculture et des systèmes régénératifs par Éric Escoffier, fondateur de Permaculture sans frontières “ La forêt précède les peuples, le désert les suit. ” (auteur inconnu) “ The major problems in the world are the result of the difference between how nature works and the way people think. ” (Gregory Bateson) “ Wealth is a deep understanding of the natural world. ” (Bill Mollison) Une petite histoire de la Terre et de la Vie Photosynthèse Comme tout animal, l'humain est trop faiblement équipé sur le plan génétique pour créer la matière organique qui le constitue. Les ressources que nous consommons sont donc fabriquées par les végétaux , qui sont capables de transformer la matière minérale en matière organique. Cette prouesse chimique, la photosynthèse la réalise depuis plus de 2 milliards d'années. Elle a lieu dans la plus extraordinaire - non, la plus naturelle ! - des usines : la feuille . properties.description: Association LESA | Liens Enseignemens Sens et Autonomie Association LESA Nouveau site Découvrez notre nouveau site internet : www.asso-lesa.com Cliquez ici pour le découvrir dès maintenant ! Le présent site ne sera plus utilisé. Merci de bien vouloir mettre à jour sur vos sites et dans vos contacts les coordonnées de notre nouveau site : www.asso-lesa.com Vous pouvez également nous contacter en cliquant ici. Nouveau site ! Cliquez : www.asso-lesa.com Propulsé par WordPress.com. Confidentialité & Cookies : Ce site utilise des cookies. En continuant à utiliser ce site, vous acceptez leur utilisation. Pour en savoir davantage, y compris comment contrôler les cookies, voir : Politique relative aux cookies Association LESA S’inscrire Connexion Copier lien court Signaler ce contenu Gérer les abonnements Chargement des commentaires… Écrire un commentaire... E-mail Nom Site web Concevoir un site comme celui-ci avec WordPress.com Commencer ##### **Le volontariat** De manière générale, être volontaire permet d’apprendre des savoir-faire en se voyant confier une mission allant de six mois à un an au sein d’une association. Le volontaire n’est pas un salarié mais il reçoit néanmoins une indemnisation pour son logement et sa nourriture. ##### **Le service civique** Le service civique a remplacé le service militaire en France. Il est proposé à des jeunes gens de moins de 26 ans domiciliés en France. Leur participation financière est prise en charge par l’État français. ##### **Le SVE (service volontaire européen)** Le SVE est un volontariat européen proposé à des jeunes européens de moins de 30 ans. Chaque volontaire européen doit trouver une structure d’envoi dans son pays d’origine. Le financement est européen mais c’est la structure d’accueil qui reçoit l’argent avant de le reverser au volontaire pour son logement, sa nourriture et son argent de poche. Nous accueillons des volontaires européens depuis le 1er mai 2013. ##### **Missions confiées:** 1) en écoconstruction: – réali

properties.name: Alôsnys-Par Nature
properties.description: Alôsnys, éco-centre pédagogique et de formation en permaculture - Formation longue, stage permaculture dans un jardin pédagogique en Bourgogne VIVEZ AU CŒUR DE LA NATURE un grand moment d'ouverture des sens et de découvertes au domaine d'Alôsnys en Bourgogne Site pédagogique de découverte et d'apprentissage en permaculture, agro-biologie et biodynamie. VISITE SEULEMENT SUR RDV AU 06.31.56.94.36 (groupe de 10 pers. Min.) Découvrez le domaine d'Alôsnys, parc de 3.5 ha avec son jardin mandala , ses parcs d'animaux (perruches, poules, chèvres, chevaux,...), son espace formation/École, le chemin pieds-nus en forme d'escargot , sa grande collection d'aromatiques, ses plantes médicinales et son potager. ÉVÈNEMENT A VENIR Juin Cueillette et commande des cassis et groseilles ------ Stage s'initiation à la permaculture le 28.03.2026 Plus dinfos En groupe, pour petits et grands: VISITER en groupe de tout âge (seulement sur RDV) APPRENDRE classes vertes et CLSH SE FORMER en permaculture TELECHARGER NOS PANNEAUX PEDAGOGIQUES Rucher et verger SAVOURER LA VIE Stage de détente et de reconnexion 5 jours + soirées: Se ressourcer avec initiation au jardin permacole, séance détente et cuisine vivante du potager Infos & inscriptions COMMANDER VOS PLANTS En mars et récupération des commandes en mai au jardin. OFFRIR UN BON CADEAU UTILE PLEIN DE DECOUVERTE pour noël ou un anniversaire VIDÉOS SUR ALÔSNYS ------------------------ Le contenu YouTube ne s'affiche pas en raison de vos paramètres des cookies actuels. properties.name: Malé'Va
properties.description: MALE'VA | Pratiques écologiques, solidarité locale et internationale Sauter à la navigation Aller au contenu principal Sauter au sidebar principal Sauter au sidebar secondaire Sauter au pied de page MALE'VA Pratiques écologiques, solidarité locale et internationale Accueil Présentation Symbolique de notre logo « l’homme palétuvier » Présentation de la Permaculture Trombinoscope Male’va Actions Maisons semences Les conférences Les formations Permatour Projet « plantes à nous » Articles-actus An 2021 An 2020 An 2019 An 2018 Documentation Livret plantes sauvages comestibles Calendriers semis et récolte Nouvelle-Calédonie et calendrier lunaire Formations et fichiers gratuits en PDF / Livres recommandés Liens vidéo sur la permaculture et l’environnement Cours de Design Certifié de Permaculture (PDC) Paniers/produits/services et Woofing Adhésion Adhérer à Male’va Faire un Don Sympathisants et bénévoles Contact Recherche Accueil Du changement en 2023 Notre site web est en pleine réorganisation : cette vieille version ne sera plus mise à jour, mais elle conservera nos précieuses archives. Cliquez sur le lien ci-dessous pour accéder au nouveau site ! A bientôt! Nouveau site web Depuis 2011, MALE’VA regroupe toutes les personnes impliquées dans une recherche et des activités visant l’amélioration des conditions de vie de l’être humain, dans son environnement naturel. Autosuffisance et Souveraineté alimentaire sont à la base de nos réflexions . properties.name: Sandrine et René JACQUET
properties.description: LES PRODUCTEURS DU PAYS ONT LEUR BOUTIQUE - Villeréalinfos.fr Lundi 29 juin 2026 - A Villeréal il est 06:02 - www.villerealinfos.fr Retour 29/04/2016 Pays' en direct › LES PRODUCTEURS DU PAYS ONT LEUR BOUTIQUE « Pays' en Direct », la Boutique des producteurs du pays, est devenue réalité, depuis le mardi 12 avril. Elle a ouvert ses portes à la place de l’ancien magasin Gitem, au carrefour de la route de Castillonnès. Une grosse trentaine d’agriculteurs – exclusivement locaux – y apportent les produits de leur ferme. Un vaste choix de qualité et un rapport-prix compétitif... L'enseigne ne laisse aucun doute sur son objet que l'on pourrait résumer ainsi : « Directement de la ferme à l'assiette ». C'est ce que les économistes nomment : les circuits courts. En l'absence d'intermédiaires, le produit - de qualité première - permet au producteur d'être justement rémunéré sans surcoût pour le consommateur. Le concept n'est pas nouveau mais il fallait que chacun ait mûri sa réflexion pour enfin parvenir à regrouper énergies et moyens. L'initiative revient à Sandrine Jacquet installée en maraîchage bio avec René, à "Laspissottes", à Doudrac. Elle a trouvé une réponse positive auprès de plusieurs paysans du Villeréalais et au-delà, dont les Deblache (Lire le portrait page 7). Le projet a plu, à tel point qu'il regroupe déjà une trentaine d'adhérents, exclusivement des producteurs de la région. La qualité de l'offre est à la mesure : viande, légumes, lait, fromage, huiles, farine, pâtes, vins, miel, confitures et même produits cosmétiques. Ici, la - fameuse - traçabilité ne pose pas problème : on sait d'où vient ce que l'on achète et l'on connait le savoir-faire du producteur concerné. Pays'en direct est ouverte du mardi au samedi de 8h30 à 12h30 et de 15h30 à 19h30 ainsi que le dimanche matin. 29/04/2016 Flux RSS Mentions Légales Votre Message

properties.name: Abastan Community Space
properties.description: Abastan Community Space. **What is Abastan?** Abastan is an international community of artists and volunteers coming together in the small town of Tumanyan amidst the mountains of northern Armenia. Our mission is to nurture an inspiring and supportive environment where creativity can flourish and where participants can find what they need most, whether healing serenity or exchange of ideas and collaboration. Our practice is guided by low-waste, low-cost, and context-sensitive making, attentive to where we are, and where we are is always a meeting point of many worlds. **What Do We Do?** _Learning and Creating Together_ We organize hands-on workshops and courses where people of all ages learn to create with what is at hand. From crafting musical instruments and toys to designing useful everyday objects, participants explore practical skills while discovering new ways of thinking about materials, waste, and art. Abastan is also a gathering place for artists and makers who spend time with us while pursuing their own projects. There is always room for new people, spontaneous ideas, and surprising collaborations! _Nurturing a Community_ Abastan is not a rigid institution but a community based on trust, friendship, and generosity. We work together and we have fun together. We create an environment of playfulness where everyone feels safe to try and fail, and where people from around the world can find solace in turbulent times. _Transforming Ordinary Spaces into Places of Creativity_ We bring exhibitions, workshops, theater and music performances into homes, gardens, bus stops, and other everyday spaces, showing that creativity belongs everywhere and shouldn't be confined to formal cultural institutions. _Caring for the Environment_ We practice low-waste living and encourage a deeper connection to the local environment. In our work, we draw on materials from our surroundings: we shape ceramics from local clay, make pigments from soils, stones, and plants, design paper with dried plants we collect

properties.name: Acorn Community Farm
properties.description: Acorn Community Farm is an egalitarian community in central Virginia founded in 1993. We are committed to non-coercive, non-hierarchical, voluntary associations both within our community as well as within the larger community in which we find ourselves. We are also committed to income-sharing, sustainable living, and creating a vibrant, eclectic culture. We are members of the [Federation of Egalitarian Communities]( which means that we hold in common our land, labor, resources, and income, and that we use this for the good of our community as a whole and our members as individuals. While all members must be enthusiastic about a culture of radical sharing and working with one another, we do not share religious, political, or philosophical beliefs. We use Consensus Decision making. Acorn is non-hierarchical. There are no bosses, owners, investors, or supervisors. Although structured in areas such as membership, we intentionally keep policies to a minimum, preferring a calm anarchy to prevail. Of the few policies that are in place, our culture encourages personal responsibility rather than supervision, as well as taking issues on a case-by-case basis keeping in mind that needs of individuals vary. Our community strives to create a stimulating social, political, feminist and intellectual environment. Acorn strongly desires to support developing egalitarian and like-minded communities through participation in the Federation of Egalitarian Communities.Our collectively owned business has been active in protesting GMO seed contamination by Monsanto. We desire to support queer and alternative lifestyles and gender expression.

properties.name: ada
properties.description: ada. ## [על תנורים וטעויות]( באדמאמא היו הרבה תנורי אדמה. כשהעץ היה בחינם והחשמל יקר ותמיד היה עוד מישהו שהיה חייב לנסות לבנות את המילה האחרונה בתנורים. חוץ מתנורי אדמה היו גם תנורי שמש וכירת רוקט סטוב (בפוסט אחר)תנורי האדמה באדמאמא לימדו אותנו הרבה דברים:לחם שנאפה בתנור עצים יכול להיות או שרוף או נפלא, לרוב אין באמצע.יום שאפו בו לחם היה יום שרק אפו בו לחם ועם כמות העבודה שהושקעה בלישה, התפחה, חימום התנור והשגחה עליו גם אם היה יוצא שרוף היה נאכל.לחם ממחמצת הוא טוב יותר בתיאוריה, לרוב הוא יוצא ממש דחוס גם אם המחמצת היא הצאצאית של המחמצת המיתולוגית של שושנה אמא של רזי.שעור ראשון: בבואכם לבנות תנור שימו לב לכיוון הרוחאת תנור האפייה הראשון באדמאמא בנינו יחד עם איתי האובן ונועה פטישי ועופר וגלית. הוא נבנה די רחוק מהמטבח המרכזי והיה צריך לסחוב את מגשי הלחם עד למטה. הוא היה בנוי מחבית חתוכה שצופתה באדמה, את האש הדליקו מתחת לחבית מה שאומר שכל האש והעשן היו באותו צד של תא האפיה. משום מה התנור נבנה מצידו הצפוני של השביל מה שאומר שכשהרוח היא צפון מערבית, שזה רוב הזמן, מי שטיפל בתנור חטף במדוייק את כל העשן.שעור שני: כיפת אדמה אפילו עם שמן צריכה גגהתנור השני נבנה כבר במעגל האש והיה קרוב יותר למטבח אבל עדיין לא ממש כי היה צריך לרדת במדרגות תוך כדי איזון המגש הענקי של הבצק לחצות את המעגל ולהגיע לתנור שנבנה על ספסל האדמה שהקיף את מעגל האש. התנור הזה היה כבר משוכלל יותר. התנור נבנה מלבני אקרשטיין מסביב לתיבת מתכת ובה מקום לכמה תבניות. ובנו אותו הילה כהן, נונה, איתי האובן ועופר שני. המקום שלו נבחר בקפידה בהתייחס לכיוון הרוח ואת העצים הכניסו מהצד והפתח של התנור היה מקדימה. היה לו אפילו מנגנון לסגירה ופתיחה של הארובה.טיח האדמה שכיסה את כיפת התנור בידיהן הטובות של הילה ונונה צופה בשמן הכי טוב, אך בכל זאת מים ואדמה לא הולכים טוב ביחד ושוב ושוב היה צריך לתקן את הטיח. חשבנו לבנות לו גגון אבל ויתרנו.משפחת בן נון היתה אופה בו חלות בכל שישי ואדם סלוואי היה אופה בו את החלות המסורתיות של טקסי טו בשבט. [Adam Solowey]( Shany]( כהן-לנג]( Dolev Hauben]( שלישי: אפילו שיש הוראות מדוייקות תמיד יהיה מי שלא יקרא אותםכשחזרנו מהודו ב 2009, מצאנו שנבנה עוד תנור באדמאמא. הפעם היה כבר צמוד למטבח מאחור. תנור יפיפה

properties.name: Agape Community
properties.description: Agape Community. When Brayton’s newly minted book arrived with its shiny flame-design cover and inspiring endorsements on the back, it was a “break open the champagne” moment. For years, Brayton and I weighed the costs in time and energy of writing a book, while leading a full community life with the accompanying dilemma of self-publishing versus seeking a publisher. We are not professional writers, but we did see the value of a chronicle of the Agape story and had been encouraged to write the narrative by many valued friends. Inevitably, we pondered questions: dual authorship, alternating co-authored chapters with distinct, yet overlapping themes; finally, the compelling direction was—write! We decided on Brayton editing some of his “greatest hits” from Servant Song and organizing them thematically. Starting the process was daunting. We put off the task for years, simply writing our Servant Song journal pieces and not tackling “the book”. Eventually our procrastination turned into serious discernment: What is God’s will in such an undertaking? Personally, around the issue of writing, I am haunted by demons of doubt. “Why would anyone want to read about our ‘little’ Agape experiment?” Or, ”We aren’t really writers.” “Do we want to write because everyone else is doing it?” Seeking guidance, I returned to Merton’s words on writing: “I am here to learn humility and how to do God’s will and to serve God the best way I can and writing has something to do with these things. …” (Echoing Silence, p. 10). Merton’s words resonated: “If you write for God you will reach many people and bring them joy.” Still, I knew that Agape’s story, and that of the hundreds who comprised it, would not be an overnight sensation. I didn’t think I was writing for self-aggrandizement, certainly not fame. Merton again: “If you write only for yourself, you can read what you yourself have written and after ten minutes you will be so disgusted you will wish that you were dead.” (Ibid. p.198) Disgust and self-loathin

properties.name: Aletheia Springs
properties.description: Aletheia Springs. **| February 22, 2023 |** Things here at the Springs have been in full swing lately! In this update, we're putting the call out for volunteers and co-learners to come join us in our springtime **Wildland Tending & Restoration Project**coming up in March and April! And save the dates for our upcoming **Aletheia Community Days**happening in February, March and April– the next one being this coming weekend on Saturday, February 25th. Finally, we'll update you on the **great progress we're making with the Repair to Reopen Campaign and pool restoration work** as we gear up for a strong return guest season opening May 6th! ![Image 1]( **CALL FOR VOLUNTEERS! :: LEARN WILDLAND RESTORATION**Come learn about tending our native Northern California oak, madrone and bay woodlands to increase biodiversity, restore habitat, and reduce hazardous fuel loads! Next month in March, we'll be hosting 2 separate Saturday work party days here at the Springs as part of our new **Wildland Tending & Restoration Project**! All levels of experience are welcome. A delicious lunch will be provided - likely from El Molino Central! To **reserve your spot** on **Saturday, March 25th** AND/OR **Saturday, April 1st**(and a save-the-back-up-date on **April 15th**), from **9am-1pm** (lunch at 1pm), please[**sign-up HERE**]( by the expertise and instruction of arborists and restoration professionals from the [Sonoma Ecology Center]( we'll be learning how to care for and be in deeper relation with our wildlands.This restoration project is made possible by two grants from the [Community Foundation Sonoma County]( the [Jack London Yacht Club]( We hope to continue this important restoration project every spring as we work to gradually restore the entire back hillside areas and the former trail network that traverses this beautiful area! **~****2023 WILDLAND RESTORATION :: VOLUNTEER DAYS****~** **SATURDAY, MARCH 25****&****SATURDAY, APRIL 1*********9am~1pm****• Learn about restoration • All experience levels

properties.name: ALT TERRA COMMONS
properties.description: ALT TERRA COMMONS. ### Become A Volunteer Details ### ~ Help Us Complete Our Project Goals ~ ### ​ ### "Making A Real Difference" Hello Friends - Potential Helpers / Consultants As you know, Alt Terra Commons is "Doing Community Differently", and will become like an Independent Village, a Small City all to itself, with most of the same needs, basic infrastructure, social framework and operating jobs of a typical small town. And because we are Pre-Designing most of our Systems and Programs now, there are plenty of Volunteer *Work opportunities for you to get involved in immediately. This means that whether you are a Layperson Helper or Professional Consultant or Aspiring Student, as long as you are an Intentional Community Enthusiast, you can Start Helping, Volunteer to Work for Free, right now, Today! ​ Volunteer Opportunities: ​ 1. Online *Work - complete Research Projects. This program is designed for those who want to Work From Home doing R esearch and completing Proposals. There are a wide variety of Community Projects, that need to be completed before we all move in together. This work requires that you have Research Skills along with applicable Intentional Community Education and/or Experience, plus a Computer with Internet Access. ​ 2. Marketing *Work - promote our Website. This program is designed for those who, are willing to Share ATC With Others. There are Two Options; A) Promotional Page Link - simply direct your Social Media Friends, via an ATC Link, to our Marketing Promotional Page. And/or B) Official Ad- advertise ATC anywhere you like, either for Free or at Your Own Expense, in Print Media, Email, Internet Sites or other creative ways. ​ 3. Fundraising *Work - promote our Financial Goals. This program is designed for those who, are willing to Ask Others To Support ATC. There are Two Options; A) Promotional Page Link - simply direct your Social Media Friends, via an ATC Link, to our Fundraising Promotional Page. And/or B) Official Campaign- start Raising Funds on behal

properties.name: AMA-GI ECO-VILLAGE (SWITZERLAND/SAN MARINO/ICELAND/VATICAN CITY)(C4TWC)
properties.description: AMA-GI ECO-VILLAGE (SWITZERLAND/SAN MARINO/ICELAND/VATICAN CITY)(C4TWC). ## **Our first Village:** Amagi’s Hidden Valley. a new model of living What if you could live in a place that feels like home, works like a startup, and grows like a movement? We’re building a village in a place you already love—where nature, community, and opportunity converge; co-designed with you. ![Image 1]( ![Image 2]( ![Image 3]( ![Image 4]( ![Image 5]( ## A Hidden Sanctuary for **Your Family**, A Shortcut to Their Future. Discover the rare balance of Amagi's Hidden Valley: A serene community for deep connection, located just 5 minutes away from the island's international schools. ![Image 6]( ## Videos Gallery [Video 7]( ## An innovative economic model ![Image 7]( Below you’ll find two ways to explore Amagi. Start with our Amagi AI chatbot or dive deeper into the full white paper when you’re ready. ![Image 8]( ### Hybrid model Invest in land, live in the village, and become a co-owner over time. **Many ways to earn equity in the village over time.** ![Image 9]( ### Balanced ecosystem Affordable eco-conscious living with nature, innovation, and community working in synchronicity. ![Image 10]( ### Community oriented Cultural and entrepreneurial programs and spaces that helps drive community engagement of people who share similar values. ## Amagi's Blog ## FAQ Amagi is a regenerative village model that combines long-term living, shared stewardship of land, and a living economic system. It is designed to support people who want stability, autonomy, and meaningful contribution without the pressure of conventional home ownership or extractive development. Amagi exists to address three failures at once: unaffordable housing, fragile communities, and extractive development. We are building a practical alternative where people can live securely, contribute meaningfully, and participate in shared upside—while regenerating land, culture, and local economies. Amagi is the first recorded word for freedom, originating in ancient Sumer. It roughly translates to “return to th

properties.name: Arboretum Cohousing
properties.description: Arboretum Cohousing. ## Welcome #### March 5, 2024 ### [![Image 1]( ### **Some thoughts from members** **_Cohousing is_**… A community of friends, family and neighbors. Never boring. Challenging. **_Surprising things about cohousing_**… There is always someone who knows the answer. How much fun it is. How much I enjoy the kids and chickens. How many community and public events we have. How much work it takes. People will actually eat lentil loaf. It’s all surprising! [![Image 2: photo of front of Erin House looking east]( Erin House at Arboretum Cohousing [![Image 3: Photo of Georgette B and baby]( Young and old together **_Commonly heard phrases at Arbco…_** Any volunteers? A week without a meeting just wouldn’t feel right. Has anyone seen the …..? Lentil loaf? Can anyone take me to the airport … bus … game … rally? **_What NOT to do at Arbco…._** Sweat the small stuff. Move the recliner. Micro-manage. [![Image 4: DSCN0060]( Chickens not eating onions. Feed onions to the chickens. **_If Arbcoteers were animals, they would be…_** Badgers … termites… bees… meerkats … prairie dogs … herds of cats.

properties.name: Arcadia Cohousing
properties.description: Arcadia Cohousing. * [Home]( * [What is Cohousing?]( * [Neighbors]( * [Arcadian Businesses]( * [Homes for Sale/Rent]( * [Photos and Videos]( * [Events]( * [Contact]( * [Link Page]( * [New Page]( This area is password protected [401] This area is password protected Please enter the password below Cohousing is a collaborative, consensus-based style of living, in which residents have an active voice in the operation and maintenance of the community. The physical layout of the neighborhood enables an individual to experience privacy and space as well as a full social life. Most cohousing communities include common areas such as a common house, playgrounds, gardens, and shared open land. The common house is the social center, where residents host meals, clubs, and parties, hold meetings, etc. The layout of homes is often a clustered pattern, with the common areas in the general center, connected by pedestrian paths. Originated in Denmark in the early 1980s, the idea of cohousing has quickly spread all over the globe and there are now hundreds of cohousing communities worldwide. The average size is 20-40 households, though this can vary significantly. Even in the largest communities, almost all residents form friendships, as each member's opinions and input are valued and respected by all peers. A main goal of cohousing is to create support and cooperation between residents of all ages. Cohousing actively provides solutions for many large issues facing society today. Relationships are fostered by teamwork and sharing of activities, raising quality of life and promoting good health. Cohousing is extremely sustainable, as shared resources reduce energy usage and cost. Many homes utilize progressive energy sources such as solar panels, and all alterations to the community are undertaken with the health of the environment in mind. Carpooling and sharing of transportation is also a frequent occurrence. In general, life is more enjoyable and relaxed; members trust each other and the close proximi

properties.name: Atelier Farm
properties.description: Atelier Farm Search this site Embedded Files Skip to main content Skip to navigation Atelier Farm & Commons Life at Human Scale A co-housing commons for a farm-y life of balance and presence; or, how to try to live well before we die. The Vision A small neighborhood / ecovillage of farmers, artisans, professionals, and just-trying-to-be-and-do good'ers who like it simple but still appreciate the mess of life, to live in a balanced, present, walkable, human-scale way, perfused by town and country. Bubbles exist, whether by intention or not; while we're still alive, let's make a beautiful one. The Nutshell Real Estate Details : 3061 Essex Rd., Willsboro, NY 12996 Location : Northeastern New York, Lake Champlain, Tiny-Town Rural, Essex NY Size : 40 5 Acres, $250-300k land + $100-300k construction (loan?) + start-up. Folks : 7-8 households, all ages , non-dogmatic, startled by the fact of being. Structure : A larger commons (~36 5 ac.) that we watch over , via a Land and Property LLC, to oversee the lease to as many or few farm operations as we wish to envision , build, or foster . Private ownership of smaller lots (~ 4 ac.) with HOA and easements, as required, to glue the whole. Our work : To make the world more good and beautiful. Likely hyphenated and often on-site in our vocations. Farm : 5-10 acres sand open to fruit & vegetable; 250 acres open clay to sheep, hay, staples, apiary, and fruit and nut trees. Farming : Whole-life considerate, often part-time, beyond organic, independent and/or collaborative. Well-suited to pasture, haying, apiary, orchard, small-fruit, and vegetables. Fundamentally considerate of life and death. Living : Private/Familial/Social Balance, Walkable, Phone-free-friendly, Natural, Simple, Agricultural Kitchen : Drug & Alcohol-free, Vegetarian'is h / moral meat, Ayurvedically-informed. Non-judgmental, non self-identifying vegans, vegetarians, omnivores, and carnivores all welcome. Status : Pre-purchase & pre-plan approval. We're looking for

properties.name: Avalon Community Land Trust Cooperative House
properties.description: Avalon Community Land Trust Cooperative House. Avalon Community Land Trust 0 Building Community Avalon Community Land Trust emerged from a group of local Bloomington residents who wanted to develop affordable and sustainable housing. We want housing that will support our neighbors and contribute to building our community. The ACLT community hopes to build affordable housing that will stay affordable for future generations. ### If you want to be a part of this effort to bring affordable - truly affordable - housing to Bloomington, you can… #### Make a Donation. We have a vision. We have the land. We have the plans, the permits, the zoning. We have closed on our loan and received funding from the Affordable Housing Fund of Bloomington. We are underway! Construction has begun; the basement has been excavated and the walls are in place. The stem walls are going in and the sill plate for the framing is set. The walls will be going up and the house will begin to take shape - very soon!! If you share this vision too, and want to play a part, you can make a donation to our building fund. We are a 501.c3, so it’s fully tax deductible. #### Become a Community Collaborator Exciting Times! If you share our vision for Bloomington; if you want affordable housing to remain affordable in perpetuity; if you want people to control their own housing and communities; then join with us. Become a Community Collaborator with the Avalon Community Land Trust. #### Learn More Fine out more about Community Land Trusts: Click below to learn about how they work and how they benefit our community. ![Image 1]( ![Image 2]( Bloomington Cooperative Living Building Community Roots to Fruit ### Cooperative living values ground our work The Avalon Community Land Trust grows out of relationships and experiences rooted in Bloomington’s long history of collective living and community building. Our leadership team of volunteer board members brings skills, inspiration, and lessons from a diverse range of projects and organizations Experience organizing

properties.name: Balsam Moon Preserve
properties.description: Balsam Moon Preserve. Join Us! | Balsam Moon Preserve top of page This website was built on Wix. Create yours today. Get Started Join Us! Become a friend, volunteer, benefactor. We’re a tax-exempt non-profit. Donations are welcome! What We Offer FRIEND : Anyone who participates in the regular programs of BMP may become a "FRIEND" by completing and submitting the form. As a "Friend" we seek your commitment to participate with us in the vision/mission via programs and events and assist in promoting the programs and events to your network of friends & family, as you are able. BENEFACTOR : Anyone who donates/contributes funds beyond that which is applied to a particular class/program. We welcome any amount of donation to aid in the operations of BMP. Balsam Moon is a 501 ( c) 3 public non-profit charity and donations are tax-deductible. VOLUNTEER : Anyone who wishes to volunteer their services in any capacity where there is a specific need, which can be verified by visiting with one of the residents or with a Board member. RESIDENT : A person who applied for short- or long-term residency, accepted and lives in community sharing skills, participating in programs, and maintaining Balsam Moon in all its capacities, in partnership with the Board of Directors. Friend/Volunteer/Benefactor/Resident Form First Name Email City Last Name Phone State I want to become a: * Required Friend Volunteer Benefactor Resident I'm interested in: * Required Sustainability Spirituality Gardening Building Simplicity Self-sufficiency Community Living Cooking Foraging Permaculture Special Events Social Justice Non-violence *By submitting this form to become a member, I pledge to participate in programs/events each year and to help promote Balsam Moon Preserve to my friends & family. Please tell us a little bit about yourself: Thanks for submitting! Submit Prefer to download our form, fill it out, and email or mail it? Click on the button below and download our PDF form. properties.name: Bastion Community of Resilience
properties.description: Bastion Community of Resilience. Ann Theriot - Bastion Skip to content Ann Theriot Board Member Ann Theriot Irwin is a community enthusiast focusing her professional career on making meaningful connections and providing creative solutions. In her various roles, she acts as an executive thought partner and creative thinker translating strategy into measurable results. Her work with community organizations focuses on collaboration, clear messaging, and compelling stories. Ann graduated from LSU with a degree in marketing and received her J.D. from Tulane University School of Law. She is currently the Director of Marketing for Phelps Dunbar, which has offices nationwide and in London. Kimberly’s passion for working with nonprofits and serving the community is evident in her efforts and successful campaign results. She executed the strategic plan with the CFC, with the goal of raising over a million dollars for southern Louisiana and Gulfport regions. Kimberly worked with the Sisters of the Holy Family’s annual Heavenly Host of Stars Gala and secured hundreds of sponsorships to help meet their annual goal. In her hometown of San Diego, California, Kimberly is well-known for her remarkable achievements in the non-profit sector. Before relocating to the Greater New Orleans area in 2018, Kimberly served as Vice President of Community Development for San Diego Community Housing Corporation, an organization for whom she has worked for the previous 22 years. While managing a full staff in this role, Kimberly wrote grants and cultivated relationships to secure sizable, notable donations and met her annual multi-million-dollar fundraising goals. With her skills and expertise, Kimberly secured a $1 million gift for a financial education program from a private donor for the state of California. She also implemented a $25 million dollar grant for California Sustainability and Multi-housing Initiative to install solar panels on affordable housing complexes to help families receive rental subsidies to lower their u

properties.name: Beach "Mommune" in Oxnard!
properties.description: Beach "Mommune" in Oxnard!. ## Affordable, communal housing for women and their children. ![Image 1]( Supporting women and their children through community. Mommune® is an evolving community-centered initiative focused on storytelling, collaboration, cultural programming, and exploring new models of support for women, mothers, children, artists, and families. Through media, partnerships, events, and future community-based concepts, Mommune aims to inspire more connected and sustainable ways of living and supporting one another. ## About MOMMUNE® **How might we improve the daily lives of children and their caretakers so that we raise healthier, happier individuals, communities and a sustainable future?** ## A Mommune is 2 or more women sharing resources and responsibilities to more efficiently care for themselves and their children. **MOMMUNE®** is an evolving initiative exploring and advocating for more affordable, communal, and community-centered living models for women and their children.By putting the best interests of the day-to-day life of a child and their caretaker at the center of decision-making, our systems naturally fall into a sustainable flow. Sharing responsibility and resources efficiently creates abundance, less waste, and more quality time for individuals and relationships. The quality of daily life and stability of a safe and supportive home and community combats the stressors that lead to declines in mental, physical and financial health creating more positively contributing members of society. The Mommune® model provides the soil to heal and grow a better world for us all. According to a 2023 report by the National Alliance to End Homelessness, over 30% of families experiencing homelessness are led by single mothers. These families often lack access to stable housing due to low wages, unaffordable childcare, and insufficient social safety nets. Shockingly, the rate of homelessness among women with children has increased by more than 20% over the past decade, driven by econo

properties.name: Bear Creek Community Land Trust
properties.description: Bear Creek Community Land Trust. 2026 Events Skip to content Listing of Events Bear Creek Work Party Weekends- Get to know the Land Trust and its members by joining us for activities like a work party at an off-grid homestead, potlucks, tour, house church, and a Q+A session. No indoor housing, camping only. Suggested donation: $50/person, $150/family , these are prices for an entire weekend. Suggested donation for one day: $25/person, $75 per family . We have work parties and will be hosting visitors this year on the third weekend of each month, April- October, unless dates are noted differently. Please contact us if you would like to join us. April: Friday 17- Sunday 19 May: Friday 15- Sunday 17 June: Friday 19- Sunday 21 July: Friday 17- Sunday 19 August: Friday 14- Sunday 16 September: Friday 18- Sunday 20 October: Friday- 16- Sunday 18 Fall Harvest Dinner- Saturday September 26, 3-6 pm – Location- Bear Creek Land Trust, Leasehold to be determined. Bear Creek Fundraiser! Celebrate the Fall Equinox by supporting your local community land trust, and enjoying an afternoon of food from the land, games, hand-crafted treats from the season and maybe even a few surprises. This event was such a hit the last two years we are hosting it again! Ticket – Suggested Donation $50 adult, kids free . * Please RSVP if you plan to attend any events or have questions. * More Event Information Accommodations — All events will be held primarily outdoors. Visitors will be invited to camp in a beautiful wooded tent camping area. Outhouses will be available at the camping site and at all event locations, which may rotate. Bathing can happen at nearby ponds. Pricing — Event fees cover hosting, materials for any projects, and two delicious meals each day, usually lunch and dinner. Need-based scholarships available upon request. All donations help us build the future of our community and we are grateful for them. properties.name: Beech Grove Bruderhof Community
properties.description: Beech Grove Bruderhof Community. ![Image 1]( ## “And they had everything in common…” We are a community of Christians who, inspired by the early church, share all our money and possessions. We believe that God wants to transform our world, here and now. This takes a life of discipleship and commitment; when you truly love your neighbor as yourself, peace and justice become a reality. * ## Full-Time Discipleship We believe Jesus calls us to dedicate every day of our lives to him, not just Sundays. In community all aspects of life can be acts of worship – [working together, sharing a meal, or singing together.]( * ## Sharing Money and Possessions None of us owns anything personally and everything we need — [food, housing, health care]( — is provided for us. We believe our way of life is a compelling answer to society’s problems, with its emphasis on wealth and self and its resulting isolation, conflict, and inequality. * ## Supporting One Another In our life together[the welfare of the oldest, youngest, and weakest is a shared priority]( family life is treasured, meaningful work is available for everyone, and there is time for laughter, friendship, and children. ![Image 2]( ## Come and See We love welcoming visitors! They bring new energy to our community and we appreciate the opportunity to learn from each other as we work, worship, share meals and times of relaxation together. [**Learn more about visiting and volunteering**]( Other ways to connect: * ## Working Together We contribute our talents and energies in whatever way we can until the end of our lives. No kind of work brings either privilege or stigma. [To work in the service of love is our joy]( * ## Upholding Family Life Children are the heart of our communities, and community life is oriented around [supporting families and welcoming single people.]( * ## Serving Our Neighbors Love of neighbor must be put into practice. We support the works of global nonprofits addressing acute crises and also help out in our local neighborhoods. We cannot

properties.name: BeeLiving Ecovillage/Ace Society
properties.description: BeeLiving Ecovillage/Ace Society. ## Where we are now The ACE (Affordable Community Environment) Society is building BeeLiving Ecovillage. Located in the Silver Valley area of Maple Ridge, we plan to build 24 units consisting of half rental units and half units for purchase in the community. We have secured a 4-acre piece of land that is suited to our vision and have taken significant pre-development steps to confirm its feasibility. The current vision for the Ecovillage consists of small homes (on foundations) with potential spaces for tiny homes. We are exploring housing forms that are best suited to the land’s natural environment and a site design concept from our engaged experts can be viewed by clicking on the link at the top of this page. The community would include many shared amenities, and design with regenerative agriculture for the natural spaces in mind. With the help of affordable housing experts, we are currently completing our feasibility study of the community we intend to build. Our development team will have a pre-application to the city completed shortly. Participation in ACES is separated into 3 categories: Board, Member and Subscriber. [about Subscribers]( [about Members]( [about The Board]( BeeLiving Ecovillage Concept | ACE Society Project Summary We believe the current way housing is built is not designed to meet the challenges of tomorrow for our communities. We believe connection with each other and our environment is the way forward to live lightly on the earth and future housing should account for these needs. Our society is designing and creating resilient community in concert with nature, for this generation and the next. Resilience to us, is to provide ongoing resources to work and share, bond with your neighbours and lessen ongoing expenses for living. Food security and natural defenses from extreme weather are important tools integrated into our community’s design to be resilient now and for future generations. IMPORTANT NOTE: CURRENTLY, WE ARE ONLY IN THE EARLY STAG

properties.name: Bezaleel Israel Eco-Village
properties.description: Bezaleel Israel Eco-Village | Skip to main content The 20-acre permaculture site is roughly half wooded with garden spaces carved out and half is rich bottom land. The BZ Permaculture Farm Collective (BZ Farm) is situated at the Bezaleel Israel Eco-Village. Refer to the navigation menu for links related to the farm. Is access to land preventing you from starting your own permaculture homestead? If so, you should consider joining the Bezaleel Israel Eco-Village. Come visit the farm! Maybe stay. Unique Regenerative Farming/Permaculture Apprenticeship Opportunity Working, Off-the-grid, 20-acre Diversified Permaculture Farm in Northeast Washington State. In Transition to an Agricultural Land Trust/Eco-village, Permaculture Education Center. Long-term residency potential. Link to Application Form Start Living Your Permaculture Lifestyle NOW! We hope you enjoy these flyovers of the BZ Farm from June, 2022 and July 2025. There are 20 acres here, arable soils, mixed forest, water, peace and quiet. Only the western half of the farm is seen in the older video. The whole farm can be seen in the newer one. You could live here too. No buy-in required. We're taking applications now for folks to join our permaculture project. 2022 - Videographer: Nikita Ostrander Your browser does not support the video tag. 2025 - Videographer: Nikita Ostrander; Music by Chrys Ostrander The Bezaleel Israel Eco-Village in far northeast Washington is named after the landowner, affectionately known as "BZ," who has put more than 30 years of effort into developing these 20 acres to support a self-sufficient Permaculture Community Land Trust. This could be your new home! Please don't let the name of the Eco-Village put you off. BZ adopted the name Bezaleel Israel when he joined the Love Family commune more than 50 years ago. All the Love Family members shared the surname Israel and it was often referred to as "Is Real." While the commune is no more, a good number of former Love Family members continue

properties.name: Bhrugu Aranya Ecovillage - Poland
properties.description: Bhrugu Aranya Ecovillage - Poland. **VOLUNTEER** At Ecovillage Bhrugu Aranya we offer positions to people interested in volunteering with us during the warmer months in exchange for accommodation and meals. Over the years many people from all over the world have volunteered at our community, experienced our unique way of living, tasted our organic vegetarian gourmet food, and helped us with our daily work. We welcome you to come and experience our daily working life at Ecovillage Bhrugu Aranya. In the warmer months, we offer volunteer positions to Wwoofers, Hwoofers*, and people interested in learning about our way of living with a practical and hands-on approach.Our volunteers should have a keen work ethic, some experience in farm work, be physically able, and have an interest in Homa Organic Farming. We welcome people with specific skill sets in: * carpentry * various building skills * gardening * landscaping * language translation * general farming experience Please read our [Volunteer FAQ]( to find out more about volunteering at Ecovillage Bhrugu Aranya. *HHOOFERs are Homa Helpers on Organic Farms [Please Contact Us to Enquire about visiting!]( Volunteer Testimonials: Over the years we have had countless volunteers come to our Ecovillage to experience our day to day life. Some feedback from some of those wonderful people you can read [here]( ![Image 1](

properties.name: Big Calm Tiny Homesteads
properties.description: Big Calm Tiny Homesteads. ![Image 1]( Welcome to the newly refreshed Big Calm website! If you’ve ever gone through a website redesign, you know it’s about a lot more than just picking new colours and updating photos. It forces you to sit down and really think about how you talk about what you’re doing. For us, putting words to the vision […] ![Image 2]( You’ve perhaps heard of Dunbar’s Number: 150. Put forward by anthropologist Robin Dunbar, humans can only maintain stable social relationships with around 150 people; a cognitive limit based on the size of the neocortex. While 150 is the most famous figure suggested by Dunbar, there were others. Home Details Video Blog Contact Us ## WELCOME TO BIG FAITH Big Faith is a grassroots ministry where Jesus-followers live together in a historic church building set in the rural Michigan countryside.Participants stay anywhere from 1–12 months, making it a great fit for anyone wanting a season of community, creativity, and spiritual growth. We live together, learn together, and follow Jesus together. This is a nonprofit community built around faith, real relationships, and experiences that stick with you long after you leave. **What you’ll experience:** * A genuinely welcoming, come-as-you-are community * Group activities, thoughtful discussions, & creative projects * Simple, shared living that keeps costs low and life rich## PARTICIPATION COST: PRIVATE ROOM: $475/month ​SHARED ROOM: $375/month This is a place to spend meaningful time with good people. To share life, have honest conversations, and build real friendships. Big Faith offers a living experience where connection, growth, and community actually happen. Our statement of faith is intentionally simple and unifying: We believe that God is the Creator of all people, and that every person is made in His image. We believe that Jesus Christ loves all people, calling us into lives marked by love, humility, and service. We believe the Church is the living Body of Christ, united not by labels, but by faith expressed through love. Big Faith seeks unity, not division — offering a place where residents can grow spiritually while remaining connected to the
